model_requirements.description: Industrial Manufacturing encompasses the design, production, and delivery of automation systems, electrification solutions, and smart infrastructure components for factories, buildings, transportation, and urban environments.

_vibe_session_metadata.summary: Model quality: 91/100 (16 warnings, 0 errors). 129 tables across 12 domains.

model.name: Manufacturing
model.description: Industrial Manufacturing encompasses the design, production, and delivery of automation systems, electrification solutions, and smart infrastructure components for factories, buildings, transportation, and urban environments.
model.core_business_processes: Product Design and Engineering, Production Planning and Scheduling, Materials Procurement and Supply Chain Management, Manufacturing Execution and Shop Floor Control, Quality Assurance and Testing, Inventory and Warehouse Management, Order Management and Fulfillment, Equipment Maintenance and Asset Management, Research and Development, Customer Service and After-Sales Support
model.data_domains: Product Engineering, Production, Supply Chain, Quality, Inventory, Sales and Orders, Customer, Asset Management, Workforce, Finance, Procurement, Logistics
model.common_business_jargons: BOM (Bill of Materials), MES (Manufacturing Execution System), OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), PLC (Programmable Logic Controller), SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition), ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning), MRP (Material Requirements Planning), MRP II (Manufacturing Resource Planning), CNC (Computer Numerical Control), SPC (Statistical Process Control), TPM (Total Productive Maintenance), MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures), MTTR (Mean Time To Repair), WIP (Work In Progress), SKU (Stock Keeping Unit), JIT (Just In Time), KPI (Key Performance Indicator), CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action), NCR (Non-Conformance Report), RMA (Return Material Authorization), ECO (Engineering Change Order), ECN (Engineering Change Notice), CAD (Computer-Aided Design), CAM (Computer-Aided Manufacturing), PLM (Product Lifecycle Management), DCS (Distributed Control System), HMI (Human-Machine Interface), IoT (Internet of Things), IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things), FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), SLA (Service Level Agreement), MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity), LTL (Less Than Truckload), FTL (Full Truckload), ROI (Return on Investment), EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization), CapEx (Capital Expenditure), OpEx (Operational Expenditure), OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer), CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System), APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling), WMS (Warehouse Management System), TMS (Transportation Management System), PDM (Product Data Management), DFM (Design for Manufacturability), DFMEA (Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), PFMEA (Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), Cp/Cpk (Process Capability Indices), Takt Time, Cycle Time, Lead Time, Throughput, Yield Rate, Scrap Rate, Downtime, Changeover Time
model.operational_systems_of_records: SAP S/4HANA (MM; PP; SD; FI; CO; PM; QM; WM), Siemens Teamcenter PLM (Product Lifecycle Management; BOM Management; Document Management), Siemens Opcenter MES (Manufacturing Execution; Shop Floor Control; Scheduling), Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud; Service Cloud; Customer Management), SAP Ariba (Procurement; Supplier Management; Sourcing), Maximo Asset Management (Equipment Maintenance; Work Orders; Asset Tracking), Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management (Demand Planning; Inventory Optimization), Aveva SCADA (Process Control; Real-Time Monitoring; Alarm Management), Workday HCM (Human Resources; Payroll; Talent Management), Informatica MDM (Master Data Management; Data Quality; Data Governance)
model.industry_governing_body: ISO (International Organization for Standardization) - ISO 9001 Quality Management; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; ISO 50001 Energy Management, IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission) - IEC 61131 PLC Programming; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity, OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration), EPA (Environmental Protection Agency), UL (Underwriters Laboratories) - Product Safety Certification, CE Marking (European Conformity), NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology) - Cybersecurity Framework, ANSI (American National Standards Institute), IPC (Association Connecting Electronics Industries) - Electronics Manufacturing Standards, IFRS/GAAP (International Financial Reporting Standards / Generally Accepted Accounting Principles)

domains.name: production
domains.description: Core manufacturing execution domain governing shop floor control, work orders, routing, scheduling, WIP tracking, cycle time, takt time, throughput, and OEE. Integrates with MES (Siemens Opcenter) and ERP (SAP PP) to orchestrate production runs, machine assignments, capacity planning, and shift-level output reporting via SCADA/DCS systems.

products.name: production_work_order
products.description: Core manufacturing work order representing a discrete production job issued to the shop floor. Authorizes manufacture of a specific quantity of finished or semi-finished goods by linking a routing, BOM revision, and production version. Tracks planned vs. actual dates, priority, order status, WIP value, and completion percentage. The SSOT for all production execution activity within this domain. Sourced from ERP production orders (e.g., SAP PP) and MES work order management (e.g., Siemens Opcenter).
products.reference: SAP PP Production Order / Siemens Opcenter Work Order

attributes.references: OEE Quality Component, SAP PP Yield

products.name: schedule
products.description: Master production schedule (MPS) record defining planned production quantities, start/finish dates, and shift assignments for a given production item across a planning horizon. Tracks schedule version, freeze horizon, planning bucket (daily/weekly), and schedule status (draft, released, frozen, revised). Represents the output of APS/MRP II scheduling runs. Sourced from ERP production planning (e.g., SAP PP) and APS systems (e.g., Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain).

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Supply-demand pegging: production schedules for externally sourced or subcontracted items are pegged to purchase orders. This link supports MRP pegging reports, supply chain visibility, and on-time de

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production routing or process plan used for this schedule. Defines the sequence of operations and work centers.

attributes.name: schedule_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.schedule_line. Business justification: MRP pegging: production schedules are generated to fulfill specific order schedule lines (confirmed delivery dates/quantities). This ATP and MRP pegging relationship is fundamental to industrial manuf

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: A production schedule is planned for a specific shift. production_schedule.shift_assignment (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the shift. Adding shift_id as a proper FK normalizes this relationsh

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Master production schedules are created per SKU; linking enables MPS reporting and demand planning.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the work center or production line assigned to execute this schedule.

attributes.name: approval_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this schedule requires management approval before release. True for high-value, high-risk, or exception schedules; false for routine schedules.

attributes.name: cancelled_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this schedule was cancelled. Null if the schedule was not cancelled.

attributes.name: capacity_requirement_hours
attributes.description: Total machine or labor hours required to complete this schedule. Used for capacity planning and load leveling.

attributes.name: completed_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when production for this schedule was completed. Used for schedule performance analysis and cycle time measurement.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and schedule age analysis.

attributes.name: firmed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this schedule is firmed (locked) and should not be automatically rescheduled by planning systems. True means the schedule is manually controlled; false means it can be adjusted by MRP/APS.

attributes.name: freeze_horizon_date
attributes.description: Date beyond which this schedule is frozen and cannot be changed without formal approval. Protects near-term execution from planning volatility.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this schedule record was last updated. Tracks schedule volatility and planning changes.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Total lead time from schedule release to completion, including queue time, setup, run, and move time. Used for order promising and planning.

attributes.name: lot_size_quantity
attributes.description: Standard lot or batch size for this production schedule. May be driven by economic order quantity, equipment constraints, or process requirements.

attributes.name: lot_sizing_rule
attributes.description: Algorithm used to determine production lot sizes. Fixed lot uses a constant quantity; lot-for-lot matches demand exactly; EOQ optimizes ordering costs; POQ aggregates demand over periods.

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: Code identifying the MRP controller or planner responsible for this schedule. Used for accountability and workload distribution.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes and comments from planners regarding special instructions, constraints, or considerations for this schedule.

attributes.name: pegging_reference
attributes.description: Reference to the demand source (sales order, forecast, safety stock) that this schedule is pegged to. Enables traceability from supply to demand.

attributes.name: planned_quantity
attributes.description: Target quantity of the material to be produced according to this schedule. Expressed in the base unit of measure for the material.

attributes.name: planning_bucket
attributes.description: Time granularity of the schedule planning horizon. Daily for short-term detailed scheduling, weekly for medium-term planning, monthly for long-term capacity planning.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_weeks
attributes.description: Number of weeks into the future that this schedule covers. Defines the forward visibility window for production planning.

attributes.name: planning_strategy
attributes.description: Manufacturing strategy governing how this schedule is planned and executed. Make-to-stock builds for inventory; make-to-order builds against customer orders; assemble-to-order configures from standard components; engineer-to-order designs and builds custom products.

attributes.name: priority_rank
attributes.description: Relative priority of this schedule compared to other schedules. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for resource allocation and sequencing decisions.

attributes.name: released_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this schedule was released for execution. Marks the transition from planning to active production.

attributes.name: run_time_hours
attributes.description: Estimated time required to produce the planned quantity, excluding setup and teardown. Used for cycle time analysis.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: Buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Influences schedule timing and quantities.

attributes.name: schedule_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the production schedule. Externally visible schedule reference number used in planning and execution systems.

attributes.name: schedule_source
attributes.description: System or process that generated this schedule. MRP run for material requirements planning; APS optimization for advanced planning and scheduling; manual planning for planner-created schedules; demand forecast for forecast-driven schedules; customer order for order-driven schedules.

attributes.name: schedule_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the production schedule. Draft schedules are under planning; released schedules are active; frozen schedules are locked for execution; revised schedules have been updated; cancelled schedules are voided; completed schedules are finished.

attributes.name: schedule_type
attributes.description: Classification of the schedule by planning level. MPS for top-level finished goods; FAS for final assembly operations; RCCP for high-level capacity validation; MRP for detailed component planning.

attributes.name: scheduled_finish_date
attributes.description: Planned date when production for this schedule is expected to be completed. Used for order promising and delivery planning.

attributes.name: scheduled_finish_time
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when production execution is scheduled to finish, used for detailed capacity loading and sequencing.

attributes.name: scheduled_start_date
attributes.description: Planned date when production for this schedule is expected to begin. Key input for capacity planning and material availability checks.

attributes.name: scheduled_start_time
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when production execution is scheduled to start, including shift and time-of-day information for detailed shop floor scheduling.

attributes.name: setup_time_hours
attributes.description: Estimated time required to set up equipment and tooling before production can begin. Part of total lead time calculation.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters, M for meters).

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version number of the schedule. Incremented each time the schedule is revised or replanned.

products.name: work_center
products.description: Master data entity representing a physical or logical production resource on the shop floor — a machine, assembly cell, production line segment, or labor group — at which manufacturing operations are performed. Captures work center code, plant, cost center linkage, capacity category, available capacity per shift, efficiency rate, utilization rate, and OEE baseline target. The SSOT for capacity planning and routing assignments within the production domain. Sourced from ERP work center master (e.g., SAP CR01).

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the work center. Primary key.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the cost center for financial tracking and allocation of work center expenses.

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Work centers operate under process qualification specifications (welding procedure specs, machine capability studies, PPAP process specs). Linking work centers to their governing engineering specifica

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this work center is located.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Work centers in industrial manufacturing controlling are assigned to profit centers for activity cost allocation and profitability analysis. Standard CO-OM configuration links work centers to profit c

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Line-side stock (supermarket/kanban staging) is assigned per work center in lean industrial manufacturing. Replenishment planning, kanban card generation, and WIP material staging reports all require

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: External/subcontracted work center: in industrial manufacturing, a work center can be designated as an external processing work center permanently assigned to a specific subcontract supplier. This is

attributes.name: available_capacity_per_shift
attributes.description: Standard available capacity of the work center per shift, measured in the capacity category unit.

attributes.name: capacity_category
attributes.description: Unit of measure for capacity planning (e.g., machine hours, labor hours, throughput units).

attributes.name: capacity_planning_group
attributes.description: Grouping code for aggregating work centers in capacity planning and leveling activities.

attributes.name: control_key
attributes.description: Control key defining how operations are processed at this work center (e.g., internal processing, external processing, inspection).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this work center record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: efficiency_rate_percent
attributes.description: Standard efficiency rate of the work center expressed as a percentage, representing the ratio of actual output to theoretical maximum output.

attributes.name: formula_key
attributes.description: Formula key used for calculating operation times and capacity requirements at this work center.

attributes.name: last_modified_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this work center record.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this work center record was last modified.

attributes.name: location_description
attributes.description: Physical location description of the work center within the plant (e.g., Building 2, Floor 3, Bay 5).

attributes.name: mes_integration_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether this work center is integrated with the MES for real-time shop floor control and data collection.

attributes.name: number_of_machines
attributes.description: Count of individual machines or equipment units within this work center.

attributes.name: number_of_operators
attributes.description: Standard number of operators or workers assigned to this work center during normal operation.

attributes.name: oee_baseline_target_percent
attributes.business_glossary_term: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) Baseline Target Percent
attributes.description: Target OEE baseline for this work center, calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality. Used for capacity planning and performance benchmarking.

attributes.name: plc_address
attributes.description: Network address or identifier of the PLC controlling this work center, used for SCADA and MES integration.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether operations at this work center require mandatory quality inspection before proceeding to the next step.

attributes.name: scada_tag_prefix
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag Prefix
attributes.description: SCADA tag prefix used to identify real-time data points from this work center in the process control system.

attributes.name: scheduling_type
attributes.description: Scheduling strategy used for operations at this work center (forward scheduling, backward scheduling, or capacity-only).

attributes.name: standard_processing_time_minutes
attributes.description: Standard processing or cycle time in minutes per unit for operations performed at this work center.

attributes.name: standard_queue_time_hours
attributes.description: Standard queue time in hours representing the typical wait time before an operation begins at this work center.

attributes.name: standard_setup_time_minutes
attributes.description: Standard setup or changeover time in minutes required to prepare the work center for a new production run.

attributes.name: standard_teardown_time_minutes
attributes.description: Standard teardown time in minutes required to complete and clean up after an operation at this work center.

attributes.name: utilization_rate_percent
attributes.description: Target utilization rate of the work center expressed as a percentage, representing the ratio of actual operating time to available time.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: Date from which this work center configuration is effective and available for production planning.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: Date until which this work center configuration is effective. Null indicates indefinite validity.

attributes.name: work_center_category
attributes.description: Classification of the work center type indicating the nature of the production resource.

attributes.name: work_center_code
attributes.description: Business identifier for the work center used in ERP and MES systems. Externally-known unique code for capacity planning and routing assignments.

attributes.name: work_center_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or description of the work center (e.g., Assembly Line 3, CNC Machining Cell A, Welding Station 5).

attributes.name: work_center_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the work center indicating its operational availability.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who created this work center record in the system.

products.name: routing
products.description: Manufacturing routing master defining the ordered sequence of operations required to produce a finished or semi-finished item. Captures routing number, routing type (production, inspection, universal), base quantity, status, and validity dates. Each routing is composed of individual operations (modeled as line items) linked to work centers with standard times for setup, machine run, and labor. The SSOT for standard production process definitions. Sourced from ERP routing master (e.g., SAP CA01) and PLM process plans (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter).
products.reference: SAP PP Routing (CA01) / PLM Process Plan

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the manufacturing routing. Primary key.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Subcontract routing costing: external processing routing operations are priced and governed by supplier agreements. Linking routing to agreement enables standard cost calculation for subcontracted ste

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) that defines the material components consumed by this routing. Links routing to BOM for integrated production planning.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA BOM (Bill of Materials) Master

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Routings define standard costs per operation assigned to cost centers for activity-based costing and standard cost calculation. In industrial manufacturing product costing, routing-to-cost-center assi

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: ECNs with routing_impact_flag=true directly trigger routing updates. Traceability from the updated routing back to the ECN that caused the change is a standard engineering change management requiremen

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Routings are revision-controlled and must align with the engineering revision of the product. When an ECN changes a component, the routing is updated and must reference the new revision. Standard PLM/

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Routing operations reference engineering/process specifications for critical parameters (weld specs, torque values, surface finish). Process engineers link routings to governing specifications for ope

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material or finished good that this routing produces. Links to the material master in inventory domain.

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Manufacturing plant or facility where this routing is executed. Links to plant master data.

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: FK to production.production_line

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Subcontracted operations: routing operations in industrial manufacturing can be externally processed (e.g., heat treatment, plating, machining). The routing must reference the subcontract supplier to

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Subcontract operation site: external processing routing steps are performed at a specific supplier site, which determines lead time, capacity, and quality certification requirements. Site-level routin

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which this routing was approved for production use.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval status of the routing. Pending routings await review; approved routings are authorized for production; rejected routings require rework; under_review routings are in the approval workflow.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person who approved this routing for production use.

attributes.name: base_quantity
attributes.description: Standard lot size or batch quantity for which the routing times and resource requirements are defined. All operation times are calculated relative to this base quantity.

attributes.name: base_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the base quantity. Standard ISO unit codes such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter).

attributes.name: change_number
attributes.description: Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) number that authorized the creation or last modification of this routing. Supports traceability of process changes.

attributes.name: cost_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost amount.

attributes.name: counter
attributes.description: Sequential counter within the routing group. Together with routing_group, forms an alternative business key.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this routing record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: is_default_routing
attributes.description: Indicates whether this routing is the default routing for the material. True if this is the primary routing; false if it is an alternative.

attributes.name: is_phantom_routing
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is a phantom routing that is automatically consumed without generating a separate production order. Used for subassemblies that are immediately incorporated into parent assemblies.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this routing record was last modified.

attributes.name: last_used_date
attributes.description: Most recent date on which this routing was used in a production order or work order. Used to identify obsolete or inactive routings.

attributes.name: lot_size_from
attributes.description: Minimum lot size for which this routing is applicable. Supports lot-size-dependent routing selection.

attributes.name: lot_size_to
attributes.description: Maximum lot size for which this routing is applicable. Null indicates no upper limit.

attributes.name: planner_group
attributes.description: Production planning team or planner responsible for maintaining and scheduling this routing.

attributes.name: routing_description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of the routing purpose, scope, and special instructions. Provides context for production planners and shop floor operators.

attributes.name: routing_group
attributes.description: Grouping code for related routings. Used to organize routings by product family, process type, or manufacturing cell.

attributes.name: routing_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the routing. Externally visible routing code used in production planning and shop floor documentation.

attributes.name: routing_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the routing. Draft routings are under development; released routings are approved for use; active routings are currently in production; inactive routings are temporarily suspended; obsolete routings are retired; blocked routings are prohibited from use.

attributes.name: routing_text
attributes.description: Extended free-form text field for detailed routing notes, special handling instructions, safety warnings, and quality requirements.

attributes.name: routing_type
attributes.description: Classification of the routing purpose. Production routings define standard manufacturing sequences; inspection routings define quality check sequences; rework routings define repair processes; universal routings apply across multiple materials; rate routings define continuous process flows; reference routings serve as templates.

attributes.name: scheduling_type
attributes.description: Scheduling strategy for this routing. Forward scheduling starts from the earliest start date; backward scheduling works back from the required finish date; midpoint scheduling balances around a target date.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: System of record from which this routing was sourced. SAP_PP indicates SAP S/4HANA Production Planning; TEAMCENTER indicates Siemens Teamcenter PLM; OPCENTER indicates Siemens Opcenter MES; MANUAL indicates manually entered routing.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier of this routing in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: standard_cost_amount
attributes.description: Standard cost to execute this routing for the base quantity. Includes labor, machine, overhead, and tooling costs. Used for cost accounting and variance analysis.

attributes.name: total_labor_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total cumulative labor time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Labor time may scale differently than machine time.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Work Center Capacity Planning

attributes.name: total_lead_time_hours
attributes.description: Total cumulative lead time in hours required to complete all operations in this routing for the base quantity. Includes setup, machine, labor, and queue times.
attributes.references: MRP (Material Requirements Planning) Lead Time Calculation

attributes.name: total_machine_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total cumulative machine processing time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Machine time scales with lot size.

attributes.name: total_operation_count
attributes.description: Total number of operations (steps) defined in this routing. Derived from routing operation line items.

attributes.name: total_setup_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total cumulative setup time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Setup time is independent of lot size.

attributes.name: usage
attributes.description: Intended application context for the routing. Standard routings are the default production method; alternative routings provide backup processes; trial routings support pilot runs; prototype routings support R&D; emergency routings address contingency scenarios.

attributes.name: usage_count
attributes.description: Total number of times this routing has been used in production orders or work orders. Indicates routing popularity and stability.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: Effective start date from which this routing is valid and can be used in production planning and execution.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: Effective end date after which this routing is no longer valid. Null indicates indefinite validity.

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version identifier for the routing. Supports versioning of routing definitions to track engineering changes and process improvements.

products.name: shift
products.description: Shift definition master representing a named working period pattern for a plant or work center. Defines shift code, start/end times, break structure, net available minutes, and shift type (regular, overtime, weekend). Used as the time-boundary reference for capacity planning, OEE shift-level reporting, takt time calculation, and workforce scheduling integration.
products.reference: SAP PP Shift Definition / Opcenter Shift

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the shift definition record. Primary key.

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Links shift to its site location, enabling shift‑level labor cost and safety incident aggregation per plant.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific work center or production line where this shift is applicable. Nullable if the shift applies plant-wide. Used for capacity planning and machine-specific scheduling.

attributes.name: break_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Sum of all scheduled break periods (lunch, rest breaks) within the shift, in minutes. Subtracted from gross duration to calculate net available time for production.

attributes.name: break_schedule
attributes.description: Textual description of the break structure, including start times and durations (e.g., '10:00-10:15 (15 min), 12:00-12:30 (30 min)'). Used for communication to shop floor personnel and HMI display.

attributes.name: changeover_allowance_minutes
attributes.description: Standard time reserved within the shift for product changeovers, tool changes, or setup activities. Used to adjust net available time for multi-product shifts and to calculate planned downtime for OEE.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this shift definition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: crew_size
attributes.description: Standard number of operators or workers assigned to this shift pattern. Used for labor capacity planning and cost allocation. Nullable if crew size varies by work center.

attributes.name: days_of_week
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of weekday codes (MON, TUE, WED, THU, FRI, SAT, SUN) indicating which days this shift pattern applies to. Used for weekly capacity planning and workforce rostering.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date after which this shift definition is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended shift patterns. Used to retire obsolete shift definitions while preserving historical data.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which this shift definition becomes active and can be used for production scheduling. Supports versioning of shift patterns over time.

attributes.name: end_time
attributes.description: Time of day when the shift ends, in HH:mm:ss format (24-hour clock). May be less than start_time for shifts that cross midnight. Used to calculate gross shift duration.

attributes.name: gross_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total elapsed time of the shift from start to end, in minutes, including all breaks and non-productive time. Used as the denominator for availability calculations.

attributes.name: handover_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Planned overlap time between consecutive shifts for handover communication, equipment status review, and safety briefing. Subtracted from net available time if included in shift boundaries. Nullable for non-continuous operations.

attributes.name: is_continuous_operation
attributes.description: Indicates whether this shift is part of a 24/7 continuous manufacturing operation (True) or a discrete batch operation (False). Used to determine handover requirements and WIP tracking rules.

attributes.name: labor_rate_multiplier
attributes.description: Multiplier applied to base labor rates for this shift type (e.g., 1.0 for regular, 1.5 for overtime, 2.0 for holiday). Used for labor cost calculation and variance analysis.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this shift definition record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this shift definition record was last updated. Used for audit trail and to track the currency of master data.

attributes.name: net_available_minutes
attributes.description: Net productive time available for manufacturing execution, calculated as gross_duration_minutes minus break_duration_minutes. Used as the planned production time for OEE availability calculation and takt time planning.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional information about the shift pattern, special instructions, or exceptions. Used for communication to planners and supervisors.

attributes.name: number_of_breaks
attributes.description: Count of distinct break periods scheduled within the shift (e.g., 2 for a shift with one lunch break and one rest break). Used for workforce scheduling and labor compliance reporting.

attributes.name: planned_output_quantity
attributes.description: Target production volume for this shift, in base unit of measure. Used as the denominator for performance OEE calculation and as input to takt time calculation. Nullable for non-production shifts.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Numeric priority ranking for shift selection when multiple shift definitions are valid for the same time period (lower number = higher priority). Used by APS systems to resolve scheduling conflicts.

attributes.name: rotation_pattern
attributes.description: Description of the multi-day or multi-week rotation cycle this shift belongs to (e.g., '2-2-3 Continental', '4-on-4-off', 'DuPont 12-hour'). Used for long-term workforce scheduling and fatigue management.

attributes.name: sequence
attributes.description: Ordinal position of this shift within a daily rotation (e.g., 1 for first shift, 2 for second shift, 3 for third shift). Used for shift handover reporting and continuous production tracking.

attributes.name: shift_category
attributes.description: Broad categorization of shift purpose. Production shifts are used for manufacturing execution and OEE calculation; non-production shifts (e.g., maintenance, training) are excluded from throughput metrics; mixed shifts include both activities.

attributes.name: shift_code
attributes.description: Unique business identifier for the shift pattern (e.g., DAY_1, NIGHT_A, SWING_2). Used as the externally-known reference in scheduling systems and MES.

attributes.name: shift_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the shift (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Morning Shift). Used for display in reports and HMI screens.

attributes.name: shift_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the shift definition. Active shifts are available for scheduling; inactive shifts are temporarily disabled; draft shifts are under review; archived shifts are retained for historical reference only.

attributes.name: shift_type
attributes.description: Classification of the shift pattern indicating the nature of the working period. Regular shifts are standard production periods; overtime, weekend, and holiday shifts may carry premium labor rates; maintenance and emergency shifts are non-production periods.

attributes.name: start_time
attributes.description: Time of day when the shift begins, in HH:mm:ss format (24-hour clock). Used to calculate shift boundaries for production tracking and OEE reporting.

attributes.name: takt_time_seconds
attributes.description: Target cycle time per unit to meet customer demand, calculated as net_available_minutes * 60 / planned_output_quantity. Used for production pacing and line balancing. Nullable if not applicable to this shift type.

attributes.name: timezone
attributes.description: IANA timezone identifier for the plant location where this shift operates (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Used to convert shift boundaries to UTC for global reporting and to handle daylight saving time transitions.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for planned_output_quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters). Must align with material master UOM for the products manufactured during this shift.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who created this shift definition record. Used for audit trail and data governance.

products.name: shift_report
products.description: Shift-level production performance report capturing actual results for a specific shift at a work center or line. Records planned vs. actual good quantity, scrap, rework, downtime minutes, OEE (availability × performance × quality), cycle time, takt time, and throughput rate. The SSOT for shift-level OEE and production KPIs. Drives daily production meetings, continuous improvement actions, and management escalation triggers. Sourced from MES shift close procedures (e.g., Siemens Opcenter) and SCADA data aggregation.
products.reference: MES Shift Report / OEE Shift Summary

attributes.name: shift_report_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the shift report record. Primary key.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Shift reports capture actual production costs (energy, labor, materials) allocated to cost centers for shift-level cost variance analysis. Industrial manufacturing controllers use shift-to-cost-center

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Allows shift reports to be tied to the plant location for accurate reporting of downtime and quality metrics per site.

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Shift reports record which lot/batch was produced each shift for quality traceability and regulatory compliance. Industrial manufacturing requires linking shift output directly to lot_batch for batch

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Shift reports track material_consumed_quantity and material_waste_quantity but have no FK to material_master. Material consumption variance reporting and shop-floor material accountability in industri

attributes.name: ncr_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: Shift reports document quality holds (quality_hold_flag) and nonconformances discovered during a shift. When a quality hold is raised, the shift report must reference the resulting NCR for shift-level

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: A shift report is generated for a specific production line. While shift_report already has work_center_id, the production line is the higher-level operational unit for OEE and throughput reporting. Di

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: A shift report captures actual production results for a specific shift, and those results are tied to the work order being executed during that shift. This link enables traceability from shift-level O

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: A shift report documents the production performance of a specific production run during a shift. The run entity represents a continuous execution campaign, and shift reports are the granular performan

attributes.name: schedule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_schedule. Business justification: A shift report captures actual vs. planned production, and the planned quantities come from the production schedule. Linking shift_report to production_schedule enables schedule adherence analysis — c

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Reference to the shift definition (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Swing Shift).

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product or material that was produced during this shift.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the work center or production line where this shift occurred.

attributes.name: actual_good_quantity
attributes.description: Actual quantity of conforming units produced during the shift that passed quality inspection.

attributes.name: actual_production_time_minutes
attributes.description: Actual time the work center was actively producing during the shift, excluding downtime.

attributes.name: availability_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of planned production time that the equipment was available and operating, calculated as (Planned Time - Downtime) / Planned Time × 100.

attributes.name: changeover_count
attributes.description: Number of production changeovers or setups performed during the shift.

attributes.name: changeover_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total time spent on changeovers and setups during the shift.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Free-text comments from the shift supervisor documenting notable events, issues, or observations during the shift.

attributes.name: cycle_time_seconds
attributes.description: Average time in seconds to complete one production cycle or unit during the shift.

attributes.name: data_source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the primary source system from which shift data was captured (MES, SCADA, ERP, or manual entry).

attributes.name: downtime_minutes
attributes.description: Total unplanned downtime during the shift due to equipment failures, material shortages, or other interruptions.

attributes.name: energy_consumption_kwh
attributes.description: Total electrical energy consumed by the work center during the shift, measured in kilowatt-hours.

attributes.name: escalation_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this shift report requires management escalation due to performance issues, quality problems, or safety concerns.

attributes.name: material_consumed_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity of raw materials or components consumed during the shift.

attributes.name: material_waste_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of material wasted or lost during production beyond normal scrap allowances.

attributes.name: oee_percentage
attributes.description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality, expressed as a percentage.

attributes.name: operator_count
attributes.description: Number of operators assigned to the work center during this shift.

attributes.name: performance_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of maximum possible output achieved during operating time, calculated as (Actual Output / Theoretical Maximum Output) × 100.

attributes.name: planned_production_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total planned available production time for the shift, excluding scheduled breaks and maintenance windows.

attributes.name: planned_quantity
attributes.description: Target production quantity planned for this shift based on production schedule and capacity.

attributes.name: quality_hold_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether production output from this shift is on quality hold pending inspection or investigation.

attributes.name: quality_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of good units produced out of total units, calculated as (Good Quantity / Total Produced Quantity) × 100.

attributes.name: report_closed_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the shift report was officially closed and finalized in the MES system.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES Shift Close Procedure

attributes.name: report_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the shift report record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: report_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the shift report, typically formatted as YYYYMMDD-SHIFT-WORKCENTER.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES Shift Close Procedure

attributes.name: rework_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of units requiring rework or reprocessing to meet quality standards.

attributes.name: safety_incident_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a safety incident occurred during this shift requiring documentation and follow-up.

attributes.name: scrap_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of units scrapped during the shift due to defects or non-conformance.

attributes.name: scrap_rate_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of total produced quantity that was scrapped, calculated as (Scrap Quantity / Total Produced Quantity) × 100.

attributes.name: shift_date
attributes.description: Calendar date on which the shift occurred.

attributes.name: shift_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total duration of the shift in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamp.

attributes.name: shift_end_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual timestamp when the shift ended production activities.

attributes.name: shift_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual timestamp when the shift started production activities.

attributes.name: takt_time_seconds
attributes.description: Target production rate in seconds per unit, calculated from customer demand and available production time.

attributes.name: throughput_rate_per_hour
attributes.description: Average number of units produced per hour during the shift.

attributes.name: total_produced_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity produced during the shift, including good, scrap, and rework units.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this report (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|TON|LB|GAL|FT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: yield_rate_percentage
attributes.description: First-pass yield rate representing the percentage of units that passed quality inspection without rework.

products.name: production_downtime_event
products.description: Downtime event capturing a planned or unplanned production stoppage at a work center or line. Records start/end timestamps, duration, downtime category (breakdown, planned maintenance, changeover, material shortage, quality hold, operator absence), root cause code, and responsible department. Critical input for OEE availability calculation, TPM analysis, and changeover time (SMED) tracking. Sourced from SCADA alarm logs and MES downtime tracking (e.g., Siemens Opcenter).
products.reference: SCADA Downtime Event / MES Downtime Record

attributes.name: production_downtime_event_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the production downtime event record. Primary key.

attributes.name: capacity_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.capacity_plan. Business justification: Downtime events directly impact capacity plan actuals. Capacity planners reconcile unplanned downtime against capacity plans to update available capacity, trigger replanning, and calculate capacity va

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Downtime events caused by component failures must reference the specific engineering component for root cause analysis, FMEA updates, and reliability engineering. Enables component-level MTBF/MTTR ana

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Downtime events generate production loss values (production_loss_value column exists) posted to cost centers for variance analysis and OEE cost impact reporting. Industrial manufacturing controllers t

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Downtime root‑cause analysis often references an engineering change notice; linking enables systematic CAPA tracking.

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the specific equipment or machine that experienced downtime. Links to equipment register.

attributes.name: failure_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Every significant production downtime event generates a failure record for FMEA and root cause analysis. Linking production_downtime_event to failure_record enables corrective action tracking, MTBF ca

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Captures the exact plant location of each downtime event, required for safety incident analysis and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: ncr_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: Downtime events caused by quality failures (nonconforming materials, tooling defects) are linked to NCRs for root cause analysis and OEE impact classification. Standard MES/QMS integration for downtim

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: A downtime event occurs at a specific production line. While production_downtime_event already has work_center_id, the production line is the primary operational unit for availability and OEE reportin

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the production order that was interrupted by this downtime event. May be null for unplanned stoppages outside scheduled production.

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Defective delivery traceability: downtime caused by a specific defective delivery is traced to the originating purchase order to support supplier quality claims, return material authorizations, and co

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: A downtime event occurs during a production run, interrupting the continuous execution campaign. Linking production_downtime_event to run enables run-level downtime aggregation (total_downtime_minutes

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the production shift during which the downtime event occurred. Links to shift schedule master data.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Supplier-caused downtime tracking: production downtime caused by defective incoming materials or late deliveries must reference the responsible supplier for root cause analysis, corrective action requ

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the work center or production line where the downtime occurred. Links to the work center master data.

attributes.name: alarm_code
attributes.description: SCADA or DCS alarm code that triggered the downtime event. Used to correlate downtime with specific equipment faults.

attributes.name: alarm_description
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the SCADA alarm that initiated the downtime event.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval status of the downtime event record. Pending indicates awaiting supervisor review, approved indicates validated, rejected indicates disputed or incorrect entry.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the downtime event record was approved by a supervisor or manager.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Additional free-text comments or notes about the downtime event from operators, technicians, or supervisors.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for production loss value (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: downtime_category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the downtime event: breakdown (unplanned equipment failure), planned_maintenance (scheduled PM), changeover (product/tool change), material_shortage (feedstock unavailable), quality_hold (production stopped for quality issue), operator_absence (staffing gap).

attributes.name: downtime_event_number
attributes.description: Business-readable unique identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by MES or SCADA system.

attributes.name: downtime_reason
attributes.description: Operator or supervisor entered reason for the downtime event. Free-text field capturing immediate observed cause.

attributes.name: downtime_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the downtime was planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover) or unplanned (breakdown, material shortage, quality hold).

attributes.name: duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total duration of the downtime event in minutes. Calculated as the difference between end_timestamp and start_timestamp, or current time if still ongoing.

attributes.name: end_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when production resumed after the downtime event. Null if downtime is still ongoing.

attributes.name: impact_on_oee
attributes.business_glossary_term: Impact on Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) Percentage
attributes.description: Calculated percentage impact of this downtime event on the work center or line OEE for the shift or day. Expressed as percentage points lost.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this downtime event record is currently active. False indicates the record has been logically deleted or superseded.

attributes.name: is_recurring
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this downtime event is part of a recurring pattern at this work center or equipment. True if similar events have occurred within the past 30 days.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this downtime event record was last updated. Audit trail field.

attributes.name: mttr_minutes
attributes.description: Mean time to repair for this type of downtime event, calculated from historical data. Used for benchmarking and performance tracking.

attributes.name: notification_sent
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether automated notifications were sent to supervisors, maintenance team, or management when this downtime event occurred.

attributes.name: preventive_action
attributes.description: Description of preventive measures implemented to avoid recurrence of this type of downtime event.

attributes.name: production_loss_units
attributes.description: Estimated quantity of production units lost due to this downtime event. Calculated based on standard cycle time and downtime duration.

attributes.name: production_loss_value
attributes.description: Estimated financial value of production lost due to this downtime event. Calculated using standard cost per unit and production loss units.

attributes.name: recorded_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this downtime event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.

attributes.name: recurrence_count
attributes.description: Number of times this type of downtime event has occurred at this work center or equipment within the past 30 days. Used for chronic loss identification.

attributes.name: resolution_action
attributes.description: Description of the corrective action taken to resolve the downtime event and restore production.

attributes.name: responsible_department
attributes.description: Department accountable for resolving the downtime event: production (operator-related), maintenance (equipment repair), quality (inspection/testing), materials (supply chain), engineering (design/process), operations (planning/scheduling).

attributes.name: root_cause_code
attributes.description: Standardized code identifying the root cause of the downtime event. Typically references a root cause taxonomy maintained in the CMMS or MES system.

attributes.name: root_cause_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the root cause analysis findings for this downtime event.

attributes.name: severity_level
attributes.description: Business impact severity of the downtime event: critical (full line stoppage, safety risk), high (major capacity loss), medium (partial degradation), low (minor impact).

attributes.name: shift_date
attributes.description: Calendar date of the shift during which the downtime occurred. Used for daily OEE reporting and trend analysis.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: System that originated this downtime event record: MES (Siemens Opcenter), SCADA (Aveva), ERP (SAP PP), CMMS (Maximo), or Manual (operator entry).

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier of this downtime event in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: start_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the downtime event began. Captured from SCADA alarm logs or MES operator entry.

products.name: goods_receipt
products.description: Goods receipt event recording formal completion and stock posting of finished or semi-finished goods produced against a work order. Captures GR document number, posting date, material, plant, storage location, received quantity, batch number, and movement type (101). Sourced from SAP PP/WM (MIGO). This is the production domain's handoff point — it triggers inventory update in the inventory domain and signals work order completion. Does NOT own ongoing stock balances (inventory domain owns those).
products.reference: SAP PP Goods Receipt (MIGO / Movement Type 101)

attributes.name: goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the production goods receipt event. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the cost center responsible for the production activity. Used for cost allocation and variance analysis.

attributes.name: delivery_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: Finished-goods GR reconciliation: when production completes and goods are received into stock, the GR is matched to the outbound delivery document to confirm fulfillment quantities and update delivery

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Goods receipts for finished goods must record the engineering revision produced for serialized product traceability, customer delivery documentation, and regulatory compliance (e.g., automotive part t

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: The quality_inspection_required flag on goods receipt triggers inspection against a specific engineering specification. Linking the receipt to the governing specification enables automated inspection

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Production goods receipts trigger inventory and WIP clearing postings to GL accounts (stock accounts, finished goods accounts). In industrial manufacturing, GR document posting requires a GL account r

attributes.name: inspection_lot_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Production goods receipts trigger quality inspection lots for usage decision. The GR document must reference the inspection lot for stock posting decisions. Standard SAP QM / ERP-QMS flow in industria

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Connects goods receipt to the receiving plant location, supporting traceability and inventory valuation per site.

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Reference to the lot or batch number assigned to the received goods for traceability and quality control purposes.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record for the finished or semi-finished good that was received. Links to the material being produced.

attributes.name: mrp_run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.mrp_run. Business justification: Goods receipts are posted as a result of an MRP run; linking enables receipt verification against the originating run in the Goods Receipt Reconciliation report.

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: Goods receipts from production confirm and close planned orders in MRP. The GR-to-planned-order confirmation is a fundamental MRP process that updates available stock and triggers downstream supply ch

attributes.name: po_line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.po_line_item. Business justification: 3-way match process: each production goods receipt maps to a specific PO line item for quantity confirmation, open-quantity reduction, and invoice verification. Industrial manufacturing ERP systems re

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Replace string line code with a proper foreign key to the production line entity for referential integrity.

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Link goods receipt to the work order it finalizes, enabling traceability from receipt to production work order.

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: GR/IR clearing process: production goods receipts must reference the originating purchase order to confirm delivery, close the GR/IR clearing account, and enable 3-way match (PO/GR/Invoice). Standard

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: A goods receipt records the formal completion and stock posting of goods produced during a production run. Linking production_goods_receipt to run enables run-level yield tracking — comparing actual_q

attributes.name: sales_order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Goods receipt against a sales order intake is the trigger for order fulfillment confirmation and revenue recognition in industrial manufacturing. Direct GR-to-order-intake traceability is required for

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: Goods receipt of serialized finished goods (automation systems, drives, PLCs) in industrial manufacturing creates a serialized_unit record. The goods receipt must reference the serialized unit for ser

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: Replace string shift identifier with a foreign key to the shift entity, allowing accurate shift‑level reporting.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Goods receipt records need the SKU to validate received quantity against the product master and enable downstream inventory updates.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the storage location within the plant where the received goods were posted to inventory. Links to warehouse storage location.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Goods receipt must record the supplying vendor to enable invoice matching and supplier performance tracking.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: A goods receipt is posted from a specific work center where the finished or semi-finished goods were produced. This link enables work-center-level yield and output tracking, connecting the physical pr

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: The batch or lot number assigned to the received goods for traceability. May be system-generated or manually assigned based on batch management configuration.

attributes.name: confirmation_number
attributes.description: The production confirmation number from the MES or ERP system that triggered this goods receipt. Links shop floor execution to inventory posting.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The system timestamp when this goods receipt record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.

attributes.name: delivery_note_number
attributes.description: Reference number from the physical delivery documentation or packing slip accompanying the goods. Used for reconciliation with physical documents.

attributes.name: document_date
attributes.description: The date on which the goods receipt document was created or initiated. May differ from posting date in cases of backdated transactions.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the goods receipt was posted. Used for monthly financial closing and reporting.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which the goods receipt was posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and inventory valuation.

attributes.name: gr_document_number
attributes.description: The unique document number generated by the ERP system for this goods receipt transaction. This is the business identifier used for tracking and audit purposes.

attributes.name: gr_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt document. Indicates whether the receipt has been successfully posted, reversed, cancelled, or is awaiting quality inspection.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The system timestamp when this goods receipt record was last updated in the data warehouse. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: movement_type
attributes.description: SAP movement type code that classifies the type of inventory transaction. For production goods receipts, typically 101 (goods receipt to stock) or 131 (goods receipt from production order).

attributes.name: note
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments entered by the operator or warehouse personnel regarding the goods receipt. Captures exceptions, observations, or special handling instructions.

attributes.name: order_quantity
attributes.description: The total quantity originally planned to be produced on the work order. Used to calculate yield and completion percentage.

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: The date on which the goods receipt was posted to inventory in the ERP system. This is the accounting-effective date for inventory valuation.

attributes.name: posting_user
attributes.description: The system user ID who posted the goods receipt transaction in the ERP system. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: production_version
attributes.description: Identifies the specific production version (combination of BOM and routing) used to manufacture the goods. Used for process traceability and costing.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the received goods must undergo quality inspection before being released to unrestricted stock. Drives QM workflow.

attributes.name: receipt_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the goods were physically received and confirmed on the shop floor or warehouse. Captures the real-world event time for production completion.

attributes.name: received_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of finished or semi-finished goods received and posted to inventory. Measured in the base unit of measure for the material.

attributes.name: reservation_number
attributes.description: The material reservation number that was created for the work order and fulfilled by this goods receipt. Links production output to material planning.

attributes.name: reversal_document_number
attributes.description: The document number of the reversal transaction if this goods receipt was reversed. Provides audit trail for corrections.

attributes.name: reversal_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled. Used to filter active vs. reversed transactions in reporting.

attributes.name: reversal_reason_code
attributes.description: Code indicating the reason for reversing the goods receipt (e.g., quantity error, wrong material, quality issue). Used for root cause analysis.

attributes.name: scrap_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of material scrapped or rejected during the production run that resulted in this goods receipt. Used for yield calculation and quality analysis.

attributes.name: special_stock_indicator
attributes.description: Code indicating if the goods receipt is for special stock types such as consignment, project stock, or sales order stock. Affects inventory valuation and availability.

attributes.name: stock_type
attributes.description: The type of stock to which the goods were received. Unrestricted stock is available for use, quality inspection stock is awaiting QA approval, blocked stock cannot be used.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters). Must match the material master base UOM.

attributes.name: valuation_type
attributes.description: Classification code used for split valuation of materials when the same material is valued differently based on procurement type or quality grade.

attributes.name: vendor_batch_number
attributes.description: External batch number from a supplier or contract manufacturer, if the goods receipt is for subcontracted production. Used for external traceability.

attributes.name: yield_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of good output relative to the total input or planned quantity. Calculated as (received_quantity / order_quantity) * 100. Key OEE component.

products.name: run
products.description: Production run entity representing a continuous execution campaign on a production line or work center, potentially spanning multiple work orders for the same or similar products. Captures run number, production line, start timestamp, end timestamp, total planned quantity, total actual quantity, total scrap quantity, overall yield rate, number of work orders included, and run status (active, completed, cancelled). Sourced from Siemens Opcenter MES campaign/run management. Enables campaign-level performance analysis beyond individual work orders.

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Primary key for run

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account for whom this production run is being executed, applicable for make-to-order scenarios.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Subcontract agreement utilization: production runs for subcontracted operations are executed under a supplier agreement. Linking enables contract utilization tracking, price validation per agreement t

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Bill of Materials used for this production run, defining the component structure and recipe.

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: A production run executes a specific product configuration in configure-to-order manufacturing. The configuration governs assembly instructions, component selection, and quality acceptance criteria fo

attributes.name: control_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Each production run executes under a specific control plan specifying process controls, measurement frequencies, and reaction plans. Control plan compliance per run is a core IATF 16949 / APQP require

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Production runs accumulate actual costs (actual_cost column exists) assigned to cost centers for cost object controlling. In industrial manufacturing, run-level cost tracking by cost center is standar

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: Production runs initiated to implement an ECO (first production run after an engineering change) must reference the governing ECO for change effectivity tracking and customer notification. Supports en

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Production runs must be traceable to the engineering revision being manufactured — fundamental for customer traceability reports, warranty analysis, and regulatory compliance (IATF 16949, ISO 9001). N

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the primary equipment or machine asset assigned to execute this production run.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Run‑level reporting and profitability analysis need a direct reference to the originating sales order.

attributes.name: inspection_plan_id
