attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Each production run is governed by a specific inspection plan for in-process SPC and quality gate decisions. Run-level inspection plan traceability is required for automotive and aerospace manufacturi

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Each production run produces a specific lot/batch. Batch genealogy, quality hold management, and lot traceability in industrial manufacturing require a direct run→lot_batch link. batch_number on run i

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the primary material or product being manufactured in this production run.

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Production run actuals (actual_quantity, oee_percentage, scrap_rate) must be reconciled against the supply plan for plan adherence reporting. Run-level plan traceability is required for S&OP review an

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: FK to production.production_line

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: A production run executes against a specific work order. The run entity tracks continuous execution campaigns on a production line, and each run is authorized by a work order. run.work_order_count con

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Subcontracted run traceability: production runs executing subcontracted operations reference the purchase order issued to the supplier. This enables cost confirmation, goods receipt posting against th

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: A production run follows a specific manufacturing routing that defines the sequence of operations. run.routing_number (STRING) is a denormalized reference to routing.routing_number. Adding routing_id

attributes.name: sales_order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Run scheduling dashboards rely on the originating sales order intake to allocate runs; the FK replaces the redundant sales_order_number field.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: FK to production.shift

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Each production run must reference the SKU to calculate yield, OEE, and cost per product.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Production runs output finished goods to a specific stock location (finished goods staging area). MES-to-WMS integration and finished goods putaway processes require the run to reference its output st

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Production runs need supplier reference to calculate cost, quality impact, and compliance per material batch.

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Reference to the warehouse or storage location where finished goods from this run are received.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: A production run is executed at a specific work center or machine. While a run is associated with a production_line (existing FK), the work center provides finer-grained resource assignment for schedu

attributes.name: actual_cost
attributes.description: Actual total cost incurred for this production run, including material, labor, and overhead costs.

attributes.name: actual_finish_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual date and time when the production run execution was completed or terminated.

attributes.name: actual_quantity
attributes.description: Total actual quantity produced during this run, representing the sum of good output across all work orders.

attributes.name: actual_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual date and time when the production run execution began on the shop floor.

attributes.name: availability_percentage
attributes.description: Equipment availability during the production run, calculated as (operating time / planned production time) * 100.

attributes.name: campaign_code
attributes.description: Campaign or project code associated with this production run, used for grouping related runs for reporting and analysis.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this production run record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all cost and financial values in this production run.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this production run record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes and comments about the production run, capturing operational observations, issues, or special instructions.

attributes.name: oee_percentage
attributes.description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness metric for this production run, calculated as availability * performance * quality, expressed as a percentage.

attributes.name: performance_percentage
attributes.description: Equipment performance efficiency during the production run, calculated as (actual output / target output) * 100.

attributes.name: planned_finish_timestamp
attributes.description: Scheduled date and time when the production run is planned to complete execution.

attributes.name: planned_quantity
attributes.description: Total planned production quantity for this run across all included work orders.

attributes.name: planned_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Scheduled date and time when the production run is planned to begin execution.

attributes.name: priority_code
attributes.description: Priority level assigned to this production run for scheduling and resource allocation purposes.

attributes.name: quality_percentage
attributes.description: Quality rate during the production run, calculated as (good units / total units produced) * 100.

attributes.name: rework_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity of material sent for rework or reprocessing during this production run.

attributes.name: run_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the production run campaign, externally visible and used for tracking and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: run_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the production run indicating its operational state.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES Shop Floor Control

attributes.name: run_type
attributes.description: Classification of the production run indicating the purpose or nature of the execution (standard production, pilot run, rework campaign, validation run, trial batch, or extended campaign).

attributes.name: scrap_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity of scrap or rejected material generated during this production run.

attributes.name: scrap_rate_percentage
attributes.description: Overall scrap rate for the production run, calculated as (scrap quantity / (actual quantity + scrap quantity)) * 100.

attributes.name: standard_cost
attributes.description: Standard cost per unit for this production run based on the costing model and BOM.

attributes.name: takt_time_minutes
attributes.description: Target production rate or takt time for this run, representing the available production time divided by customer demand, measured in minutes per unit.

attributes.name: throughput_rate
attributes.description: Average throughput rate achieved during the production run, measured as units produced per hour.

attributes.name: total_cycle_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total cycle time for the production run, representing the sum of all processing time across all operations, measured in minutes.

attributes.name: total_downtime_minutes
attributes.description: Total unplanned downtime during the production run, measured in minutes.

attributes.name: total_setup_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total setup and changeover time for the production run, measured in minutes.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this production run (e.g., EA, KG, L, M).

attributes.name: work_order_count
attributes.description: Number of individual work orders included or consolidated within this production run campaign.

attributes.name: yield_rate_percentage
attributes.description: Overall yield rate for the production run, calculated as (actual quantity / (actual quantity + scrap quantity)) * 100.

products.name: production_line
products.description: Production line master entity representing a named, configured manufacturing line within a plant, composed of an ordered set of work centers and machines. Captures line code, line name, plant, line type (assembly, machining, fabrication, painting, testing), design throughput rate (units/hour), takt time target, number of stations, automation level (manual, semi-automated, fully automated), and current operational status. The SSOT for production line configuration used in scheduling, OEE reporting, and capacity planning.
products.reference: SAP PP Production Line / MES Line Master

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the production line. Primary key for the production line master entity.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Production line overhead is budgeted and reported per cost center; the link supports line‑level cost reporting.

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Production lines in industrial manufacturing are typically dedicated to specific product families (e.g., switchgear assembly line, motor winding line). This link drives capacity planning by product fa

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Needed to associate each production line with its plant location for capacity planning and regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: A production line is physically located within a plant. This is a critical missing master data relationship — production lines are plant-specific assets and all capacity, OEE, and throughput reporting

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Production lines are assigned to profit centers for line-level profitability reporting in industrial manufacturing. Controllers use production line-to-profit-center mapping for contribution margin ana

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Production lines are commissioned, upgraded, or decommissioned as part of engineering capital projects. Linking production lines to their governing engineering project enables project cost tracking, c

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Production lines have dedicated line-side stock locations (supermarket/kanban input buffers and finished goods output staging). Lean manufacturing replenishment planning and line-feeding logistics req

attributes.name: actual_oee_percentage
attributes.description: Most recent calculated Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for this production line based on actual performance data.

attributes.name: automation_level
attributes.description: Degree of automation implemented on the production line, ranging from manual operations to fully automated lights-out manufacturing.
attributes.references: IEC 62443 Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security

attributes.name: capacity_constraint_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this production line is identified as a bottleneck or capacity constraint in the manufacturing process requiring special attention in scheduling.
attributes.references: Theory of Constraints, Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS)

attributes.name: changeover_time_minutes
attributes.description: Total time from last good piece of previous run to first good piece of new run, including setup and stabilization time.

attributes.name: commissioning_date
attributes.description: Date when the production line was first commissioned and put into operational service.
attributes.references: Asset Lifecycle Management, ISO 55000 Asset Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this production line master record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: cycle_time_seconds
attributes.description: Actual average time required to complete one production cycle on this line. Used to compare against takt time for performance analysis.

attributes.name: data_source_system
attributes.description: Identifier of the source system from which this production line master data originated or is synchronized.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM Master Data Management, Data Lineage

attributes.name: design_throughput_rate

products.name: routing_operation
products.description: This association product represents the assignment of a work center to a specific operation step within a manufacturing routing. It captures the ordered sequence of operations that constitute a routing, with each record linking one routing to one work center at a defined operation step. Attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment — operation sequence, time standards for setup/processing/labor/machine, control key, and costing standard values — are stored here. This is the SSOT for operation-level process definitions and is the primary source for capacity planning, production scheduling, and standard cost calculation. Corresponds to SAP PP table PLPO (routing operation) and Siemens Opcenter process step assignment.. Existence Justification: In manufacturing ERP systems (SAP PP, Siemens Opcenter), a routing is composed of ordered operation steps, and each operation step is explicitly assigned to a specific work center. One routing references many work centers (one per operation step, and potentially the same work center at multiple steps), and one work center appears in many routings across different products and production scenarios. The 'routing operation' is a well-recognized, actively managed business entity in manufacturing — planners create, update, and delete operation assignments as part of process engineering, and each assignment carries its own time standards, control keys, and costing data that belong neither to the routing header nor to the work center master alone.

attributes.name: routing_operation_id
attributes.description: Primary key for the routing_operation association

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking this operation step to its parent manufacturing routing header.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking this operation step to the work center where the operation is performed.

attributes.name: control_key
attributes.description: Control key defining how this specific operation is processed — whether it triggers confirmations, quality inspections, costing, or external processing. Operation-level override of the work center default control key.

attributes.name: labor_time_minutes
attributes.description: Standard labor time in minutes per base quantity for this operation step. May differ from machine time when operator and machine run concurrently.

attributes.name: machine_time_minutes
attributes.description: Standard machine time in minutes per base quantity for this operation step. Used for machine capacity planning and OEE calculations.

attributes.name: operation_description
attributes.description: Short description of the work to be performed at this operation step within this routing. Describes the specific task (e.g., 'Rough mill housing bore'), not the work center capability in general.

attributes.name: operation_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the operation step within the routing (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030). Determines the display and reference sequence of operations. Corresponds to SAP PP field VORNR.

attributes.name: processing_time_minutes
attributes.description: Standard machine or processing time in minutes per base quantity for this operation step. Used for capacity planning and standard cost calculation.

attributes.name: sequence_number
attributes.description: Scheduling sequence number defining the execution order of operations within the routing. Used for parallel or alternative sequences. Corresponds to SAP PP field PLFL.

attributes.name: setup_time_minutes
attributes.description: Standard setup or changeover time in minutes required to prepare the work center for this specific operation within this routing. This value is operation-specific and may differ from the work center default.

attributes.name: standard_values
attributes.description: Standard value key defining which standard values (setup, machine, labor, teardown) are relevant for this operation and how they are used in costing and scheduling formulas.

domains.name: engineering
domains.description: Product design and engineering lifecycle domain covering CAD/CAM models, BOMs, ECOs, ECNs, DFM analysis, DFMEA, PFMEA, and PLM data managed in Siemens Teamcenter. Serves as the SSOT for product structure, revision history, engineering change governance, technical specifications, drawings, and prototypes for all manufactured automation systems.

products.name: component
products.description: Master record for every discrete engineered part, sub-assembly, or raw material managed in PLM. Serves as the SSOT for component identity, revision state, lifecycle phase, engineering classification, material specification, weight, dimensional envelope, tolerance class, CAD model reference, PLM item ID, and approved substitutes. Covers all manufactured automation system components including electrification modules, PLCs, HMIs, drives, sensors, and structural enclosures. Includes classification taxonomy (commodity, technology family, functional group) for BOM analytics and sourcing alignment.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the component. Primary key for the component master record.

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: Component sourcing governance: the AVL is the formal approved source list for each component. Procurement uses AVL to validate sourcing decisions. A manufacturing domain expert expects a direct compon

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Components are classified into commodity categories for strategic sourcing, spend analytics, and procurement contract coverage. commodity_code on component is a denormalized representation of the comm

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Component cost tracking for budgeting and cost allocation per cost center; required for cost of goods sold reporting.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Component expense posting to a GL account enables accurate financial statements and inventory valuation.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Inventory Management tracks stock of each engineered component via Material Master for procurement and WIP planning.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: PLM-to-ERP handoff: when an engineering component is released to production, it becomes a procurable/sellable SKU. Industrial manufacturing engineers routinely trace component_id to sku_master for cos

attributes.name: substitute_component_id
attributes.description: Identifier of an approved substitute component that can be used interchangeably. Supports supply chain flexibility and risk mitigation.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the preferred supplier for purchased components. Links to supplier master for procurement planning.

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: Inventory classification by value contribution. A items are high-value requiring tight control, C items are low-value with relaxed management.

attributes.name: cad_model_reference
attributes.description: File path or URI reference to the authoritative CAD model in PLM. Links to 3D geometry for design, simulation, and manufacturing.

attributes.name: ce_marking_flag
attributes.description: Indicates CE marking compliance for European market access. Confirms conformity with EU health, safety, and environmental protection standards.

attributes.name: component_description
attributes.description: Detailed technical description of the component including functional characteristics, application context, and distinguishing features.

attributes.name: component_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the component. Primary human-readable identifier used in engineering documentation and BOMs.

attributes.name: component_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the component. Human-readable unique code used across engineering, procurement, and manufacturing.

attributes.name: component_type
attributes.description: Classification of the component by procurement and manufacturing strategy. Determines BOM explosion logic and sourcing approach.

attributes.name: cost_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for standard cost. Enables multi-currency cost management.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the component record was first created in PLM. Audit trail for lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: dfm_score
attributes.description: Quantitative assessment of component manufacturability. Higher scores indicate easier, lower-cost manufacturing.

attributes.name: dfmea_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) Reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier to the DFMEA document analyzing potential design failure modes and mitigation strategies.

attributes.name: drawing_number
attributes.description: Engineering drawing number for 2D technical documentation. References detailed manufacturing and inspection drawings.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the current component revision becomes effective for use in new designs and BOMs. Supports ECO implementation.

attributes.name: functional_group
attributes.description: Functional classification of the component within the product architecture. Used for design reuse and modular engineering.

attributes.name: hazardous_material_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the component contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, and disposal procedures.

attributes.name: height_mm
attributes.description: Maximum height dimension of the component envelope in millimeters. Part of dimensional specification for packaging and assembly planning.

attributes.name: lead_time_days

attributes.name: length_mm
attributes.description: Maximum length dimension of the component envelope in millimeters. Part of dimensional specification for packaging and assembly planning.

attributes.name: lifecycle_phase
attributes.description: Current phase in the component lifecycle from concept through obsolescence. Governs release status and usage authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|design|prototype|validation|production|phase_out|obsolete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: lot_size
attributes.description: Standard production or procurement lot size. Defines batch quantity for manufacturing execution and inventory replenishment.

attributes.name: make_or_buy
attributes.description: Strategic sourcing decision indicating whether the component is manufactured in-house, purchased from suppliers, or both.

attributes.name: material_specification
attributes.description: Material composition and grade specification. Defines raw material requirements for manufactured components.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum order quantity required by supplier or manufacturing process. Constraint for procurement and production planning.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User identifier of the engineer who last modified the component record. Audit trail for change management.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the component record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data currency.

attributes.name: obsolescence_date
attributes.description: Planned or actual date when the component will be obsoleted and no longer available for new designs. Supports lifecycle planning.

attributes.name: pfmea_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) Reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier to the PFMEA document analyzing potential manufacturing process failure modes and controls.

attributes.name: plm_item_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier for this component in Siemens Teamcenter PLM system. External system reference for PLM integration.

attributes.name: reach_compliant_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates compliance with EU REACH regulation for chemical substance registration and safety assessment.

attributes.name: release_status
attributes.description: Engineering release status indicating approval state for manufacturing and procurement use.

attributes.name: reorder_point
attributes.description: Inventory level that triggers replenishment action. Calculated from lead time demand and safety stock.

attributes.name: revision
attributes.description: Current engineering revision level of the component. Tracks design changes through ECO/ECN processes.

attributes.name: rohs_compliant_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates compliance with EU RoHS directive restricting use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum inventory buffer quantity to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Input to MRP calculations.

attributes.name: standard_cost
attributes.description: Standard unit cost for the component in base currency. Used for BOM costing, inventory valuation, and financial planning.

attributes.name: technology_family
attributes.description: Engineering technology classification grouping components by functional domain (e.g., electrification, automation, control systems).

attributes.name: tolerance_class
attributes.description: Manufacturing tolerance classification defining acceptable dimensional variation. Drives manufacturing process selection and quality inspection requirements.

attributes.name: ul_certification_number
attributes.description: UL certification identifier for product safety compliance. Required for electrical components in North American markets.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Standard unit of measure for inventory, procurement, and BOM quantity calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|G|M|CM|L|ML|SET|KIT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: weight_kg
attributes.description: Net weight of a single component unit in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, shipping cost calculation, and product specifications.

attributes.name: width_mm
attributes.description: Maximum width dimension of the component envelope in millimeters. Part of dimensional specification for packaging and assembly planning.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User identifier of the engineer who created the component record in PLM. Audit trail for data governance.

products.name: bom
products.description: Bill of Materials master record defining the structured product hierarchy for a given component or finished assembly at a specific revision. Captures BOM type (engineering BOM, manufacturing BOM, service BOM), effectivity dates, quantity basis, unit of measure, and the owning PLM item. Acts as the SSOT for product structure consumed by MRP, MES, and production planning.
products.reference: SAP PP BOM / Teamcenter BOM Management

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the Bill of Materials record. Primary key for the BOM master data entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑specific BOMs are created for contract fulfillment; linking BOM to customer_account enables contract‑BOM reconciliation reports.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: A BOM is defined for a specific top-level component or finished assembly. The bom description explicitly states 'defining the structured product hierarchy for a given component or finished assembly.'

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: BOM standard costing and manufacturing overhead allocation require associating a BOM with a cost center. Industrial manufacturing ERP systems (SAP, Oracle) assign BOMs to cost centers for cost rollup

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: A BOM is revision-specific — it represents the product structure at a particular engineering revision state. The bom table has a `revision` STRING field which is a denormalized label. A proper FK to e

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Every BOM in manufacturing belongs to a header material master (the finished assembly). MRP, cost rollup, and production planning all require knowing which material master a BOM defines. Standard ERP

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: BOMs are plant-specific in ERP systems (SAP, Oracle MFG). The existing bom.plant_code is a denormalized plain-text reference to production.plant. Replacing with a proper FK enforces referential integr

attributes.name: product_bom_header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.bom_header. Business justification: eBOM-to-mBOM promotion: the engineering BOM is the upstream source that is transferred and transformed into the manufacturing/product BOM header. Change impact analysis and BOM synchronization reports

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: BOMs are created and managed within engineering projects during product development. Linking bom to engineering_project enables project-level BOM tracking, cost rollup by project, and lifecycle govern

attributes.name: alternative_bom_indicator
attributes.description: Identifier for alternative BOM variants of the same product, used to represent different manufacturing methods, material substitutions, or regional variations. Blank for primary BOM.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state of the BOM in the engineering change workflow. Pending indicates awaiting review, Approved means released for use, Rejected means not accepted, Conditional means approved with restrictions or prerequisites.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this BOM revision. Supports audit trail and accountability for engineering change management.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date on which this BOM revision was formally approved for release. Critical for traceability and compliance with engineering change control processes.

attributes.name: base_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The fundamental unit of measure in which the finished product is stocked and sold, independent of the BOM quantity basis. Used for inventory and sales order processing.

attributes.name: bom_category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the BOM structure. Material BOM lists physical components, Document BOM references technical drawings and specifications, Equipment BOM defines installed assets, Variant BOM supports product families, Configurable BOM enables customer-specific selections.

attributes.name: bom_description
attributes.description: Free-text description of the BOM, providing additional context about the product structure, manufacturing method, or special handling requirements. Supplements the BOM number and type.

attributes.name: bom_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the BOM, typically a human-readable code used across engineering, manufacturing, and procurement systems. Serves as the externally-known unique reference.

attributes.name: bom_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the BOM. Draft indicates work in progress, In Review means under engineering approval, Approved is ready for release, Active is in production use, Obsolete is no longer valid, Superseded has been replaced by a newer revision, and Frozen is locked for regulatory or contractual reasons. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|active|obsolete|superseded|frozen — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: bom_type
attributes.description: Classification of the BOM by its intended business purpose. Engineering BOM (EBOM) represents design intent, Manufacturing BOM (MBOM) reflects shop floor assembly, Service BOM supports after-sales maintenance, Sales BOM defines customer-facing configurations, Planning BOM is used for MRP, and As-Maintained BOM tracks actual installed configurations.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: configuration_profile
attributes.description: Identifier for a variant configuration profile or product family definition. Used in configurable BOMs to define which components are included based on customer selections or feature options.

attributes.name: cost_estimate_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the BOM cost estimate. Supports multi-currency costing for global manufacturing operations.

attributes.name: cost_estimate_total
attributes.description: Estimated total cost of the BOM including all material, labor, and overhead components. Rolled up from component costs and routing operations. Used for product costing and pricing decisions.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this BOM record was first created in the PLM system. Provides audit trail for data governance and compliance.

attributes.name: effective_from_date
attributes.description: Date from which this BOM revision becomes valid and can be used for production planning, procurement, and manufacturing execution. Supports time-phased BOM management for product transitions.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: effective_to_date
attributes.description: Date until which this BOM revision remains valid. Nullable for open-ended BOMs. Used to manage phase-out of legacy product structures and ensure correct BOM selection in MRP and MES systems.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: engineering_change_order_number
attributes.description: Reference to the ECO that created or last modified this BOM revision. Provides traceability to the engineering change management process and links to ECO approval workflows.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, APQP Advanced Product Quality Planning

attributes.name: explosion_type
attributes.description: Defines how the BOM structure should be expanded for planning and execution. Single Level shows only immediate children, Multi Level recursively expands all sub-assemblies, Summarized aggregates quantities across all levels.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: is_configurable
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this BOM supports variant configuration, allowing customer-specific or order-specific component selection. True for configurable BOMs, False for fixed BOMs.

attributes.name: is_critical_bom
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this BOM is for a critical or high-value product requiring special handling, approval workflows, or regulatory compliance. True for critical BOMs, False for standard BOMs.

attributes.name: is_phantom_bom
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this is a phantom (transient) BOM that is not stocked as a discrete item but is exploded through to its components during MRP and production. True for phantom BOMs, False for standard stocked assemblies.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: lot_size
attributes.description: Standard production lot or batch size for which this BOM is optimized. Used in MRP calculations to determine component requirements and setup costs. Nullable if not applicable.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person or system that last modified this BOM record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this BOM record was last updated. Distinct from approval and effectivity dates. Provides audit trail for data governance.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or instructions related to this BOM. May include manufacturing guidelines, quality requirements, or special handling instructions for production planning and shop floor execution.

attributes.name: plm_item_code
attributes.description: Reference to the owning PLM item in Siemens Teamcenter that this BOM structure represents. Links the BOM to its parent product or assembly definition.

attributes.name: production_version
attributes.description: Identifier linking this BOM to a specific production version in SAP PP, which combines a BOM with a routing to define a complete manufacturing process. Supports multiple production methods for the same product.

attributes.name: quantity_basis
attributes.description: The base quantity for which this BOM is defined, typically 1 for single-unit BOMs or a batch size for process manufacturing. All component quantities in the BOM structure are expressed relative to this basis.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: revision
attributes.description: Revision level or version identifier for this BOM structure. Tracks engineering changes and ensures traceability across ECO and ECN processes. Critical for change management and configuration control.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, APQP Advanced Product Quality Planning

attributes.name: scrap_percentage
attributes.description: Expected scrap or waste percentage at the BOM header level, applied to all components. Used in MRP to inflate material requirements. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifier of the source system from which this BOM record originated, typically Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Supports data lineage and integration traceability across the enterprise data landscape.

attributes.name: source_system_key
attributes.description: The primary key or unique identifier of this BOM record in the source system. Enables bidirectional traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational PLM system.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the quantity basis, such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter). Must align with the PLM item and material master UOM definitions.
attributes.references: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units, APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: usage
attributes.description: Defines the business context in which this BOM is intended to be used. Production for shop floor execution, Costing for standard cost calculation, Engineering for design and development, Maintenance for service and repair, Sales Order for customer-specific configurations, Project for one-time builds.
attributes.references: APICS Supply Chain Operations Reference (SCOR) Model

attributes.name: weight_total
attributes.description: Total weight of the finished assembly including all components, calculated from the BOM structure. Used for logistics planning, freight cost estimation, and product specifications.

attributes.name: weight_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the total BOM weight. KG (kilogram), LB (pound), G (gram), OZ (ounce), MT (metric ton).

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person or system that originally created this BOM record. Supports audit trail and data lineage.

products.name: engineering_bom_line
products.description: Individual line item within a Bill of Materials, representing a single parent-child component relationship. Captures position number, find number, quantity per assembly, unit of measure, reference designator, substitute component references (approved alternates with qualification status and usage restrictions), phantom flag, effectivity start/end dates, and engineering notes. Enables full BOM explosion and implosion queries for MRP, supports alternate sourcing without ECO, and provides the granular structure for production routing and cost rollup.

attributes.name: engineering_bom_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the BOM line item. Primary key for the BOM line entity representing a single parent-child component relationship within a bill of materials structure.

attributes.name: bom_header_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent BOM header that this line belongs to. Links the line item to its containing bill of materials assembly structure.

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: bom_line represents a line item belonging to a BOM; adding bom_id creates the required parent-child relationship and eliminates the BOM silo.

attributes.name: primary_engineering_component_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent assembly or product that contains this component. Represents the 'parent' side of the parent-child relationship in the BOM structure.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Each BOM line must record the supplier providing that part for purchase order creation and cost tracking.

attributes.name: tertiary_engineering_substitute_component_id
attributes.description: Reference to an approved alternate component that can be used in place of the primary component without requiring an Engineering Change Order (ECO). Enables flexible sourcing and supply chain continuity.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Alternate Parts Management

attributes.name: assembly_instruction
attributes.description: Specific instructions for assembling or installing this component into the parent assembly. May reference work instructions, torque specifications, or special tooling requirements.

attributes.name: bulk_material_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this component is a bulk material (e.g., paint, adhesive, lubricant) that is consumed in variable quantities and may not be tracked at individual unit level.

attributes.name: change_number
attributes.description: The Engineering Change Order (ECO) or Engineering Change Notice (ECN) number that introduced or last modified this BOM line. Provides traceability to engineering change governance processes.

attributes.name: co_product_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line represents a co-product or by-product that is produced alongside the main assembly. Co-products have negative quantities in the BOM and represent outputs rather than inputs.

attributes.name: cost_rollup_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the cost of this component should be included in the parent assembly cost rollup calculation. Some components (e.g., reference items, tooling) may be excluded from product costing.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this BOM line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: critical_component_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this component is critical to product functionality, safety, or regulatory compliance. Critical components may require additional quality controls, traceability, or supply chain management.

attributes.name: effectivity_end_date
attributes.description: The date after which this component is no longer effective in the parent assembly. Used to manage engineering changes and phase-out of obsolete components. Null indicates the component is currently effective with no planned end date.

attributes.name: effectivity_serial_number_end
attributes.description: The ending serial number of the parent assembly for which this component is effective. Null indicates the component remains effective for all subsequent serial numbers.

attributes.name: effectivity_serial_number_start
attributes.description: The starting serial number of the parent assembly for which this component is effective. Enables serial-number-based effectivity control for mid-production engineering changes.

attributes.name: effectivity_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this component becomes effective in the parent assembly. Used to manage engineering changes and phase-in of new components without creating new BOM versions.

attributes.name: engineering_bom_line_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this BOM line. Indicates whether the component relationship is currently in use, pending approval, or has been superseded.

attributes.name: engineering_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes from engineering regarding special assembly instructions, design considerations, quality requirements, or other technical information relevant to this component usage.

attributes.name: find_number
attributes.description: Reference designator or callout number used to locate this component on engineering drawings, assembly diagrams, and technical documentation. Also known as item number or balloon number.

attributes.name: fixed_quantity_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the component quantity is fixed regardless of parent assembly batch size. When true, the quantity does not scale with production order quantity (e.g., setup materials, tooling).

attributes.name: installation_point
attributes.description: The specific location or mounting point within the parent assembly where this component is installed. Used in assembly instructions and maintenance documentation.

attributes.name: lead_time_offset_days
attributes.description: The number of days before the parent assembly start date that this component must be available. Used in production scheduling and material requirements planning (MRP) to ensure timely component availability.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: The user or system account that last modified this BOM line record. Used for audit trail and change management purposes.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this BOM line record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: phantom_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this component is a phantom (transient) assembly that is not stocked or tracked in inventory. Phantom items are exploded through to their child components during MRP processing.

attributes.name: position_number
attributes.description: Sequential position or line number of this component within the parent assembly BOM. Used for ordering and identifying components in engineering drawings and manufacturing instructions.

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Indicates how this component is obtained: manufactured in-house (make), purchased from suppliers (buy), transferred from another plant (transfer), or subcontracted to external manufacturers.

attributes.name: quantity_per_assembly
attributes.description: The number of units of this component required to build one unit of the parent assembly. Used for material requirements planning (MRP) calculations and cost rollup.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP Material Requirements Planning

attributes.name: reference_designator
attributes.description: Alphanumeric code identifying the specific location or function of the component in the assembly (e.g., R1, C5, U3 for electronics; A-01, B-12 for mechanical). Used in assembly instructions and maintenance documentation.
attributes.references: IPC Association Connecting Electronics Industries Standards

attributes.name: revision_level
attributes.description: The revision or version of the component that is specified for use in this assembly. Ensures that the correct component revision is used for manufacturing and quality control.

attributes.name: scrap_factor_percentage
attributes.description: Expected scrap or waste percentage for this component during assembly or manufacturing. Used to adjust material requirements planning (MRP) calculations to account for normal production losses.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP Scrap Calculation

attributes.name: sort_sequence
attributes.description: Numeric value used to control the display order of BOM lines in reports, pick lists, and assembly instructions. Lower values appear first.

attributes.name: substitute_qualification_status
attributes.description: Approval status of the substitute component for use in this assembly. Indicates whether the alternate has passed qualification testing and is approved for production use.

attributes.name: substitute_usage_restriction
attributes.description: Conditions or limitations on when the substitute component may be used (e.g., only for specific customer orders, only when primary is unavailable, geographic restrictions). Ensures proper alternate sourcing governance.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit in which the component quantity is expressed (e.g., each, kilogram, meter, liter). Must align with the component material master UOM for accurate MRP and inventory calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|G|L|ML|M|CM|FT|IN|SET|PAIR|BOX|ROLL|SHEET — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: The user or system account that created this BOM line record. Used for audit trail and change management purposes.

products.name: drawing
products.description: Engineering drawing record representing the formal 2D technical drawing released for a component or assembly. Captures drawing number, revision level, sheet count, drawing standard (ISO, ANSI), tolerance class, surface finish specification, material callout, approval status, release date, and associated CAD model reference. Serves as the authoritative manufacturing and inspection reference document.

attributes.name: drawing_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the engineering drawing record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Drawings released for a particular customer project must reference the customer_account for release approval and traceability.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: A drawing documents a component; linking drawing to component provides traceability and connects the drawing product.

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: The drawing table has an `eco_number` STRING field which is a denormalized reference to the Engineering Change Order that triggered the drawing release or revision. Adding a proper eco_id FK normalize

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: A technical drawing is associated with a specific revision of a component. The drawing table has `revision_level` STRING which is a display label, not a normalized FK. Linking drawing to engineering_r

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the drawing was approved by the authorized engineering authority. Precedes the release date.

attributes.name: assembly_level
attributes.description: Hierarchical level of the item depicted (component, subassembly, assembly, system). Indicates the complexity and position in the product structure.

attributes.name: cad_model_reference
attributes.description: Reference to the associated 3D CAD model file or identifier in the PLM system (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter item ID). Links the 2D drawing to its source 3D model.
attributes.references: ISO 16792 Digital Product Definition Data Practices

attributes.name: checked_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the engineer who reviewed and checked the drawing for accuracy and completeness before approval.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems - Document Control

attributes.name: confidentiality_level
attributes.description: Data classification level of the drawing (public, internal, confidential, restricted, proprietary). Governs access control and distribution.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the drawing record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.

attributes.name: drawing_number
attributes.description: The formal drawing number assigned per engineering numbering scheme. Serves as the externally-known unique identifier for this technical drawing across the product lifecycle.

attributes.name: drawing_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the drawing (draft, in_review, approved, released, obsolete, superseded). Governs whether the drawing can be used for manufacturing.
attributes.references: ISO 16792 Technical Product Documentation - Digital Product Definition Data Practices

attributes.name: drawing_type
attributes.description: Classification of the drawing by its purpose and content (assembly, detail, schematic, layout, installation, wiring, piping, fabrication). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: assembly|detail|schematic|layout|installation|wiring|piping|fabrication — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ASME Y14.24 Types and Applications of Engineering Drawings

attributes.name: drawn_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the engineer or drafter who created the drawing. Recorded in the title block.
attributes.references: ISO 7200 Technical Product Documentation - Data Fields in Title Blocks

attributes.name: export_control_classification
attributes.description: Export control classification number (ECCN) or similar designation if the drawing contains controlled technical data. Required for international compliance.
attributes.references: ITAR International Traffic in Arms Regulations; EAR Export Administration Regulations

attributes.name: file_format
attributes.description: Digital file format of the drawing (PDF, DWG, DXF, STEP, IGES, JT). Determines compatibility and usage.
attributes.references: ISO 10303 STEP Standard; ISO 16792 Digital Product Definition

attributes.name: file_path
attributes.description: Storage location or URI of the drawing file in the document management system or file server.
attributes.references: ISO 16792 Digital Product Definition Data Practices

attributes.name: is_master_drawing
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this is the master (authoritative) drawing for the part. True if this is the primary reference; false if it is a derivative or copy.
attributes.references: ISO 16792 Digital Product Definition Data Practices

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: ISO 639 language code for the primary language of text and annotations on the drawing (e.g., ENG, DEU, FRA, JPN).

attributes.name: material_callout
attributes.description: Material specification called out on the drawing (e.g., AISI 304, Al 6061-T6, SAE 1020). Defines the material from which the part is manufactured.
attributes.references: ISO 4063 Welding and Allied Processes; ASTM Material Standards

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the drawing record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: General notes, instructions, or annotations recorded on the drawing. May include manufacturing instructions, inspection requirements, or special handling.

attributes.name: part_number
attributes.description: The part number of the component or assembly depicted in this drawing. Links the drawing to the Bill of Materials (BOM) and inventory systems.
attributes.references: ISO 6428 Technical Drawings - Requirements for Microcopying

attributes.name: plm_item_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the drawing object in the Siemens Teamcenter PLM system. Enables traceability to the system of record.

attributes.name: projection_method
attributes.description: Orthographic projection method used (first_angle per ISO, third_angle per ANSI). Determines the arrangement of views.
attributes.references: ISO 128-30 Technical Drawings - Projection Methods; ASME Y14.3

attributes.name: release_date
attributes.description: Date when the drawing was officially released for manufacturing and procurement. Marks the transition from engineering to production.
attributes.references: ISO 16792 Digital Product Definition Data Practices

attributes.name: revision_level
attributes.description: Current revision level or version of the drawing (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Increments with each approved engineering change.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.35M Revision of Engineering Drawings

attributes.name: scale
attributes.description: Scale ratio of the drawing (e.g., 1:1, 1:2, 2:1, NTS for Not To Scale). Indicates the relationship between drawing dimensions and actual part dimensions.

attributes.name: sheet_count
attributes.description: Total number of sheets in this drawing set. Multi-sheet drawings are common for complex assemblies.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.1 Decimal Inch Drawing Sheet Size and Format

attributes.name: sheet_size
attributes.description: Standard sheet size designation (ISO: A0, A1, A2, A3, A4; ANSI: A, B, C, D, E, F) used for this drawing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A0|A1|A2|A3|A4|A|B|C|D|E|F — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 5457 Technical Product Documentation - Sizes and Layout of Drawing Sheets; ASME Y14.1

attributes.name: standard
attributes.description: The drafting standard governing this drawing (ISO, ANSI, ASME, DIN, JIS, BS). Determines dimensioning, tolerancing, and symbology conventions.
attributes.references: ISO 128 Technical Drawings - General Principles of Presentation; ASME Y14.5 Dimensioning and Tolerancing

attributes.name: superseded_by_drawing_number
attributes.description: Drawing number that supersedes this drawing when status is obsolete or superseded. Maintains traceability in the revision chain.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.35M Revision of Engineering Drawings

attributes.name: supersedes_drawing_number
attributes.description: Previous drawing number that this drawing supersedes. Establishes the backward link in the revision history.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.35M Revision of Engineering Drawings

attributes.name: surface_finish_specification
attributes.description: Surface roughness or finish requirement specified on the drawing (e.g., Ra 0.8, N6, 125 microinch). Critical for functional surfaces.
attributes.references: ISO 1302 Geometrical Product Specifications - Indication of Surface Texture; ASME B46.1

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: The descriptive title of the drawing that identifies the component, assembly, or system being documented.
attributes.references: ISO 7200 Technical Product Documentation - Data Fields in Title Blocks

attributes.name: tolerance_class
attributes.description: General tolerance class applied to dimensions not individually toleranced (fine, medium, coarse, precision, general). Defines default dimensional accuracy.
attributes.references: ISO 2768 General Tolerances; ASME Y14.5 Dimensioning and Tolerancing

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Primary unit of measure used for dimensions on the drawing (mm, cm, m, in, ft). Typically millimeters for ISO and inches for ANSI.
attributes.references: ISO 1000 SI Units; ASME Y14.5M Metric Dimensioning

attributes.name: weight_kg
attributes.description: Calculated or estimated weight of the part or assembly in kilograms. Used for logistics, handling, and structural analysis.

products.name: eco
products.description: Engineering Change Order — the formal governance record initiating, tracking, approving, and closing a controlled change to a released product design. Captures ECO number, change type (design, material, process), priority, reason code, affected items with disposition (use-as-is, rework, scrap, retrofit), effectivity date, cost/schedule impact, initiator, approver chain, closure status, and revision transition (from/to). Includes affected item detail: item reference, type, current/proposed revision, disposition action, quantity affected, and implementation status per item. Upon approval, also serves as the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) — the formal communication record distributed to production, supply chain, quality, and supplier portals capturing notification distribution list, acknowledgement tracking, implementation date, and affected BOM/drawing revisions. SSOT for engineering change governance and change communication per ISO 9001 change control requirements.
products.reference: ECO / Engineering Change Order (Teamcenter Change Management)

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the engineering change order record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑driven engineering change orders are tracked via ECO linked to the requesting customer_account for audit trails.

attributes.name: budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: ECOs consume engineering change budgets independently of projects. ECOs can exist without an engineering_project (standalone change orders). Direct ECO→finance_budget link enables budget vs. actual tr

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: ECOs affecting contracted products require customer approval per the sales contract terms. The eco table has customer_approval_required and customer_approval_date fields — linking to the specific sale

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: ECOs carry actual_cost_impact and estimated_cost_impact fields. Engineering change management requires tracking ECO costs against cost centers for financial accountability and change program reporting

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Engineering Change Orders are initiated within the context of engineering projects. The eco table has `initiator_department` and project-related metadata but no FK to the governing project. Linking ec

attributes.name: acknowledgement_count
attributes.description: Number of stakeholders who have acknowledged receipt of the ECN.

attributes.name: acknowledgement_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether recipients of the ECN are required to formally acknowledge receipt and understanding.

attributes.name: actual_cost_impact
attributes.description: Actual financial impact realized after implementation, including all direct and indirect costs.

attributes.name: actual_schedule_impact_days
attributes.description: Actual impact on production schedule in days realized after implementation.

attributes.name: affected_items_count
attributes.description: Total number of product items (parts, assemblies, drawings, documents) affected by this engineering change.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the ECO received final approval authorization to proceed with implementation.

attributes.name: approved_by_name
attributes.description: Full name of the final approver who authorized the engineering change.

attributes.name: approved_by_title
attributes.description: Job title or role of the final approver (e.g., Chief Engineer, VP Engineering, Quality Manager).

attributes.name: change_priority
attributes.description: Business priority level for implementing the change. Critical = safety/regulatory; High = customer impact; Medium = quality improvement; Low = cost reduction.

attributes.name: change_type
attributes.description: Classification of the engineering change by category: design (product geometry/function), material (BOM substitution), process (manufacturing method), documentation (drawing/spec update), specification (performance criteria), or tooling (fixture/jig modification).

attributes.name: closure_date
attributes.description: Date when the ECO was formally closed after successful implementation and verification.

attributes.name: cost_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost impact amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this ECO record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: customer_approval_date
attributes.description: Date when customer approval was received for the engineering change.

attributes.name: customer_approval_received
attributes.description: Indicates whether required customer approval has been obtained.

attributes.name: disposition_action
attributes.description: Primary disposition action for existing inventory and work-in-progress: use_as_is (no action required), rework (modify to new spec), scrap (discard), retrofit (field upgrade), return_to_supplier (vendor return).

attributes.name: eco_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the engineering change including technical rationale, scope, and implementation approach.

attributes.name: eco_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the engineering change order, externally visible and used across systems. Typically follows format ECO-NNNNNN.

attributes.name: effectivity_date
attributes.description: Date when the engineering change becomes effective and must be implemented in production. Controls when new revision supersedes old revision.

attributes.name: effectivity_reference
attributes.description: Reference value for effectivity (e.g., serial number, lot number, or date) that defines the cutover point for the change.

attributes.name: effectivity_type
attributes.description: Method by which the change takes effect: date (on specific date), serial_number (from specific unit serial), lot_batch (from specific production lot), immediate (next unit produced).

attributes.name: erp_system_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier linking this ECO to the corresponding engineering change record in the ERP system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).

attributes.name: estimated_cost_impact
attributes.description: Estimated financial impact of implementing the change, including material, labor, tooling, and scrap costs. Positive values indicate cost increase; negative values indicate savings.

attributes.name: estimated_schedule_impact_days
attributes.description: Estimated impact on production schedule in days. Positive values indicate delay; negative values indicate acceleration.

attributes.name: from_revision
attributes.description: Current revision level of the primary affected item before the change is applied.

attributes.name: implementation_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the engineering change was implemented in production systems and processes.

attributes.name: initiated_date
attributes.description: Date when the engineering change order was formally initiated and entered into the system.

attributes.name: initiator_department
attributes.description: Organizational department or functional area of the change initiator (e.g., Product Engineering, Quality, Manufacturing Engineering).

attributes.name: initiator_email
attributes.description: Email address of the change initiator for communication and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: last_modified_by
attributes.description: Name or user ID of the person who last modified this ECO record.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this ECO record was last updated.

attributes.name: lifecycle_status
attributes.description: Current state of the ECO in its approval and implementation workflow: draft (being prepared), submitted (awaiting review), under_review (in approval chain), approved (authorized for implementation), rejected (not approved), in_implementation (being executed), completed (closed), cancelled (withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|in_implementation|completed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: plm_system_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or URL linking this ECO to the corresponding record in the PLM system (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter).

attributes.name: reason_code
attributes.description: Root cause or business driver for initiating the engineering change: safety issue, regulatory compliance, quality improvement, cost reduction, component obsolescence, or customer-requested modification.

attributes.name: reason_description
attributes.description: Detailed explanation of the business or technical reason necessitating the change, including problem statement and justification.

attributes.name: requires_customer_approval
attributes.description: Indicates whether this engineering change requires formal customer notification and approval before implementation.

attributes.name: requires_supplier_notification
attributes.description: Indicates whether this change requires notification to suppliers for purchased components or materials.

attributes.name: submitted_date
attributes.description: Date when the ECO was submitted for formal review and approval.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Short descriptive title summarizing the nature of the engineering change.

attributes.name: to_revision
attributes.description: New revision level of the primary affected item after the change is applied.

products.name: ecn
products.description: Engineering Change Notice — the released notification record that communicates an approved and implemented engineering change to all affected stakeholders including production, supply chain, and quality. Captures ECN number, linked ECO, effective revision transition (from/to), implementation date, affected BOM revisions, affected drawings, distribution list, and acknowledgement tracking. Downstream consumers include MES, MRP, and supplier portals.

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the engineering change notice record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: ECNs in industrial manufacturing require formal customer notification and approval workflows (ecn.customer_notification_required flag exists but has no FK target). Linking ECN to customer_account iden

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: ECN-driven AVL governance: engineering changes that affect part form/fit/function require AVL re-evaluation. Linking ECN to the impacted AVL entry enables sourcing teams to trigger re-qualification an

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: ECNs have cost_impact_estimate and cost_impact_currency. In industrial manufacturing, ECN cost impacts are charged to cost centers for financial accountability. ECN is distinct from ECO — it is the fo

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent engineering change order that was approved and is now being communicated via this ECN.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: ECNs directly affect material masters in ERP — when an ECN is released, the material master revision level and effectivity are updated. Change management processes require knowing the primary material

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: ECNs with bom_impact_flag or routing_impact_flag affect specific production lines. Change impact assessment requires knowing which production lines must implement the ECN. Enables production line chan

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: ECN Supplier Notification process: when `supplier_notification_required=true`, procurement must track which supplier receives the ECN. A manufacturing domain expert expects ECNs to be directed at spec

attributes.name: acknowledgement_count
attributes.description: Number of stakeholders who have formally acknowledged receipt and understanding of this ECN. Used to track distribution completion.

attributes.name: acknowledgement_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether recipients must formally acknowledge receipt and understanding of this ECN. True = acknowledgement tracking enabled; False = informational only.

attributes.name: acknowledgement_target_count
attributes.description: Total number of stakeholders required to acknowledge this ECN before it can be marked as fully distributed.

attributes.name: affected_drawing_count
attributes.description: Total number of engineering drawings, Computer-Aided Design (CAD) models, or technical documents affected by this ECN.

attributes.name: affected_part_count
attributes.description: Total number of distinct part numbers or Bill of Materials (BOM) items affected by this ECN.

attributes.name: affected_product_lines
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or narrative of product lines, families, or platforms impacted by this ECN. Used for scoping and impact analysis.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the ECN was formally approved for release by the designated approver or change control board.

attributes.name: bom_impact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this ECN requires changes to one or more Bill of Materials (BOM) structures. True = BOM changes required; False = no BOM impact.

attributes.name: change_category
attributes.description: Primary category of the engineering change. Design = product design modification; Material = material substitution; Process = manufacturing process change; Documentation = drawing/spec update; Specification = requirement change; Supplier = vendor change.

attributes.name: change_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the engineering change being communicated, including technical modifications, affected components, and implementation instructions.

attributes.name: change_reason
attributes.description: Business justification and rationale for the engineering change, including root cause, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, or quality improvement drivers.

attributes.name: closure_date
attributes.description: Date when the ECN was closed after successful implementation and acknowledgement by all required stakeholders.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Additional notes, instructions, or clarifications related to the ECN implementation, distribution, or acknowledgement process.

attributes.name: cost_impact_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost impact estimate (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: cost_impact_estimate
attributes.description: Estimated total cost impact of implementing this ECN, including material, labor, tooling, and inventory disposition costs. Positive values indicate cost increase; negative values indicate cost savings.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this ECN record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: customer_notification_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether customers must be notified of this ECN due to form, fit, function, or regulatory changes. True = customer notification required; False = internal only.

attributes.name: distribution_list
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or structured text of roles, departments, or individuals who must receive and acknowledge this ECN (e.g., Production, Quality, Supply Chain, Engineering).

attributes.name: ecn_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the engineering change notice, typically formatted as ECN-NNNNNN. Used for external communication and tracking across systems.

attributes.name: ecn_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the ECN. Draft = being prepared; Released = approved for distribution; Distributed = sent to stakeholders; Acknowledged = recipients confirmed receipt; Closed = implementation complete; Cancelled = voided.

attributes.name: ecn_type
