attributes.description: Classification of the ECN based on urgency and scope. Standard = normal process; Urgent = expedited review; Emergency = immediate action required; Field Change = affects deployed products; Design Improvement = enhancement.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the engineering change becomes effective and must be implemented in production, procurement, and quality systems.

attributes.name: erp_sync_status
attributes.description: Status of synchronization of this ECN to downstream Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (SAP S/4HANA). Pending = awaiting sync; Synced = successfully transmitted; Failed = sync error; Not Required = no ERP impact.

attributes.name: implementation_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the engineering change was fully implemented across all affected systems and processes.

attributes.name: inventory_impact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this ECN affects existing inventory, requiring disposition decisions (scrap, rework, use-as-is). True = inventory action required; False = no inventory impact.

attributes.name: mes_sync_status
attributes.description: Status of synchronization of this ECN to Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for shop floor implementation. Pending = awaiting sync; Synced = successfully transmitted; Failed = sync error; Not Required = no MES impact.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this ECN record was last modified or updated.

attributes.name: plm_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier for this ECN in the source Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for system integration and traceability.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Priority level for implementation of the ECN. Critical = safety/regulatory; High = customer impact; Medium = quality improvement; Low = documentation only.

attributes.name: regulatory_impact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this ECN affects regulatory compliance, certifications, or safety approvals (UL, CE, ISO). True = regulatory review required; False = no regulatory impact.

attributes.name: release_date
attributes.description: Date when the ECN was officially released and approved for distribution to affected stakeholders.

attributes.name: revision_from
attributes.description: The previous revision level of the affected item(s) before this ECN takes effect. Used to track revision transitions.

attributes.name: revision_to
attributes.description: The new revision level of the affected item(s) after this ECN is implemented. Represents the target state.

attributes.name: routing_impact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this ECN requires changes to manufacturing routings or process plans. True = routing changes required; False = no routing impact.

attributes.name: supplier_notification_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether external suppliers must be notified of this ECN due to purchased part changes or specification updates. True = supplier notification required; False = internal only.

products.name: engineering_revision
products.description: Revision history record for a component or assembly, capturing each discrete revision state managed in PLM. Tracks revision label (A, B, C or 01, 02), lifecycle state (in-work, released, obsolete), release date, superseded revision, release authority, change justification summary, linked ECO, and effectivity window. Provides full revision lineage for traceability, regulatory compliance, configuration audits, and as-designed vs as-built reconciliation. Supports multi-level revision schemes (major/minor) and parallel branch revisions for variant management.

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the revision record. Primary key for the revision entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑specific revisions (e.g., custom part revisions) are tracked per customer_account for change management.

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: Revision-controlled sourcing: procurement must verify the AVL covers the new revision before issuing POs. `ppap_required` and `ppap_level` on engineering_revision confirm this APQP gate. Domain expert

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Reference to the component or assembly that this revision applies to. Links to the master component record in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system.

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) that communicates this revision to stakeholders. Links the revision to the change notification process.

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Engineering Change Order (ECO) that authorized and governs this revision. Links the revision to the formal change management process.

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: The engineering_revision table has a `specification_document` STRING field which is a denormalized reference to the governing engineering specification. Each revision is governed by a specific enginee

attributes.name: primary_superseded_by_revision_engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Reference to the newer revision that supersedes this revision. Establishes the revision lineage chain for traceability and configuration management. Null if this is the current active revision.

attributes.name: qualification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.qualification. Business justification: PPAP/APQP process: a new engineering revision with `ppap_required=true` triggers a supplier qualification activity. Linking revision to the qualification record enables PPAP status tracking against th

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date when this revision was formally approved by the release authority. Distinct from release date, represents the approval event in the workflow.

attributes.name: cad_file_reference
attributes.description: Reference path or identifier to the Computer-Aided Design (CAD) model file associated with this revision in the Product Data Management (PDM) or PLM system.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Document Management, ISO 16792 Digital Product Definition

attributes.name: ce_marking_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether CE marking (European Conformity) is required for this revision. True indicates CE marking is mandatory for European market release.

attributes.name: change_category
attributes.description: Classification of the reason for the revision change. Categorizes the business driver behind the Engineering Change Order (ECO). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design_improvement|cost_reduction|quality_issue|regulatory_compliance|customer_request|obsolescence|manufacturing_improvement|safety_enhancement — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: change_impact_level
attributes.description: Assessment of the impact magnitude of this revision on form, fit, function, quality, cost, or schedule. Critical indicates significant impact requiring extensive validation, low indicates minimal impact.
attributes.references: APQP - Change Management, FMEA Failure Mode and Effects Analysis

attributes.name: change_justification
attributes.description: Business justification and summary description of why this revision was created. Captures the rationale for the design change, improvement, or correction.

attributes.name: configuration_baseline
attributes.description: The configuration baseline identifier that this revision belongs to. Used for configuration management and as-designed vs as-built reconciliation.
attributes.references: ISO 10007 - Configuration Management, MIL-STD-973 Configuration Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this revision record was first created in the PLM system. Provides audit trail for record creation event.

attributes.name: dfm_analysis_completed
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether Design for Manufacturability (DFM) analysis has been completed for this revision. True indicates DFM review is complete and findings are incorporated.
attributes.references: APQP - Design for Manufacturability, Lean Manufacturing Principles

attributes.name: dfmea_completed
attributes.business_glossary_term: Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) Completed
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) has been completed for this revision. True indicates DFMEA is complete and risk mitigation actions are defined.

attributes.name: drawing_number
attributes.description: The engineering drawing number associated with this revision. Links the revision to the formal technical drawing documentation.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.35M - Revision of Engineering Drawings, ISO 5457 Technical Product Documentation

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date when this revision ceases to be effective and is superseded or obsoleted. Defines the end of the effectivity window. Null indicates the revision is currently effective with no planned end date.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this revision becomes effective for manufacturing, procurement, and engineering activities. Defines the beginning of the effectivity window.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 - Configuration Management, APQP Advanced Product Quality Planning

attributes.name: export_control_classification
attributes.description: Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or similar export control designation for this revision. Used to determine export licensing requirements and trade compliance.
attributes.references: US Export Administration Regulations (EAR), International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR)

attributes.name: interchangeability_code
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision is interchangeable with previous revisions. Fully interchangeable means drop-in replacement, form-fit-function means functionally equivalent, retrofit required means modification needed, not interchangeable means incompatible.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.35M - Revision of Engineering Drawings, MIL-STD-31000 Technical Data Packages

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The human-readable revision identifier following organizational revision scheme (e.g., A, B, C or 01, 02, 03). Represents the discrete revision state of the component.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 - Document and Data Control, ASME Y14.35M Revision of Engineering Drawings

attributes.name: lifecycle_state
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the revision within the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) workflow. In-work indicates active development, in-review indicates pending approval, approved indicates ready for release, released indicates production-ready, obsolete indicates no longer valid, superseded indicates replaced by newer revision, frozen indicates locked for reference. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_work|in_review|approved|released|obsolete|superseded|frozen — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 9001 - Document Control, Siemens Teamcenter PLM - Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: mass_production_approved
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this revision has been approved for mass production. True indicates all validation activities are complete and production release is authorized.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who last modified this revision record. Provides audit trail for record updates.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this revision record was last modified. Provides audit trail for the most recent update event.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or detailed description of changes made in this revision. Provides supplementary context beyond the change justification summary.

attributes.name: pfmea_completed
attributes.business_glossary_term: Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) Completed
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) has been completed for this revision. True indicates PFMEA is complete and process controls are established.

attributes.name: ppap_level
attributes.description: The PPAP submission level required for this revision (1-5). Level 1 is warrant only, Level 5 is full submission with samples. Null if PPAP is not required.

attributes.name: ppap_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) submission is required for this revision. True indicates customer or regulatory requirement for PPAP documentation.
attributes.references: AIAG PPAP-4 - Production Part Approval Process

attributes.name: prototype_test_date
attributes.description: The date when prototype testing was completed for this revision. Null if prototype testing has not been performed.

attributes.name: prototype_tested
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether a physical prototype of this revision has been built and tested. True indicates prototype validation is complete.

attributes.name: reach_compliant
attributes.business_glossary_term: Registration, Evaluation, Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this revision complies with REACH regulation for chemical substances. True indicates compliance with EU chemical safety requirements.

attributes.name: regulatory_compliance_status
attributes.description: Status of regulatory compliance assessment for this revision. Indicates whether the revision meets applicable regulatory requirements (ISO, IEC, UL, CE, OSHA, EPA).
attributes.references: ISO 9001 - Compliance Obligations, IEC 61131, UL Certification, CE Marking

attributes.name: release_authority
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person, role, or committee that authorized the release of this revision. Captures the approval authority for traceability and compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 - Document Control, 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records

attributes.name: release_authority_role
attributes.description: The organizational role or title of the release authority (e.g., Chief Engineer, Engineering Manager, Change Control Board). Provides context for the approval level.

attributes.name: release_date
attributes.description: The date when this revision was officially released for production use. Represents the business event timestamp when the revision transitioned to released state.

attributes.name: revision_type
attributes.description: Classification of the revision indicating the magnitude of change. Major revisions represent significant design changes, minor revisions represent incremental improvements, patch revisions represent corrections, branch revisions represent parallel variant development, and prototype revisions represent pre-release experimental versions.

attributes.name: rohs_compliant
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this revision complies with Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directive. True indicates the design meets RoHS material restrictions.

attributes.name: ul_certification_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether Underwriters Laboratories (UL) product safety certification is required for this revision. True indicates UL certification is mandatory for market release.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or engineer who created this revision record in the PLM system. Provides audit trail for record creation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 - Document Control, 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records

products.name: engineering_specification
products.description: Engineering specification document record defining technical requirements, performance criteria, material standards, and acceptance criteria for a component, assembly, or system. Captures specification number, revision, specification type (material, functional, interface, environmental, safety), applicable standards (IEC, ISO, UL, CE), scope description, approval status, and linked components. Serves as the authoritative technical requirements baseline for design validation and supplier qualification.

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the engineering specification document record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑specific specifications are maintained for contract compliance; linking to customer_account enables specification‑customer mapping.

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: Specification-restricted sourcing: engineering specifications in industrial manufacturing often restrict which suppliers can manufacture to the spec (e.g., special process approvals, Nadcap). Linking

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Specification applies to a component; adding component_id FK normalizes the relationship and removes the denormalized linked_component_ids list.

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: The engineering_specification table has an `ecn_number` STRING field which is a denormalized reference to the Engineering Change Notice that communicated the specification change. Adding a proper ecn_

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: The engineering_specification table has an `eco_number` STRING field which is a denormalized reference to the ECO that created or revised the specification. Adding a proper eco_id FK normalizes this r

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Engineering specifications for supplier-qualified materials reference the governing procurement contract. In industrial manufacturing, supplier qualification specs are contractually bound — linking sp

attributes.name: qualification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.qualification. Business justification: Specification-driven qualification: `supplier_qualification_required` flag on engineering_specification mandates a qualification activity. Linking to the qualification record enables APQP teams to tra

attributes.name: superseded_by_specification_engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specification that supersedes this specification. Null if this specification is still current. Supports specification revision history and traceability.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control and Change Management

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: SPECIFICATION CONTROL: Supplier‑specific specifications are linked to the responsible supplier for qualification and audit reporting.

attributes.name: acceptance_criteria
attributes.description: Specific criteria and test methods used to determine whether a component or assembly meets the specification requirements. Defines pass/fail thresholds for inspection, testing, and validation activities.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: applicable_standards
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of industry, regulatory, and international standards that this specification must comply with (e.g., IEC 61131, ISO 9001, UL 508, CE Marking, ANSI standards). Critical for design validation, supplier qualification, and regulatory compliance.
attributes.references: IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission), ISO (International Organization for Standardization), UL (Underwriters Laboratories), CE Marking, ANSI (American National Standards Institute)

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the specification was formally approved and released for use. Key milestone in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) workflow.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the specification document: draft (initial creation), in_review (under engineering review), approved (released for use), obsolete (no longer valid), or superseded (replaced by newer revision or specification).

attributes.name: approver_name
attributes.description: Name of the authorized individual who approved the specification for release. Part of the document control and approval workflow.

attributes.name: change_reason
attributes.description: Textual description of the reason for the most recent change to the specification. Captures the business or technical justification for the revision, supporting change traceability and audit requirements.

attributes.name: confidentiality_level
attributes.description: Data classification level of the specification document: public (no restrictions), internal (internal use only), confidential (business-sensitive), or restricted (highly sensitive, limited access). Governs access control and distribution policies.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the specification record was first created in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Records Management, Siemens Teamcenter PLM

attributes.name: design_authority
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the engineering team, department, or individual responsible for the technical content and maintenance of this specification. Serves as the point of contact for specification-related questions and change requests.

attributes.name: dfm_analysis_completed
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Design for Manufacturability (DFM) analysis has been completed for this specification. DFM analysis ensures that the design can be efficiently and cost-effectively manufactured.
attributes.references: DFM (Design for Manufacturability) Best Practices, APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: dfmea_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) Reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or document link to the Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (DFMEA) associated with this specification. DFMEA identifies potential failure modes in the design and their effects on product performance and safety.
attributes.references: DFMEA (Design Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: document_format
attributes.description: File format of the specification document (e.g., PDF for released documents, DOCX for drafts, DWG for CAD drawings, STEP/IGES for 3D models, XML for structured data exchange).
attributes.references: ISO 10303 STEP Standard, ISO 9001 Document Control

attributes.name: document_location
attributes.description: File path, URL, or document management system reference where the full specification document is stored. Typically points to a location in Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management or a shared engineering repository.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the specification becomes effective and mandatory for use in design, procurement, and manufacturing activities. Aligns with Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) release workflows.

attributes.name: environmental_conditions
attributes.description: Specified environmental operating conditions and limits, including temperature range, humidity, vibration, shock, altitude, and exposure to chemicals or contaminants. Critical for environmental-type specifications.
attributes.references: ISO 14001 Environmental Management, IEC 60068 Environmental Testing

attributes.name: language
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code indicating the primary language of the specification document (e.g., ENG for English, DEU for German, FRA for French). Supports multi-language engineering documentation in global manufacturing operations.

attributes.name: material_standard
attributes.description: Specific material standard or grade required for the component or assembly (e.g., ASTM A36, SAE 304, ISO 898-1). Applicable primarily to material-type specifications.
attributes.references: ASTM International, SAE International, ISO Material Standards

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the individual who last modified the specification record in the PLM system. Tracks responsibility for the most recent change.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the specification record was last modified in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system. Tracks the most recent update to the record for audit and change tracking purposes.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Records Management, Siemens Teamcenter PLM

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or clarifications related to the specification. Captures supplementary information that does not fit into other structured fields.

attributes.name: obsolete_date
attributes.description: Date when the specification was marked as obsolete or superseded. Null if the specification is still active.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control and Records Management

attributes.name: performance_criteria
attributes.description: Detailed performance requirements and acceptance criteria that the component, assembly, or system must meet. Includes quantitative metrics such as load capacity, operating temperature range, voltage ratings, cycle life, and reliability targets.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 Requirements Engineering, IEC 61131 PLC Standards

attributes.name: pfmea_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) Reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or document link to the Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) associated with this specification. PFMEA identifies potential failure modes in the manufacturing process and their effects on product quality.
attributes.references: PFMEA (Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: prototype_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a prototype must be built and validated before the specification is approved for production use. Supports the Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP) and Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) workflows.
attributes.references: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), PPAP (Production Part Approval Process)

attributes.name: revision
attributes.description: Current revision level of the specification document, typically alphanumeric (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Incremented with each approved change through the Engineering Change Order (ECO) or Engineering Change Notice (ECN) process.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control and Change Management

attributes.name: safety_requirements
attributes.description: Safety-related requirements and compliance criteria, including electrical safety, mechanical safety, functional safety, and hazard mitigation measures. Applicable to safety-type specifications and critical for certification.
attributes.references: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety, IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity, UL Safety Certification, CE Marking

attributes.name: scope_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the scope, applicability, and boundaries of the specification. Defines what is covered and what is explicitly excluded from the specification requirements.

attributes.name: specification_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the specification document, typically following organizational numbering conventions. Externally-known unique code used for reference in engineering documentation, supplier communications, and design reviews.

attributes.name: specification_type
attributes.description: Classification of the specification document by its primary purpose: material (material properties and composition), functional (operational requirements), interface (connection and integration standards), environmental (operating conditions and environmental compliance), safety (safety requirements and certifications), or performance (performance criteria and benchmarks).
attributes.references: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 Systems and Software Engineering Requirements

attributes.name: supplier_qualification_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether suppliers must undergo formal qualification and approval before being authorized to supply components or materials meeting this specification. Supports supplier management and procurement processes.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Supplier Management, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process)

attributes.name: test_method
attributes.description: Standardized test method or procedure reference used to verify compliance with the specification (e.g., ASTM test methods, IEC test standards, internal test procedures). Links to quality inspection plans and validation protocols.
attributes.references: ASTM International, IEC Testing Standards, ISO 17025 Testing and Calibration Laboratories

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Descriptive title of the specification document that clearly identifies the component, assembly, or system being specified.

attributes.name: tolerance_specification
attributes.description: Dimensional, geometric, and functional tolerances specified for the component or assembly. Defines acceptable variation ranges for critical dimensions and characteristics, supporting Design for Manufacturability (DFM) and Statistical Process Control (SPC).
attributes.references: ISO 286 Tolerances and Fits, ASME Y14.5 Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing (GD&T)

attributes.name: validation_date
attributes.description: Date when the design validation was completed. Null if validation is not yet complete. Key milestone in the product development lifecycle.

attributes.name: validation_status
attributes.description: Current status of the design validation and verification activities for this specification: not_started (validation not yet initiated), in_progress (validation activities underway), completed (validation successfully completed), or failed (validation did not meet acceptance criteria).
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Design Verification and Validation, APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the individual who created the specification record in the PLM system. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.

products.name: project
products.description: Engineering project master record representing a discrete R&D or product development initiative. Captures project code, project name, project type (new product development, product improvement, platform, cost reduction), program phase (concept, development, validation, launch), target launch date, project manager, budget allocation, priority, and linked product family. Serves as the organizing entity for all engineering deliverables, prototypes, and design reviews within a development program.

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the engineering project record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, engineering projects are routinely initiated for specific customer programs (custom automation systems, electrification solutions). Linking engineering_project to customer

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Development supply agreement: engineering projects (NPI, platform development) operate under specific supplier agreements (NDAs, development contracts, tooling agreements). Linking project→agreement e

attributes.name: budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Each engineering project references a budget record for approved spending limits and forecasting.

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Engineering projects are executed within a legal entity (company code) for intercompany cost allocation, capitalization (CAPEX/OPEX classification exists on the product), and statutory reporting. Indu

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Projects are budgeted to specific cost centers for expense tracking and variance analysis.

attributes.name: product_family_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product family or platform that this engineering project is developing or enhancing.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Project revenue and profitability are reported to profit centers for segment performance reporting.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Project Execution tracks the main stock location for on‑site inventory used by the engineering project.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: PROJECT MANAGEMENT: Engineering projects track a lead supplier for budgeting, risk assessment, and contract governance.

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Project Planning assigns a primary warehouse for storing project‑specific materials and components.

attributes.name: actual_launch_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the engineering project completed and the product was launched to production or market.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who approved the engineering project for execution.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the engineering project was formally approved for execution.

attributes.name: budget_allocated_amount
attributes.description: Total budget amount allocated to the engineering project for all development activities and resources.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI (Financial Accounting) Project Budget

attributes.name: budget_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the project budget amounts.

attributes.name: budget_spent_amount
attributes.description: Cumulative amount of budget spent to date on the engineering project.

attributes.name: business_justification
attributes.description: Strategic rationale and business case for undertaking the engineering project, including expected ROI and market drivers.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) Business Case Standards

attributes.name: capex_opex_classification
attributes.description: Financial classification of the project budget as capital expenditure, operational expenditure, or a mix of both.

attributes.name: collaboration_partners
attributes.description: List of external partners, suppliers, or research institutions collaborating on the engineering project.

attributes.name: complexity_score
attributes.description: Numerical score (1-10) representing the technical and organizational complexity of the engineering project.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the engineering project record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: design_methodology
attributes.description: Engineering design and development methodology being applied to the project.

products.name: test_result
products.description: Engineering test and validation result record capturing outcomes of design verification and validation (DV&V) activities performed on prototypes, pre-production units, or production samples. Captures test type (functional, environmental, EMC, safety, endurance, performance, reliability), test standard reference, tested unit identification (prototype phase, build number, component revision), test date, test facility, pass/fail outcome, measured values vs acceptance criteria, failure description, linked DFMEA failure mode, and prototype build context (phase, purpose, disposition). Supports DVP&R evidence for PPAP, regulatory certification submissions, and R&D program milestone tracking.

The test_result table has `test_specification_v

attributes.name: vendor_site_id

attributes.name: acceptance_criteria_lower_limit
attributes.description: Minimum acceptable value for the test measurement. Defines the lower boundary of the pass/fail acceptance range.

attributes.name: acceptance_criteria_target
attributes.description: Target or nominal value for the test measurement. Represents the ideal design specification value within the acceptance range.

attributes.name: acceptance_criteria_upper_limit
attributes.description: Maximum acceptable value for the test measurement. Defines the upper boundary of the pass/fail acceptance range.

attributes.name: build_number
attributes.description: Prototype build iteration number or production batch identifier. Tracks which manufacturing build or prototype iteration the tested unit belongs to.

attributes.name: certification_body
attributes.description: Name of the regulatory or certification authority for which this test evidence is required (e.g., UL, TUV, CSA, FCC). Applicable when regulatory submission flag is true.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this test result record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.description: Reference to the Design Verification Plan and Report document that governs this test. Links test execution to the master validation plan.

attributes.name: environmental_conditions
attributes.description: Description of environmental conditions during test execution (e.g., temperature, humidity, pressure). Captures test environment context for result interpretation.

attributes.name: failure_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of any failure, defect, or non-conformance observed during the test. Captures failure mode, symptoms, and conditions. Populated only when test outcome is fail or conditional pass.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Non-Conformance Documentation, FMEA Failure Mode and Effects Analysis

attributes.name: failure_mode_code
attributes.description: Standardized code or identifier for the observed failure mode. Links test failure to DFMEA or PFMEA failure mode catalog for root cause analysis.
attributes.references: FMEA Failure Mode and Effects Analysis, AIAG FMEA Handbook

attributes.name: measured_value
attributes.description: Primary quantitative measurement or test result value obtained during the test. The principal measured outcome of the test activity.

attributes.name: measured_value_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the measured value (e.g., volts, amps, degrees Celsius, newtons, cycles). Ensures correct interpretation of test measurements.

attributes.name: measurement_uncertainty
attributes.description: Quantified uncertainty or tolerance associated with the measured value. Expresses the confidence interval or margin of error in the measurement.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the person who last modified this test result record. Audit trail for record changes.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this test result record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: prototype_phase
attributes.description: Development phase or maturity stage of the tested unit. Indicates whether the unit is a concept prototype, alpha build, beta unit, pre-production sample, or production unit.

attributes.name: regulatory_submission_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this test result is required for regulatory certification or compliance submission (e.g., UL, CE, FCC). Flags test data needed for regulatory dossiers.
attributes.references: UL Standards, CE Marking Directives, IEC Compliance

attributes.name: retest_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this test is a retest of a previously failed or inconclusive test. Used to track test iteration history.

attributes.name: root_cause_analysis_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a formal root cause analysis (RCA) is required for this test result. Typically true for test failures or unexpected results.

attributes.name: test_date
attributes.description: Date when the test was executed. Principal business event timestamp for the test activity.

attributes.name: test_duration_hours
attributes.description: Total elapsed time for test execution measured in hours. Captures the actual test run time for endurance and long-duration tests.

attributes.name: test_end_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the test execution completed. Used for duration calculation and detailed test timeline tracking.

attributes.name: test_notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes, observations, or comments recorded by the test engineer during test execution. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields.

attributes.name: test_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the test execution. Externally-known unique test reference number used for traceability and reporting.

attributes.name: test_outcome
attributes.description: Overall pass/fail result of the test execution. Indicates whether the tested unit met the acceptance criteria defined in the test specification.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management, PPAP Production Part Approval Process

attributes.name: test_purpose
attributes.description: Business objective or reason for conducting the test. Describes the validation goal, such as design verification, production qualification, regulatory compliance, or failure investigation.

attributes.name: test_specification_version
attributes.description: Version or revision of the test specification document used for this test execution. Ensures traceability to the correct test procedure version.

attributes.name: test_standard_reference
attributes.description: Industry or regulatory standard governing the test procedure. References the specific standard, specification, or protocol followed during test execution (e.g., IEC 61131-2, UL 508, ISO 16750).
attributes.references: IEC Standards, UL Standards, ISO Standards

attributes.name: test_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the test execution began. Used for duration calculation and detailed test timeline tracking.

attributes.name: test_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the test execution. Tracks the test through its workflow from scheduling to completion.

attributes.name: test_type
attributes.description: Classification of the test performed. Defines the category of validation activity conducted on the unit under test. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: functional|environmental|emc|safety|endurance|performance|reliability|thermal|vibration|shock|humidity|salt_spray|dust|ingress_protection — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IEC 61131 PLC Programming Standards, ISO 9001 Quality Management

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the person who created this test result record in the system. Audit trail for record creation.

products.name: affected_item
products.description: This association product represents the Affected Item relationship between an Engineering Change Order (ECO) and a Component. It captures the formal per-component change record within an ECO — the operational entity that engineers create, manage, and close for each component impacted by a change. Each record links one ECO to one component and carries the disposition instruction, revision transition, effectivity data, and implementation status that exist only in the context of this specific ECO-component pairing. Serves as the SSOT for affected item detail required by ISO 9001 change control, PPAP submissions, and quality audit trails.. Existence Justification: In PLM and engineering change management, an ECO formally governs changes to multiple components simultaneously (e.g., a design change affecting a PLC module, its mounting bracket, and a connector), while a single component can be affected by many ECOs over its lifecycle (initial release, design iterations, material substitutions, regulatory updates). This is a first-class operational relationship — engineers actively create, manage, and close 'Affected Item' records per ECO-component pair, each carrying disposition instructions, revision transitions, and effectivity data that belong to neither the ECO nor the component alone.

attributes.name: affected_item_id
attributes.description: Primary key for the affected_item association

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the Component master record that is affected by the engineering change.

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the Engineering Change Order that governs this affected item record.

attributes.name: disposition_action
attributes.description: The required action for existing inventory and work-in-progress of this specific component under this ECO. Belongs to the affected item relationship, not to the ECO globally or the component master, as disposition varies per component per change.

attributes.name: effectivity_date
attributes.description: The date on which this specific component change becomes effective for use in production. May differ from the ECO-level effectivity date when components within the same ECO have staggered implementation schedules.

attributes.name: from_revision
attributes.description: The current revision level of this component before the engineering change is applied. Captured per affected item because different components on the same ECO may be transitioning from different revision levels.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

attributes.name: implementation_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the engineering change was implemented for this specific component in production systems. Captured per affected item for traceability and PPAP compliance.

attributes.name: implementation_status
attributes.description: Current implementation status of the change for this specific component. Tracked per affected item to allow partial ECO closure when some components are implemented ahead of others.

attributes.name: quantity_affected
attributes.description: The quantity of this component in existing inventory or WIP that is subject to the disposition action. Belongs to the affected item relationship as it is specific to the ECO-component pairing at the time of change.

attributes.name: to_revision
attributes.description: The target revision level of this component after the engineering change is applied. Defines the post-change revision state for this specific component-ECO pairing.
attributes.references: ASME Y14.35 Revision of Engineering Drawings

domains.name: quality
domains.description: Quality assurance and control domain encompassing SPC, Cp/Cpk indices, inspection plans, NCRs, CAPAs, PPAP, APQP, and FMEA records. Manages in-process and final inspection results, supplier quality audits, compliance testing, and regulatory conformance data aligned with ISO 9001 and SAP QM.

products.name: inspection_plan
products.description: Master definition of inspection plans aligned with SAP QM, specifying inspection characteristics, sampling procedures, control methods, and acceptance criteria for in-process and final inspection operations. Covers PPAP, APQP, and routine production inspection plans per ISO 9001 requirements.

attributes.name: inspection_plan_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the inspection plan record. Primary key for the inspection_plan data product in the quality domain.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Plan (PLKO/PLMK); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑Specific Inspection Plan process requires linking each inspection_plan to the owning customer_account for contract compliance reporting.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Inspection plans are created for each component to define inspection characteristics; linking ensures traceability from plan to the exact component inspected.

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: APQP/PPAP requires configuration-specific inspection plans — a 480V drive variant has different inspection criteria than a 240V variant. Quality engineers must link plans to the exact product configur

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Planning costs for inspections are budgeted to a cost center; required for inspection budgeting and variance analysis.

attributes.name: drawing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: Inspection plans reference engineering drawings for dimensional characteristics and GD&T callouts. This is a fundamental industrial manufacturing practice — inspection plans cite the governing drawing

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: When an ECO changes a component, the associated inspection plan must be revised. The existing eco_number plain-text field is a denormalized ECO reference; replacing it with eco_id FK enforces referent

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Inspection plans must be updated when engineering revisions change part tolerances or specifications. Linking inspection_plan to engineering_revision is a core APQP/PPAP traceability requirement — any

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Inspection plans are derived directly from engineering specifications defining tolerances, acceptance criteria, and test methods. APQP and PPAP require inspection plans to reference the governing engi

attributes.name: fmea_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: inspection_plan carries fmea_reference as a plain STRING field referencing the FMEA that drove the inspection plan's characteristic definitions and control limits. This is a denormalized text referenc

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Inspection plans are plant-specific in SAP QM and industrial QMS systems. inspection_plan.plant_code is a denormalized plain text reference to the plant. Proper FK enables plant-level inspection plan

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection plans are defined per product SKU for quality control; linking enables plan retrieval by SKU in the Inspection Planning process.

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Customer-specific inspection plans in industrial manufacturing are contractually mandated by SLA agreements specifying AQL levels, PPAP requirements, and APQP phases. Linking inspection_plan to sla_ag

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the supplier to whom this inspection plan applies, for incoming or supplier-specific inspection plans. Relevant for receiving inspection and supplier quality audit plans managed via SAP Ariba.
attributes.references: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; SAP QM Vendor; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the inspection plan was formally approved for use. Provides an audit trail for ISO 9001 document control and regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: apqp_phase
attributes.description: APQP phase during which this inspection plan was developed or is applicable. Phase 1: Plan and Define; Phase 2: Product Design and Development; Phase 3: Process Design and Development; Phase 4: Product and Process Validation; Phase 5: Feedback, Assessment and Corrective Action.

attributes.name: aql_level
attributes.description: Acceptable Quality Level expressed as a percentage defective, defining the maximum tolerable defect rate for the sampling plan. Used in conjunction with ISO 2859-1 / ANSI Z1.4 sampling tables.
attributes.references: ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; SAP QM Sampling Scheme

attributes.name: control_method_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the measurement or control method used during inspection (e.g., visual, dimensional, functional, chemical, SPC chart). References the SAP QM master inspection characteristic control indicator.
attributes.references: SAP QM QPAM.PRFME; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; SPC

attributes.name: control_plan_reference
attributes.description: Document number of the associated APQP Control Plan that this inspection plan implements. The control plan defines the process controls and reaction plans linked to inspection characteristics.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP Control Plan; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

attributes.name: cpk_minimum
attributes.description: Minimum acceptable Cpk (Process Capability Index) value required for this inspection characteristic. Typically 1.33 for standard processes and 1.67 for safety-critical characteristics per PPAP requirements.
attributes.references: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; APQP; SAP QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; Cp/Cpk

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the inspection plan record was first created in the source system. Provides audit trail for data governance and ISO 9001 document control.

attributes.name: customer_specific_requirement
attributes.description: Indicates whether this inspection plan was created or modified to satisfy a specific customer quality requirement (CSR) beyond standard internal or regulatory requirements. Relevant for OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) supply relationships.

attributes.name: effective_from
attributes.description: Date from which this inspection plan version becomes valid and applicable for production inspection operations. Aligns with SAP QM validity start date.

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date on which this inspection plan version expires and is no longer valid for new inspection lots. Null indicates the plan is open-ended with no scheduled expiry. Aligns with SAP QM validity end date.

attributes.name: equipment_category
attributes.description: Category of measurement equipment or test instrument required to perform the inspection. Used for calibration scheduling and resource allocation in Maximo Asset Management.
attributes.references: SAP QM Equipment Assignment; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025

attributes.name: inspection_method_code
attributes.description: Reference to the standardized inspection method or test procedure document used to perform the inspection. Links to the SAP QM inspection method master record.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Method (QPAM); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

attributes.name: inspection_scope
attributes.description: Defines the intensity of inspection applied: full (normal), reduced (lower frequency for trusted suppliers/processes), tightened (increased scrutiny after failures), or skip-lot (periodic sampling). Aligned with SPC-driven dynamic inspection level adjustments.
attributes.references: ISO 2859-1 Switching Rules; SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: inspection_stage
attributes.description: Stage in the production or supply chain process at which the inspection is performed. Incoming covers receiving inspection; in-process covers WIP (Work In Progress) checks; final covers end-of-line; outgoing covers pre-shipment; skip indicates skip-lot logic.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Stage; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 and 8.6; AIAG PPAP

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the inspection plan record was last updated in the source system. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental lakehouse ingestion.

attributes.name: long_text_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the inspection plan scope, special instructions, safety precautions, and any additional context required by the inspector. Corresponds to SAP QM long text on the task list header.
attributes.references: SAP QM Task List Long Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

attributes.name: lower_tolerance_limit
attributes.description: Lower specification limit for the primary inspection characteristic. Values below this limit result in a non-conformance. Used in SPC and Cp/Cpk capability index calculations.
attributes.references: SAP QM QPAM.UNTOL; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SPC; Cp/Cpk

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Date by which the inspection plan must be reviewed and revalidated to ensure continued suitability and compliance with current product specifications and regulatory requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Internal Quality Management Procedure

attributes.name: operation_number
attributes.description: Routing operation number within the inspection plan, corresponding to the SAP QM task list operation (PLPO.VORNR). Defines the sequence of inspection steps within the plan.
attributes.references: SAP QM PLPO.VORNR; SAP PP Routing

attributes.name: plan_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or short description of the inspection plan, identifying the product, process, or operation it governs.

attributes.name: plan_number
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the inspection plan, aligned with SAP QM plan group and plan group counter. Used for cross-system reference and document control.

attributes.name: plan_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the inspection plan. Controls whether the plan is available for use in production inspection operations. Draft plans are under development; active plans are approved for use; obsolete plans are superseded.

attributes.name: plan_type
attributes.description: Classification of the inspection plan by its operational purpose. Covers in-process, final inspection, receiving inspection, supplier audit, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), and first article inspection types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_process|final_inspection|receiving|supplier_audit|ppap|apqp|first_article — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Type (PLKO.INSPK); AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; APQP; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: plan_version
attributes.description: Version or revision number of the inspection plan, corresponding to the SAP QM plan group counter. Tracks engineering changes and revisions aligned with ECO/ECN processes.
attributes.references: SAP QM PLKO.ZAEHL; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; ECO/ECN process

attributes.name: ppap_level
attributes.description: PPAP submission level (1–5) required for this inspection plan, per AIAG PPAP standard. Level 1 requires only a Part Submission Warrant; Level 3 is the default full submission; Level 5 requires records reviewed at the manufacturing site.

attributes.name: product_group_code
attributes.description: SAP material group or product family code to which this inspection plan applies. Enables grouping of inspection plans by product category for reporting and analytics.
attributes.references: SAP MM MARA.MATKL; SAP QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

attributes.name: revision_reason
attributes.description: Description of the reason for the current plan version, such as an ECO (Engineering Change Order), CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) outcome, customer complaint, or regulatory update.
attributes.references: SAP QM Change Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; ECO/ECN

attributes.name: sample_size
attributes.description: Number of units to be drawn from the inspection lot for evaluation. Determined by the sampling procedure and AQL level. A value of zero indicates 100% inspection.
attributes.references: SAP QM QPQMEL.STPRO; ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: sample_size_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the sample size quantity (e.g., EA for each/piece, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|M|L|M2|M3 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP MM Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000-1

attributes.name: sampling_procedure_code
attributes.description: Code referencing the statistical sampling procedure applied in this inspection plan, including sample size, acceptance number, and rejection number per AQL (Acceptable Quality Level) tables.
attributes.references: SAP QM Sampling Procedure (QPQMEL); ISO 2859-1 (AQL Sampling); ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: spc_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether SPC (Statistical Process Control) charting and Cp/Cpk capability monitoring are activated for this inspection plan. When true, measurement results feed into control charts and process capability analysis.
attributes.references: SAP QM SPC Indicator; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; SPC; Cp/Cpk

attributes.name: target_value
attributes.description: Nominal or target value for the primary inspection characteristic. Represents the ideal measurement around which tolerances are defined. Used as the centerline reference in SPC control charts.
attributes.references: SAP QM QPAM.SOLLWERT; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SPC

attributes.name: upper_tolerance_limit
attributes.description: Upper specification limit for the primary inspection characteristic. Values exceeding this limit result in a non-conformance. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) and Cp/Cpk capability index calculations.
attributes.references: SAP QM QPAM.OBTOL; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; SPC; Cp/Cpk

attributes.name: usage_decision_code
attributes.description: SAP QM usage decision code that defines the default disposition action (accept, reject, rework) applied when inspection results meet or fail acceptance criteria.
attributes.references: SAP QM Usage Decision (QMEL); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: work_center_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the work center or production cell where the inspection operation is performed. Corresponds to SAP PP/QM work center assignment on the inspection routing.
attributes.references: SAP QM PLPO.ARBID; SAP PP Work Center; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User ID of the quality engineer or planner who originally created this inspection plan record in SAP QM or the source system.

products.name: inspection_lot
products.description: Transactional inspection lot record representing a single inspection event triggered by goods receipt, production order completion, in-process milestone, periodic schedule, or customer return receipt. Captures lot origin, material/part reference, inspection quantity, inspection type (incoming, in-process, final, recurring, RMA-receipt), current status (created, released, results-recorded, usage-decided), and disposition decision (accept, reject, rework, scrap). Links to inspection plan for execution instructions and generates inspection_result child records. Triggers NCR creation upon reject disposition or non-conformance detection. Central transactional entity for quality execution aligned with SAP QM inspection lot processing.

attributes.name: inspection_lot_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single inspection lot record in the quality execution system. Primary key for the inspection_lot entity.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer Inspection Lot Reporting mandates associating each inspection_lot with the customer_account to deliver lot‑level quality results to the customer.

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: Inspection lots must be traceable to the specific product configuration being produced for defect analysis by configuration variant. Quality reporting on defect rates per configuration (e.g., specific

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost of inspection activities allocated to cost center for cost‑of‑quality reporting; finance tracks these expenses.

attributes.name: delivery_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: Outbound delivery inspections and inbound goods-receipt inspection lots are directly triggered by a delivery in SAP QM. Quality teams need delivery_id on inspection_lot to trace which shipment trigger

attributes.name: goods_issue_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.goods_issue. Business justification: In SAP QM and industrial manufacturing, a goods issue movement can trigger an outbound inspection lot (e.g., final inspection before shipment). Linking inspection_lot to goods_issue enables quality ga

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Reference to the sales order associated with this inspection lot, applicable for customer-specific inspection requirements, pre-shipment inspections, or RMA receipt inspections. Links quality execution to order fulfillment.
attributes.references: SAP SD Sales Order; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.3

attributes.name: inspection_plan_id
attributes.description: Reference to the inspection plan (control plan) that defines the inspection operations, characteristics, sampling procedures, and acceptance criteria to be executed for this lot. Aligns with SAP QM inspection plan assignment.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; APQP Control Plan

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: In SAP QM and IATF 16949 manufacturing, every inspection lot is raised against a specific lot/batch. This link enables lot-level quality traceability for PPAP, containment, and disposition decisions.

attributes.name: material_requirement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: Inspection lots for incoming goods trace back to the material requirement that drove the purchase order, enabling planning-to-quality traceability reporting. In industrial manufacturing MRP/QM integra

attributes.name: ncr_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) record created as a result of this inspection lot's non-conformance or reject disposition. Enables traceability from inspection event to corrective action workflow.
attributes.references: SAP QM Quality Notification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; NCR/CAPA Process

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: In MRP-driven manufacturing, inspection lots for incoming goods or production outputs are traceable to the planned order that initiated the requirement. Quality-to-planning traceability reports and MR

attributes.name: po_line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.po_line_item. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, incoming inspection lots are created at the PO line item level (each line item may have different material, quantity, and quality requirements). Line-item-level lot tracea

attributes.name: primary_inspection_material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material or part master record subject to this inspection lot. Links to the product/material master in SAP MM/PLM for BOM and specification retrieval.
attributes.references: SAP MM Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

attributes.name: procurement_goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Reference to the goods receipt document (SAP material document) that triggered this incoming inspection lot. Applicable for inspection types 01 (GR from PO) and 04 (GR from Production Order). Enables traceability from inspection to inventory movement.
attributes.references: SAP MM Goods Receipt; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Inspection lots are created for production on specific lines; line-level quality performance reporting and SPC require knowing which production line generated the lot. Essential for line-level first-p

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufacturing/production order that triggered this inspection lot, applicable for in-process and final inspection types. Links quality execution to production scheduling in SAP PP / Siemens Opcenter MES.
attributes.references: SAP PP Production Order; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the purchase order associated with this inspection lot, applicable for incoming goods receipt inspections from suppliers. Links to SAP MM purchasing documents.
attributes.references: SAP MM Purchase Order; SAP Ariba; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_rma. Business justification: Returned goods under an RMA require a dedicated inspection lot in industrial manufacturing (incoming inspection of returned material). The inspection_lot table carries a denormalized rma_number plain

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Inspection lots are created per production run in process and discrete manufacturing. Run-level quality disposition and traceability require direct linkage. Enables run-level quality yield reporting a

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: For serialized industrial products (drives, PLCs, switchgear), inspection lots are raised against individual serial numbers. Serial-level inspection traceability is required for warranty, field servic

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Inspection lot must be linked to the physical storage location for traceability in the warehouse inspection process, required by ISO 9001 audit.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier or vendor master record from whom the material was received, applicable for incoming inspection lots. Enables supplier quality performance tracking and audit.
attributes.references: SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; PPAP

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Incoming inspection lots in industrial manufacturing originate from a specific supplier site. Site-level quality performance tracking (PPM by site, AQL pass rates by site) is a standard SQE activity a

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the work center or production cell where the in-process inspection is performed. Applicable for in-process and milestone inspection types. Links to SAP PP work center master.
attributes.references: SAP PP Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

attributes.name: certificate_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the quality certificate or Certificate of Conformance (CoC) issued for this inspection lot upon acceptance. Supports customer documentation requirements and regulatory compliance traceability.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; CE Marking; UL Certification

attributes.name: certificate_of_conformance_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Certificate of Conformance (CoC) or quality certificate must be generated and issued upon acceptance of this inspection lot. Driven by customer contract requirements or regulatory mandates.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; CE Marking; UL Certification; Customer SLA

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp recording when this inspection lot record was first persisted in the data platform (Silver layer). Used for data lineage and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST SP 800-53 AU-3

attributes.name: defect_count
attributes.description: Total number of defects or non-conformances detected across all inspection characteristics within this lot. Used for defect rate calculation, SPC analysis, and NCR triggering thresholds.
attributes.references: SAP QM Defect Recording; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SPC

attributes.name: disposition_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the quality engineer or authorized personnel who recorded the usage decision and disposition for this inspection lot. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.
attributes.references: SAP QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3 (Roles and Responsibilities)

attributes.name: disposition_code
attributes.description: SAP QM usage decision code (e.g., A1=Accept, R1=Reject, R2=Rework, S1=Scrap) providing a standardized coded classification of the disposition decision for system processing and reporting.
attributes.references: SAP QM Usage Decision Code; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: disposition_decision
attributes.description: Final disposition decision made upon completion of inspection results evaluation. Determines the fate of the inspected material: 'accept' = material released to stock/use; 'reject' = material blocked/returned; 'rework' = material sent for corrective rework; 'scrap' = material scrapped; 'conditional_release' = accepted with documented deviations/concessions.
attributes.references: SAP QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; PPAP

attributes.name: disposition_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the usage decision (disposition) was formally recorded in the quality management system. Marks the closure of the active inspection phase and triggers downstream stock posting or NCR creation.
attributes.references: SAP QM Usage Decision; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: dynamic_modification_rule
attributes.description: Code or name of the dynamic modification rule applied to automatically adjust the inspection level (normal/tightened/reduced/skip) based on cumulative quality history for this material-supplier or material-plant combination. Aligns with SAP QM dynamic modification.
attributes.references: SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: inspection_end_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when all inspection activities and results recording were completed for this lot. Combined with inspection_start_timestamp to compute inspection cycle time (Lead Time metric).
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: inspection_level
attributes.description: Current inspection severity level applied to this lot based on the supplier or process quality history. 'normal' = standard AQL sampling; 'tightened' = increased scrutiny due to recent failures; 'reduced' = relaxed sampling due to consistent quality; 'skip' = lot accepted without inspection based on skip-lot qualification.
attributes.references: SAP QM Dynamic Modification; ISO 2859-1 Switching Rules; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: inspection_method
attributes.description: Primary inspection method applied to this lot: 'visual' = visual examination; 'dimensional' = measurement against dimensional tolerances; 'functional' = operational/performance testing; 'destructive' = destructive testing (sample consumed); 'non_destructive' = NDT methods (ultrasonic, X-ray, etc.); 'chemical' = chemical composition analysis; 'electrical' = electrical parameter testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual|dimensional|functional|destructive|non_destructive|chemical|electrical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Method; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IEC 61131

attributes.name: inspection_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when physical inspection activities commenced for this lot. Used to calculate inspection cycle time and monitor SLA compliance for quality turnaround.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: inspection_type_code
attributes.description: SAP QM inspection type code classifying the origin and nature of the inspection lot. Standard SAP codes: 01=Goods Receipt from Purchase Order (incoming), 04=Goods Receipt from Production Order (final), 05=Goods Issue, 06=Delivery to Customer, 08=In-Process Inspection, 10=Recurring Inspection, 89=RMA/Customer Return Receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|04|05|06|08|10|89 — promote to reference product for full SAP inspection type catalogue]
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: inspection_type_description
attributes.description: Business-readable classification of the inspection event type: 'incoming' = supplier goods receipt; 'in_process' = mid-production milestone check; 'final' = post-production completion check; 'recurring' = periodic scheduled inspection; 'rma_receipt' = customer return receipt inspection; 'delivery' = pre-shipment inspection; 'goods_issue' = outbound goods inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incoming|in_process|final|recurring|rma_receipt|delivery|goods_issue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
