attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; PPAP

attributes.name: lot_number
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the inspection lot by the quality management system (e.g., SAP QM lot number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with shop floor, suppliers, and auditors.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: lot_origin_timestamp
attributes.description: The principal real-world business event timestamp recording when the inspection lot was triggered and created in the quality management system. Distinct from ETL audit timestamps. Aligns with SAP QM lot creation date/time.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: lot_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity of material or units submitted for inspection in this lot. Represents the population from which samples are drawn. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot Quantity; ISO 2859-1 Sampling Procedures; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: lot_quantity_uom
attributes.business_glossary_term: Inspection Lot Quantity Unit of Measure (UoM)
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the inspection lot quantity (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, M=Meter, L=Liter, M2=Square Meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure from material master.
attributes.references: SAP MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

attributes.name: lot_remarks
attributes.description: Free-text field for quality engineers to record observations, special conditions, deviations, or contextual notes relevant to this inspection lot that are not captured in structured fields. Supports audit documentation.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

attributes.name: lot_status
attributes.description: Current workflow state of the inspection lot within the quality execution lifecycle. Drives downstream actions: 'created' = lot opened awaiting release; 'released' = inspection authorized to begin; 'results_recorded' = all characteristic results entered; 'usage_decided' = disposition decision made; 'closed' = lot archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|results_recorded|usage_decided|closed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: ncr_triggered
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) was automatically or manually triggered as a result of this inspection lot's reject disposition or detected non-conformances. True = NCR created; False = no NCR required.
attributes.references: SAP QM Defect Notification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; NCR Process

attributes.name: nonconforming_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of units or material within the lot that failed to meet specification requirements. Expressed in the same unit of measure as lot_quantity. Used to calculate reject rate and determine disposition scope.
attributes.references: SAP QM Defect Recording; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; NCR

attributes.name: overall_result
attributes.description: Summarized quality outcome of the inspection lot based on all characteristic results evaluated against acceptance criteria. 'passed' = all characteristics within specification; 'failed' = one or more characteristics out of specification; 'conditionally_passed' = passed with documented deviations; 'pending' = results not yet fully recorded.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot Result; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; SPC

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the inspection lot was created and executed. Supports multi-plant quality reporting and compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

attributes.name: required_end_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the inspection lot must be completed and a usage decision recorded, as defined by the inspection plan or production schedule. Supports SLA monitoring and escalation management.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: revision_level
attributes.description: Engineering revision or drawing revision level of the material or part at the time of inspection. Ensures inspection results are traceable to the correct design revision, supporting Engineering Change Order (ECO) and ECN management.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP QM; ECO/ECN Process; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

attributes.name: sample_drawing_procedure
attributes.description: Code or name of the sampling procedure applied to determine the sample size for this inspection lot (e.g., AQL 1.0, AQL 2.5, 100% inspection, skip-lot). Derived from the inspection plan sampling scheme.
attributes.references: SAP QM Sampling Procedure; ISO 2859-1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: sample_size
attributes.description: Actual number of units or quantity drawn from the lot for physical inspection, as determined by the sampling procedure in the inspection plan. May differ from the planned sample size if adjustments were made.
attributes.references: SAP QM Sample Management; ISO 2859-1 Sampling Procedures; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp recording when this inspection lot record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change detection and incremental processing.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST SP 800-53 AU-3

products.name: inspection_result
products.description: Detailed measurement and characteristic result records captured during inspection lot execution. Stores individual measured values, attribute results (pass/fail), SPC data points, Cp/Cpk indices, and defect codes per inspection characteristic. Feeds SPC charts and process capability reporting.

attributes.name: inspection_result_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each inspection characteristic result record within an inspection lot. Primary key for the inspection_result data product in the quality domain.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot Result; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: calibration_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.calibration_record. Business justification: ISO 9001/IATF 16949 require measurement results to be traceable to the calibrated instrument used. Linking inspection_result to the specific calibration_record used during measurement is a mandatory m

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the measurement equipment or gauge used to capture the inspection result. Supports gauge R&R studies and calibration traceability per MSA requirements.
attributes.references: SAP PM Equipment (EQUI); Maximo Asset Management; ISO/IEC 17025; IATF 16949 MSA

attributes.name: inspection_characteristic_id
attributes.description: Reference to the master inspection characteristic definition (e.g., dimension, hardness, visual attribute) as configured in the inspection plan. Enables aggregation of results across lots for SPC analysis.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Characteristic (QPCD); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: inspection_lot_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent inspection lot under which this characteristic result was recorded. Links the result to its originating inspection event in SAP QM.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Lot (QALS); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: inspection_plan_id
attributes.description: Reference to the inspection plan (control plan) that defines the characteristic being measured. Ties the result back to the approved quality plan and sampling procedure.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Plan (QPAM); APQP Control Plan; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Inspection results must be traceable to the specific lot/batch for PPAP documentation, SPC analysis by batch, and customer-specific traceability requirements (IATF 16949). batch_number on inspection_r

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material or product being inspected. Enables quality performance analysis by SKU, part number, or finished good across production runs.
attributes.references: SAP MM Material Master (MARA); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: ncr_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: inspection_result currently carries ncr_number as a plain STRING reference to the NCR triggered by an out-of-spec or out-of-control measurement. This is a denormalized text reference that should be re

attributes.name: order_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Required for linking each inspection result to its specific order line, enabling quality reports and order fulfillment decisions.

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: PPAP submissions require inspection results traceable to the specific product revision inspected. revision_level is a denormalized plain attribute on inspection_result; replacing it with a proper FK t

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufacturing production order associated with the inspected batch or lot. Enables traceability from quality result back to the production execution event.
attributes.references: SAP PP Production Order (AUFK); Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for Supplier Performance Report linking inspection results to the purchase order that supplied the material, enabling root‑cause analysis per supplier.

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Run‑level quality reports aggregate inspection results per production run for OEE and compliance dashboards.

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: End-of-line test results for serialized automation equipment must link to the specific serialized unit for warranty claims, field failure analysis, and customer-specific serial traceability. serial_nu

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded for each inspected product SKU; linking allows quality dashboards to aggregate results by SKU.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufacturing work center or production cell where the inspection was performed. Supports OEE and quality-by-location analysis.
attributes.references: SAP PP Work Center (CRHD); Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: attribute_result
attributes.description: Pass/fail judgment for attribute-type inspection characteristics (e.g., visual defect present/absent, functional test pass/fail). Null for variable characteristics where measured_value is populated.
attributes.references: SAP QM Attribute Result; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: cp_index
attributes.description: Process capability index Cp measuring the ratio of the specification width to the process spread (6-sigma). Indicates whether the process is capable of meeting specification limits regardless of centering. Cp ≥ 1.33 is typically required per PPAP.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: cpk_index
attributes.description: Process capability index Cpk measuring the ratio of the nearest specification limit to the process mean, accounting for process centering. Cpk ≥ 1.33 is typically required for PPAP approval. Key metric for supplier quality and process performance reporting.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; PPAP 4th Edition

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this inspection result record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking per lakehouse governance requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: defect_code
attributes.description: Standardized defect classification code identifying the type of nonconformance detected (e.g., DIM-OOT for out-of-tolerance dimension, SURF-SCR for surface scratch). Feeds Pareto analysis and CAPA root cause categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for defect code catalog]
attributes.references: SAP QM Defect Code (Q-Notification); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; PFMEA defect taxonomy

attributes.name: defect_count
attributes.description: Number of defects or nonconformances detected for this characteristic within the inspected sample. Used for defect rate calculation, Pareto analysis, and NCR triggering thresholds.
attributes.references: SAP QM Defect Recording (QF01); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: defect_description
attributes.description: Free-text description of the nonconformance or defect observed during inspection. Provides context beyond the defect code for root cause analysis and CAPA documentation.
attributes.references: SAP QM Q-Notification Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; CAPA process

attributes.name: inspection_date
attributes.description: Calendar date on which the inspection was performed. Used for daily/weekly quality trend reporting and shift-level aggregation without requiring timestamp precision.

attributes.name: inspection_method
attributes.description: Method used to perform the inspection: 'manual' (operator measurement), 'automated' (CMM or inline sensor), 'semi_automated', 'destructive' (material consumed in test), or 'non_destructive' (NDT methods such as ultrasonic, X-ray). Affects result reliability and sampling plan selection.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Method; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ISO/IEC 17025; ASTM NDT Standards

attributes.name: inspection_stage
attributes.description: Stage in the production or supply chain process at which the inspection was performed: 'incoming' (goods receipt), 'in_process' (during manufacturing), 'final' (finished goods), 'outgoing' (pre-shipment), or 'supplier' (at supplier site).
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 and 8.6; PPAP

attributes.name: inspection_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the inspection measurement was physically recorded. The principal business event timestamp for this result. Used for SPC time-series analysis, shift-based quality reporting, and audit trail.
attributes.references: SAP QM Results Recording Timestamp; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: is_out_of_control
attributes.description: Indicates whether the measurement triggered an SPC out-of-control signal (e.g., point beyond control limits, run rule violation per Western Electric rules). Distinct from out-of-spec: a process can be in-spec but out-of-control. Triggers SPC investigation workflow.
attributes.references: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: is_out_of_spec
attributes.description: Indicates whether the measured value falls outside the engineering specification limits (USL or LSL). True when measured_value > upper_spec_limit or measured_value < lower_spec_limit. Used for rapid filtering of nonconforming results and NCR triggering.

attributes.name: lower_control_limit
attributes.description: The lower statistical process control limit for the characteristic, typically set at ±3 sigma from the process mean. Used in SPC X-bar and R charts to detect process shifts.
attributes.references: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2 Control Charts; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: lower_spec_limit
attributes.description: The lower engineering specification limit for the characteristic. Measurements below this value are out-of-specification. Used in Cp/Cpk calculation and SPC control chart setup.
attributes.references: SAP QM LSL; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ASME Y14.5

attributes.name: measured_value
attributes.description: The actual numeric measurement recorded for a variable inspection characteristic (e.g., 24.987 mm for a diameter). Null for attribute-type characteristics. Core data point for SPC charting and Cp/Cpk calculation.
attributes.references: SAP QM Results Recording (QE01); SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: nominal_value
attributes.description: The engineering design target or nominal value for the characteristic as specified in the drawing or specification. Used to calculate deviation from target and for SPC centering analysis.
attributes.references: SAP QM Inspection Plan Nominal; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; GD&T ASME Y14.5

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the inspection was performed. Enables multi-plant quality benchmarking and regulatory reporting by site.

attributes.name: remarks
attributes.description: Free-text remarks or observations entered by the inspector at the time of measurement. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields, such as environmental conditions, equipment anomalies, or visual observations.
attributes.references: SAP QM Long Text; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: result_status
attributes.description: Current disposition status of the inspection characteristic result: 'accepted' (within specification), 'rejected' (out of specification), 'conditional' (requires further review or deviation approval), 'open' (not yet evaluated), or 'cancelled'.
attributes.references: SAP QM Usage Decision (UD); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: sample_size
attributes.description: Number of units or specimens included in the inspection sample for this characteristic result. Determined by the sampling plan (e.g., AQL per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 or Z1.9). Required for statistical validity assessment.
attributes.references: SAP QM Sample Size; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9; ISO 2859-1

attributes.name: sampling_procedure
attributes.description: Sampling procedure or plan applied for this inspection characteristic (e.g., AQL 1.0 Level II, 100% inspection, skip-lot). Determines the statistical confidence of the inspection result.
attributes.references: SAP QM Sampling Procedure; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ISO 2859-1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: shift_code
attributes.description: Production shift during which the inspection was performed (e.g., day, afternoon, night). Enables shift-based quality performance analysis to identify systematic variation by shift.
attributes.references: SAP PP Shift; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: spc_chart_type
attributes.description: Type of SPC control chart applied to this characteristic: 'xbar_r' (X-bar and R for subgroups), 'i_mr' (Individuals and Moving Range), 'p_chart' (proportion defective), 'np_chart' (number defective), 'c_chart' (count of defects), 'u_chart' (defects per unit). Determines the statistical method for control limit calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: xbar_r|xbar_s|i_mr|p_chart|np_chart|c_chart|u_chart — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2; ISO 7870-3; ISO 7870-4

attributes.name: subgroup_number
attributes.description: Sequential subgroup number within the SPC control chart for this characteristic. Used to order data points on the SPC chart and identify the rational subgroup to which this measurement belongs.
attributes.references: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Engineering unit of the measured value (e.g., mm, kg, MPa, Ra, °C, %). Follows ISO 80000 and SAP UoM conventions. Required for dimensional analysis and SPC chart labeling.
attributes.references: SAP QM UoM; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inspection result record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: upper_control_limit
attributes.description: The upper statistical process control limit for the characteristic, typically set at ±3 sigma from the process mean. Triggers investigation when exceeded even if within specification. Used in SPC X-bar and R charts.
attributes.references: SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 7870-2 Control Charts; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: upper_spec_limit
attributes.description: The upper engineering specification limit for the characteristic. Measurements above this value are out-of-specification. Used in Cp/Cpk calculation and SPC control chart setup.
attributes.references: SAP QM USL; SPC Reference Manual (AIAG); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ASME Y14.5

attributes.name: usage_decision_code
attributes.description: SAP QM usage decision code indicating the final disposition of the inspected material based on this and other characteristic results: 'accept' (release to stock), 'reject' (return or scrap), 'rework' (send for rework), 'scrap', or 'conditional_release' (deviation approval). Drives inventory posting in SAP MM.
attributes.references: SAP QM Usage Decision (QA11); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6 and 8.7

products.name: ncr
products.description: Non-Conformance Report (NCR) serving as the single, unified quality event record for ALL deviations from specifications, standards, or requirements regardless of detection source or reporting channel. Encompasses internal defects, customer complaints (including 8D problem-solving), supplier non-conformances, field returns/RMA dispositions, and audit-triggered findings. Captures non-conformance type (internal/customer/supplier/field-return/audit), affected material/product/serial number, detection source (incoming inspection, in-process, final inspection, field/customer, audit, supplier delivery), severity classification, containment actions, root cause analysis, disposition decision (use-as-is, rework, scrap, return-to-supplier, repair, replace, credit, no-fault-found), RMA tracking fields (return authorization number, customer reference, repair actions, return shipment status), SAP QM notification type mapping (Q1/Q2/Q3), and linkage to CAPA for corrective/preventive action. Supports 8D methodology for customer-facing issues. Aligned with ISO 9001 clause 8.7 (control of nonconforming outputs), clause 10.2 (corrective action), and integrates with Salesforce Service Cloud for customer-originated events.

attributes.name: ncr_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) record. Primary key for the quality event.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 — Control of Nonconforming Outputs

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer account associated with the NCR, applicable for customer complaint and field return NCR types. Links to Salesforce CRM customer account for 8D reporting and customer communication.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2 — Customer Satisfaction

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: NCRs triggered by customer-reported nonconformances are tied to the specific customer site where nonconforming product is installed or received. Site-level NCR tracking drives field corrective action

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Non‑conformance remediation costs are charged to a cost center; required for cost of poor quality analysis.

attributes.name: delivery_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: NCRs in industrial manufacturing are frequently raised against a specific delivery (damaged goods on arrival, wrong items shipped). Linking ncr to delivery enables direct traceability from nonconforma

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: ECNs are issued in response to quality NCRs when design changes are required. Quality teams reference the ECN that resolved the nonconformance for regulatory audit traceability and PPAP documentation

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: NCRs in industrial manufacturing frequently trigger or reference an ECO to address root causes at the design level. Linking NCR to ECO enables closed-loop quality-to-engineering change traceability re

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: NCRs reference the engineering revision of the nonconforming part, enabling revision-level defect rate tracking and supporting disposition decisions (use-as-is vs. rework). This is standard practice i

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: NCRs document which engineering specification was violated. Linking ncr to engineering_specification enables specification-level nonconformance analysis and supports regulatory compliance reporting —

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Allows NCR investigations to identify the exact equipment causing non‑conformance, required for root‑cause analysis reports.

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the non-conformance was detected or originated. Aligns with SAP plant organizational unit.

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: NCRs in industrial manufacturing are raised against specific lots/batches for quarantine, disposition, and regulatory traceability (ISO 9001, IATF 16949). Lot-level NCR tracking drives containment sco

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the material, component, or finished product involved in the non-conformance. Maps to SAP MM material master.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: material_requirement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: When an NCR results in batch rejection, the material requirement that drove the procurement must be flagged so MRP can re-plan the net requirement. Industrial manufacturing MRP exception management de

attributes.name: order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: NCRs in industrial manufacturing are raised against specific customer orders. Linking NCR to order_intake enables RMA processing, customer notification, disposition tracking, and order-level nonconfor

attributes.name: po_line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.po_line_item. Business justification: NCRs on incoming material in industrial manufacturing must reference the specific PO line item for financial charge-back, return quantity calculation, and open-quantity adjustment. purchase_order_id a

attributes.name: procurement_goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: NCRs on incoming material must reference the specific goods receipt for RMA processing, supplier charge-back, and inventory reversal. In industrial manufacturing, GR-to-NCR traceability is a core inco

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: NCRs are associated with the production line where nonconformance occurred for line-level quality performance tracking and Pareto analysis. Line-level NCR reporting is a standard KPI in industrial man

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: NCRs are raised against specific work orders for nonconforming production output. Direct FK enables work-order-level nonconformance reporting, cost of quality analysis, and scrap/rework traceability —

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Needed for NCR Traceability Report to associate non‑conformances with the originating purchase order for supplier corrective actions.

attributes.name: quality_audit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: A quality audit (internal, supplier, or process audit) frequently generates NCRs as findings. quality_audit tracks major_ncr_count and minor_ncr_count as aggregate counts, but there is no FK from indi

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: NCRs against serialized industrial units (defective servo drives, circuit breakers) must reference the specific serialized_unit to support containment, RMA processing, and field recall management. ser

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Non‑conformance reports are issued for specific product SKUs; FK supports NCR trend analysis per SKU.

attributes.name: sourcing_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_rule. Business justification: Supplier NCRs directly trigger sourcing rule reviews and potential supplier suspension in industrial manufacturing. Quality teams and supply planners jointly review the active sourcing rule when an NC

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: NCR records the location of non‑conforming material in the warehouse for quarantine and retrieval, essential for corrective action tracking.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the supplier associated with the non-conformance, applicable for supplier-type NCRs (incoming material failures) or return-to-supplier dispositions. Maps to SAP Ariba/MM vendor master.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master; SAP Ariba Supplier Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: NCRs on incoming material in industrial manufacturing must be traceable to the specific supplier site that produced the nonconforming material. Site-level NCR metrics drive supplier development decisi

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the specific work center, production cell, or shop floor station where the non-conformance was detected during manufacturing execution. Sourced from SAP PP/MES.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP — Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: actual_closure_date
attributes.description: Actual date on which the NCR was formally closed after verification of all corrective actions and disposition completion. Used for cycle time KPI calculation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Notification Completion Date

attributes.name: containment_action
attributes.description: Description of immediate containment actions taken to prevent further non-conforming product from reaching the customer or next process step (e.g., quarantine, 100% inspection, production hold). Corresponds to 8D Step D3.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; 8D Problem Solving Methodology D3

attributes.name: containment_completed_date
attributes.description: Date by which all immediate containment actions were verified as complete. Used for 8D D3 closure tracking and customer reporting.
attributes.references: 8D Problem Solving Methodology D3; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when the NCR record was first created in the data platform (Silver layer ingestion). Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: customer_complaint_number
attributes.description: Customer-provided reference number for the complaint or quality issue, as received from the customer or logged in Salesforce Service Cloud. Enables cross-reference with customer records.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2

attributes.name: customer_notification_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer must be formally notified of this non-conformance (e.g., for shipped non-conforming product, field safety issues, or contractual quality notification requirements).

attributes.name: defect_code
attributes.description: Standardized defect classification code from the quality defect catalog (e.g., dimensional deviation, surface defect, functional failure). Maps to SAP QM defect code catalog. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full defect code catalog]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA QM — Defect Code Catalog; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: defect_location
attributes.description: Physical location or feature on the part/product where the defect was found (e.g., weld seam, connector pin 3, housing surface). Supports targeted rework and process improvement.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA QM — Defect Location Catalog; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: detection_source
attributes.description: The process stage or channel where the non-conformance was first detected: incoming_inspection (receiving), in_process (shop floor/WIP), final_inspection (end-of-line), field_customer (reported by customer or field), audit (internal/external audit), supplier_delivery (at point of supplier delivery).
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; Siemens Opcenter MES Inspection

attributes.name: detection_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the non-conformance was first detected or observed. This is the principal business event timestamp for the NCR lifecycle.

attributes.name: disposition
attributes.description: Formal disposition decision for the non-conforming material: use_as_is (accept with deviation), rework (correct in-house), scrap (destroy), return_to_supplier, repair, replace (issue replacement), credit (issue financial credit), no_fault_found. Drives material handling and financial impact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: use_as_is|rework|scrap|return_to_supplier|repair|replace|credit|no_fault_found — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7.1; SAP QM Usage Decision

attributes.name: disposition_authority
attributes.description: Name or role of the authorized person or body (e.g., Material Review Board, Quality Manager, Engineering) who approved the disposition decision. Required for regulatory and customer-specific quality requirements.

attributes.name: disposition_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the formal disposition decision was made and approved. Used for cycle time measurement and regulatory compliance documentation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7.1; SAP QM Usage Decision Date

attributes.name: eight_d_report_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the formal 8D problem-solving report associated with this NCR. Applicable when is_8d_required is true. Enables cross-reference to the 8D documentation submitted to the customer.
attributes.references: 8D Problem Solving Methodology; Customer-Specific Quality Requirements (IATF 16949)

attributes.name: is_8d_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the 8D (Eight Disciplines) structured problem-solving methodology is required for this NCR, typically mandated for customer-facing complaints or high-severity issues.
attributes.references: 8D Problem Solving Methodology; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Customer-Specific Quality Requirements

attributes.name: material_description
attributes.description: Short description of the affected material or product as defined in the material master. Provides human-readable context without requiring a join to the material master.

attributes.name: ncr_number
attributes.description: Human-readable, externally-visible NCR reference number used in communications with suppliers, customers, and auditors. Format: NCR-YYYY-NNNNNN. Maps to SAP QM notification number.

attributes.name: ncr_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the NCR workflow: draft (being authored), open (submitted and active), under_review (root cause analysis in progress), disposition_pending (awaiting disposition decision), closed (all actions complete), cancelled (voided).
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Notification Status

attributes.name: ncr_type
attributes.description: Classification of the NCR by originating channel: internal (detected in-house), customer (complaint from customer), supplier (incoming material non-conformance), field_return (RMA/field failure), or audit (finding from internal or external audit).
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Notification Types Q1/Q2/Q3

attributes.name: nonconformance_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the non-conformance, including the observed defect, deviation from specification, or failure mode. Supports root cause analysis and CAPA.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Notification Long Text

attributes.name: nonconforming_qty
attributes.description: Quantity of units, parts, or material identified as non-conforming. Used for scrap cost calculation, yield rate analysis, and containment scope.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Defect Quantity

attributes.name: qty_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the non-conforming quantity (e.g., EA=each, KG=kilogram, M=meter, PC=piece). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|LB|M|M2|M3|L|PC|SET|BOX — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1

attributes.name: regulatory_reportable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this non-conformance must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., OSHA, EPA, CE/UL product safety authority). Triggers mandatory regulatory notification workflows.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; EPA Regulations; CE Marking Directive; UL Product Safety

attributes.name: reported_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the NCR was formally submitted and entered into the quality management system. May differ from detection_timestamp if there is a reporting lag.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Notification Creation Date

attributes.name: return_shipment_status
attributes.description: Current status of the physical return shipment for RMA-linked NCRs: not_applicable (no physical return), pending (RMA issued, awaiting shipment), in_transit (goods in transit), received (goods received at facility), inspected (incoming inspection complete), closed (return process complete).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD — Returns; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: root_cause_category
attributes.description: Standardized category of the identified root cause for trend analysis and SPC reporting: design, process, material, equipment, human_error, measurement, supplier, or environment. Enables Pareto analysis of quality failures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design|process|material|equipment|human_error|measurement|supplier|environment — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Ishikawa Cause Categories; SPC

attributes.name: root_cause_description
attributes.description: Narrative description of the identified root cause of the non-conformance, derived from root cause analysis methods (e.g., 5-Why, Ishikawa/fishbone, FMEA). Corresponds to 8D Step D4.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; 8D Problem Solving D4; PFMEA

attributes.name: sap_qm_notification_type
attributes.description: SAP QM notification type code mapped to this NCR: Q1 (customer complaint), Q2 (internal quality notification), Q3 (supplier quality notification). Enables direct traceability to the SAP QM source record.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA QM Module — Notification Types

attributes.name: severity
attributes.description: Severity level of the non-conformance based on impact to safety, quality, regulatory compliance, or customer satisfaction: critical (safety/regulatory risk), major (significant quality impact), minor (limited impact), observation (potential risk noted).
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 Inspection Levels

attributes.name: target_closure_date
attributes.description: Planned date by which the NCR is expected to be fully closed, including all corrective actions verified. Used for SLA tracking and escalation management.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to the NCR record. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.

products.name: capa
products.description: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record managing the full lifecycle of quality improvement actions triggered by NCRs, audit findings, customer complaints, or internal quality events. Tracks root cause analysis (5-Why, Ishikawa), corrective action plan, preventive action plan, responsible owner, target dates, verification of effectiveness, and closure status per ISO 9001 clause 10.2.
products.reference: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) / ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: capa_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record. Primary key for the capa data product in the quality domain.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the customer associated with this CAPA when triggered by a customer complaint, field failure, or warranty claim. Links to the customer account for customer-facing quality reporting and Salesforce CRM integration.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: CAPA actions are often triggered by defects in a particular component; linking enables root‑cause tracking and corrective action reporting.

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Sales contracts in industrial manufacturing define warranty obligations, SLA terms, and quality requirements that mandate CAPAs. Linking CAPA to sales_contract enables contract-level corrective action

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Corrective action implementation costs are allocated to a cost center; finance tracks CAPA expenses for effectiveness evaluation.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: CAPAs requiring design changes reference the ECO that implements the corrective action. The existing eco_number plain-text field is denormalized; replacing with eco_id FK enables closed-loop CAPA-to-E

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: CAPAs triggered by lot-level quality failures (contaminated raw material batch, out-of-spec production lot) must reference the specific lot_batch to define containment scope and verify effectiveness.

attributes.name: ncr_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) that triggered this CAPA. Establishes the traceability link between the nonconformity detection record and the corrective action response.

attributes.name: order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: CAPAs in industrial manufacturing are triggered by nonconformances on specific customer orders. Linking CAPA to order_intake enables customer notification tracking, warranty claim processing, and orde

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Ensures CAPA actions are tied to the specific procurement contract governing the supplier, required for contract compliance audits.

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: CAPAs with ppap_impact_flag=true must reference the product revision to assess whether corrective actions require a formal revision change. PPAP re-submission decisions depend on knowing which revisio

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: CAPAs initiated from production nonconformances reference the originating work order for traceability and effectiveness verification against production quality metrics. Required for IATF 16949 CAPA pr

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: CAPAs arising from new product development quality issues are linked to the engineering project for APQP phase tracking and project-level quality metrics. This link enables program managers to track o

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: CAPAs involving process changes reference the affected routing for change control and re-validation. APQP/PPAP process change notifications require routing reference to assess impact on process steps

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: CAPA actions address root causes of defects on a particular product SKU; linking provides product‑centric corrective action tracking.

attributes.name: sourcing_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_rule. Business justification: CAPAs triggered by supplier quality failures frequently result in sourcing rule changes — switching preferred suppliers, adjusting allocation percentages, or suspending a rule. Industrial manufacturin

attributes.name: supplier_audit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_audit. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, CAPAs are routinely initiated from supplier audit findings (major/critical nonconformances). IATF 16949 requires traceability from audit finding to corrective action. sour

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the supplier associated with this CAPA when the root cause is attributed to a supplied material, component, or service. Links to the supplier master for supplier quality performance tracking via SAP Ariba.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Supplier-directed CAPAs in industrial manufacturing are site-specific — a multi-site supplier may have one site with a process failure. Site-level CAPA tracking is essential for supplier development p

attributes.name: action_implementation_date
attributes.description: The date on which the corrective and/or preventive actions were fully implemented in the production process or quality system. Marks the transition from planning to execution phase.

attributes.name: actual_closure_date
attributes.description: The actual calendar date on which the CAPA was formally closed after successful verification of effectiveness. Null if the CAPA is still open. Used to calculate actual cycle time versus target.

attributes.name: affected_process_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the manufacturing process, work center, or production routing step where the nonconformity was detected or originated. Sourced from SAP PP routing or Siemens Opcenter MES process definition.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; SAP PP; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which the CAPA was formally approved for closure by the authorized quality approver. Represents the final lifecycle milestone before the record is archived.

attributes.name: capa_number
attributes.description: Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the CAPA record, typically formatted as CAPA-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in communications, audit trails, and regulatory submissions. Sourced from SAP QM notification numbering.

attributes.name: capa_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the CAPA record. Tracks progression from initial creation through root cause analysis, action implementation, effectiveness verification, and formal closure per ISO 9001 Clause 10.2.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Notification Status

attributes.name: capa_type
attributes.description: Classifies whether the action is corrective (addressing an existing nonconformity), preventive (eliminating a potential nonconformity), or both. Drives workflow routing and reporting in SAP QM.

attributes.name: containment_completion_date
attributes.description: The date on which all immediate containment actions were completed and verified. Used to measure response speed and compliance with customer-mandated containment timelines.

attributes.name: corrective_action_plan
attributes.description: Detailed description of the permanent corrective actions planned to eliminate the root cause of the nonconformity and prevent recurrence. Includes process changes, design modifications, procedure updates, or training interventions.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp recording when the CAPA record was first created in the quality management system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: customer_notification_date
attributes.description: The date on which the customer was formally notified of the nonconformity and the corrective action plan. Null if customer notification is not required or has not yet occurred.

attributes.name: customer_notification_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer must be formally notified of the nonconformity and the corrective action taken. Driven by customer contract requirements, product safety implications, or regulatory obligations.

attributes.name: department_code
attributes.description: Organizational department or cost center responsible for the process or area where the nonconformity originated. Used for departmental quality KPI reporting and accountability tracking.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 5.3; SAP CO Cost Center

attributes.name: effectiveness_verification_date
attributes.description: The date on which the effectiveness of the implemented actions was formally verified and documented. Required for CAPA closure per ISO 9001 Clause 10.2.

attributes.name: effectiveness_verification_method
attributes.description: Description of the method used to verify that the implemented corrective and preventive actions were effective in eliminating the root cause and preventing recurrence. May include SPC monitoring, re-audit, production run data review, or customer feedback analysis.

attributes.name: effectiveness_verified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the corrective and preventive actions have been formally verified as effective. True when verification is complete and documented; False when verification is pending or failed. Required gate for CAPA closure.

attributes.name: immediate_containment_action
attributes.description: Description of the short-term containment actions taken immediately upon detection of the nonconformity to prevent further defective product from reaching the customer or next process step. Corresponds to D3 in the 8D methodology.

attributes.name: initiated_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the CAPA was formally opened and initiated. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the CAPA lifecycle. Used to calculate cycle time and compliance with response time SLAs.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the CAPA record. Used for change detection in incremental data pipeline loads and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: lessons_learned
attributes.description: Summary of key lessons learned from the CAPA investigation and resolution that should be shared across the organization to prevent similar issues. Feeds into the knowledge management and APQP process for future product launches.

attributes.name: n8d_report_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the formal 8D (Eight Disciplines) problem-solving report associated with this CAPA, if applicable. Used when customer-mandated 8D reporting is required, particularly in automotive and industrial OEM supply chains.

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the nonconformity occurred or where the CAPA is being executed. Enables site-level quality performance reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure

attributes.name: ppap_impact_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Impact Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this CAPA requires a new or revised Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) submission due to process or design changes resulting from the corrective action. Relevant for automotive and industrial OEM supply chains.

attributes.name: preventive_action_plan
attributes.description: Description of proactive actions planned to eliminate the causes of potential nonconformities before they occur. Addresses systemic risks identified during root cause analysis that may affect similar processes or products.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to the CAPA based on risk severity, customer impact, regulatory exposure, or recurrence frequency. Drives escalation timelines and resource allocation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; FMEA Risk Priority Number (RPN)

attributes.name: problem_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative describing the nonconformity, defect, or quality event that triggered the CAPA. Includes observed symptoms, affected product or process, and initial impact assessment. Corresponds to the problem statement in the 8D or CAPA methodology.

attributes.name: quality_standard_reference
attributes.description: The specific quality standard clause, regulatory requirement, or customer specification that was violated or is being addressed by this CAPA. Examples: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.2, IATF 16949, customer-specific quality requirements.

attributes.name: recurrence_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this CAPA addresses a recurring nonconformity that has been previously reported. True if the same or similar root cause has been identified in a prior CAPA. Used for chronic problem identification and escalation.

attributes.name: regulatory_impact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this CAPA has regulatory reporting implications, such as mandatory notification to ISO certification bodies, OSHA, EPA, or CE/UL certification authorities. Triggers compliance escalation workflow when True.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015; OSHA; EPA; CE Marking; UL

attributes.name: root_cause_analysis_method
attributes.description: The structured methodology used to identify the root cause of the nonconformity. Common methods include 5-Why, Ishikawa (fishbone) diagram, 8D problem solving, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA), fault tree analysis, or Pareto analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 5_why|ishikawa|8d|fmea|fault_tree|pareto|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: root_cause_category
attributes.description: High-level categorization of the root cause using the 6M Ishikawa framework: Man (human error), Machine (equipment), Material (raw material/component), Method (process/procedure), Measurement (inspection/calibration), or Environment (facility/conditions). Enables systemic trend analysis.

attributes.name: root_cause_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative of the identified root cause(s) of the nonconformity as determined through the root cause analysis. This is the foundational finding that drives the corrective action plan.

attributes.name: source_reference_number
attributes.description: The identifier of the originating document or record that triggered this CAPA, such as an NCR number, audit finding reference, customer complaint ticket number, or SAP QM notification number. Enables traceability back to the triggering event.

attributes.name: source_type
attributes.description: Category of the triggering event that initiated the CAPA. Indicates whether the CAPA originated from a Non-Conformance Report (NCR), customer complaint, internal or external audit finding, shop floor quality event, supplier quality issue, or regulatory inspection finding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ncr|customer_complaint|audit_finding|internal_quality_event|supplier_issue|regulatory_finding|field_failure|warranty_claim — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: target_closure_date
attributes.description: The planned date by which all corrective and preventive actions must be implemented and verified as effective. Used for on-time closure KPI tracking and escalation management.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Short, descriptive title summarizing the quality problem or improvement opportunity addressed by this CAPA. Used in dashboards, reports, and management reviews.

products.name: fmea
products.description: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) master record covering both DFMEA (Design FMEA) and PFMEA (Process FMEA). Stores failure modes, effects, causes, current controls, severity/occurrence/detection ratings, Risk Priority Number (RPN), recommended actions, and revised RPN after action implementation. Managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and aligned with AIAG-VDA FMEA methodology.

attributes.name: fmea_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) ID
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the FMEA record. Primary key for the fmea data product in the quality domain.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer or OEM for whom this FMEA is being prepared. Customer-specific FMEA requirements (e.g., Ford, GM, Stellantis CSRs) may impose additional rating criteria or documentation standards.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); IATF 16949:2016 Customer-Specific Requirements

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: DFMEAs are conducted at the component level. The existing part_number and part_name plain-text fields are denormalized component references; replacing with component_id FK enables component-level risk

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: APQP mandates FMEA at the configuration level — configurable industrial products (e.g., automation systems with selectable voltage/communication options) have configuration-specific failure modes. FME

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: FMEAs must be reviewed and updated when engineering revisions change part design or process. Linking fmea to engineering_revision ensures DFMEA/PFMEA currency with design changes — a mandatory IATF 16

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: FMEA in industrial manufacturing is performed on specific equipment/machines to analyze failure modes. IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 require FMEA records traceable to the analyzed asset. Quality and mainten

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: FMEAs are conducted within engineering projects as APQP phase deliverables. Linking fmea to engineering_project enables project-level quality risk tracking and APQP gate review reporting — a standard

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufacturing process or process step being analyzed in a PFMEA. Links to the process routing or operation master in Siemens Opcenter MES or SAP S/4HANA PP. Applicable primarily for PFMEA type records.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product or component that is the subject of this FMEA analysis. Links the FMEA to the product master record managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or SAP S/4HANA MM.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier responsible for the component or sub-process being analyzed, when the FMEA covers a supplier-provided item. Links to the supplier master in SAP Ariba or SAP S/4HANA MM for supplier quality management purposes.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4; SAP Ariba Supplier Management

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Process FMEAs are conducted at the work center level to identify failure modes for specific manufacturing operations. Work-center-level FMEA is a standard IATF 16949 requirement; fmea.process_step and

attributes.name: action_priority
attributes.description: AIAG-VDA Action Priority classification (High, Medium, Low) assigned to the failure mode based on the combination of Severity, Occurrence, and Detection ratings. Replaces the legacy RPN-only prioritization approach introduced in the 2019 AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook. Core AIAG-VDA Step 7 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7 Action Priority Table

attributes.name: action_taken
attributes.description: Description of the actual corrective or preventive action implemented to address the failure mode. Documents what was done versus what was recommended, enabling traceability for PPAP and audit purposes. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 9; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949:2016

attributes.name: actual_completion_date
attributes.description: Date on which the recommended action was actually implemented and verified as complete. Compared against target_completion_date to measure action closure timeliness. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date on which the FMEA was formally reviewed and approved by the responsible engineering and quality authority. Required for PPAP submission and regulatory compliance documentation. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp recording when the FMEA record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Informatica MDM Data Governance

attributes.name: current_detection_controls
attributes.description: Description of existing design verification or process monitoring controls that detect the failure mode or cause before the product reaches the customer. Examples include inspection plans, SPC monitoring, functional testing, and CMM measurement. Core AIAG-VDA Step 7 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; IEC 60812:2018

attributes.name: current_prevention_controls
attributes.description: Description of existing design or process controls that prevent the failure cause from occurring or reduce the occurrence rate. Examples include design standards, material specifications, process parameters, and mistake-proofing (poka-yoke) devices. Core AIAG-VDA Step 6 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; IEC 60812:2018

attributes.name: detection_rating
attributes.description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the ability of current controls to detect the failure cause or failure mode before the product reaches the customer. A rating of 10 indicates no detection capability; 1 indicates near-certain detection. Core AIAG-VDA Step 7 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

attributes.name: failure_cause
attributes.description: Root cause or mechanism that leads to the failure mode. Describes the specific design weakness, process deficiency, or material condition that initiates the failure (e.g., 'insufficient weld penetration', 'incorrect torque specification'). Core AIAG-VDA Step 4 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: failure_effect
attributes.description: Description of the consequence of the failure mode on the customer, end user, or downstream process. Defines what the customer experiences when the failure occurs (e.g., 'loss of function', 'noise', 'injury risk'). Core AIAG-VDA Step 4 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.5; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: failure_mode
attributes.description: The specific manner in which the item, component, or process step could potentially fail to perform its intended function. Represents the physical or chemical description of the failure (e.g., 'fracture', 'short circuit', 'dimensional out-of-tolerance'). Core AIAG-VDA Step 4 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 4; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: fmea_number
attributes.business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Number
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable document control number assigned to the FMEA record in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Used for cross-referencing in engineering change orders (ECOs), PPAP packages, and APQP deliverables.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Numbering Standard

attributes.name: fmea_scope
attributes.description: Description of the boundaries and scope of the FMEA analysis, including what is included and excluded from the analysis. Defines the system boundary, interfaces, and assumptions. Required for AIAG-VDA Step 2 (Structure Analysis).
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 2; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.2

attributes.name: fmea_status
attributes.business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the FMEA document within the Siemens Teamcenter PLM workflow. Controls whether the FMEA is editable, under review, formally released for production use, or retired.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Workflow States

attributes.name: fmea_type
attributes.business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Type
attributes.description: Classification of the FMEA by analysis scope: DFMEA (Design FMEA) evaluates design-related failure modes; PFMEA (Process FMEA) evaluates manufacturing process failure modes; SFMEA (System FMEA) evaluates system-level interactions; MFMEA (Machinery FMEA) evaluates equipment failure modes. Aligned with AIAG-VDA methodology.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3; IEC 60812:2018

attributes.name: function_description
attributes.description: Description of the intended function or purpose of the item, component, or process step being analyzed. Defines what the element is supposed to do under normal operating conditions. Core AIAG-VDA Step 3 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 3; IEC 60812:2018 Clause 5.3

attributes.name: initiated_date
attributes.description: Date on which the FMEA analysis was formally initiated or first created. Marks the start of the FMEA lifecycle and is used for APQP timing and project milestone tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1; APQP Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent periodic review of the FMEA. FMEAs must be reviewed and updated when design or process changes occur, when field failures are reported, or on a scheduled basis per the quality management system. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3

attributes.name: occurrence_rating
attributes.description: Numeric rating (1–10) estimating the likelihood or frequency of the failure cause occurring. A rating of 10 indicates near-certain occurrence; 1 indicates extremely unlikely. Based on historical failure data, process capability (Cp/Cpk), or engineering judgment. Core AIAG-VDA Step 6 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 6; IEC 60812:2018; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or production site where the process or product being analyzed is produced. Used to scope the FMEA to a specific production location and align with site-specific process controls.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

attributes.name: process_step
attributes.description: Name or description of the specific manufacturing process step or operation being analyzed in a PFMEA (e.g., 'Welding Station 3', 'CNC Milling Op 20'). Derived from the process routing in SAP S/4HANA PP or Siemens Opcenter MES.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Opcenter MES Shop Floor Control

attributes.name: recommended_action
attributes.description: Proposed corrective or preventive action to reduce the severity, occurrence, or detection rating of the failure mode. Drives CAPA initiation and engineering change orders (ECOs). Core AIAG-VDA Step 8 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 10.2.3

attributes.name: regulatory_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the failure mode is associated with a regulatory compliance requirement (e.g., CE Marking, UL certification, EPA, OSHA). Failure modes with this flag require documented evidence of control effectiveness in regulatory submissions.
attributes.references: CE Marking Directive 2006/42/EC; UL Standards; EPA Regulations; OSHA 29 CFR; IEC 62443

attributes.name: revised_action_priority
attributes.description: Updated AIAG-VDA Action Priority (High, Medium, Low) recalculated after implementation of the recommended action. Confirms whether the residual risk level is acceptable or requires further action.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 9 Action Priority Table

attributes.name: revised_detection_rating
attributes.description: Updated detection rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects improved detection capability resulting from new controls, inspection methods, or monitoring systems. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.

attributes.name: revised_occurrence_rating
attributes.description: Updated occurrence rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Reflects the reduced likelihood of the failure cause occurring following corrective action. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.

attributes.name: revised_rpn
attributes.description: Recalculated Risk Priority Number (Revised S × Revised O × Revised D) after implementation of the recommended action. Used to verify that the corrective action achieved the desired risk reduction. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 9; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: revised_severity_rating
attributes.description: Updated severity rating (1–10) after implementation of the recommended action. Severity typically does not change unless a design change eliminates the failure effect. Core AIAG-VDA Step 9 field for post-action re-evaluation.

attributes.name: revision
attributes.description: Document revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02) tracking the version history of the FMEA in Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Incremented upon each approved change to the FMEA content.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Version Control

attributes.name: rpn
attributes.description: Risk Priority Number calculated as the product of Severity × Occurrence × Detection ratings (S × O × D). Ranges from 1 to 1000. Used to prioritize failure modes for corrective action. Higher RPN indicates greater risk requiring immediate attention. Stored as a business-critical field per AIAG-VDA methodology.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 7; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.3

attributes.name: safety_critical_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the failure mode has a safety-critical or regulatory compliance impact (severity rating 9 or 10). Safety-critical failure modes require mandatory escalation, special controls, and PPAP documentation. Aligned with OSHA and CE Marking requirements.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); OSHA 29 CFR 1910; CE Marking Directive; ISO 45001:2018

attributes.name: severity_rating
attributes.description: Numeric rating (1–10) assessing the seriousness of the failure effect on the customer or downstream process. A rating of 10 indicates a safety-critical or regulatory non-compliance failure; 1 indicates no discernible effect. Defined per AIAG-VDA severity evaluation criteria. Core AIAG-VDA Step 5 field.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 5; IEC 60812:2018; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this FMEA record was sourced. Supports data lineage and master data management in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. Typical values: Teamcenter (Siemens Teamcenter PLM), SAP_QM (SAP S/4HANA Quality Management), Opcenter (Siemens Opcenter MES), Manual (direct entry).
attributes.references: Informatica MDM Data Governance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Architecture

attributes.name: special_characteristic_code
attributes.description: Designation indicating whether the failure mode relates to a special product or process characteristic requiring enhanced control. SC = Special Characteristic; CC = Critical Characteristic; KPC = Key Product Characteristic; KCC = Key Control Characteristic. Drives control plan and inspection plan requirements.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3.3; APQP Manual

attributes.name: target_completion_date
attributes.description: Planned date by which the recommended action must be implemented and verified. Used for action tracking and escalation management in the FMEA review cycle. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 8; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: team_members
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or description of cross-functional team members who participated in the FMEA analysis session. Typically includes representatives from design engineering, manufacturing, quality, and supplier quality. Required for AIAG-VDA Step 1 documentation.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019) Step 1; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.2

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Descriptive title of the FMEA document identifying the product, component, or process being analyzed. Used for search, retrieval, and display in PLM and quality management systems.
attributes.references: AIAG-VDA FMEA Handbook (2019); Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp recording when the FMEA record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; Informatica MDM Data Governance

products.name: control_plan
products.description: Manufacturing control plan defining the process controls, inspection frequency, measurement methods, reaction plans, and control limits for each critical-to-quality (CTQ) characteristic at each process step. Links to PFMEA and inspection plans. Core APQP deliverable managed per AIAG standards and ISO 9001 clause 8.5.

attributes.name: control_plan_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the control plan record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Control plans are defined per customer requirements; linking to customer_account enables traceability of customer‑driven control specifications.

attributes.name: component_id
