attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Control plans govern specific components/parts. The existing part_number and part_name plain-text fields are denormalized component references; replacing with component_id FK enables component-level c

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: Control plans in APQP are developed per product configuration — different power ratings or communication protocols require different control limits and measurement methods. Customer-specific PPAP subm

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Implementation and maintenance costs of control plans are charged to a cost center, supporting cost‑of‑quality accounting.

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Control plans govern a specific part revision; the existing part_revision plain-text field is a denormalized revision reference. Replacing with engineering_revision_id FK enables automated control pla

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Control plans are built from engineering specifications defining control characteristics, tolerances, and measurement methods. Linking control_plan to engineering_specification is standard IATF 16949

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Binds control plans to the exact equipment they govern, supporting process control and audit compliance.

attributes.name: fmea_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA) ID
attributes.description: Reference to the PFMEA (Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) record from which this control plan characteristic was derived. Ensures traceability between risk analysis and process controls per AIAG APQP linkage requirements.
attributes.references: AIAG PFMEA Manual 1st Edition (2019); AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual

attributes.name: inspection_plan_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SAP QM inspection plan or characteristic catalog entry that operationalizes this control plan characteristic on the shop floor. Links the APQP control plan to the executable inspection instructions.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA QM (Quality Management); ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: FK to production.plant

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Control Plan records the supplier responsible for a characteristic; linking enables supplier performance tracking and compliance reporting (Control Plan – Supplier Responsibility report).

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: FK to production.work_center

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or employee ID of the authorized approver (typically Quality Manager or Engineering Manager) who formally approved this control plan revision. Required for ISO 9001 document control compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.2; AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this control plan revision was formally approved. Provides the authoritative approval event timestamp for document control audit trails and PPAP submission packages.

attributes.name: characteristic_class
attributes.description: Classification of the quality characteristic by its impact on safety, regulatory compliance, or fit/function. Critical (safety/regulatory impact), Significant (functional impact), Major (customer-noticeable), Minor (cosmetic). Drives inspection frequency and reaction plan severity.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3.3; ISO 9001:2015

attributes.name: characteristic_name
attributes.description: Name or description of the quality characteristic being controlled (e.g., 'Weld Bead Width', 'Torque Value', 'Surface Roughness Ra'). Directly corresponds to the CTQ characteristic defined in the PFMEA.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; AIAG PFMEA Manual

attributes.name: characteristic_number
attributes.description: Sequential number identifying the specific quality characteristic within the process step of this control plan row. Enables precise referencing of individual CTQ (Critical-to-Quality) characteristics.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: characteristic_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the characteristic is measured on a variable (continuous numeric) or attribute (pass/fail, go/no-go) scale. Determines the applicable SPC (Statistical Process Control) methodology and control chart type.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: control_method
attributes.description: The process control technique applied to monitor and control this characteristic (e.g., 'SPC X-bar/R Chart', 'Pre-control Chart', 'Poka-yoke', 'Visual Standard', 'Attribute Control Chart', 'Process Parameter Monitoring'). Core APQP deliverable field.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; AIAG SPC Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

attributes.name: control_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the control is a prevention control (prevents the defect from occurring) or a detection control (detects the defect after it has occurred). Aligns with PFMEA prevention/detection control columns.
attributes.references: AIAG PFMEA Manual 1st Edition (2019); AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual

attributes.name: cpk_minimum_required
attributes.description: The minimum acceptable Cpk (Process Capability Index) value required for this characteristic to be considered capable. Typically 1.33 for production and 1.67 for critical/safety characteristics per AIAG PPAP requirements.
attributes.references: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; AIAG SPC Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.1

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this control plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under ISO 9001 document control and data lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date from which this revision of the control plan becomes active and must be followed on the shop floor. Aligns with the ECO/ECN implementation date in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual

attributes.name: error_proofing_method
attributes.description: Description of the poka-yoke or error-proofing device/method applied at this process step to prevent or detect non-conformances (e.g., 'Torque monitoring with automatic shutdown', 'Vision system barcode verification', 'Fixture limit switch'). Reduces reliance on operator inspection.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; IATF 16949:2016

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which this control plan revision expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended plans. Used to enforce periodic review cycles mandated by ISO 9001 and customer-specific requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3; AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual

attributes.name: gauge_type
attributes.description: Type or category of measurement instrument or gauge used for this characteristic (e.g., 'Vernier Caliper', 'CMM', 'Go/No-Go Gauge', 'Torque Tester', 'Vision System'). Used for gauge calibration scheduling and MSA planning.
attributes.references: AIAG MSA Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.1

attributes.name: is_ctq
attributes.description: Indicates whether this characteristic is designated as Critical-to-Quality (CTQ), meaning it directly impacts customer satisfaction, safety, or regulatory compliance. CTQ characteristics receive enhanced monitoring and are mandatory in PPAP submissions.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; IATF 16949:2016; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.3.3

attributes.name: is_regulatory_requirement
attributes.description: Indicates whether this characteristic is controlled to meet a specific regulatory or certification requirement (e.g., CE Marking, UL, IEC 62443, EPA, OSHA). Drives mandatory retention of inspection records for regulatory audit purposes.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2; CE Marking Directive; UL Standards; IEC 62443; EPA; OSHA

attributes.name: is_safety_characteristic
attributes.description: Indicates whether this characteristic is classified as a safety-critical characteristic (SC) per customer-specific requirements or regulatory mandates. Safety characteristics require 100% inspection or validated error-proofing (poka-yoke) and special PPAP documentation.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3.3; OSHA; UL Certification

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this control plan record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance in the silver layer.

attributes.name: lower_control_limit
attributes.description: The lower statistical process control limit (LCL) for the SPC control chart associated with this characteristic. Calculated as the process mean minus three standard deviations. Triggers reaction plan when breached.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.1

attributes.name: lower_spec_limit
attributes.description: The lower engineering specification limit (LSL) for the quality characteristic. Measurements below this value are considered non-conforming. Used in Cp/Cpk process capability index calculations.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9

attributes.name: measurement_method
attributes.description: Description of the measurement technique or method used to evaluate the characteristic (e.g., 'CMM dimensional measurement', 'Torque wrench with digital readout', 'Visual inspection per drawing'). Supports Measurement System Analysis (MSA) traceability.
attributes.references: AIAG MSA Reference Manual 4th Edition; AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

attributes.name: nominal_value
attributes.description: The target or nominal engineering specification value for the quality characteristic (e.g., 25.00 mm, 45 Nm). Used as the center point for control limit calculations and SPC charting.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9

attributes.name: plan_number
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the control plan document, typically formatted as CP-<part/process code>-<sequence>. Used for cross-referencing in APQP packages, PPAP submissions, and SAP QM inspection plans.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1

attributes.name: plan_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the control plan document. Drives whether the plan is actively used on the shop floor. Managed through the document control workflow in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5.3 (Control of Documented Information); AIAG APQP

attributes.name: plan_type
attributes.description: Phase classification of the control plan per AIAG APQP methodology: prototype (early design validation), pre-launch (pilot/trial production), or production (full-rate manufacturing). Determines the rigor and scope of controls applied.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual 4th Edition; AIAG PPAP 4th Edition

attributes.name: process_step_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the manufacturing process step or operation (e.g., 'Welding', 'CNC Machining', 'Final Assembly Inspection'). Provides human-readable context for the control being defined.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: process_step_number
attributes.description: Sequential identifier for the manufacturing process step or operation within the control plan (e.g., 10, 20, 30). Aligns with the process flow diagram and PFMEA step numbering per AIAG standards.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; AIAG PFMEA Manual

attributes.name: reaction_plan
attributes.description: Prescribed corrective actions to be taken when the process goes out of control or a non-conformance is detected for this characteristic (e.g., 'Stop production, quarantine last 2 hours output, notify quality engineer, initiate NCR'). Mandatory APQP control plan field.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; AIAG PFMEA Manual

attributes.name: revision_number
attributes.description: Alphanumeric revision level of the control plan document (e.g., A, B, 01, 02). Incremented each time the plan is formally revised via an Engineering Change Order (ECO) or Engineering Change Notice (ECN).
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Document Control)

attributes.name: sample_frequency
attributes.description: Frequency or trigger condition for performing the inspection (e.g., 'Every 2 hours', 'Every 50 pieces', 'First-off and last-off', 'Each lot', '100% inspection'). Defines the inspection cadence on the shop floor.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6

attributes.name: sample_size
attributes.description: Number of parts or units to be measured per inspection event for this characteristic. Defined per statistical sampling plan (e.g., ANSI/ASQ Z1.4, Z1.9) or engineering judgment. Drives inspection workload planning.
attributes.references: AIAG APQP & Control Plan Reference Manual; ANSI/ASQ Z1.4; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9; ISO 2859

attributes.name: spc_chart_type
attributes.description: Type of SPC (Statistical Process Control) control chart applied to monitor this characteristic. Determines the statistical rules and control limit formulas used. Applicable only when control_method includes SPC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: xbar_r|xbar_s|individuals_mr|p_chart|np_chart|c_chart|u_chart — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 7870 (Control Charts); ANSI/ASQ B1/B2/B3

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Engineering unit of measure for the characteristic's nominal value and specification limits (e.g., mm, Nm, MPa, °C, %). Aligns with SAP MM unit of measure codes and ISO 80000 international measurement standards.
attributes.references: ISO 80000 (Quantities and Units); SAP S/4HANA MM; ANSI/ASQ

attributes.name: upper_control_limit
attributes.description: The upper statistical process control limit (UCL) for the SPC control chart associated with this characteristic. Calculated as the process mean plus three standard deviations. Triggers reaction plan when exceeded.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.1

attributes.name: upper_spec_limit
attributes.description: The upper engineering specification limit (USL) for the quality characteristic. Measurements exceeding this value are considered non-conforming. Used in Cp/Cpk process capability index calculations.
attributes.references: AIAG SPC Reference Manual 2nd Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.6; ANSI/ASQ Z1.9

products.name: customer_complaint
products.description: Customer complaint and field quality issue record capturing reported defects, failures, or dissatisfaction from OEM customers, distributors, or end users. Tracks complaint description, affected product/serial number, failure mode, 8D problem-solving steps, containment actions, root cause, corrective actions, and customer response. Integrates with Salesforce Service Cloud and SAP QM QN.
products.reference: 8D Problem Solving / SAP QM Quality Notification (QN)

attributes.name: customer_complaint_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the customer complaint record. Primary key for this entity.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 — Nonconformity and Corrective Action

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account (OEM, distributor, or end user) who submitted the complaint. Links to the customer domain.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Salesforce CRM

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Industrial manufacturing complaints are site-specific — a defective automation or electrification component is installed at a specific customer plant. Linking to account_site enables site-level defect

attributes.name: capa_id
attributes.description: Reference to the formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record created to address the root cause of this complaint. Links complaint to the CAPA management process.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Customer complaints in industrial manufacturing are typically about specific components. Linking customer_complaint to component enables component-level complaint trend analysis and field failure inve

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Customer complaint resolution in industrial manufacturing is governed by warranty terms, SLA response times, and penalty clauses defined in the sales contract. This link enables contract-compliance re

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Customer complaint resolution costs (rework, replacement shipments, 8D investigation, warranty claims) are tracked against cost centers for warranty cost reporting and P&L allocation. Industrial manuf

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific contact person at the customer organization who reported the complaint.

attributes.name: delivery_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: Customer complaints in manufacturing are often triggered by a specific delivery (wrong shipment, damaged goods, missing items). Complaint resolution and 8D reporting require knowing which delivery was

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Customer complaints in manufacturing are tied to specific production lots for root cause analysis, recall scope determination, and 8D reporting. Lot-level complaint traceability is a core requirement

attributes.name: ncr_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: Customer Complaint may stem from an NCR; link complaint to NCR for traceability.

attributes.name: order_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Links complaints to the precise order line, enabling root‑cause analysis and targeted corrective actions.

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: Customer complaints must be analyzed by product revision to determine if a revision change resolves the issue or if all revision levels are affected. Field failure analysis and warranty cost reporting

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Customer complaints reference the production work order that produced the nonconforming product for traceability, warranty analysis, and field failure investigation. customer_complaint.production_orde

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Customer complaints are linked to the originating purchase order to identify supplier responsibility in complaint handling process.

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_rma. Business justification: Customer complaints in manufacturing routinely result in an RMA for product return. The customer_complaint table carries a denormalized rma_number plain text field; a proper FK to order_rma enables co

attributes.name: sales_order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Complaints are investigated against the exact order intake that generated the sale, supporting root‑cause analysis.

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: Customer complaints for serialized industrial products (automation systems, drives) reference the specific returned or failed unit for warranty processing, RMA tracking, and field failure analysis. af

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Customer complaints reference the affected product SKU; FK enables complaint analysis and warranty reporting per SKU.

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Customer complaints in industrial manufacturing must be resolved within SLA-mandated response and resolution windows. Linking complaint to sla_agreement enables automated SLA breach detection, penalty

attributes.name: closure_date
attributes.description: Date on which the complaint was formally closed following customer acceptance of the resolution and verification of corrective action effectiveness.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM QN Completion Date

attributes.name: complaint_description
attributes.description: Full narrative description of the customer complaint including observed symptoms, conditions of failure, and customer-reported impact. Corresponds to SAP QM QN Problem Description field.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM QN Problem Description

attributes.name: complaint_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible, human-readable complaint reference number used in customer communications and SAP QM Quality Notification (QN). Format: CC-YYYY-NNNNNN.
attributes.references: SAP QM Quality Notification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: complaint_source
attributes.description: Origin channel or party type from which the complaint was received. Supports segmentation of complaint volumes by customer tier.

attributes.name: complaint_status
attributes.description: Current workflow state of the complaint record. Drives escalation, SLA tracking, and reporting. 'pending_customer' indicates awaiting customer feedback or approval.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Quality Notification Status

attributes.name: complaint_title
attributes.description: Short, human-readable summary title of the complaint as entered by the quality engineer or service representative. Used in dashboards and notification emails.
attributes.references: SAP QM Quality Notification Subject; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: complaint_type
attributes.description: Categorization of the complaint by nature of the issue. Used for trend analysis, routing, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product_defect|field_failure|delivery_issue|documentation_error|safety_concern|performance_deviation — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Notification Type

attributes.name: containment_action
attributes.description: Immediate containment action taken to prevent further defective product from reaching the customer, corresponding to Step D3 of the 8D problem-solving methodology. Includes field holds, sorting, or shipment stops.

attributes.name: containment_date
attributes.description: Date on which the containment action was implemented. Used to measure response speed and SLA compliance for initial containment.

attributes.name: corrective_action_completed_date
attributes.description: Actual date on which the permanent corrective action was fully implemented and verified effective.

attributes.name: corrective_action_description
attributes.description: Description of the permanent corrective action selected and implemented to eliminate the root cause, corresponding to 8D Steps D5 (select) and D6 (implement). May trigger an Engineering Change Order (ECO) or Engineering Change Notice (ECN).
attributes.references: AIAG 8D D5/D6; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2.1(c); CAPA Process

attributes.name: corrective_action_due_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the permanent corrective action must be implemented and verified. Used for SLA tracking and escalation management.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System audit timestamp recording when this complaint record was first created in the data platform.

attributes.name: customer_acceptance_status
attributes.description: Status of the customer's acceptance of the proposed resolution or 8D corrective action response. Determines whether the complaint can be formally closed.

attributes.name: customer_order_number
attributes.description: SAP SD sales order number associated with the delivery of the affected product. Links complaint to order fulfillment and revenue impact analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Order; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2.2

attributes.name: customer_response_date
attributes.description: Date on which the formal response (e.g., 8D report, corrective action plan) was communicated to the customer. Used for SLA compliance measurement.

attributes.name: defect_location
attributes.description: Physical location or component area on the product where the defect or failure was observed. Supports PFMEA and design engineering root cause analysis.
attributes.references: AIAG PFMEA; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Defect Location

attributes.name: eight_d_report_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the formal 8D (Eight Disciplines) problem-solving report issued to the customer. Required by many OEM customers as part of complaint resolution documentation.
attributes.references: AIAG 8D Problem Solving Methodology; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: failure_code
attributes.description: Standardized alphanumeric code from the quality defect code catalog identifying the failure mode. Used for statistical analysis and SPC trending. Corresponds to SAP QM defect code.
attributes.references: SAP QM Defect Code Catalog; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: failure_mode
attributes.description: Specific manner in which the product or component failed as identified during complaint investigation. Aligned with FMEA failure mode taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for standardized failure mode codes]
attributes.references: AIAG FMEA 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM Defect Code

attributes.name: is_regulatory_reportable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this complaint must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., OSHA, EPA, UL, CE Marking body). Triggers compliance workflow and documentation requirements.
attributes.references: OSHA; EPA; UL Standards; CE Marking Directive; IEC 62443

attributes.name: is_safety_related
attributes.description: Indicates whether the complaint involves a potential safety hazard to personnel or end users. Triggers mandatory escalation to product safety and regulatory teams per OSHA and CE Marking requirements.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; CE Marking Directive; ISO 45001:2018

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the affected product was produced. Enables complaint analysis by production site.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4.1

attributes.name: preventive_action_description
attributes.description: Description of systemic preventive actions taken to prevent recurrence of similar complaints across other products or processes, corresponding to 8D Step D7.

attributes.name: quantity_complained
attributes.description: Number of units reported as defective or non-conforming in the complaint. Used for severity assessment and potential recall scope estimation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; SAP QM QN Defect Quantity

attributes.name: quantity_returned
attributes.description: Number of units physically returned by the customer under a Return Material Authorization (RMA). May differ from quantity complained if partial returns occur.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Returns; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

attributes.name: received_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the complaint was received and logged in the system. Used for SLA response-time measurement.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Salesforce Service Cloud Case Created Time

attributes.name: reported_date
attributes.description: Calendar date on which the customer formally reported or submitted the complaint. Used as the principal business event date for SLA and aging calculations.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP QM QN Creation Date

attributes.name: resolution_type
attributes.description: Type of resolution provided to the customer. Drives financial impact tracking (credit notes, replacements) and customer satisfaction measurement.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP S/4HANA SD Returns

attributes.name: root_cause_category
attributes.description: Standardized category of the identified root cause. Enables Pareto analysis of complaint drivers across design, process, material, and supplier dimensions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design|process|material|supplier|handling|measurement|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: root_cause_description
attributes.description: Narrative description of the verified root cause of the complaint, corresponding to Step D4 of the 8D methodology. May reference 5-Why analysis, fishbone diagram, or PFMEA findings.
attributes.references: AIAG 8D D4; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2.1(b); AIAG PFMEA

attributes.name: salesforce_case_number
attributes.description: Cross-reference identifier from Salesforce Service Cloud case record linked to this complaint, enabling traceability between CRM and quality management systems.

attributes.name: sap_qn_number
attributes.business_glossary_term: SAP Quality Management (QM) Quality Notification (QN) Number
attributes.description: SAP QM Quality Notification number corresponding to this complaint, used for traceability within SAP S/4HANA QM module.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: severity_level
attributes.description: Severity classification of the complaint based on customer impact, safety risk, and regulatory implications. Drives escalation rules and response SLA targets. Aligned with FMEA severity ranking.
attributes.references: AIAG FMEA Severity Scale; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; OSHA Safety Classification

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: System audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this complaint record.

products.name: quality_audit
products.description: Quality audit record documenting all scheduled and unscheduled audits regardless of target — internal departments, supplier facilities, subcontractor sites, or customer-mandated assessments. Captures audit type (system audit, process audit, product audit, layered process audit), target scope (internal department/process, supplier site, subcontractor, remote), audit program/schedule reference, checklist criteria and results, findings classification (major non-conformance, minor non-conformance, observation, opportunity for improvement), audit score/rating, auditor qualification and independence verification, evidence collected, corrective action requirements with CAPA linkage, re-audit schedule, and closure status. Supports ISO 9001 clause 9.2 (internal audit), clause 8.4 (externally provided processes — supplier control), VDA 6.3 process audit methodology, and SAP Ariba supplier qualification workflows.

attributes.name: quality_audit_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the quality audit record. Primary key for the audit entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Second-party audits (customer auditing the manufacturer) are a named audit type in industrial manufacturing. quality_audit has audited_entity_name as plain text but no FK to customer_account. Linking

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Sales contracts in industrial manufacturing frequently mandate customer-directed quality audits with defined frequency, scope, and SLA uptime requirements. Linking quality_audit to sales_contract enab

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quality audit costs (auditor fees, travel, lab expenses) are charged to cost centers for cost-of-quality reporting and budget control. Industrial manufacturing finance controllers require cost center

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Quality audits in industrial manufacturing are frequently scoped to a product family (e.g., ISO 9001 audit of the entire drives product family, or IATF audit of automation systems). audit_scope on qua

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.location. Business justification: Quality audits in manufacturing are conducted at specific physical locations (plant areas, production zones). ISO 9001 audit records must identify the audited location. audited_entity_location on qual

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Quality audits (ISO 9001, IATF 16949) are conducted at specific plants. quality_audit.audited_entity_location is a plain text field denormalizing the plant reference. Linking audit to plant enables pl

attributes.name: previous_audit_quality_audit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the previous audit of the same entity, enabling audit history tracking and trend analysis.

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Procurement contracts in industrial manufacturing routinely include audit-rights clauses and mandatory supplier quality audit schedules. Linking quality_audit to the governing procurement_contract ena

attributes.name: sourcing_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_rule. Business justification: Supplier quality audit outcomes (score, pass/fail, major NCR count) directly gate sourcing rule activation or suspension in industrial manufacturing supplier qualification processes. Procurement teams

attributes.name: supplier_audit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_audit. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, internal quality audits of suppliers cross-reference the corresponding supplier_audit record for CAPA closure traceability and regulatory compliance (IATF 16949). SQEs nee

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier master record if the audit is a supplier audit. Null for internal or customer audits.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Supplier quality audits in industrial manufacturing are conducted at a specific supplier site. audited_entity_location is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to supplier.site enables site-level aud

attributes.name: actual_end_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the audit was completed, including closing meeting and final observations.

attributes.name: actual_start_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the audit commenced, which may differ from the planned start date due to scheduling changes.

attributes.name: audit_category
attributes.description: Categorization of the audit by the relationship to the audited party: internal (own departments/processes), external supplier (supplier facilities), external subcontractor (subcontractor sites), external customer (customer-mandated), certification (third-party certification body), surveillance (ongoing certification monitoring), or special (triggered by incident or complaint). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal|external_supplier|external_subcontractor|external_customer|certification|surveillance|special — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: audit_method
attributes.description: The method by which the audit is conducted: on-site (physical presence at audited location), remote (virtual audit via video/document sharing), hybrid (combination of on-site and remote), or document review (desk audit of documentation only).

attributes.name: audit_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the audit, externally visible and used for tracking and reference across systems and documentation.

attributes.name: audit_result
attributes.description: Overall result or conclusion of the audit: conforming (no major issues), non-conforming (major issues found), conditional pass (minor issues requiring follow-up), fail (critical issues), acceptable, or unacceptable.

attributes.name: audit_scope
attributes.description: Detailed description of the audit scope, including the processes, departments, product lines, or ISO clauses covered. Defines the boundaries and focus areas of the audit.

attributes.name: audit_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the audit: planned (in audit program), scheduled (dates confirmed), in progress (audit underway), report draft (findings being documented), report issued (formal report distributed), CAPA pending (corrective actions awaited), CAPA verification (actions being verified), closed (audit complete and verified), or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|scheduled|in_progress|report_draft|report_issued|capa_pending|capa_verification|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: audit_type
attributes.description: Classification of the audit by its focus area: system audit (QMS compliance), process audit (manufacturing process conformance per VDA 6.3), product audit (finished goods verification), layered process audit (LPA short-interval checks), supplier audit (external provider assessment), or customer audit (customer-mandated assessment).

attributes.name: audited_entity_country
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the audited entity location.

attributes.name: audited_entity_type
attributes.description: Type of entity being audited: internal department, supplier facility, subcontractor site, customer site, manufacturing plant, warehouse, or laboratory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_department|supplier|subcontractor|customer_site|manufacturing_plant|warehouse|laboratory — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: auditee_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the auditee contact for audit coordination and communication.

attributes.name: auditee_contact_name
attributes.description: Name of the primary contact person at the audited entity responsible for coordinating the audit and providing access to information.

attributes.name: auditor_independence_verified
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether auditor independence and impartiality have been verified per ISO 19011 requirements, ensuring no conflict of interest.

attributes.name: capa_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether corrective and preventive actions are required based on the audit findings.

attributes.name: closure_date
attributes.description: Date when the audit was formally closed after all findings were addressed, CAPAs verified, and re-audit (if required) completed.

attributes.name: conforming_items_count
attributes.description: Number of checklist items found to be in full conformance with the audit criteria.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: duration_days
attributes.description: Total duration of the audit in days, calculated from actual start to actual end date.

attributes.name: evidence_collected
attributes.description: Description or reference to the objective evidence collected during the audit, such as documents reviewed, records examined, interviews conducted, and observations made.

attributes.name: findings_summary
attributes.description: Executive summary of the audit findings, highlighting key non-conformances, observations, strengths, and areas for improvement.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the audit record was last updated, supporting audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: lead_auditor_name
attributes.description: Full name of the lead auditor for quick reference and reporting.

attributes.name: lead_auditor_qualification
attributes.description: Certification or qualification held by the lead auditor, such as ISO 9001 Lead Auditor, VDA 6.3 Process Auditor, or internal auditor certification.

attributes.name: major_ncr_count
attributes.description: Number of major non-conformances identified during the audit. Major NCRs represent significant deviations that affect the QMS effectiveness or product quality.

attributes.name: minor_ncr_count
attributes.description: Number of minor non-conformances identified during the audit. Minor NCRs represent isolated or less significant deviations that do not critically impact the QMS.

attributes.name: objective
attributes.description: Statement of the audit's purpose and intended outcomes, such as QMS conformance verification, process effectiveness assessment, supplier qualification, or compliance validation.

attributes.name: observation_count
attributes.description: Number of observations or opportunities for improvement noted during the audit that do not constitute non-conformances but warrant attention.

attributes.name: planned_end_date
attributes.description: Scheduled end date for the audit as defined in the audit program or audit plan.

attributes.name: planned_start_date
attributes.description: Scheduled start date for the audit as defined in the audit program or audit plan.

attributes.name: re_audit_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether a follow-up re-audit is required to verify the effectiveness of corrective actions or to address major non-conformances.

attributes.name: re_audit_scheduled_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the follow-up re-audit to verify corrective action effectiveness.

attributes.name: report_document_reference
attributes.description: Document management system reference or file path to the formal audit report document.

attributes.name: report_issued_date
attributes.description: Date when the formal audit report was issued and distributed to relevant stakeholders.

attributes.name: score
attributes.description: Numerical score or rating assigned to the audit based on conformance level, typically expressed as a percentage or on a defined scale (e.g., VDA 6.3 scoring).

attributes.name: standard
attributes.description: The quality standard or framework against which the audit is conducted, such as ISO 9001:2015, IATF 16949, VDA 6.3, AS9100, ISO 13485, or customer-specific requirements.

attributes.name: team_members
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of audit team member names or employee IDs participating in the audit alongside the lead auditor.

attributes.name: total_checklist_items
attributes.description: Total number of checklist items or audit criteria evaluated during the audit.

products.name: inspection_characteristic
products.description: Master reference table for inspection_characteristic. Referenced by characteristic_id.

attributes.name: inspection_characteristic_id
attributes.description: Primary key for inspection_characteristic

attributes.name: measurement_equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Inspection characteristics define the gauge/instrument required for measurement. IATF 16949 MSA (Measurement System Analysis) requires traceability from each inspection characteristic to the specific

attributes.name: parent_inspection_characteristic_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on inspection_characteristic (parent_inspection_characteristic_id)

attributes.name: product_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Inspection characteristics (upper_spec_limit, lower_spec_limit, target_value, tolerance) are directly derived from product specifications. This is a fundamental quality-product relationship: character

attributes.name: characteristic_type
attributes.description: Category of the characteristic indicating the nature of the measurement.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the characteristic record was first created.

attributes.name: criticality_level
attributes.description: Impact level of the characteristic on product quality and compliance.

attributes.name: data_collection_system
attributes.description: System that records the measurement data (e.g., MES, PLC, manual log).

attributes.name: effective_from
attributes.description: Date when the characteristic becomes valid for use.

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date when the characteristic is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).

attributes.name: frequency_per_shift
attributes.description: Number of times this characteristic is inspected each production shift.

attributes.name: inspection_characteristic_code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code assigned by engineering to identify the characteristic across systems.

attributes.name: inspection_characteristic_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of what the characteristic measures and why it is important.

attributes.name: inspection_characteristic_name
attributes.description: Human‑readable name of the inspection characteristic used in reports and work instructions.

attributes.name: inspection_method
attributes.description: Method used to capture the characteristic (e.g., manual, automated sensor).

attributes.name: is_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the characteristic must be inspected for every unit.

attributes.name: is_statistical_process_control
attributes.description: True if the characteristic is used in SPC calculations (Cp/Cpk).

attributes.name: lifecycle_status
attributes.description: Current status of the characteristic definition.

attributes.name: lower_spec_limit
attributes.description: Minimum acceptable value for the characteristic.

attributes.name: measurement_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure used for the characteristic (e.g., mm, °C, psi).

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.

attributes.name: sampling_plan
attributes.description: Definition of the sampling approach (e.g., 1‑in‑5, 100% inspection).

attributes.name: spec_source
attributes.description: Origin of the specification limits for the characteristic.

attributes.name: target_value
attributes.description: Nominal target value that the characteristic is expected to meet.

attributes.name: tolerance
attributes.description: Allowed deviation from the target value, expressed in the same unit as measurement_unit.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the characteristic record.

attributes.name: upper_spec_limit
attributes.description: Maximum acceptable value for the characteristic.

products.name: plan_characteristic_assignment
products.description: This association product represents the formal Assignment between an inspection_plan and an inspection_characteristic within a quality management system. It captures the operational configuration of each characteristic as it applies within a specific plan, including sequencing, mandatory status, and plan-specific overrides for sampling, tolerance, and control method. Each record links one inspection_plan to one inspection_characteristic and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment — not on the plan or characteristic master alone. Supports PPAP, APQP, and ISO 9001 traceability requirements.. Existence Justification: In SAP QM and ISO 9001-aligned quality management systems, inspection plans are formally composed of multiple inspection characteristics, and each master characteristic (e.g., 'surface roughness', 'tensile strength') is deliberately reused across many inspection plans for different components, SKUs, or production stages. This is not an analytical correlation — quality engineers actively create, sequence, and configure characteristic assignments within each plan, with plan-specific overrides for tolerances, sample sizes, and control methods. The business concept 'Inspection Plan Characteristic Assignment' is a recognized SAP QM entity (QP01 operation characteristic) that carries its own operational data.

attributes.name: plan_characteristic_assignment_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Plan Characteristic Assignment - Plan Characteristic Assignment Id
attributes.description: Primary key for the plan_characteristic_assignment association

attributes.name: inspection_characteristic_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Plan Characteristic Assignment - Inspection Characteristic Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the master inspection characteristic being assigned to the plan.

attributes.name: inspection_plan_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Plan Characteristic Assignment - Inspection Plan Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the parent inspection plan that this characteristic assignment belongs to.

attributes.name: characteristic_count
attributes.description: Total number of inspection characteristics (measurement parameters) defined within this inspection plan. Provides a quick summary of plan complexity for scheduling and resource planning. [Moved from inspection_plan: This is a derived aggregate (count of assigned characteristics) that should be computed from the association table rather than stored as a denormalized field on the plan header. Storing it on the plan creates a maintenance burden and risks inconsistency with the actual assignment records.]

attributes.name: characteristic_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the inspection characteristic tolerance limits and target value (e.g., mm, µm, N, bar, °C, %). Aligns with SAP QM master inspection characteristic unit. [Moved from inspection_plan: The unit of measure for tolerance limits is a property of the specific characteristic being measured, not of the plan as a whole. A plan covers many characteristics with different units (mm, °C, N·m). This attribute belongs on the assignment or on the characteristic master, not on the plan header. If it represents a plan-level default, it is misleading; if it represents a specific characteristic's unit, it belongs on the assignment.]
attributes.references: SAP QM QPAM.MSEHI; ISO 80000-1; SAP T006

attributes.name: control_method_override
attributes.description: Overrides the default inspection method defined on the master characteristic for this plan. For example, a final inspection plan may require CMM measurement where the in-process plan uses a manual gauge. Null if the master control method applies.

attributes.name: is_mandatory
attributes.description: Indicates whether this characteristic must be measured for every unit inspected under this plan. A characteristic may be mandatory in one plan (safety-critical context) and optional in another (reduced inspection scope). Cannot reside on the characteristic master because the obligation is plan-specific.

attributes.name: sample_size_override
attributes.description: Overrides the default sampling plan defined on the master inspection_characteristic for this specific plan context. For example, a PPAP plan may require 100% inspection of a characteristic that is normally sampled 1-in-5 in routine production. Null if the characteristic's default sampling plan applies.

attributes.name: sequence_number
attributes.description: Defines the ordered position of this characteristic within the inspection plan's operation sequence. Corresponds to the SAP QM characteristic sequence within a plan operation. Required for structured work instruction generation and operator guidance.

attributes.name: tolerance_override
attributes.description: Overrides the master characteristic's default tolerance value for this specific plan. Allows tightening of tolerances for customer-specific or PPAP requirements without modifying the master characteristic definition. Null if the master tolerance applies.

domains.name: asset
domains.description: Equipment and asset lifecycle management domain tracking physical assets, machinery maintenance (CMMS), preventive maintenance scheduling, TPM, work order execution, MTBF/MTTR analysis, downtime events, spare parts, condition monitoring, and CapEx asset registers via Maximo. Manages production equipment, PLCs, CNC machines, robotics, and facility infrastructure.

products.name: equipment_register
products.description: Master record of all physical assets and production equipment in the CapEx asset register, including CNC machines, PLCs, robotics, conveyors, HVAC, and facility infrastructure. Serves as the SSOT for equipment identity, classification, parent-child hierarchy (structural decomposition into sub-assemblies and components), installation details, manufacturer specifications, commissioning date, depreciation method, book value, current operational status, criticality ranking, and location assignment history (including transfer/relocation audit trail with origin, destination, reason, condition at transfer, and authorization reference). Sourced from Maximo Asset Management and SAP S/4HANA PM module.
products.reference: Maximo Asset / SAP PM Equipment Master

attributes.name: equipment_register_id

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: As-built configuration tracking: industrial asset management requires knowing the exact product configuration installed (e.g., drive configured for specific voltage/power rating). This supports engine

attributes.name: drawing_id

attributes.name: fixed_asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Capital asset lifecycle management requires linking the physical equipment record to its financial fixed_asset record for depreciation tracking, net book value reporting, insurance valuation, and CAPE

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.location. Business justification: Every physical asset in the equipment register must be placed at a specific location (plant, building, floor, production cell). This is a fundamental CMMS relationship — equipment is installed at a lo

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Equipment is physically installed on a production line; essential for line-level OEE aggregation, production line capacity analysis, and maintenance impact assessment. Industrial manufacturing asset r

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Equipment traceability requires knowing the specific supplier manufacturing site of origin — critical for warranty claims, product recalls, and regulatory compliance. equipment_register.manufacturer_n

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Business process: Asset Receiving & Storage requires tracking which warehouse bin new equipment is stored in before installation, enabling inventory valuation and location reporting.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Procurement process records primary equipment supplier for warranty and support contracts.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Equipment is physically assigned to a work center; this is fundamental to capacity planning, OEE reporting, and maintenance scheduling. work_center_code on equipment_register is a denormalization of t

attributes.name: asset_category
attributes.description: Broad category grouping for the asset used in CapEx asset register classification, depreciation policy assignment, and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP fixed asset schedules.
attributes.value_regex: Production Equipment|Facility Infrastructure|Utility System|Safety System|Measurement and Test Equipment|IT and Automation
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Module; IFRS 16; GAAP ASC 360; ISO 55000

attributes.name: asset_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique asset tag or equipment number assigned in the CapEx asset register and stamped on the physical asset. Corresponds to the Maximo Asset Number and SAP S/4HANA Equipment Number (PM module). Serves as the primary business identifier for cross-system reconciliation.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000 Asset Management

attributes.name: capacity_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the rated capacity value (e.g., 'units/hr', 'kg/hr', 'tonnes/day', 'cycles/min'). Required to correctly interpret and compare capacity figures across heterogeneous equipment types.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 80000; ANSI

attributes.name: commissioning_date
attributes.description: Date on which the equipment was formally commissioned and placed into productive service following installation and acceptance testing. Marks the start of the asset's operational lifecycle and triggers depreciation commencement in SAP FI-AA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Module; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000; IFRS 16; GAAP ASC 360

attributes.name: condition_at_transfer
attributes.description: Recorded physical condition grade of the equipment at the time of its most recent transfer or relocation. Provides a point-in-time condition snapshot for the location assignment history audit trail and insurance/liability documentation.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000; ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring

attributes.name: condition_grade
attributes.description: Current physical condition assessment grade of the equipment based on the most recent inspection or condition monitoring evaluation. Used in asset lifecycle management decisions (repair vs. replace), maintenance prioritization, and insurance assessments.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000; ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring

attributes.name: criticality_ranking
attributes.description: Business criticality classification of the equipment based on its impact on production throughput, safety, and quality if it fails. Critical assets receive priority maintenance resources and are included in TPM and FMEA programs. Used in maintenance strategy selection and spare parts stocking decisions.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000; IEC 62443; FMEA Methodology

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this equipment record (acquisition cost, book value, replacement value). Supports multi-currency CapEx reporting for global manufacturing operations.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Module; IFRS; GAAP

attributes.name: decommission_date
attributes.description: Date on which the equipment was formally decommissioned and removed from productive service. Triggers asset write-off or disposal processing in SAP FI-AA. Null for active assets.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Module; ISO 55000; IFRS 16; GAAP ASC 360

attributes.name: equipment_class
attributes.description: High-level classification of the equipment type used for asset categorization, maintenance strategy assignment, and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CNC Machine|PLC|Robotics|Conveyor|HVAC|Compressor|Pump|Electrical Panel|Facility Infrastructure|Inspection Equipment — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000 Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: equipment_name
attributes.description: Human-readable descriptive name of the equipment or asset (e.g., 'CNC Milling Machine #3', 'Conveyor Belt Line A'). Used as the primary identity label for display in reports, dashboards, and work orders.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: functional_location
attributes.description: Hierarchical functional location code (e.g., PLANT-AREA-LINE-STATION) representing the physical and functional position of the equipment within the facility structure. Corresponds to the SAP PM Functional Location and Maximo Location fields. Supports spatial analytics and maintenance planning.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000

attributes.name: installation_date
attributes.description: Date on which the equipment was physically installed at its designated location. May precede commissioning date if acceptance testing or qualification activities are required before productive use.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: last_inspection_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent formal inspection or condition assessment performed on the equipment. Used to track regulatory inspection compliance intervals and condition monitoring schedules.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; OSHA; ISO 45001; ISO 55000

attributes.name: last_maintenance_date
attributes.description: Date on which the most recent completed maintenance activity (preventive or corrective) was performed on the equipment. Used to calculate maintenance intervals, overdue PM alerts, and MTBF analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: last_transfer_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent location transfer or relocation of the equipment. Part of the location assignment history audit trail tracking asset movements between plants, production lines, or storage areas.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: last_transfer_destination
attributes.description: Functional location or plant code to which the equipment was most recently transferred. Provides the destination leg of the location transfer audit trail for asset movement traceability.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: last_transfer_origin
attributes.description: Functional location or plant code from which the equipment was most recently transferred. Provides the origin leg of the location transfer audit trail for asset movement traceability.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: last_transfer_reason
attributes.description: Business reason code for the most recent equipment relocation or transfer. Part of the location assignment history audit trail required for asset tracking compliance and CapEx asset register accuracy.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: maintenance_strategy
attributes.description: Assigned maintenance approach for the equipment as defined in the Maximo CMMS and SAP PM maintenance plan. Drives scheduling of preventive maintenance (PM) work orders, TPM activities, and condition monitoring programs.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000; TPM Methodology

attributes.name: manufacture_date
attributes.description: Date on which the equipment was manufactured by the OEM. Used to calculate equipment age, assess remaining useful life, and determine warranty expiry.

attributes.name: mean_time_between_failures
attributes.description: Average elapsed time in hours between successive equipment failures, calculated from historical work order and downtime event data. Key reliability KPI used in TPM programs, maintenance strategy optimization, and OEE analysis. Expressed in hours.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000; TPM Methodology; IEC 60300

attributes.name: mean_time_to_repair
attributes.description: Average elapsed time in hours required to restore the equipment to operational status following a failure, calculated from historical corrective maintenance work orders. Key maintainability KPI used in TPM and SLA management. Expressed in hours.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000; TPM Methodology; IEC 60300

attributes.name: model_number
attributes.description: OEM-assigned model or part number for the equipment. Used for spare parts procurement, technical documentation lookup, and warranty claims.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: next_maintenance_due_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next planned preventive maintenance activity on the equipment, as calculated by the maintenance plan in SAP PM or Maximo. Drives work order generation and maintenance scheduling.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: operational_status
attributes.description: Current operational lifecycle state of the equipment. Drives maintenance scheduling, OEE calculations, and asset availability reporting. 'In Service' indicates active production use; 'Decommissioned' triggers asset write-off in SAP FI-AA.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: power_rating_kw
attributes.description: Nominal electrical power consumption rating of the equipment in kilowatts as specified by the OEM. Used for energy management planning, ISO 50001 energy audits, electrical load balancing, and CapEx infrastructure sizing.
attributes.references: IEC 60034; ISO 50001 Energy Management; EPA Energy Star; ANSI

attributes.name: rated_capacity
attributes.description: OEM-specified nominal production capacity or throughput rating of the equipment (e.g., units per hour, tonnes per day). Serves as the denominator for OEE availability and performance calculations and production planning in APS.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 22400 OEE Standard

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the equipment record was first created in the master data system (Maximo or SAP S/4HANA). Provides the record creation audit trail for data governance and MDM lineage tracking.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM; Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the equipment record in the master data system. Used for change detection, data synchronization between Maximo and SAP, and MDM data quality monitoring.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM; Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: replacement_value
attributes.description: Current estimated cost to replace the equipment with an equivalent new asset. Used for insurance valuation, CapEx planning, and asset renewal decision-making. Updated periodically based on market pricing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Module; ISO 55000; IFRS

attributes.name: safety_classification
attributes.description: Safety classification of the equipment based on occupational health and safety risk profile. Determines required safety permits, lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures, PPE requirements, and regulatory inspection schedules under OSHA and ISO 45001.
attributes.value_regex: Hazardous|Non-Hazardous|Pressure Vessel|Electrical High Voltage|Confined Space|Radiation Source
attributes.references: OSHA 1910.147 (LOTO); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; IEC 62443; NFPA 70E

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the specific equipment unit. Used for warranty registration, OEM service calls, and regulatory compliance traceability. Classified as confidential device identifier.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; UL Certification; CE Marking; ISO 9001

attributes.name: transfer_authorization_ref
attributes.description: Reference number of the authorization document (e.g., work order, change request, or management approval) that sanctioned the most recent equipment transfer or relocation. Provides the authorization audit trail for asset movement governance.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000; IFRS

attributes.name: warranty_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which the OEM or supplier warranty for the equipment expires. Used to trigger preventive maintenance strategy transitions from warranty-covered to self-maintained, and to initiate extended warranty negotiations.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: weight_kg
attributes.description: Physical weight of the equipment in kilograms as specified by the OEM. Used for facility floor load planning, rigging and relocation logistics, and transportation planning during asset transfers.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; OSHA 1910 (Material Handling); ANSI

products.name: location
products.description: Hierarchical location structure for physical asset placement, covering plant, building, floor, production line, work center, and exact bay/cell coordinates with optional GIS/GPS reference points. Tracks functional location hierarchy per Maximo FL structure and IEC 81346 reference designation system, enabling spatial queries for maintenance routing, safety zone mapping, environmental zone classification (ATEX zones, clean rooms), and OEE analysis by location. Serves as the SSOT for where assets can be installed and the physical topology of the manufacturing facility.
products.reference: Maximo Functional Location / SAP PM Functional Location

attributes.name: cost_center_id

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Project asset management assigns physical locations to engineering projects for planning and reporting.

attributes.name: territory_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, plant and site locations are mapped to sales territories for service coverage assignment, revenue attribution by region, and key account management. Territory-based servic

attributes.name: address_line1
attributes.description: Primary street address line of the facility or plant location. Used for regulatory reporting, logistics coordination, emergency services, and legal documentation. Classified as confidential organizational address data.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management; OSHA Facility Registration; EPA Facility Reporting
