attributes.description: Physical floor area of the location in square metres. Used for capacity planning, safety zone sizing, equipment layout design, and facility management reporting. Represents the principal quantitative measurement of the location resource.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Facility Safety

attributes.name: atex_zone_classification
attributes.description: Explosive atmosphere zone classification per ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU and IEC 60079 standard (e.g., Zone 0, Zone 1, Zone 2 for gas/vapour; Zone 20, Zone 21, Zone 22 for dust). Mandatory for equipment selection, safety inspections, and regulatory compliance in hazardous areas. Null if location is not classified as hazardous.
attributes.references: ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU; IEC 60079 Explosive Atmospheres; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.303

attributes.name: building_code
attributes.description: Alphanumeric code identifying the specific building or structure within the plant site where this location resides (e.g., 'BLD-A', 'HALL-3'). Used for maintenance routing, emergency response planning, and facility management.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Facility Safety

attributes.name: capex_asset_register_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA (Fixed Asset Accounting) asset register code associated with this location for CapEx tracking, depreciation calculation, and balance sheet reporting. Links the physical location to the financial asset register per IFRS/GAAP fixed asset accounting standards.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Fixed Asset Accounting; IFRS 16 Leases; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360

attributes.name: ceiling_height_m
attributes.description: Clear ceiling height at this location in metres. Determines suitability for tall equipment installation (e.g., overhead cranes, tall CNC machines, robotic gantries), forklift operation clearance, and ventilation system design.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City or municipality where the facility location is situated. Used for regulatory reporting, tax jurisdiction determination, logistics routing, and multi-site geographic analytics.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management; EPA Facility Reporting; OSHA Establishment Registration

attributes.name: clean_room_classification
attributes.description: ISO 14644-1 clean room classification for controlled environment locations (ISO 1 through ISO 9, representing particle count per cubic metre). Governs equipment installation requirements, personnel access protocols, and environmental monitoring for precision manufacturing, electronics assembly, or pharmaceutical-adjacent processes. Null if not a controlled environment.
attributes.references: ISO 14644-1 Cleanrooms and Associated Controlled Environments; IPC-A-610 Electronics Manufacturing

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where this location is situated (e.g., 'DEU', 'USA', 'CHN'). Drives regulatory compliance framework selection (CE marking, OSHA, EPA), tax jurisdiction, and multi-national OEE benchmarking.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; CE Marking Directive; OSHA; EPA

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this asset location record was first created in the system (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Provides audit trail for data governance, MDM lineage tracking via Informatica MDM, and compliance with ISO 9001 record control requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Record Control; Informatica MDM Data Governance; ISO 8601

attributes.name: effective_from_date
attributes.description: Date from which this location record became operationally active and valid for asset installation and maintenance work order assignment. Supports temporal validity tracking for location hierarchy changes, plant expansions, and decommissioning events.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; ISO 55001 Asset Management; IFRS/GAAP Asset Accounting

attributes.name: effective_until_date
attributes.description: Date on which this location record ceases to be operationally valid (e.g., due to decommissioning, demolition, or restructuring). Null for currently active locations. Enables historical analysis of plant topology changes and supports CapEx asset retirement workflows.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; ISO 55001 Asset Management; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

attributes.name: elevation_m
attributes.description: Elevation of the location above mean sea level in metres (WGS84). Used for environmental zone classification, flood risk assessment, HVAC system design, and precision asset installation requirements in multi-storey or outdoor facilities.
attributes.references: ISO 6709 Geographic Point Location; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

attributes.name: energy_zone_code
attributes.description: Energy management zone code assigned to this location per ISO 50001 energy management system. Groups locations into energy monitoring boundaries for sub-metering, energy consumption reporting, and energy efficiency KPI calculation by production area.
attributes.references: ISO 50001 Energy Management; EPA Energy Star Industrial Program

attributes.name: environmental_zone_code
attributes.description: Environmental monitoring zone code per ISO 14001 environmental management system. Groups locations for emissions monitoring, waste management tracking, and environmental compliance reporting to EPA and regulatory bodies.
attributes.references: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; EPA 40 CFR Environmental Regulations

attributes.name: fire_zone_code
attributes.description: Fire compartment or zone code assigned to this location per fire safety regulations and building codes (e.g., 'FZ-A1', 'COMP-03'). Used for fire suppression system mapping, emergency response planning, and compliance with NFPA and local fire codes.
attributes.references: NFPA 72 National Fire Alarm Code; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.157

attributes.name: floor_level
attributes.description: Identifier for the floor or level within a building where this location is situated (e.g., 'G', '1', '2', 'B1', 'MEZZANINE'). Supports maintenance technician routing, emergency evacuation planning, and vertical spatial queries.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

attributes.name: functional_location_code
attributes.description: The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the functional location per Maximo FL structure and IEC 81346 reference designation system (e.g., PLT01-BLD02-FL03-WC04). Used as the primary business identifier for spatial referencing, maintenance routing, and asset installation tracking.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; IEC 81346 Reference Designation System; ISO 14224

attributes.name: hierarchy_level
attributes.description: Numeric depth of this location within the functional location hierarchy (1 = plant/site, 2 = building/area, 3 = floor/zone, 4 = production line, 5 = work center, 6 = bay/cell). Enables hierarchical rollup queries for OEE analysis, maintenance cost aggregation, and spatial reporting.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; IEC 81346 Reference Designation System

attributes.name: iec_reference_designation
attributes.description: Structured reference designation code per IEC 81346 standard for industrial systems (e.g., '=A1+B2-C3'). Provides internationally standardized identification of the location within the technical system hierarchy, enabling interoperability with engineering documentation, CAD/CAM systems, and PLM.
attributes.references: IEC 81346 Reference Designation System; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

attributes.name: installed_asset_count
attributes.description: Current count of physical assets installed at this location as recorded in Maximo. Provides a snapshot of location utilization density for capacity planning, maintenance workload estimation, and facility layout optimization. Updated by the asset installation/deinstallation process.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Asset Installation; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: is_outdoor
attributes.description: Indicates whether this location is an outdoor or open-air area (True) versus an enclosed indoor facility (False). Determines weatherproofing requirements for installed assets, IP rating specifications for equipment, and applicable environmental protection standards.
attributes.references: IEC 60529 IP Rating Standard; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

attributes.name: is_restricted_access
attributes.description: Indicates whether this location requires special authorization, permits, or PPE for personnel access (True) beyond standard plant access controls. Drives access management workflows, permit-to-work systems, and safety induction requirements for maintenance technicians.
attributes.references: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 Lockout/Tagout; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this asset location record (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Supports change detection for ETL incremental loads, audit trail maintenance, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Record Control; Informatica MDM Data Governance; ISO 8601

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the asset location for GIS-based spatial queries, maintenance technician navigation, and facility mapping. Applicable primarily to outdoor locations, large plant areas, or when GPS-enabled asset tracking is deployed.
attributes.references: ISO 6709 Geographic Point Location; NIST SP 800-82 Industrial Control Systems

attributes.name: location_description
attributes.description: Free-text detailed description of the asset location including physical characteristics, operational context, special conditions, and any relevant notes for maintenance technicians (e.g., 'North-east corner of Building A, adjacent to emergency exit 3, high-vibration environment due to press operations').
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; ISO 9001 Quality Management

attributes.name: location_name
attributes.description: Human-readable descriptive name of the asset location (e.g., 'Assembly Line 3 - Bay 7', 'CNC Machining Cell A'). Used in maintenance work orders, safety documentation, and OEE reporting to identify the physical location clearly.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; ISO 9001 Quality Management

attributes.name: location_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the asset location indicating whether it is operationally active, temporarily inactive, decommissioned, under construction, or reserved for future use. Controls whether assets can be installed at this location and whether maintenance work orders can be raised against it.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; ISO 9001 Quality Management

attributes.name: location_type
attributes.description: Classification of the location within the facility hierarchy (e.g., plant, building, floor, production_line, work_center, bay, cell). Drives hierarchy navigation, maintenance routing logic, and spatial analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: plant|building|floor|production_line|work_center|bay|cell|storage_area|utility_room|outdoor_area — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; IEC 81346 Reference Designation System

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the asset location for GIS-based spatial queries, maintenance technician navigation, and facility mapping. Applicable primarily to outdoor locations, large plant areas, or when GPS-enabled asset tracking is deployed.
attributes.references: ISO 6709 Geographic Point Location; NIST SP 800-82 Industrial Control Systems

attributes.name: maintenance_planner_group
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA PM or Maximo planner group code responsible for planning and scheduling preventive maintenance (PM) and corrective maintenance work orders at this location. Drives maintenance routing, resource allocation, and TPM scheduling.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Planner Group; Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Planning; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: maintenance_work_center
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA PM maintenance work center responsible for executing maintenance activities at this location. Distinct from the production work center; this identifies the maintenance crew or workshop assigned for TPM, corrective maintenance, and breakdown response.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Maintenance Work Center; Maximo Asset Management - Labor; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: max_asset_capacity
attributes.description: Maximum number of assets that can be physically installed at this location based on space, power, and structural constraints. Used for capacity planning, CapEx project scoping, and facility layout design to prevent over-installation.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Functional Location; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: max_load_capacity_kg
attributes.description: Maximum permissible floor or structural load capacity at this location in kilograms. Critical for equipment installation planning, CapEx asset placement decisions, and structural safety compliance. Prevents overloading of floors, platforms, or mezzanines during asset installation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.22 Walking-Working Surfaces; ANSI/AISC 360 Structural Steel

attributes.name: maximo_location_code
attributes.description: Native location identifier from IBM Maximo Asset Management system (LOCATIONS.LOCATION field). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and the Maximo CMMS source of record for work order routing and preventive maintenance scheduling.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - LOCATIONS Table; IBM Maximo 7.6 Data Dictionary

attributes.name: network_segment
attributes.description: Industrial network segment or VLAN identifier assigned to this location for IIoT device connectivity, SCADA/DCS integration, and PLC communication (e.g., 'OT-ZONE-A', 'SCADA-NET-02'). Supports IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity zone and conduit model for network segmentation compliance.
attributes.references: IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity; NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Aveva SCADA Network Architecture

attributes.name: pm_schedule_type
attributes.description: Type of preventive maintenance scheduling strategy applied to assets at this location (time_based, usage_based, condition_based, or none). Supports TPM strategy configuration, maintenance planning in Maximo, and predictive maintenance analytics.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - PM Schedule; ISO 55001 Asset Management; TPM Methodology

attributes.name: power_supply_type
attributes.description: Primary electrical power supply specification available at this location (e.g., AC_220V, AC_480V, DC_24V). Governs equipment compatibility for asset installation, PLC and CNC machine power requirements, and electrical safety compliance per IEC 61131 and NEC standards.
attributes.references: IEC 61131 PLC Programming Standards; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity; ANSI/NFPA 70 National Electrical Code

attributes.name: production_line_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the production line associated with this location (e.g., 'LINE-01', 'ASSY-B'). Links the physical location to production scheduling, OEE measurement, and Siemens Opcenter MES work center definitions for throughput and cycle time analysis.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES - Work Center; SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Work Center; ISO 22400 OEE KPIs

attributes.name: safety_zone_type
attributes.description: Safety zone designation for the location per OSHA and ISO 45001 requirements (e.g., general access, restricted, exclusion zone, emergency assembly point, fire zone, chemical storage). Drives access control, PPE requirements, and emergency response planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: general|restricted|exclusion|emergency_assembly|fire_zone|chemical_storage — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; NFPA 70E

attributes.name: site_code
attributes.description: Alphanumeric code identifying the manufacturing site or plant to which this location belongs (e.g., 'PLT01', 'SITE-DE-01'). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA Plant code (MM/PM module) and Maximo site identifier for cross-system integration and multi-site OEE benchmarking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Plant; Maximo Asset Management - Site

attributes.name: site_name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the manufacturing site or plant (e.g., 'Stuttgart Automation Plant', 'Detroit Assembly Facility'). Used in reporting, safety documentation, and regulatory submissions to identify the physical facility.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Plant; ISO 9001 Quality Management

attributes.name: timezone
attributes.description: IANA time zone identifier for the location (e.g., 'Europe/Berlin', 'America/Detroit'). Critical for accurate timestamp interpretation of SCADA/MES events, shift scheduling, maintenance work order timing, and cross-site production reporting across time zones.
attributes.references: IANA Time Zone Database; ISO 8601 Date and Time; Aveva SCADA Time Synchronization

attributes.name: work_center_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA or Opcenter MES work center code associated with this functional location. Enables direct linkage between the physical location and production planning, capacity scheduling, and cost center allocation for manufacturing execution analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Work Center; Siemens Opcenter MES - Work Center

products.name: asset_work_order
products.description: Maintenance work order record capturing all maintenance activity types executed against equipment assets: corrective, preventive, predictive, condition-based, emergency, and shutdown/turnaround maintenance. Tracks work order type, priority, TPM pillar classification, originating failure code, assigned craft/trade, planned vs actual labor hours, planned vs actual material costs, spare parts reserved and consumed (part number, quantity, issue date, cost), start/finish dates, downtime duration, and completion status. For shutdown-type work orders, additionally captures scope of work summary, affected production lines, shutdown coordinator, planned start/end dates, and estimated total cost. Core transactional entity sourced from Maximo Work Management module and SAP PM orders. Serves as the SSOT for all maintenance execution activity.
products.reference: Maximo Work Order / SAP PM Order

attributes.name: asset_work_order_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the maintenance work order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Management

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Work Order Billing & SLA: associates work orders with the requesting customer to allocate costs and measure SLA performance per account.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Work orders on customer-installed equipment require the specific customer site for field service dispatch, site access coordination, and site-specific safety requirements. A customer may have multiple

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Work orders executed under contracted maintenance service agreements (OEM contracts, third-party service contracts) must reference the governing agreement for billing validation, SLA compliance tracki

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: COSTING: Work order expenses are charged to a cost center to track production cost and variance.

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_contact. Business justification: Field service work orders require a customer contact for site access coordination, work approval sign-off, and completion notification — standard field service management practice in industrial manufa

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Implementation work orders must reference the specific engineering revision they are executing (ECO/ECN driven change).

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical equipment or asset against which this work order is executed. Links to the asset master record in the asset management domain (e.g., CNC machine, PLC, robotic cell, HVAC unit).
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Asset Registry; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: fixed_asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: CAPEX work orders that improve or modify capitalized equipment must reference the fixed_asset record to drive asset value updates and capitalize-vs-expense decisions. asset_work_order already has cape

attributes.name: job_plan_id
attributes.description: Reference to the maintenance plan or strategy record under which this work order was generated. Links to the strategic maintenance plan defining the maintenance approach for the asset class.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM - Maintenance Plans; Maximo Asset Management - PM

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the functional location or plant area where the asset resides and the maintenance work is performed (e.g., production line, bay, building). Corresponds to LOCATION in Maximo and Functional Location in SAP PM.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Locations; SAP S/4HANA PM - Functional Location

attributes.name: parent_work_order_asset_work_order_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing identifier linking this work order to a parent work order, used for shutdown/turnaround scope decomposition or hierarchical work order structures. Null for top-level work orders.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA PM - Order Hierarchy

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: Execution of a Planned Order creates a Work Order; required for the 'Planned Order Execution' report linking production planning to execution.

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Maintenance work orders raised due to equipment failure during production are linked to the triggering production work order for cost allocation, OEE impact analysis, and production loss attribution —

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Work orders are cost‑tracked and reported under the engineering project that originated the change.

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Work orders on customer equipment are governed by SLA agreements (initial_response_time_hours, resolution_time_hours, field_service_included). Direct link enables automated SLA response time complianc

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Outsourced maintenance work orders require linking to the external service vendor for cost tracking and compliance.

attributes.name: actual_finish_date
attributes.description: Actual date and time when maintenance execution was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used for MTTR calculation, downtime reporting, and OEE analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Actuals; ISO 14224

attributes.name: actual_labor_cost
attributes.description: Actual cost of labor incurred for executing this work order, calculated from actual labor hours and applicable craft rates. Used for total maintenance cost reporting and OpEx analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Labor Cost; SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: actual_labor_hours
attributes.description: Total labor hours actually expended by all technicians in executing this work order. Captured from time reporting in Maximo or Workday. Used for cost variance analysis and workforce productivity reporting.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Labor Actuals; Workday HCM

attributes.name: actual_material_cost
attributes.description: Actual cost of spare parts and materials consumed during execution of this work order, in the local operating currency. Sourced from inventory issue transactions in Maximo/SAP MM. Used for maintenance cost variance and OpEx reporting.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Cost Actuals; SAP S/4HANA MM; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: actual_start_date
attributes.description: Actual date and time when maintenance execution commenced on this work order. Captured by the technician via MES or Maximo mobile. Used for MTTR calculation and schedule adherence analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Actuals; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: affected_production_lines
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or description of production lines impacted by this work order's execution. Applicable primarily for shutdown/turnaround and emergency work orders. Used for production scheduling coordination.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES - Production Scheduling; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: asset_criticality
attributes.description: Criticality classification of the asset at the time this work order was created, indicating the business impact of asset failure. Drives maintenance prioritization and resource allocation. Derived from the asset master criticality assessment.
attributes.references: ISO 55001 Asset Management; FMEA Methodology; ANSI/SMRP Best Practices

attributes.name: capex_opex_classification
attributes.description: Financial classification of the work order costs as either Capital Expenditure (CapEx) for asset improvements/replacements or Operational Expenditure (OpEx) for routine maintenance and repairs. Critical for financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.
attributes.references: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP ASC 360; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: completion_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the outcome or resolution of the work order upon completion (e.g., repaired, replaced, adjusted, no fault found, deferred). Used for failure analysis and maintenance effectiveness reporting. Corresponds to ACTFINISH resolution codes in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Completion Codes; ISO 14224

attributes.name: completion_remarks
attributes.description: Free-text technician notes documenting the work performed, findings, parts replaced, and any follow-up actions recommended upon work order completion. Captured in Maximo WORKLOG or SAP PM notification.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Log; ISO 9001 Quality Records

attributes.name: craft_type
attributes.description: The maintenance craft or trade skill classification required and assigned to execute this work order (e.g., Electrician, Millwright, Instrumentation Technician, Welder, Pipefitter). Corresponds to CRAFT in Maximo Labor records.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Craft Management; ANSI/SMRP Best Practices

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this work order record was first captured in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and governance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Record Control; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary cost fields on this work order (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency reporting for global manufacturing operations.

attributes.name: downtime_duration_hours
attributes.description: Total production downtime in hours caused by the failure or maintenance activity associated with this work order. Core input for OEE availability calculation and MTTR analysis. Null if the work order did not cause production downtime.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing; OEE Methodology; ANSI/SMRP Best Practices

attributes.name: failure_code
attributes.description: Standardized failure classification code identifying the root cause or failure mode that originated this work order. Used for FMEA analysis, MTBF/MTTR trending, and CAPA tracking. Corresponds to FAILURECODE in Maximo and Cause Code in SAP PM.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Codes; ISO 14224 Reliability Data; FMEA Methodology

attributes.name: failure_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative describing the observed failure, fault symptom, or maintenance trigger that initiated this work order. Captured by the originating technician or operator at time of request.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Description; SAP S/4HANA PM

attributes.name: is_production_impacting
attributes.description: Indicates whether this work order caused or required a production stoppage or capacity reduction. True = production was impacted; False = maintenance performed without production impact (e.g., running maintenance). Key filter for OEE and availability reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing; OEE Methodology

attributes.name: long_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative of the scope of work, maintenance instructions, safety precautions, and special requirements for executing this work order. Corresponds to LDTEXT in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Long Description; OSHA Safety Requirements

attributes.name: ncr_reference
attributes.description: Reference number of any Non-Conformance Report (NCR) or CAPA record linked to this work order, where the maintenance activity was triggered by or resulted in a quality non-conformance event.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Nonconformity and Corrective Action; CAPA Methodology

attributes.name: planned_finish_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date and time when execution of this work order is planned to be completed. Used for production downtime planning and resource scheduling. Corresponds to SCHEDFINISH in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PM

attributes.name: planned_labor_hours
attributes.description: Total labor hours estimated and planned for executing this work order across all assigned crafts. Used for resource capacity planning, scheduling, and budget estimation. Corresponds to ESTLABHRS in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Labor Planning; ANSI/SMRP Best Practices

attributes.name: planned_material_cost
attributes.description: Estimated cost of spare parts and materials planned for this work order, in the local operating currency. Used for maintenance budget planning and CapEx/OpEx classification. Corresponds to ESTMATCOST in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Cost Management; IFRS/GAAP - IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

attributes.name: planned_start_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date and time when execution of this work order is planned to begin. Used for maintenance scheduling, resource planning, and production coordination. Corresponds to SCHEDSTART in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PM

attributes.name: pm_schedule_id
attributes.description: Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule master record that generated this work order, if applicable. Null for corrective or emergency work orders. Corresponds to PM record in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - PM Schedules; ISO 55001

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Priority ranking assigned to the work order indicating urgency and scheduling precedence. Priority 1 (Emergency) requires immediate response; Priority 5 (Low) is deferred maintenance. Drives scheduling in APS and resource allocation decisions.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Priority Codes; ISO 55001

attributes.name: reported_date
attributes.description: Date and time when the maintenance need was first reported or the work order was created in the source system. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Corresponds to REPORTDATE in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Management

attributes.name: safety_permit_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the safety permit (e.g., LOTO permit, hot work permit) issued for this work order. Null if no permit is required. Supports OSHA compliance audit trails.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 Lockout/Tagout; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

attributes.name: safety_permit_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a safety permit (e.g., Lockout/Tagout, hot work permit, confined space entry permit) is required before executing this work order. Drives safety compliance workflow in Maximo. Mandatory for OSHA and ISO 45001 compliance.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 Lockout/Tagout; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

attributes.name: shutdown_scope_summary
attributes.description: For shutdown or turnaround-type work orders only: a structured summary of the full scope of work to be executed during the planned outage. Null for non-shutdown work orders.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Shutdown Management; SMRP Best Practices

attributes.name: total_estimated_cost
attributes.description: Total estimated cost for this work order encompassing planned labor, materials, and any additional service costs. Used for maintenance budget approval workflows and CapEx authorization. Corresponds to ESTLABCOST + ESTMATCOST + ESTSERVCOST in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Cost Estimation; IFRS/GAAP - IAS 16; CapEx Authorization

attributes.name: tpm_pillar
attributes.description: Classification of the work order under the applicable Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) pillar framework. Enables TPM program performance tracking and OEE improvement analysis. Null if the work order is not part of a formal TPM program.
attributes.references: TPM Methodology - JIPM; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this work order record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Record Control; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: work_order_description
attributes.description: Short descriptive title or summary of the maintenance task to be performed. Displayed on work order printouts and shop floor scheduling boards. Corresponds to DESCRIPTION in Maximo WORKORDER table.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Management

attributes.name: work_order_number
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable work order number assigned by the source system (Maximo or SAP PM). Used for cross-system reference, shop floor communication, and audit trails. Corresponds to WONUM in Maximo and Order Number in SAP PM.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: work_order_source
attributes.description: System or channel through which this work order was originated. Enables source system traceability and analysis of maintenance demand drivers (e.g., SCADA alarm-triggered vs. manually created vs. IoT condition-based alert). attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or employee identifier of the maintenance manager or engineering authority who approved this PM schedule for execution. Required for regulatory compliance and quality management system documentation.

attributes.name: approved_date

attributes.name: asset_pm_schedule_description

attributes.name: condition_threshold_value
attributes.description: Numeric threshold value for condition-based PM triggering. When the monitored parameter (e.g., vibration amplitude, temperature, oil viscosity) exceeds this value, a PM work order is generated. Applicable when trigger_type is CONDITION.

attributes.name: created_timestamp

attributes.name: crew_size

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated_material_cost field (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date after which this PM schedule is no longer active and will not generate new work orders. Null for open-ended schedules. Used when an asset is decommissioned or a maintenance program is superseded.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which this PM schedule becomes active and eligible to generate work orders. Supports phased rollout of new maintenance programs and asset commissioning workflows.

attributes.name: estimated_downtime_hours
attributes.description: Estimated number of hours the asset will be unavailable for production during execution of this PM task. Used in OEE calculations, production scheduling, and downtime planning.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — PM Downtime; OEE Framework; ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing

attributes.name: estimated_duration_hours
attributes.description: Estimated number of labor hours required to complete the preventive maintenance task. Used for maintenance scheduling, craft resource planning, and downtime window estimation.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — PM Estimated Duration; SAP S/4HANA PM Order Duration

attributes.name: estimated_material_cost
attributes.description: Estimated cost of materials and spare parts required to execute this PM task, in the local operating currency. Used for maintenance budget planning and CapEx/OpEx cost allocation.

attributes.name: frequency_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the PM recurrence frequency. Combined with frequency_value to define the full interval (e.g., 30 DAYS, 500 HOURS, 10000 CYCLES). Determines whether the schedule is calendar-based or meter-based. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAYS|WEEKS|MONTHS|YEARS|HOURS|CYCLES|KILOMETERS — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — PM Frequency Unit; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2

attributes.name: frequency_value
attributes.description: Numeric value representing how often the PM task should recur. Interpreted in conjunction with frequency_unit (e.g., a value of 30 with unit DAYS means every 30 days; a value of 500 with unit HOURS means every 500 operating hours).
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — PM Frequency; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2

attributes.name: is_critical_for_pm
attributes.description: Indicates whether this spare part is critical for PM execution — i.e., the PM task cannot be safely or effectively completed without this part being available. Drives pre-staging and kitting priority decisions.

attributes.name: is_regulatory_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether this PM schedule is mandated by a regulatory body (e.g., OSHA, EPA, UL, CE Marking authority) and must be completed and documented for compliance purposes. Drives audit trail requirements.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; EPA Regulations; UL Certification Requirements; CE Marking Directive

attributes.name: is_safety_critical
attributes.description: Indicates whether this PM schedule is classified as safety-critical, meaning non-execution could result in personnel injury, environmental incident, or regulatory violation. Safety-critical PMs require mandatory completion tracking.
attributes.references: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 General Industry Standards

attributes.name: last_completion_date
attributes.description: Date on which the most recent execution of this PM schedule was completed and the corresponding work order was closed. Used as the baseline for calculating the next due date.

attributes.name: last_revised_date
attributes.description: Date on which this PM schedule definition was last revised or updated. Supports document control, audit trails, and change management processes.

attributes.name: lead_time_buffer_days
attributes.description: Number of additional buffer days beyond the spare part's standard lead time that the planner has specified for this PM-part combination. Accounts for PM-specific procurement complexity or criticality. Belongs to the relationship, not to the part master or PM schedule alone.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Number of days before the next_due_date that the PM work order should be generated to allow sufficient time for parts procurement, scheduling, and preparation. Supports JIT maintenance planning.

attributes.name: maintenance_type
attributes.description: Classification of the maintenance activity type. PREVENTIVE: time/usage-based task to prevent failure. PREDICTIVE: condition-monitoring-driven task. INSPECTION: regulatory or quality inspection. LUBRICATION: lubrication task. CALIBRATION: instrument calibration. OVERHAUL: major scheduled overhaul.

attributes.name: meter_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the meter reading used in meter-based PM scheduling (e.g., HOURS for operating hours, CYCLES for press strokes, KILOMETERS for vehicle mileage). Null when trigger_type is CALENDAR only.

attributes.name: next_due_date
attributes.description: Calculated date on which the next preventive maintenance work order is due to be generated or executed. Derived from last_completion_date plus the frequency interval. Drives maintenance scheduling and resource planning.

attributes.name: next_due_meter_reading

attributes.name: priority

attributes.name: quantity_required

attributes.name: regulatory_reference

attributes.name: revision_number

attributes.name: schedule_name

attributes.name: sequence_in_job_plan

attributes.name: tolerance_days

attributes.name: tpm_pillar

attributes.name: job_plan_description

attributes.name: job_plan_number

attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 - General Industry Safety; Maximo Job Plan Crew Size

attributes.name: required_tools
attributes.description: List of specialized tools, equipment, and instruments required to execute this job plan (e.g., torque wrench, vibration analyzer, multimeter, alignment laser, hydraulic jack). Used for tool reservation and kitting in the CMMS.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Tools; SAP PM Task List Tools

attributes.name: revision_number
attributes.description: Version or revision identifier of the job plan, incremented each time the procedure is formally updated (e.g., REV-01, REV-02). Supports change control and audit traceability per ISO 9001 document control requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5 - Documented Information; Maximo Job Plan Revision

attributes.name: safety_precautions
attributes.description: Narrative description of mandatory safety precautions, lockout/tagout (LOTO) requirements, personal protective equipment (PPE), and hazard controls that must be observed before and during execution of this job plan. Compliance with OSHA and ISO 45001 requirements.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 - Lockout/Tagout; ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; ANSI Z244.1

attributes.name: shutdown_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether execution of this job plan requires a planned production shutdown or equipment isolation. Used for maintenance window scheduling and production planning coordination to minimize OEE impact.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Shutdown Required; OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness); ISO 55001

attributes.name: site_code
attributes.description: Plant or facility site code where this job plan is applicable. Enables site-specific job plan management in multi-site manufacturing operations. Aligns with SAP Plant code and Maximo Site.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Site; SAP S/4HANA Plant Code; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Operational system of record from which this job plan record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer. MAXIMO = IBM Maximo Asset Management; SAP_PM = SAP Plant Maintenance; MANUAL = manually entered; TEAMCENTER = Siemens Teamcenter PLM; OPCENTER = Siemens Opcenter MES.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM - Data Lineage; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Governance

attributes.name: source_system_key
attributes.description: Natural key or identifier of this job plan record in the originating source system (e.g., Maximo Job Plan Number, SAP PM Task List Number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM - Source System Key; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage

attributes.name: spare_parts_list
attributes.description: Narrative or structured list of spare parts and materials required for this job plan, including part numbers and quantities (e.g., 'Bearing SKF 6205-2RS x2, O-Ring Kit x1, Filter Element x1'). Used for pre-kitting and MRP-driven spare parts reservation.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Materials; SAP MM Material Requirements Planning (MRP); SAP PM Planned Materials

attributes.name: task_step_count
attributes.description: Total number of discrete task steps defined within this job plan. Provides a quick indicator of procedure complexity and is used for work order progress tracking in Maximo and Siemens Opcenter MES.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Tasks; SAP PM Task List Operations

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Human-readable descriptive title of the job plan summarizing the maintenance activity (e.g., 'Annual Preventive Maintenance - CNC Spindle Assembly', 'Hydraulic System Inspection'). Used in work order templates and PM schedules.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Job Plan Description; SAP PM Task List Short Text

attributes.name: work_category
attributes.description: Trade or discipline category of the maintenance work defined in this job plan. Used to route work orders to the correct craft group and for workforce planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MECHANICAL|ELECTRICAL|INSTRUMENTATION|CIVIL|HVAC|HYDRAULIC|PNEUMATIC|LUBRICATION|AUTOMATION|WELDING — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Category; SAP PM Work Center

attributes.name: work_center
attributes.description: Maintenance work center or department responsible for executing this job plan (e.g., MAINT-MECH-01, MAINT-ELEC-02). Maps to SAP PM Work Center and Maximo Labor Group for capacity planning and cost center assignment.
attributes.references: SAP PM Work Center; Maximo Asset Management - Labor Group; SAP CO Cost Center

attributes.name: work_order_type
attributes.description: Default work order type that should be generated when this job plan is applied. PM = Preventive Maintenance; CM = Corrective Maintenance; EM = Emergency Maintenance; INSPECTION = Inspection only; OVERHAUL = Major overhaul; PROJECT = Capital project work.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Type; SAP PM Order Type

products.name: failure_record
products.description: Failure event record capturing equipment breakdowns and fault occurrences, including failure date/time, failure class, failure mode code, failure cause code, remedy code, affected component, downtime start/end, production impact (units lost), and MTBF contribution data. Embeds the full failure mode classification taxonomy: failure mode description, affected component class, potential cause, detection method, severity rating, occurrence rating, detection rating, and RPN (Risk Priority Number) derived from FMEA/PFMEA analysis. Serves as both the transactional failure event record and the reference catalog for standardized failure modes per equipment class. Feeds MTBF/MTTR reliability analysis, FMEA validation, and maintenance strategy selection. Sourced from Maximo Failure Reporting and SAP PM Notifications.
products.reference: Maximo Failure Report / SAP PM Notification

attributes.name: failure_record_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each failure event record in the system. Primary key for the failure_record data product.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Reporting; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Failure records on customer-installed equipment must be traceable to the customer account for SLA breach reporting, warranty claim processing, and customer-facing incident notifications — a core field

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Failure analysis and PFMEA traceability: when a component fails, engineers must trace back to the engineering component record to update PFMEA risk ratings and trigger ECOs. `affected_component_code`

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Failure records in industrial manufacturing must be linked to the governing service or warranty contract for warranty claim processing, SLA breach penalty calculation, and MTBF-based contract performa

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical asset or equipment that experienced the failure event. Core foreign key linking the failure record to the asset master in the asset management domain.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Asset Master; ISO 55001:2014 Asset Management

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.location. Business justification: Failure events occur at a specific physical location. The failure_record currently stores functional_location_code as a STRING, which is a denormalized reference to the location hierarchy. Adding a pr

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production line or work center where the failed asset is installed and where the failure event occurred. Enables production impact analysis by line and supports OEE reporting at the line level.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES - Work Center; SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Work Center; ISO 22400-2:2014

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Equipment failures during production are linked to the active work order for scrap attribution, warranty claims, and production loss costing. Industrial manufacturing FMEA processes require tracing fa

attributes.name: rma_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_rma. Business justification: Warranty claim initiation: field equipment failures documented in failure_record drive warranty RMA creation. In industrial manufacturing, the failure record is the source document for warranty claims

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Failure records must attribute supplier-caused failures (defective components, substandard materials) to the responsible supplier. Supplier quality teams use this link to initiate Supplier Corrective

attributes.name: asset_operating_hours_at_failure
attributes.description: Total cumulative operating hours recorded on the asset at the time of the failure event. Used as a key input for reliability analysis, MTBF trending, and predictive maintenance model training.
attributes.references: ISO 14224:2016 Reliability Data Collection; Maximo Asset Management - Meter Readings; IEC 60300-3-1

attributes.name: capa_reference_number
attributes.business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Reference Number
attributes.description: The reference number of the formal CAPA record initiated in response to this failure event. Populated when capa_required_flag is True. Enables traceability between the failure event and the quality management corrective action process.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Quality Notification

attributes.name: capa_required_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) process must be initiated as a result of this failure event. True if CAPA is required; False otherwise. Driven by RPN threshold, safety impact, or quality management system requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 Nonconformity and Corrective Action; IEC 60812 FMEA; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time at which this failure record was first created in the system of record. Serves as the audit creation timestamp, distinct from the actual failure event time. Used for data lineage and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Maximo Asset Management Audit Trail

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the repair_cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global manufacturing sites.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: detection_method_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating how the failure was detected (e.g., operator observation, automated alarm, predictive sensor alert, scheduled inspection, SCADA alarm, condition monitoring). Sourced from FMEA detection method taxonomy and Aveva SCADA alarm data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operator_observation|scada_alarm|condition_monitoring|scheduled_inspection|predictive_alert|customer_report|automated_sensor — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IEC 60812 FMEA; Aveva SCADA - Alarm Management; ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: detection_rating
attributes.description: Numeric detection rating (1-10 scale) from the FMEA/PFMEA analysis indicating the ability of current controls to detect the failure cause or mode before it reaches the customer or causes production impact. A rating of 10 represents the lowest detectability. Used in RPN calculation.
attributes.references: IEC 60812 FMEA; AIAG FMEA Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015; PFMEA Standard

attributes.name: downtime_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total duration of equipment downtime in minutes resulting from this failure event, calculated as the difference between downtime_end_datetime and downtime_start_datetime. Stored as a business-captured field from the source system (Maximo) rather than derived at query time. Feeds OEE, MTTR, and production impact analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Downtime Tracking; ISO 22400-2:2014 OEE KPIs; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: downtime_end_datetime
attributes.description: The precise date and time at which the equipment was restored to operational status following the failure event. Used in conjunction with downtime_start_datetime to calculate total downtime duration and MTTR. Null if equipment has not yet been restored.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Downtime Tracking; ISO 22400-2:2014 OEE KPIs; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: downtime_start_datetime
attributes.description: The precise date and time at which the equipment became unavailable for production due to this failure event. Used to calculate total downtime duration and OEE impact. Feeds MTTR analysis and production loss quantification.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Downtime Tracking; ISO 22400-2:2014 OEE KPIs; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: environmental_incident_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this failure event resulted in an environmental incident such as a spill, emission exceedance, or hazardous material release. True if an environmental event occurred; False otherwise. Triggers EPA and ISO 14001 reporting obligations.
attributes.references: ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management; EPA Environmental Regulations; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: failure_cause_code
attributes.description: Standardized code identifying the root cause of the failure event (e.g., inadequate lubrication, overload, material defect, operator error, design deficiency). Sourced from the Maximo Failure Cause catalog and used in CAPA and FMEA root cause analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Cause; IEC 60812 FMEA; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 CAPA; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: failure_cause_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the identified root cause of the failure, providing narrative context for the failure_cause_code. Supports CAPA documentation and FMEA validation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; IEC 60812 FMEA; Maximo Asset Management - Failure Cause Catalog

attributes.name: failure_class_code
attributes.description: Standardized classification code categorizing the type of failure at the highest level of the failure taxonomy (e.g., mechanical, electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, software, structural). Sourced from the Maximo Failure Class hierarchy and aligned with ISO 14224 failure classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|pneumatic|software|structural|thermal|instrumentation|process — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Class; ISO 14224:2016 Reliability Data Collection; IEC 60812 FMEA

attributes.name: failure_class_description
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the failure class, providing context for the failure_class_code. Used in maintenance reports, FMEA documentation, and reliability dashboards.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Class; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: failure_datetime
attributes.description: The precise date and time at which the equipment failure or fault was detected or reported. This is the principal real-world event timestamp for the failure record, distinct from the record creation audit timestamp. Used as the anchor for MTBF calculations and downtime duration derivation.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Reporting; SAP S/4HANA PM Notification; ISO 14224:2016 Reliability Data Collection

attributes.name: failure_impact_type
attributes.description: Classification of the operational impact of the failure event on production and safety. 'production_stoppage' indicates complete halt of production; 'partial_degradation' indicates reduced throughput or capacity; 'quality_impact' indicates output quality was affected; 'safety_incident' indicates a safety hazard was created; 'no_production_impact' indicates the failure was detected without affecting output.
attributes.references: ISO 14224:2016; ISO 22400-2:2014 OEE KPIs; ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety

attributes.name: failure_mode_code
attributes.description: Standardized code identifying the specific manner in which the equipment failed (e.g., wear, fracture, corrosion, overheating, short circuit, vibration). Sourced from the Maximo Failure Mode catalog and aligned with FMEA/PFMEA failure mode taxonomy. Feeds FMEA validation and maintenance strategy selection.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Mode; IEC 60812 FMEA; ISO 14224:2016; SAP S/4HANA PM Catalog

attributes.name: failure_mode_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the failure mode, explaining how the equipment deviated from its intended function. Embedded from the FMEA/PFMEA failure mode classification taxonomy. Used in reliability analysis and maintenance strategy documentation.
attributes.references: IEC 60812 FMEA; ISO 14224:2016; Maximo Asset Management - Failure Mode Catalog

attributes.name: failure_narrative
attributes.description: Free-text narrative description of the failure event as recorded by the reporting technician or operator. Captures contextual details not covered by structured codes, including observed symptoms, environmental conditions, and sequence of events. Used in root cause analysis and knowledge base enrichment.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Reporting Long Description; SAP S/4HANA PM Notification Long Text; ISO 14224:2016

attributes.name: failure_number
attributes.description: Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this failure event record. Used in maintenance reports, CAPA documentation, and cross-system references. Follows the format FR-XXXXXXXXXX.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Failure Reporting; SAP S/4HANA PM Notification Number

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time at which this failure record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest update for change management, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Maximo Asset Management Audit Trail

attributes.name: maintenance_type
attributes.description: Classification of the maintenance response triggered by this failure event. 'corrective' indicates planned corrective maintenance; 'emergency' indicates unplanned breakdown response; 'predictive' indicates maintenance triggered by condition monitoring prediction; 'preventive' indicates a failure discovered during scheduled PM; 'condition_based' indicates maintenance triggered by condition threshold breach.
attributes.references: ISO 14224:2016; EN 13306:2017 Maintenance Terminology; Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Type; TPM Methodology

attributes.name: mtbf_contribution_hours
attributes.business_glossary_term: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) Contribution Hours
attributes.description: The operating hours accumulated on the asset since the previous failure event of the same failure class, contributing to the MTBF calculation for this asset and failure class combination. Stored as a raw business input value from Maximo rather than an aggregated metric. Feeds reliability analysis and TPM strategy.
attributes.references: ISO 14224:2016 Reliability Data Collection; IEC 60300-3-1 Dependability Analysis; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: ncr_reference_number
attributes.description: The reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised in the quality management system as a result of this failure event. Links the failure record to the formal quality non-conformance documentation in SAP QM.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7 Control of Nonconforming Outputs; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; IPC Standards

attributes.name: occurrence_rating
attributes.description: Numeric occurrence rating (1-10 scale) from the FMEA/PFMEA analysis indicating the likelihood or frequency of the failure cause occurring. A rating of 10 represents the highest probability of occurrence. Used in RPN calculation and preventive maintenance scheduling.
attributes.references: IEC 60812 FMEA; AIAG FMEA Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015; PFMEA Standard

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the failure event occurred. Enables geographic and site-level failure analysis and supports multi-plant reliability benchmarking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Plant; ISO 55001:2014 Asset Management

attributes.name: potential_failure_effect
attributes.description: Description of the potential effect or consequence of the failure mode on the process, product quality, equipment, or safety. Embedded from the FMEA failure mode classification taxonomy. Used in FMEA validation and maintenance strategy documentation.
attributes.references: IEC 60812 FMEA; AIAG FMEA Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015

attributes.name: production_units_lost
attributes.description: Estimated number of production units (parts, assemblies, or output units) lost due to this failure event and associated downtime. Quantifies the direct production impact for OEE performance loss analysis and financial impact assessment.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES - Production Reporting; ISO 22400-2:2014 OEE KPIs; SAP S/4HANA PP Module

attributes.name: record_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the failure record workflow. 'open' indicates the failure has been reported but not yet actioned; 'in_progress' indicates active remediation; 'closed' indicates the failure has been resolved and verified; 'cancelled' indicates the record was voided; 'pending_review' indicates awaiting quality or engineering sign-off.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Work Order Status; SAP S/4HANA PM Notification Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: remedy_code
attributes.description: Standardized code identifying the corrective action or remedy applied to resolve the failure (e.g., replacement, repair, adjustment, cleaning, lubrication, recalibration). Sourced from the Maximo Remedy catalog and SAP PM catalog codes.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Remedy Catalog; SAP S/4HANA PM Catalog; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: remedy_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the corrective action taken to resolve the failure event. Provides narrative context for the remedy_code and supports maintenance knowledge base and CAPA documentation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Maximo Asset Management - Remedy Catalog

attributes.name: repair_cost
attributes.description: Total cost incurred to remediate this failure event, including labor, spare parts, and external contractor costs. Expressed in the local operating currency. Used for maintenance cost analysis, CapEx vs OpEx classification, and total cost of ownership (TCO) reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Cost Management; IFRS/GAAP Asset Maintenance Accounting; Maximo Asset Management - Cost Tracking

attributes.name: risk_priority_number
attributes.description: Risk Priority Number derived from FMEA/PFMEA analysis, calculated as the product of severity_rating × occurrence_rating × detection_rating (range 1-1000). Stored as a business-captured value from the FMEA source record rather than computed at query time. Used to prioritize corrective actions and maintenance strategy selection.
attributes.references: IEC 60812 FMEA; AIAG FMEA Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; PFMEA Standard

attributes.name: safety_incident_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this failure event resulted in or contributed to a safety incident, near-miss, or occupational hazard. True if a safety event occurred; False otherwise. Triggers mandatory OSHA/ISO 45001 reporting workflows and CAPA initiation.
attributes.references: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 General Industry Standards; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2

attributes.name: severity_rating
attributes.description: Numeric severity rating (1-10 scale) from the FMEA/PFMEA analysis indicating the seriousness of the failure effect on the product, process, or safety. A rating of 10 represents the most severe impact (safety hazard or regulatory non-compliance). Used in RPN calculation and maintenance strategy prioritization.
attributes.references: IEC 60812 FMEA; AIAG FMEA Manual 4th Edition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; PFMEA Standard

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this failure record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer. 'maximo' = IBM Maximo Asset Management; 'sap_pm' = SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance; 'opcenter_mes' = Siemens Opcenter MES; 'scada' = Aveva SCADA; 'manual' = manually entered record.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM - Data Lineage; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Governance

attributes.name: spare_part_consumed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether one or more spare parts were consumed during the remediation of this failure event. True if spare parts were used; False otherwise. Feeds spare parts inventory consumption analysis and reorder planning.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management - Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Materials Management

products.name: asset_downtime_event
products.description: Discrete downtime event record tracking unplanned and planned equipment stoppages on the shop floor, including start timestamp, end timestamp, total duration minutes, downtime category (breakdown, changeover, planned maintenance, tooling, material shortage, quality hold, no operator), affected equipment asset, affected production line, shift context, root cause code, and OEE availability impact percentage. Integrates with MES/SCADA downtime capture systems and manual operator entry. Feeds OEE calculation pipelines, downtime Pareto analysis, and production loss costing. Serves as the SSOT for all equipment stoppage events regardless of cause.
products.reference: Siemens Opcenter MES Downtime / Aveva SCADA Alarm

attributes.name: asset_downtime_event_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each discrete downtime event record. Primary key for the downtime_event data product in the asset domain.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Work Order / Downtime Record

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Downtime events on customer-installed equipment must be reported to the customer account for SLA uptime compliance tracking and customer-facing OEE reporting — a contractual obligation in industrial m

attributes.name: affected_sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: OEE reporting by product SKU: downtime events must be linked to the specific product SKU being produced to calculate availability losses per product, a mandatory KPI in industrial manufacturing. affec

attributes.name: asset_work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Maximo work order created or associated with this downtime event, particularly for corrective or preventive maintenance activities triggered by the stoppage.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Work Order; SAP S/4HANA PM (Plant Maintenance)

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Downtime events must be linked to the governing service contract to calculate SLA uptime compliance, trigger penalty clauses, and generate contract performance reports. The sales_contract has sla_upti

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: LOSS ANALYSIS: Downtime cost allocation requires linking each event to a cost center.

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical equipment or asset that experienced the stoppage. Links to the asset master record (e.g., CNC machine, PLC, robot, conveyor) managed in Maximo Asset Management.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Asset Master; ISO 55000 Asset Management

attributes.name: failure_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: A downtime event is often caused by or associated with a specific failure record. While asset_downtime_event already links to asset_work_order (which links to failure_record), a direct FK from asset_d

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.location. Business justification: Downtime events occur at a specific physical location on the shop floor. asset_downtime_event currently stores plant_code (STRING) as a coarse location reference, but lacks a proper FK to the location

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production line or work center affected by the downtime event. Used for line-level OEE availability analysis and production loss costing.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES — Work Center; SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning)

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SAP production order or MES work order that was interrupted or delayed by this downtime event. Enables production loss costing and schedule impact analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP — Production Order; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Asset downtime events interrupt specific production runs; linking enables run-level OEE availability impact calculation and production loss attribution per run — essential for manufacturing performanc

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production shift during which the downtime event occurred. Enables shift-level downtime analysis and operator accountability reporting.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES — Shift Schedule; SAP S/4HANA PP

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Downtime events directly trigger SLA penalty clause evaluation (uptime_target_pct, penalty_clause_applicable). A customer may have multiple SLA agreements; the direct link to the governing agreement e

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Downtime events caused by supplier-quality failures or late deliveries must be attributed to the responsible supplier for supplier performance reporting, SCAR initiation, and OEE impact analysis — a s

attributes.name: capa_reference
attributes.description: Reference number of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record initiated as a result of this downtime event. Links the downtime event to the quality management system for systematic root cause elimination. Sourced from SAP QM or the plant's CAPA system.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management — CAPA; SAP S/4HANA QM (Quality Management)

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the downtime event record was first created in the system, either automatically by MES/SCADA detection or manually by an operator. Audit trail field. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 9001 Audit Trail

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated_loss_cost field (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency enterprise reporting.

attributes.name: detection_method
attributes.description: Method by which the downtime event was detected and captured. Distinguishes automated detection (SCADA alarm, MES alert, IIoT sensor trigger, predictive maintenance alert) from manual detection (operator report, visual inspection, manual entry). Supports data quality assessment and IIoT maturity tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scada_alarm|mes_alert|operator_report|sensor_trigger|visual_inspection|predictive_alert|manual_entry — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Aveva SCADA; Siemens Opcenter MES; IIoT / Industry 4.0 Standards

attributes.name: downtime_category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the downtime event type. Drives OEE availability loss categorization and Pareto analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: breakdown|planned_maintenance|changeover|tooling|material_shortage|quality_hold|no_operator|utility_failure|scheduled_break|trial_run — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing Operations Management; OEE Industry Standard

attributes.name: downtime_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the downtime event was unplanned (unexpected equipment failure, breakdown) or planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover, tooling change). Critical discriminator for OEE availability calculation — only unplanned downtime reduces OEE availability.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing Operations Management; OEE Industry Standard

attributes.name: duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total elapsed duration of the downtime event in minutes, calculated as the difference between end_timestamp and start_timestamp. Stored as a business field for direct use in OEE availability calculations, Pareto analysis, and production loss costing. Null if event is still open.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing Operations Management; OEE Industry Standard

attributes.name: end_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the equipment was restored to operational status and production resumed. Null if the event is still open/in-progress. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: Aveva SCADA — Alarm/Event Timestamp; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 22400

attributes.name: environmental_impact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this downtime event resulted in an environmental impact (e.g., spill, emission exceedance, energy waste) requiring EPA or ISO 14001 reporting. True = environmental impact occurred.
attributes.references: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency); ISO 14001 Environmental Management

attributes.name: estimated_loss_cost
attributes.description: Estimated financial cost of production loss attributable to this downtime event, expressed in the plant's local currency. Calculated from estimated_production_loss_units multiplied by the standard cost per unit. Used for downtime Pareto cost analysis and CapEx justification.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO — Production Cost; IFRS/GAAP; CapEx/OpEx

attributes.name: estimated_production_loss_units
