attributes.description: Estimated number of production units lost due to this downtime event, calculated based on the equipment's standard throughput rate and the downtime duration. Used for production loss costing and OEE throughput impact analysis.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing Operations Management; OEE — Throughput Loss

attributes.name: event_number
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the downtime event (e.g., DT-2024-000123). Used for cross-system reference, operator communication, and audit trail. Sourced from Maximo or Siemens Opcenter MES downtime capture.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Downtime Record Number; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: event_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the downtime event record. 'open' indicates the stoppage is active; 'in_progress' indicates maintenance is underway; 'resolved' indicates equipment is restored; 'closed' indicates the record is fully documented and approved; 'cancelled' indicates a false or erroneous entry.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Work Order Status; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: failure_class
attributes.description: High-level classification of the failure mode grouping (mechanical, electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, software, instrumentation, structural, operator_error, external). Aligns with Maximo failure class hierarchy and supports FMEA/PFMEA analysis and spare parts categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|pneumatic|software|instrumentation|structural|operator_error|external — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Failure Class; ISO 14224; DFMEA / PFMEA

attributes.name: failure_code
attributes.description: Maximo/CMMS standardized failure code classifying the type of equipment failure (e.g., bearing failure, seal leak, motor burnout, software fault). Distinct from root_cause_code — failure_code describes WHAT failed, root_cause_code describes WHY. Used for MTBF analysis and spare parts planning.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Failure Code Library; ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data

attributes.name: is_repeat_failure
attributes.description: Indicates whether this downtime event is a recurrence of a previously recorded failure on the same asset within a defined lookback period (typically 30 or 90 days). True = repeat failure. Used to trigger CAPA escalation and identify chronic equipment issues.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management — CAPA; Maximo Asset Management; TPM

attributes.name: is_safety_incident
attributes.description: Indicates whether this downtime event was associated with or caused a safety incident (True) or not (False). Triggers mandatory safety reporting workflows and OSHA recordkeeping requirements when True.
attributes.references: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the downtime event record (e.g., root cause update, closure, CAPA linkage). Supports change tracking and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer; ISO 9001 Audit Trail

attributes.name: maintenance_type
attributes.description: Classification of the maintenance activity associated with this downtime event. Distinguishes corrective (reactive repair), preventive (scheduled PM), predictive (condition-triggered), condition-based, and emergency maintenance. Supports TPM and maintenance strategy analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Maintenance Type; ISO 55000; TPM

attributes.name: ncr_reference
attributes.description: Reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) associated with this downtime event, applicable when the stoppage was caused by or resulted in a quality non-conformance (e.g., quality hold, defective material). Links downtime to quality management records.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management — NCR; SAP S/4HANA QM

attributes.name: oee_availability_impact_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) Availability Impact Percentage
attributes.description: The percentage reduction in OEE availability attributable to this specific downtime event, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 4.17 = 4.17%). Calculated as (duration_minutes / planned_production_time_minutes) * 100 for the affected shift/period. Feeds OEE calculation pipelines.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPIs for Manufacturing Operations Management; OEE Industry Standard (Availability = Run Time / Planned Production Time)

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the downtime event occurred. Enables multi-plant downtime benchmarking and OEE comparison across the enterprise.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP/PM — Plant; ISO 55000 Asset Management

attributes.name: plc_fault_code
attributes.description: The fault or error code reported by the Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) or DCS at the time of the downtime event. Provides low-level diagnostic information for maintenance engineers and supports automated fault classification.
attributes.references: IEC 61131 PLC Programming Standards; Aveva SCADA; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: problem_code
attributes.description: Maximo CMMS problem code describing the observed symptom or problem that triggered the downtime event (e.g., vibration, overheating, noise, no output). Part of the Maximo failure reporting triad: problem_code → failure_code → root_cause_code.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Problem Code; ISO 14224 Reliability Data

attributes.name: remedy_code
attributes.description: Maximo CMMS remedy code describing the corrective action taken to restore the equipment (e.g., replaced bearing, adjusted alignment, reset PLC, cleaned filter). Completes the Maximo failure reporting triad and supports maintenance knowledge base and CAPA closure.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Remedy Code; ISO 9001 CAPA; TPM

attributes.name: repair_time_minutes
attributes.description: Elapsed time in minutes from when maintenance work began to when the equipment was restored to operational status. Core component of Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation. Excludes waiting/response time.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Repair Time; MTTR; ISO 55000; TPM

attributes.name: response_time_minutes
attributes.description: Elapsed time in minutes from the downtime event start_timestamp to when a maintenance technician acknowledged or began working on the issue. Component of Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) analysis and maintenance SLA performance tracking.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Response Time; MTTR; ISO 55000; TPM

attributes.name: root_cause_code
attributes.description: Standardized code identifying the root cause of the downtime event (e.g., mechanical failure, electrical fault, operator error, material defect, software fault). Sourced from the plant's root cause taxonomy maintained in Maximo or the CMMS. Used for CAPA initiation and FMEA updates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product with full root cause taxonomy]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Failure Code; ISO 14224 Reliability Data; FMEA / PFMEA

attributes.name: root_cause_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative description of the identified root cause of the downtime event, as entered by the maintenance technician or engineer. Complements the structured root_cause_code with contextual detail for CAPA and FMEA analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Failure Description; ISO 9001 Quality Management; CAPA

attributes.name: safety_incident_reference
attributes.description: Reference number of the associated safety incident report when is_safety_incident is True. Links the downtime event to the EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) management system for OSHA compliance reporting.
attributes.references: OSHA — Incident Reporting; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

attributes.name: source_event_reference
attributes.description: The native identifier of this downtime event in the originating source system (e.g., Maximo work order number, Opcenter MES downtime record ID, SCADA alarm ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Siemens Opcenter MES; Aveva SCADA

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system of record from which this downtime event was captured or originated (e.g., Maximo, Siemens Opcenter MES, Aveva SCADA, manual operator entry, SAP PM). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and integration audit.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Siemens Opcenter MES; Aveva SCADA; SAP S/4HANA PM

attributes.name: spare_parts_used
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or free-text description of spare parts consumed during the repair of this downtime event (e.g., part numbers, descriptions). Used for spare parts consumption tracking, inventory replenishment, and maintenance cost analysis. Detailed parts data is managed in Maximo inventory.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Inventory / Spare Parts; SAP S/4HANA MM

attributes.name: start_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the equipment stoppage began. Principal real-world event timestamp for the downtime event. Captured from SCADA/MES automated detection or manual operator entry. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: Aveva SCADA — Alarm/Event Timestamp; Siemens Opcenter MES; ISO 22400

attributes.name: technician_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for maintenance technician observations, diagnostic findings, and repair actions taken during the downtime event. Captured in Maximo work order long description. Supports knowledge management and future FMEA updates.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Work Order Long Description; ISO 9001

products.name: condition_reading
products.description: Time-series asset measurement record capturing condition monitoring data (vibration, temperature, pressure, oil viscosity, acoustic emission, ultrasound) and cumulative meter/counter readings (runtime hours, cycle counts, production units, energy consumption, distance traveled) from equipment sensors, IIoT gateways, SCADA systems, and manual operator inputs. Stores reading timestamp, sensor/meter identifier, reading type (condition indicator vs cumulative meter), measurement unit, current value, delta since last reading, threshold breach flag, alert severity, and reading source. Serves as the single source of truth for all equipment measurement data — supports predictive maintenance (PdM) trigger logic, condition-based maintenance (CBM), meter-based PM schedule triggering, lubrication route monitoring, and asset utilization rate calculation.
products.reference: IIoT Condition Monitoring / Aveva SCADA Telemetry

attributes.name: condition_reading_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each condition monitoring or meter reading record. Primary key for the condition_reading data product in the asset domain.
attributes.references: ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring and Diagnostics of Machines

attributes.name: asset_downtime_event_id
attributes.description: Reference to a downtime event record if this reading was captured in the context of or immediately preceding an equipment downtime event. Enables correlation of condition readings with downtime causation for MTBF/MTTR analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Siemens Opcenter MES; OEE; MTBF; MTTR

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical asset or equipment from which this reading was captured. Links to the asset master record in Maximo Asset Management.
attributes.references: ISO 55000 Asset Management; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical plant location or functional location where the asset and sensor are installed at the time of reading. Supports geographic and facility-level aggregation of condition data.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management Location Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location

attributes.name: pm_schedule_id
attributes.description: Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule record that was triggered or updated by this reading. Populated when pm_trigger_flag is true.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000 Asset Management

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production line or manufacturing cell on which the asset is operating at the time of reading. Enables correlation of condition readings with production output, OEE, and downtime events.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP; OEE

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Condition readings (vibration, temperature, pressure) captured during production are linked to the active work order for SPC, process quality control, and batch genealogy traceability — a standard req

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Condition readings are captured during specific production runs; run-level traceability is essential for process capability studies, batch genealogy, and identifying which run produced out-of-spec con

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production shift during which this reading was captured. Enables shift-level analysis of equipment condition trends and correlation with operator-specific maintenance practices.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Shift Management

attributes.name: alert_severity
attributes.description: Severity classification assigned to this reading when a threshold breach is detected. 'none' indicates no breach; 'informational' is advisory only; 'warning' requires monitoring; 'critical' requires prompt maintenance action; 'emergency' requires immediate shutdown or intervention. Drives maintenance prioritization and escalation routing.
attributes.references: ISO 13374; Aveva SCADA Alarm Management; IEC 62443; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: ambient_temperature_c
attributes.description: The ambient environmental temperature in degrees Celsius at the measurement location at the time of reading. Used as a contextual covariate for temperature-sensitive condition measurements such as bearing temperature and oil viscosity.

attributes.name: asset_operating_state
attributes.description: The operational state of the asset at the time the reading was captured. Condition readings taken during different operating states (running vs idle vs startup) have different baseline norms and must be segregated for accurate trend analysis and anomaly detection.
attributes.references: ISO 13374; Siemens Opcenter MES; Aveva SCADA; OEE

attributes.name: asset_tag_number
attributes.description: The externally visible physical tag or barcode number affixed to the equipment from which this reading was taken. Provides a human-readable cross-reference to the physical asset in the field, independent of the system surrogate key.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 55000 Asset Management

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: Identifier of the data ingestion batch or pipeline run that loaded this reading into the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, reprocessing, and audit trail for the condition monitoring data pipeline.

attributes.name: calibration_factor
attributes.description: Multiplicative or additive calibration coefficient applied to the raw sensor value to derive the engineering-unit reading value. Supports traceability of measurement accuracy and recalculation when sensor calibration is updated.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management; ISO/IEC 17025 Calibration; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this condition reading record was first created or ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Distinct from reading_timestamp which captures the real-world event time.

attributes.name: data_quality_score
attributes.description: Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the assessed quality and reliability of this reading, computed by the IIoT gateway, SCADA historian, or data ingestion pipeline. Factors include signal noise, transmission completeness, and calibration currency. Used to weight readings in predictive maintenance models.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM Data Quality; ISO 13374; Aveva SCADA

attributes.name: delta_value
attributes.description: The incremental change in the reading value since the previous reading for the same sensor or meter. For cumulative meters, this represents consumption or usage since the last reading (e.g., runtime hours accumulated since last meter read). Used for utilization rate calculation and meter-based PM trigger logic.
attributes.references: ISO 13374; Maximo Asset Management Meter Delta

attributes.name: equipment_class
attributes.description: Classification of the equipment type from which the reading was taken (e.g., rotating_machinery, static_equipment, electrical_panel, cnc_machine, robot, plc, hvac). Enables class-level benchmarking of condition data and FMEA alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rotating_machinery|static_equipment|electrical_panel|cnc_machine|robot|plc|hvac|conveyor|compressor|pump — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 14224 Reliability Data; Maximo Asset Management Classification; FMEA

attributes.name: frequency_band
attributes.description: For vibration and acoustic emission readings, the frequency band or spectral range (e.g., '10-1000 Hz', '1-10 kHz') within which the measurement was taken. Enables frequency-domain analysis for bearing fault detection and rotating machinery diagnostics.

attributes.name: functional_location_code
attributes.description: Alphanumeric code identifying the functional location (plant area, production line, or equipment position) within the facility hierarchy where the reading was taken. Aligns with SAP PM functional location structure and Maximo location hierarchy.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 14224

attributes.name: is_manual_override
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating that the reading value was manually entered or corrected by an operator, overriding an automated sensor value. When true, the original automated value is preserved in raw_value for audit purposes.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001 Quality Management; Aveva SCADA

attributes.name: load_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of rated capacity or load at which the asset was operating when this reading was taken (0.00–100.00). Load normalization is essential for accurate condition trending — the same vibration amplitude at 50% load vs 100% load has different diagnostic significance.

attributes.name: measurement_direction
attributes.description: The physical axis or direction in which the measurement was taken for directional sensors such as vibration accelerometers (axial, radial, tangential) or displacement probes. Null for non-directional measurements such as temperature or pressure.

attributes.name: measurement_type
attributes.description: Specific physical or operational quantity being measured. Examples include vibration, temperature, pressure, oil_viscosity, acoustic_emission, ultrasound, runtime_hours, cycle_count, energy_consumption_kwh, distance_km, production_units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vibration|temperature|pressure|oil_viscosity|acoustic_emission|ultrasound|runtime_hours|cycle_count|energy_consumption|distance|production_units|current|voltage|flow_rate — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 13373 Vibration Condition Monitoring; ISO 13374; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: override_reason
attributes.description: Free-text or coded reason provided by the operator when manually overriding or correcting an automated reading. Required when is_manual_override is true. Supports audit trail and data quality governance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management; Maximo Asset Management; CAPA

attributes.name: pm_trigger_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this reading caused a meter-based or condition-based preventive maintenance (PM) schedule to be triggered in the CMMS. True when the cumulative meter value crossed a PM due-point or a condition indicator breached a CBM trigger threshold.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management PM Scheduling; ISO 55000; TPM

attributes.name: raw_value
attributes.description: The unprocessed, uncalibrated value as received directly from the sensor or IIoT gateway before any engineering unit conversion, calibration offset, or data quality correction is applied. Preserved for audit, recalibration, and data lineage purposes.
attributes.references: Aveva SCADA; IEC 61131; ISO 13374 Data Processing

attributes.name: reading_source
attributes.description: Origin system or method by which the reading was captured. Distinguishes automated IIoT gateway feeds, Aveva SCADA real-time data, Siemens Opcenter MES inputs, manual operator entries, CMMS (Maximo) imports, and energy management system (EMS) feeds. Critical for data lineage and trust scoring.
attributes.references: Aveva SCADA; Siemens Opcenter MES; Maximo Asset Management; IIoT

attributes.name: reading_status
attributes.description: Quality or lifecycle status of the reading record. 'valid' indicates a confirmed good reading; 'suspect' flags a reading that may be erroneous pending review; 'invalid' marks a rejected reading; 'estimated' indicates an interpolated or manually estimated value; 'overridden' indicates the original value was corrected by an operator.
attributes.references: ISO 13374; Maximo Asset Management; Aveva SCADA Data Quality

attributes.name: reading_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the condition measurement or meter reading was recorded. This is the principal business event timestamp used for time-series analysis, predictive maintenance (PdM) trigger logic, and condition-based maintenance (CBM) scheduling.

attributes.name: reading_type
attributes.description: Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a condition indicator (instantaneous measurement such as vibration, temperature, pressure) or a cumulative meter/counter reading (runtime hours, cycle counts, energy consumption, distance traveled). Drives downstream PM trigger logic.
attributes.references: ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring; Maximo Asset Management Meter Types

attributes.name: reading_value
attributes.description: The principal observed numeric measurement or meter count captured at the reading timestamp. For condition indicators this is the instantaneous measured value (e.g., 2.45 mm/s vibration velocity). For cumulative meters this is the absolute odometer-style counter value at the time of reading.
attributes.references: ISO 13374 Condition Monitoring; Maximo Asset Management Meter Readings

attributes.name: sampling_interval_sec
attributes.description: The configured data acquisition interval in seconds at which this sensor or meter is polled or sampled. Used to validate reading frequency, detect missed readings, and calculate expected vs actual data completeness.
attributes.references: Aveva SCADA; IEC 61131; ISO 13374 Data Acquisition

attributes.name: sensor_code
attributes.description: Reference to the specific sensor, transducer, or meter instrument that produced this reading. Identifies the IIoT device, SCADA tag, or manual meter point.
attributes.references: IEC 61131 PLC Programming; Aveva SCADA; ISO 13374

attributes.name: source_system_record_code
attributes.description: The native record identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Maximo meter reading ID, Aveva SCADA historian tag timestamp key, Siemens Opcenter MES event ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the system of record.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM; Maximo Asset Management; Aveva SCADA; ISO 13374

attributes.name: threshold_breached
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the reading value exceeded either the low or high threshold limit at the time of capture. True triggers downstream alert generation and may initiate predictive maintenance (PdM) or corrective maintenance workflows.
attributes.references: ISO 13374; Maximo Asset Management; Aveva SCADA Alarm Management

attributes.name: threshold_high
attributes.description: Upper boundary of the acceptable operating range for this measurement type on this asset. A reading above this value triggers a high-threshold breach flag and may initiate a CBM alert or PdM workflow.
attributes.references: ISO 13374; ISO 13373; Maximo Asset Management Threshold Configuration

attributes.name: threshold_low
attributes.description: Lower boundary of the acceptable operating range for this measurement type on this asset. A reading below this value triggers a low-threshold breach flag and may initiate a condition-based maintenance (CBM) alert or predictive maintenance (PdM) workflow.
attributes.references: ISO 13374; ISO 13373; Maximo Asset Management Threshold Configuration

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Engineering unit associated with the reading value (e.g., mm/s, °C, bar, cSt, dB, kWh, hours, cycles, units, km). Follows SI and industry-standard unit conventions to ensure dimensional consistency across analytics.
attributes.references: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; ANSI; IEC 61131

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this condition reading record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer, for example following a manual override, data quality correction, or status update.

products.name: spare_part
products.description: Spare parts and MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) materials master record specific to asset maintenance, including part number, description, manufacturer part number, OEM compatibility list (linked to equipment classes and specific assets), criticality classification (insurance spares, critical, non-critical), ABC classification for inventory prioritization, reorder point, safety stock level, lead time, unit of measure, storeroom bin location, interchangeability/supersession chain, and shelf life expiry where applicable. Distinct from production inventory — focused on maintenance-specific materials managed in Maximo Inventory and SAP MM-PM integration. Serves as the SSOT for maintenance material identity and stocking parameters.
products.reference: Maximo Item Master / SAP MM Material Master (MRO)

attributes.name: spare_part_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the spare part master record in the Maximo Inventory and SAP MM-PM integration. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: Critical MRO spare parts are governed by the AVL — only approved vendors may supply them. Maintenance planners reference the AVL entry when raising purchase requisitions for spare parts, ensuring comp

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Spare parts are classified into commodity categories for strategic sourcing, MRO spend analysis, and contract coverage reporting. spare_part has mro_category and material_group_code as denormalized si

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Spare‑part inventory matches parts to engineering component definitions for correct replacement and cost estimation.

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Spare part qualification and procurement: spare parts must conform to engineering specifications defining material standards, dimensional tolerances, and qualification requirements. Procurement and qu

attributes.name: fixed_asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: High-value rotable spare parts are capitalized as fixed assets. spare_part has capex_asset_flag confirming this business reality. When capex_asset_flag=true, the spare part must reference its fixed_as

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Spare parts require GL account assignment for inventory valuation and expense posting when consumed. spare_part has valuation_class (denormalized GL mapping signal). Direct FK to gl_account is standar

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Spare parts are stored at a physical location; linking to asset_location eliminates redundant location fields and enables location‑based inventory queries.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: MRO inventory management requires spare parts to reference their material master record for stock availability checks, reorder point management, valuation, and procurement. Maintenance planners always

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: MRO spare parts are sourced under specific procurement contracts governing pricing, lead times, and quality. Linking spare_part to procurement_contract enables contract compliance tracking, spend-unde

attributes.name: product_certification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_certification. Business justification: Regulatory compliance for spare parts: maintenance teams must verify spare parts meet product certifications (CE, ATEX, UL) before installation in certified industrial equipment. This is a mandatory c

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: Spare part revision control: engineering change management requires knowing which product revision a spare part corresponds to, ensuring maintenance uses the correct revision and identifying supersede

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_info_record. Business justification: Purchase info records hold negotiated price, lead time, and vendor material number for a spare part from a specific supplier. MRP controllers and maintenance planners use this to auto-populate PRs and

attributes.name: safety_stock_policy_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supply Safety Stock Policy Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_safety_stock_policy. Business justification: Critical spare parts management: industrial manufacturers maintain centralized safety stock policies for MRO spares tied to equipment criticality. Linking spare_part to supply_safety_stock_policy enab

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Spare parts are sourced from specific supplier sites, not just the supplier entity. Procurement and maintenance planners track the supplying site for lead time, MOQ, and delivery performance reporting

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Enables inventory, procurement and cost tracking by linking each spare part to its SKU definition used in MRP and purchasing.

attributes.name: sourcing_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_rule. Business justification: MRO procurement process: maintenance planners must know which sourcing rule governs spare part replenishment (make-or-buy, preferred supplier, MOQ, lead time). Industrial manufacturing domain experts

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Spare‑part inventory management uses stock_location to locate bins; linking enables accurate stock counts, replenishment planning, and pick‑list generation.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Spare‑part purchasing tracks which vendor supplies each part for inventory valuation and PO generation.

attributes.name: abc_class
attributes.description: ABC classification for inventory prioritization based on annual consumption value. 'A' items represent high-value, high-priority parts requiring tight inventory control; 'B' items are moderate value; 'C' items are low-value, high-volume parts with relaxed control. Aligns with SAP MM ABC Indicator and supports MRP/MRP II planning.

attributes.name: average_annual_consumption
attributes.description: Average quantity of this spare part consumed per year across all work orders and maintenance activities. Used for ABC classification, reorder point calculation, and inventory optimization in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

attributes.name: capex_asset_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this spare part is classified as a capital expenditure (CapEx) item that must be capitalized on the asset register rather than expensed as operational expenditure (OpEx). Drives financial treatment in SAP FI/CO and supports CapEx asset register management.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS IAS 16; GAAP; ISO 55001

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the spare part master record was first created in the source system (Maximo or SAP MM). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle tracking per ISO 9001 document control requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001; Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

attributes.name: criticality_class
attributes.description: Maintenance criticality classification of the spare part. 'insurance_spare' denotes a high-value, long-lead-time part held solely to protect against catastrophic equipment failure; 'critical' denotes parts whose absence would cause significant production downtime; 'non_critical' denotes parts with readily available substitutes or low downtime impact. Drives stocking policy and capital allocation decisions.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard cost and last purchase price fields (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency reporting across global manufacturing plants.

attributes.name: drawing_number
attributes.description: Reference to the engineering drawing or technical document number in Siemens Teamcenter PLM (PDM) that defines the specifications, dimensions, and tolerances for this spare part. Supports design-to-maintenance traceability.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; ISO 9001; ANSI Y14.5

attributes.name: equipment_category
attributes.description: Broad category of production equipment or facility asset for which this spare part is applicable. Supports OEM compatibility list management and enables filtering of applicable parts during maintenance planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cnc_machine|plc|robotics|conveyor|hvac|electrical_panel|pump|motor|sensor|general — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 14224

attributes.name: equipment_class_code
attributes.description: Equipment class or functional location class code identifying the category of production equipment (e.g., CNC machines, PLCs, robotics, conveyors) for which this spare part is applicable. Supports OEM compatibility list management and preventive maintenance (PM) task planning in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 14224

attributes.name: hazardous_material_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this spare part is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, or disposal procedures under OSHA or EPA regulations. Drives safety data sheet (SDS) requirements and storeroom segregation rules.

attributes.name: hazmat_class_code
attributes.description: Regulatory hazardous material classification code (e.g., UN/DOT hazard class) for spare parts flagged as hazardous. Required for compliant storage, transport documentation, and EPA/OSHA reporting. Null when hazardous_material_flag is false.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; EPA; DOT 49 CFR; ISO 45001

attributes.name: interchangeable_part_numbers
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of alternative part numbers that are functionally interchangeable with this spare part. Supports maintenance technician substitution decisions when the primary part is out of stock. Managed in Maximo Item Cross-Reference.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

attributes.name: last_issued_date
attributes.description: Date on which this spare part was most recently issued from the storeroom to a maintenance work order. Used to identify slow-moving or dormant inventory for stock rationalization and obsolescence review.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the spare part master record was most recently updated in the source system. Used for change tracking, delta load processing in the Databricks Silver Layer, and audit compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001; Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA MM; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: last_purchase_price
attributes.description: Most recent purchase price per unit of measure paid to a supplier for this spare part. Sourced from SAP MM purchasing info record (EINA/EINE) or Maximo PO history. Used for budget estimation and procurement benchmarking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; SAP Ariba; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: last_received_date
attributes.description: Date on which the most recent goods receipt was posted for this spare part in the storeroom. Used to track replenishment activity and assess supplier delivery performance.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

attributes.name: maintenance_strategy
attributes.description: Maintenance strategy associated with this spare part, indicating the primary maintenance approach for which the part is consumed. Supports Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) planning and work order scheduling in Maximo and SAP PM.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 55001; TPM

attributes.name: manufacturer_name
attributes.description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or component manufacturer who produces this spare part. Used for sourcing, warranty management, and OEM compatibility validation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: manufacturer_part_number
attributes.description: The Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) or component manufacturer's part number. Used for cross-referencing during procurement, warranty claims, and interchangeability validation. Corresponds to SAP Manufacturer Part Number (MFRPN) and Maximo Manufacturer Part Number field.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management; ANSI

attributes.name: max_stock_qty
attributes.description: Maximum allowable on-hand inventory quantity for this spare part in the storeroom. Used to prevent overstocking and manage storeroom space. Corresponds to SAP MM Maximum Stock Level (MABST) and Maximo Maximum Quantity.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: minimum_order_qty
attributes.description: Minimum quantity that must be ordered per purchase order line for this spare part, as stipulated by the supplier or procurement policy. Corresponds to SAP MM Minimum Order Quantity (MINBE) and Maximo Order Unit.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; SAP Ariba; ANSI

attributes.name: part_description
attributes.description: Long-form technical description of the spare part including functional purpose, specifications, and application context. Supports maintenance technician identification and procurement sourcing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001

attributes.name: part_name
attributes.description: Short descriptive name of the spare part as used in maintenance work orders, purchase requisitions, and storeroom pick lists. Corresponds to SAP Material Short Text (MAKTX) and Maximo Item Description.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: part_number
attributes.description: The internal part number (material number) assigned to the spare part in SAP MM / Maximo Inventory. This is the primary business identifier used across procurement, maintenance work orders, and storeroom operations. Corresponds to SAP Material Number (MATNR) and Maximo Item Number.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management; ANSI

attributes.name: part_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the spare part master record. 'active' indicates the part is in use and available for procurement and work orders; 'obsolete' indicates the part is no longer supported; 'superseded' indicates it has been replaced by another part number; 'pending_approval' indicates the record is under review; 'blocked' indicates procurement or usage is temporarily suspended.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001

attributes.name: part_type
attributes.description: Technical classification of the spare part by engineering discipline. Drives routing to the correct maintenance trade, storeroom zone, and procurement category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|electronic|hydraulic|pneumatic|consumable|structural|instrumentation — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management; IEC 61131

attributes.name: preferred_vendor_number
attributes.description: SAP Vendor Account Number (LIFNR) or SAP Ariba supplier ID of the preferred supplier for this spare part. Used to default the vendor on purchase requisitions and supports supplier performance management.

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the spare part is procured externally from a supplier ('external'), produced internally ('internal'), or both. Corresponds to SAP MM Procurement Type (BESKZ) and drives MRP planning logic.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether this spare part requires a quality inspection upon goods receipt before it can be issued to a work order. Corresponds to SAP QM Inspection Type 01 (Goods Receipt Inspection) and supports ISO 9001 incoming quality control requirements.

attributes.name: replenishment_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Number of calendar days from purchase order placement to receipt of the spare part at the storeroom. Used in reorder point calculation and MRP planning. Corresponds to SAP MM Planned Delivery Time (PLIFZ) and Maximo Lead Time.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001

attributes.name: shelf_life_days
attributes.description: Maximum number of days this spare part can be stored before it expires or degrades below acceptable quality standards. Applicable to consumables, lubricants, seals, gaskets, and chemical materials. Corresponds to SAP MM Total Shelf Life (MHDRZ). Zero or null indicates no shelf life restriction.

attributes.name: standard_cost
attributes.description: Standard cost per unit of measure for the spare part as maintained in SAP MM valuation (moving average or standard price). Used for inventory valuation, work order cost accounting, and CapEx/OpEx reporting. Expressed in the plant's local currency.

attributes.name: superseded_by_part_number
attributes.description: Part number of the replacement spare part that supersedes this record in the interchangeability/supersession chain. Populated when part_status is 'superseded'. Enables maintenance planners to identify the correct current part for work orders.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 9001

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Base unit of measure for the spare part as defined in SAP MM (MEINS) and Maximo. Governs how the part is stocked, issued, and ordered. Common values include EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (litre), M (metre), SET, BOX. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|SET|BOX|ROLL|PAIR — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: warranty_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which the manufacturer's warranty for this spare part expires. Used to trigger warranty claims before expiry and to avoid unnecessary procurement of parts still under warranty coverage.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 9001

attributes.name: weight_kg
attributes.description: Gross weight of the spare part per base unit of measure, expressed in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, freight cost estimation, and storeroom load calculations. Corresponds to SAP MM Gross Weight (BRGEW).

products.name: inspection_event
products.description: Formal equipment inspection event record capturing scheduled and ad-hoc inspections including safety inspections, regulatory compliance inspections (OSHA, UL, CE), and quality-related equipment audits. Stores inspection type, inspector identity, inspection date, checklist results, pass/fail outcome, findings count, corrective action required flag, and next inspection due date.
products.reference: Maximo Inspection / SAP QM Inspection Lot

attributes.name: inspection_event_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the inspection event record in the asset management domain. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Inspections on customer-installed equipment must be reported to the customer account for regulatory compliance certificate delivery and contractual inspection obligation fulfillment — standard practic

attributes.name: asset_work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Maximo work order under which this inspection was executed, linking the inspection event to the broader maintenance or compliance work order lifecycle.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Work Order; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Inspection services in industrial manufacturing are performed under service contracts that define inspection scope, certification requirements, and billing. Linking inspection_event to the governing s

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the financial cost center responsible for the inspection activity. Used for CapEx/OpEx cost allocation of inspection labor and any remediation costs identified during the inspection.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Module — Cost Center; IFRS/GAAP CapEx/OpEx Classification

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: ECN implementation verification: when an ECN is released, affected equipment must be physically inspected to confirm the change was correctly implemented. This inspection-to-ECN traceability is a mand

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Revision implementation verification: inspections confirm that a specific engineering revision has been correctly implemented on physical assets. This link supports as-built configuration audits and r

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical asset, equipment, or facility infrastructure component that is the subject of this inspection event (e.g., CNC machine, PLC, robotic cell, HVAC unit).
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Asset Record; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: failure_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Inspections frequently discover failures or defects that are then recorded as failure records. Linking inspection_event to failure_record enables traceability from inspection finding to formal failure

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the plant, facility, or shop floor location where the inspected asset is installed. Supports geographic and site-level compliance reporting and maintenance planning.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Location Record; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

attributes.name: pm_schedule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_pm_schedule. Business justification: Inspection events are frequently generated from preventive maintenance schedules — a PM schedule of type 'inspection' triggers an inspection_event. Linking inspection_event to asset_pm_schedule enable

attributes.name: product_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Specification-based inspection: inspectors verify equipment condition against product specifications (IP rating, operating temperature, voltage rating). This is a core industrial inspection process —

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: External inspection agencies are contracted; the link is required for audit traceability and regulatory reports.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the inspection results were formally reviewed and approved by the authorized approver. Establishes the official close of the inspection event for audit and compliance purposes.

attributes.name: asset_operational_status_at_inspection
attributes.description: The operational state of the asset at the time the inspection was conducted: running (in active production), shutdown (powered down for inspection), standby (idle but available), or under_maintenance (already in a maintenance window). Relevant for OEE downtime attribution and safety compliance.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Aveva SCADA; ISO 55001:2014

attributes.name: capa_reference_number
attributes.business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Reference Number
attributes.description: The externally visible CAPA or NCR reference number raised in the quality management system as a result of this inspection's findings. Populated when corrective_action_required is True. Enables cross-system traceability between inspection events and quality corrective actions.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

attributes.name: certificate_expiry_date
attributes.description: The date on which the compliance or safety certificate issued as a result of this inspection expires. Populated only when certificate_issued is True. Drives certificate renewal scheduling and regulatory compliance monitoring.
attributes.references: UL Certification Standards; CE Marking Directive; ISO 9001:2015

attributes.name: certificate_issued
attributes.description: Indicates whether a compliance certificate, safety certificate, or inspection certificate was issued as a result of this inspection event (True) or not (False). Relevant for UL, CE, and ISO certification inspections.
attributes.references: UL Certification Standards; CE Marking Directive; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: corrective_action_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether one or more findings from this inspection require a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) to be initiated. When True, triggers CAPA workflow creation in the quality management system per ISO 9001 Clause 10.2.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this inspection event record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail entry point. Distinct from inspection_date which records when the physical inspection occurred.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: critical_findings_count
attributes.description: Number of findings classified as critical or safety-critical, requiring immediate corrective action before the asset may return to service. Supports OSHA compliance reporting and safety escalation workflows.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 45001:2018 Clause 10.2; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: downtime_caused
attributes.description: Indicates whether this inspection event caused a production downtime event (True) or was conducted without interrupting production (False). Used for OEE downtime attribution and production loss reporting.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Siemens Opcenter MES; OEE Industry Standard

attributes.name: downtime_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Duration in minutes of production downtime directly attributable to this inspection event. Populated only when downtime_caused is True. Feeds OEE availability calculations and production loss reporting.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Siemens Opcenter MES; OEE Industry Standard

attributes.name: external_audit_reference
attributes.description: Reference number or identifier assigned by an external regulatory body, certification authority, or third-party auditor for inspections conducted as part of an external audit program (e.g., OSHA inspection citation number, UL audit reference, ISO certification audit ID). Nullable for internal inspections.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; UL Certification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.2

attributes.name: findings_count
attributes.description: Total number of distinct findings, deficiencies, or non-conformances identified during the inspection event. A finding may span multiple checklist items; this field captures the consolidated count used for CAPA prioritization and NCR generation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.217; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: findings_summary
attributes.description: Free-text narrative summary of the key findings, deficiencies, and observations recorded during the inspection. Provides the qualitative context for the quantitative findings_count and supports CAPA root cause analysis.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Remarks

attributes.name: inspection_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the inspection was physically performed. This is the principal real-world event date used for compliance reporting, regulatory submissions, and maintenance history analysis.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: inspection_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total elapsed time in minutes from inspection start to completion. Derived from start and end timestamps at source but stored as a business fact for resource utilization reporting, labor cost estimation, and inspection planning benchmarks.

attributes.name: inspection_end_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the inspector completed all inspection activities and closed the event. Combined with start timestamp to derive inspection duration for resource planning.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Record; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: inspection_interval_days
attributes.description: The prescribed frequency interval in calendar days between successive inspections of this asset, as defined by the preventive maintenance plan, regulatory requirement, or manufacturer specification. Used to calculate next_inspection_due_date.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — PM Frequency; ISO 55001:2014; OEM Maintenance Specifications

attributes.name: inspection_method
attributes.description: The primary technique used to conduct the inspection: visual (physical observation), functional_test (operational test of equipment), measurement (dimensional or parametric measurement), non_destructive_testing (NDT methods such as ultrasonic or thermographic), or document_review (records and certification review). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual|functional_test|measurement|non_destructive_testing|document_review|combined — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5; ANSI/ASNT Standards; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: inspection_number
attributes.description: Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the inspection event, used in regulatory submissions, audit trails, and cross-system references (e.g., INSP-2024-000123). Aligns with Maximo inspection record numbering conventions.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Record; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: inspection_outcome
attributes.description: Overall pass/fail result of the inspection event: pass (all checklist items met), fail (one or more critical items failed), conditional_pass (minor findings with corrective action required but asset may continue operating), or incomplete (inspection not fully executed).
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; UL Certification Standards; CE Marking Directive

attributes.name: inspection_scope
attributes.description: Indicates the breadth of the inspection performed: full (all checklist items evaluated), partial (subset of items evaluated due to access or time constraints), or spot_check (random sampling of key items). Affects the validity weight of the inspection outcome for compliance purposes.

attributes.name: inspection_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the inspector began the inspection activity on-site. Used for duration calculation, shift alignment, and OEE downtime impact analysis.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Record; Siemens Opcenter MES

attributes.name: inspection_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the inspection event: scheduled (planned but not started), in_progress (inspector on-site), completed (results recorded), cancelled (inspection voided), or overdue (past due date without completion). Drives maintenance scheduling and compliance dashboards.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Status; ISO 55001:2014

attributes.name: inspection_type
attributes.description: Classification of the inspection by its business purpose: preventive (scheduled TPM), safety (OSHA/facility safety), regulatory_compliance (UL, CE, ISO certification), quality_audit (QM equipment audit), condition_based (triggered by sensor/SCADA alert), ad_hoc (unplanned), or acceptance (new asset commissioning). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preventive|safety|regulatory_compliance|quality_audit|condition_based|ad_hoc|acceptance — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Type

attributes.name: inspector_certification_number
attributes.description: The professional certification or qualification number of the inspector, confirming their authority to conduct this type of inspection (e.g., OSHA-certified safety inspector, UL-authorized auditor, ISO lead auditor credential). Required for regulatory compliance evidence.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; UL Certification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.2; ISO 45001:2018

attributes.name: inspector_name
attributes.description: Full name of the qualified inspector or technician who performed the inspection. Stored for audit trail and regulatory evidence purposes. Classified as confidential as it is an employee business record.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.2; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; Workday HCM

attributes.name: inspector_remarks
attributes.description: Free-text field for the inspector's additional observations, recommendations, or notes that do not constitute formal findings but provide context for maintenance planning and asset condition assessment.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Remarks; ISO 55001:2014

attributes.name: items_failed
attributes.description: Count of checklist line items that did not meet acceptance criteria during this inspection. Drives findings count reporting, CAPA initiation thresholds, and regulatory non-conformance escalation.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: items_passed
attributes.description: Count of checklist line items that met acceptance criteria during this inspection. Together with total_checklist_items and items_failed, provides the raw data for compliance rate reporting without pre-aggregating the metric.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this inspection event record was most recently modified, supporting data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: next_inspection_due_date
attributes.description: The date by which the next inspection of this asset must be completed, as determined by the inspection interval, regulatory requirement, or inspector recommendation. Drives the preventive maintenance scheduling engine in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — PM Schedule; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the inspected asset resides. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Maximo and SAP PM/QM modules and for plant-level compliance reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PM Module — Plant; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1

attributes.name: regulatory_body
attributes.description: The governing body or certification authority whose standard applies to this inspection (e.g., OSHA, ISO, IEC, UL, CE, EPA, NIST, ANSI, IPC). Used to filter and group compliance inspections for regulatory reporting dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OSHA|ISO|IEC|UL|CE|EPA|NIST|ANSI|IPC|NONE — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: OSHA; ISO; IEC; UL; CE Marking; EPA; NIST Cybersecurity Framework; ANSI; IPC

attributes.name: regulatory_standard
attributes.description: The specific regulatory or certification standard under which this inspection was conducted (e.g., OSHA 29 CFR 1910, ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, UL 508A, CE Directive 2006/42/EC, IEC 62443). Mandatory for compliance inspections to support regulatory reporting and audit evidence.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 9001:2015; ISO 45001:2018; UL Standards; CE Marking Directive; IEC 62443

attributes.name: risk_level
attributes.description: Risk classification assigned to the inspection outcome based on the severity and nature of findings: critical (immediate safety or production risk), high (significant risk requiring prompt action), medium (moderate risk with planned remediation), or low (minor or no risk). Drives escalation and prioritization workflows.
attributes.references: ISO 45001:2018 Clause 6.1; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; FMEA Methodology

attributes.name: scheduled_date
attributes.description: The originally planned date for this inspection as generated by the preventive maintenance schedule or regulatory compliance calendar. Compared against inspection_date to measure schedule adherence.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — PM Schedule; ISO 55001:2014 Clause 6.2

attributes.name: total_checklist_items
attributes.description: Total number of inspection checklist line items evaluated during this inspection event. Used as the denominator for compliance rate calculations and inspection completeness metrics.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Inspection Checklist; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1

products.name: calibration_record
products.description: Instrument and measurement equipment calibration record tracking calibration events for gauges, sensors, PLCs, and precision measurement tools. Captures calibration date, calibration standard used, as-found reading, as-left reading, tolerance limits, pass/fail result, calibration interval, next due date, and certifying technician. Supports ISO 9001 measurement system compliance and SPC instrument traceability.

attributes.name: calibration_record_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for each calibration record event. Primary key for the calibration_record data product.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5 — Monitoring and Measuring Resources

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Calibration records for customer-installed instruments must be traceable to the customer account for ISO/IEC 17025 calibration certificate delivery and contractual calibration service obligation track

attributes.name: asset_work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Maximo work order under which this calibration activity was planned and executed. Enables traceability from calibration event back to maintenance scheduling.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management — Work Order Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SAP cost center responsible for the calibration activity. Used for OpEx allocation of calibration labor and external service costs.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) — Cost Center; IFRS/GAAP OpEx classification

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: ISO 9001/ISO 17025 calibration compliance: calibration procedures and acceptance criteria (tolerance limits, measurement uncertainty) are defined in engineering specifications. Calibration labs must r

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical instrument, gauge, sensor, PLC, or precision measurement tool that was calibrated. Links to the asset master record in Maximo Asset Management.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; Maximo Asset Management — Asset Object

attributes.name: location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the plant, facility, or functional location where the instrument is installed and where the calibration was performed. Supports site-level compliance reporting and geographic traceability.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; Maximo Asset Management — Location Object; SAP S/4HANA PM

attributes.name: pm_schedule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_pm_schedule. Business justification: Calibration activities for instruments and measurement equipment are typically scheduled via preventive maintenance schedules. Linking calibration_record to asset_pm_schedule enables calibration compl

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Calibration activities are performed on specific instrument models; linking to SKU provides traceability for compliance and performance reporting.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Calibration labs are external suppliers; linking supports compliance and lab‑performance tracking.

attributes.name: adjustment_description
attributes.description: Narrative description of the adjustment or corrective action applied to the instrument during calibration (e.g., zero offset correction, span adjustment, sensor replacement). Populated only when adjustment_made is True.

attributes.name: adjustment_made
attributes.description: Indicates whether a physical adjustment or correction was applied to the instrument during this calibration event. True if the instrument was adjusted; False if it was verified only. Drives downstream impact assessment for prior measurements.

attributes.name: as_found_error
attributes.description: The calculated deviation of the as-found reading from the reference value (as_found_reading minus reference_value). Stored as a business fact to support drift trend analysis and SPC instrument capability studies without requiring recalculation.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; ANSI Z540.3; SPC (Statistical Process Control) methodology

attributes.name: as_found_reading
attributes.description: The measured value of the instrument at the start of the calibration event, before any adjustment is made. Used to assess instrument drift, determine if prior measurements were valid, and support SPC instrument traceability analysis.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; ANSI Z540.3

attributes.name: as_left_error
attributes.description: The calculated deviation of the as-left reading from the reference value (as_left_reading minus reference_value). Confirms residual error after adjustment and is used for measurement uncertainty budgets.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; ANSI Z540.3; GUM (Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement)

attributes.name: as_left_reading
attributes.description: The measured value of the instrument at the conclusion of the calibration event, after any adjustment or correction has been applied. Confirms the instrument is within tolerance before being returned to service.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; ANSI Z540.3

attributes.name: calibration_date
attributes.description: The date on which the calibration activity was physically performed. This is the principal business event date used for certificate issuance, compliance records, and next-due-date calculation.

attributes.name: calibration_due_date
attributes.description: The date by which the next calibration must be completed, calculated from calibration_date plus the calibration_interval. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and compliance monitoring.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; Maximo Asset Management — PM Schedule

attributes.name: calibration_interval_days
attributes.description: The approved recalibration frequency expressed in calendar days. Used to compute calibration_due_date and to schedule preventive maintenance in Maximo. Determined by instrument criticality, historical drift, and manufacturer recommendations.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; Maximo Asset Management — PM Frequency; ANSI Z540.3

attributes.name: calibration_lab
attributes.description: Indicates whether the calibration was performed by an internal calibration lab, an externally accredited laboratory (ISO/IEC 17025), an external non-accredited service provider, or the OEM service team.

attributes.name: calibration_method
attributes.description: The documented calibration procedure or method reference used (e.g., internal procedure number, NIST traceable method, manufacturer procedure). Ensures repeatability and traceability of the calibration process.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.2; NIST Handbook 150; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

attributes.name: calibration_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for the technician to record observations, anomalies, special conditions, or additional context about the calibration event not captured in structured fields.

attributes.name: calibration_number
attributes.description: Externally visible, human-readable unique identifier for this calibration event, used on calibration certificates and quality records. Format: CAL-{ASSET_CODE}-{SEQUENCE}.

attributes.name: calibration_status
attributes.description: Overall result and lifecycle status of the calibration event. 'pass' indicates the instrument is within tolerance; 'fail' triggers quarantine and CAPA; 'conditional_pass' indicates marginal acceptance with restrictions; 'out_of_tolerance' indicates as-found deviation requiring adjustment.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; ANSI Z540.3

attributes.name: calibration_type
attributes.description: Classification of the calibration event by trigger or purpose. 'initial' is performed on new instruments; 'periodic' is routine scheduled calibration; 'after_repair' follows maintenance; 'unscheduled' is triggered by suspected drift or incident.

attributes.name: capa_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) Reference Number
attributes.description: Reference number of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record initiated as a result of a calibration failure or out-of-tolerance finding. Links the calibration event to the quality management corrective action workflow.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2; CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) process; SAP QM

attributes.name: certificate_issue_date
attributes.description: The date on which the calibration certificate was formally issued and signed. May differ from calibration_date if review and approval occur after the physical calibration activity.

attributes.name: certificate_number
attributes.description: The official certificate number issued upon successful calibration, as printed on the calibration certificate. Used for document control, quality records management, and regulatory audit evidence.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this calibration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record retention tracking.

attributes.name: environmental_humidity_pct
attributes.description: Relative humidity percentage recorded at the time and location of calibration. Required environmental condition documentation per ISO/IEC 17025 for humidity-sensitive instruments.

attributes.name: environmental_temperature_c
attributes.description: Ambient temperature in degrees Celsius recorded at the time and location of calibration. Environmental conditions can affect measurement accuracy and are required to be documented per ISO/IEC 17025.

attributes.name: external_lab_accreditation_number
attributes.description: Accreditation body certificate number for the external calibration laboratory (e.g., A2LA, NVLAP, UKAS accreditation number). Validates the laboratory's ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation status.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.6; ANSI Z540.3; A2LA/NVLAP accreditation requirements

attributes.name: external_lab_name
attributes.description: Name of the external calibration service provider or accredited laboratory, when calibration_lab is 'external_accredited' or 'external_non_accredited'. Used for supplier qualification and audit trail.

attributes.name: instrument_tag
attributes.description: Plant or facility tag number assigned to the measurement instrument per ISA-5.1 instrumentation standards (e.g., TI-101, PT-205). Used for shop floor identification and P&ID cross-reference.
attributes.references: ISA-5.1 Instrumentation Symbols and Identification; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5

attributes.name: instrument_type
attributes.description: Classification of the measurement instrument or device being calibrated. Drives applicable calibration procedures and tolerance standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gauge|sensor|transducer|transmitter|analyzer|controller|plc|cnc_probe|torque_wrench|caliper|micrometer|thermometer|pressure_meter|flow_meter — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: measurement_parameter
attributes.description: The physical or process parameter being measured by the instrument (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow, voltage, torque, dimension, weight). Determines the applicable calibration standard and tolerance regime.

attributes.name: measurement_uncertainty
attributes.description: The expanded measurement uncertainty (k=2, 95% confidence) associated with the calibration result, expressed in the same unit as measurement_unit. Required for ISO/IEC 17025 accredited calibration certificates.

attributes.name: measurement_unit
attributes.description: The unit of measure applicable to as_found_reading, as_left_reading, reference_value, and tolerance fields (e.g., °C, bar, mA, mm, Nm, V, kg). Must conform to SI units per ISO 80000.
attributes.references: ISO 80000 (SI Units); ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; NIST SP 811

attributes.name: out_of_service
attributes.description: Indicates whether the instrument has been placed out of service as a result of this calibration event (e.g., due to a fail or out-of-tolerance result). When True, the instrument must be quarantined and a CAPA initiated before return to service.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5; CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) process; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: prior_measurement_impact
attributes.description: Assessment of whether measurements taken by this instrument since its last calibration may have been affected by the out-of-tolerance condition found. Required for product conformity risk assessment when a calibration failure is detected.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.1.5.2; ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: reference_value
attributes.description: The true or nominal value against which the instrument reading is compared during calibration. Established by the reference standard and used to compute as-found and as-left errors.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 7.6; NIST Handbook 44; ANSI Z540.3

attributes.name: standard_certificate_number
attributes.description: Certificate number of the reference standard used during calibration, establishing the NIST/SI traceability chain. Required for ISO/IEC 17025 compliance documentation.
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 Clause 6.4.7; NIST Traceability Policy; ANSI Z540.3

attributes.name: technician_certification_number
attributes.description: The qualification or certification number of the technician who performed the calibration, as issued by an accredited body (e.g., ASQ, NIST, internal competency register). Required for ISO/IEC 17025 personnel traceability.

attributes.name: technician_name
