attributes.description: Three-digit SAP movement type code that classifies the nature of the inventory transaction (e.g., 101=Goods Receipt from PO, 261=Goods Issue to Production Order, 311=Transfer Posting, 551=Scrapping). This code determines the accounting impact and stock ledger behavior.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Movement Type Configuration

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: The date on which the stock movement was posted to the inventory ledger and became effective for stock balance and financial accounting purposes. This is the principal business event timestamp for the transaction.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of material moved in this transaction, expressed in the base unit of measure. Positive for receipts, negative for issues in some system configurations.

attributes.name: reference_document_item
attributes.description: Line item number within the reference document that corresponds to this stock movement.

attributes.name: reference_document_number
attributes.description: The document number of the source transaction (e.g., purchase order number, production order number, sales order number) that initiated this stock movement.

attributes.name: reference_document_type
attributes.description: Type of source document that triggered this stock movement (e.g., purchase order for goods receipt, production order for goods issue, delivery for shipment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase_order|production_order|sales_order|delivery|transfer_order|physical_inventory|reservation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: reversal_indicator
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this movement is a reversal (correction) of a previously posted transaction.

attributes.name: reversed_material_document_number
attributes.description: The material document number of the original transaction that this movement reverses, establishing audit trail linkage for corrections.

attributes.name: source_bin_location
attributes.description: Specific bin, shelf, or warehouse location identifier from which the material was picked or moved, enabling precise warehouse management and cycle count accuracy.

attributes.name: special_stock_indicator
attributes.description: Code indicating special stock categories such as consignment stock, project stock, sales order stock, or returnable packaging.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Special Stock Management

attributes.name: stock_type
attributes.description: Classification of the stock status: unrestricted (available for use), quality_inspection (awaiting QA approval), blocked (not usable), or restricted (limited use).
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Stock Status Control

attributes.name: storage_location_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the storage location (warehouse, stockroom, or inventory area) within the plant where the material is stored or was moved from.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Location Master

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure in which the quantity is expressed (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, M=Meter). Follows ISO standard UOM codes.

attributes.name: valuation_type
attributes.description: Split valuation indicator used when the same material is valued differently based on procurement type or quality grade (e.g., own production vs. external procurement).

products.name: cycle_count
products.description: Cycle count event header record capturing scheduled and ad-hoc physical inventory counts for specific materials, storage locations, or zones. Tracks count document number, count date, count type (ABC cycle, annual physical, ad-hoc recount), counter assignment, zone or location scope, planned vs actual count date, overall variance summary, approval status, and posting status. Supports ABC-based cycle count programs, annual physical inventory compliance, and inventory accuracy KPI tracking.

attributes.name: cycle_count_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the cycle count event. Primary key for the cycle count record.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Cycle counts for VMI/consignment inventory must be attributed to the specific customer site where the count occurs. Site-level inventory accuracy reporting, SLA compliance tracking, and consignment re

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory count variances in manufacturing are posted as adjustments to cost centers (inventory shrinkage, write-offs). Domain experts expect cycle_count.cost_center_id to attribute variance costs to

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Cycle Count Stock Location Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Link cycle count header to the physical location where the count occurs; replace code with FK.

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: cycle_count has a warehouse_number STRING column that is a denormalized reference to the warehouse master. A cycle count is scoped to a specific warehouse facility. Adding warehouse_id as a proper FK

attributes.name: abc_indicator
attributes.description: ABC classification of materials included in this count. A=high-value/high-frequency, B=medium, C=low-value/low-frequency, X=unclassified. Drives cycle count frequency per ABC methodology.

attributes.name: accuracy_percentage
attributes.description: Inventory accuracy rate for this count, calculated as (items with zero variance / total items counted) × 100. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for inventory management.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval workflow status for the cycle count results. Counts with material variances typically require supervisor or manager approval before posting.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the cycle count was approved. Used for compliance reporting and cycle time tracking.

attributes.name: count_date
attributes.description: The actual date when the physical count was performed. Principal business event timestamp for the cycle count activity.

attributes.name: count_document_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the cycle count document. Externally-known unique reference number used in SAP WM and warehouse operations.

attributes.name: count_end_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when the physical counting activity was completed. Used to calculate count duration and resource utilization.

attributes.name: count_method
attributes.description: Technology or method used to perform the physical count. Impacts count accuracy and efficiency metrics.

attributes.name: count_scope
attributes.description: Defines the breadth of the counting activity. Indicates whether count covers entire warehouse, specific zones, material categories, or individual items.

attributes.name: count_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when the physical counting activity began. Used for labor tracking and cycle time analysis.

attributes.name: count_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the cycle count event. Tracks progression from planning through execution, approval, and posting to inventory ledger. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Planned|In Progress|Completed|Approved|Posted|Cancelled|On Hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: count_type
attributes.description: Classification of the cycle count event. Defines the counting methodology and business purpose (ABC-based frequency, annual compliance, variance investigation, or continuous counting program).
attributes.value_regex: ABC Cycle|Annual Physical|Ad-Hoc Recount|Spot Count|Blind Count|Perpetual Inventory

attributes.name: count_zone
attributes.description: Physical zone or area within the warehouse designated for this count. Used to organize counting activities by geographic sections or material groupings.

attributes.name: counter_name
attributes.description: Full name of the employee who performed the physical count. Used for operational visibility and audit trail.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the cycle count record was first created in the system. Audit field for record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Fiscal year in which the cycle count was performed. Used for annual physical inventory compliance and year-over-year accuracy trending.

attributes.name: freeze_book_inventory_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether book inventory was frozen (locked from transactions) during the count to ensure data integrity. True=frozen, False=not frozen.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the cycle count record was last updated. Audit field for tracking record changes and data currency.

attributes.name: material_document_number
attributes.description: SAP Material Management (MM) document number generated when inventory adjustments are posted. Links cycle count to inventory movement transactions.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about the cycle count event. Used to document unusual conditions, issues encountered, or explanations for variances.

attributes.name: planned_count_date
attributes.description: The originally scheduled date for the cycle count. Used to track adherence to ABC cycle count schedules and annual physical inventory calendars.

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: Accounting date when inventory adjustments from this count were posted to the financial ledger. May differ from count_date for period-end controls.

attributes.name: posting_period
attributes.description: Fiscal period (YYYY-MM format) to which inventory adjustments are posted. Used for period-end close and financial reporting.

attributes.name: posting_status
attributes.description: Status of inventory adjustment posting to the general ledger and inventory subledger. Posted=variances reflected in stock balances, Not Posted=count complete but not yet posted.

attributes.name: recount_reason
attributes.description: Business reason or justification for requiring a recount. Populated when recount_required_flag is True.

attributes.name: recount_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a recount is required due to significant variances exceeding tolerance thresholds. True=recount needed, False=count accepted.

attributes.name: tolerance_percentage
attributes.description: Acceptable variance threshold as a percentage of book quantity. Variances within tolerance may auto-approve; exceeding tolerance triggers recount or management review.

attributes.name: total_items_counted
attributes.description: Total number of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) or material numbers included in this cycle count event.

attributes.name: total_variance_quantity
attributes.description: Aggregate quantity variance across all materials in this count (sum of absolute differences between book and physical quantities). Measured in base unit of measure.

attributes.name: total_variance_value
attributes.description: Aggregate financial value of inventory variances in this count. Calculated as sum of (variance quantity × standard cost) across all materials. Used for materiality assessment and financial impact analysis.

attributes.name: variance_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total variance value. Typically plant or company code default currency.

products.name: cycle_count_line
products.description: Individual line item within a cycle count document representing the count result for a specific material-batch-bin combination. Captures line number, material, batch, storage bin, book quantity, counted quantity, difference quantity, unit of measure, recount indicator, and posting result. Enables granular variance analysis and audit trail for physical inventory reconciliation per ISO 9001 inventory accuracy requirements.

attributes.name: cycle_count_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the cycle count line item. Primary key for the cycle count line entity.

attributes.name: cycle_count_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the parent cycle count document header. Links this line item to its containing cycle count execution.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each cycle count line difference generates a GL posting to an inventory adjustment account in manufacturing. Domain experts expect this FK to trace count variances to specific GL accounts for audit co

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: cycle_count_line has a batch_number STRING column representing the specific lot/batch being counted at a location. The lot_batch table is the authoritative master for batch data. Adding lot_batch_id t

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the material master record being counted. Identifies the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) or raw material subject to physical verification.

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where the cycle count is being performed. Supports multi-site inventory management and consolidation.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the storage location where the material is physically located. Represents the warehouse or plant-level storage area.

attributes.name: accounting_document_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the financial accounting document generated when the variance was posted. Links the physical inventory adjustment to General Ledger (GL) entries in SAP Financial Accounting (FI).

attributes.name: book_quantity
attributes.description: System-recorded quantity of material in the storage bin at the time of cycle count initiation. Represents the expected quantity per Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) records before physical verification.

attributes.name: count_date
attributes.description: Business date on which the physical count was scheduled or performed. May differ from counted_timestamp for counts spanning multiple shifts or days.

attributes.name: count_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this cycle count line item. Tracks progression from initiation through counting, verification, and posting stages.

attributes.name: counted_quantity
attributes.description: Actual physical quantity counted by warehouse personnel during the cycle count execution. This is the verified on-hand quantity observed in the storage bin.

attributes.name: counted_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the physical count was completed by warehouse personnel. Represents the actual business event time of inventory verification.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this cycle count line record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: difference_quantity
attributes.description: Variance between book quantity and counted quantity. Calculated as counted_quantity minus book_quantity. Positive values indicate surplus, negative values indicate shortage. Critical for inventory accuracy Key Performance Indicator (KPI) measurement.

attributes.name: freeze_book_inventory_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the book inventory quantity was frozen at the time of count initiation to prevent changes during the count period. True if frozen, false if dynamic.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this cycle count line record was last updated. Audit trail field for change tracking and data governance.

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line item number within the cycle count document. Determines the ordering and position of this count line within the parent document.

attributes.name: material_document_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the material document generated when the variance was posted. Links the cycle count result to the inventory movement transaction in SAP MM.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for counter observations, exceptions, or additional context regarding the count result. Supports audit trail and Non-Conformance Report (NCR) documentation.

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: Date when the inventory variance was posted to the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system and financial ledgers. Null if not yet posted. Critical for period-end inventory valuation per International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) and Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP).

attributes.name: posting_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the variance on this line has been posted to inventory and financial ledgers. True if posted, false if pending approval or recount.

attributes.name: recount_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this line item requires or has undergone a recount due to variance exceeding tolerance thresholds. True if recount is required or completed, false otherwise.

attributes.name: recount_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the reason a recount was triggered. Used for root cause analysis and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) processes per ISO 9001.

attributes.name: special_stock_indicator
attributes.description: Indicator for special stock categories that require separate tracking. Consignment stock is vendor-owned, project stock is allocated to specific projects, sales order stock is reserved for customer orders.

attributes.name: stock_type
attributes.description: Classification of inventory stock status at the time of count. Unrestricted stock is available for use, quality inspection stock is pending Quality Management (QM) approval, blocked stock is unavailable due to quality or legal holds.

attributes.name: storage_bin
attributes.description: Specific bin, shelf, or rack location within the storage location where the material is stored. Enables precise physical location tracking for Warehouse Management System (WMS) operations.

attributes.name: tolerance_percentage
attributes.description: Acceptable variance threshold as a percentage of book quantity. Variances within this tolerance may be auto-posted without recount. Supports Just In Time (JIT) inventory control and Material Requirements Planning (MRP) accuracy.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for all quantity fields on this line. Examples include EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter). Must align with material master base unit of measure.

attributes.name: valuation_type
attributes.description: Split valuation indicator for materials managed with multiple valuation types. Examples include origin-based valuation, quality-based valuation. Null for materials without split valuation.

attributes.name: variance_reason_code
attributes.description: Root cause classification for inventory variance identified during cycle count. Enables variance analysis and process improvement initiatives. Required when difference_quantity is non-zero and exceeds tolerance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: counting_error|theft|damage|obsolescence|system_error|goods_receipt_not_posted|goods_issue_not_posted|transfer_not_posted|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: verified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the count result was verified by a supervisor or second counter. Null if verification is not required or not yet completed.

products.name: replenishment_order
products.description: Planning-driven, kanban-triggered, or manually-created replenishment and reservation record for restocking materials to safety stock or reorder point levels, or committing stock for planned consumption. Captures order number, material, plant, storage location, replenishment type (planning-driven, kanban, min-max, JIT, manual reservation), required quantity, requested delivery date, source (purchase requisition, production order, stock transfer), reference order (production order, sales order, maintenance order), priority, reserved quantity, and fulfillment status. Unifies demand commitment and replenishment execution into a single record for available-to-promise calculation.
products.reference: SAP MM MRP Planned Order / Replenishment Request

attributes.name: replenishment_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the replenishment order record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Vendor‑Managed Inventory (VMI) orders are generated per customer; linking the order to the customer account enables demand‑driven replenishment.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: VMI replenishment orders are fulfilled to a specific customer manufacturing site. Site-level inventory planning, dock scheduling, and delivery routing all require the destination site. Industrial manu

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Replenishment orders executed under a supply agreement (scheduling agreement or blanket order) must reference that agreement for contract compliance tracking, price determination, and spend-against-co

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: Source determination for replenishment orders uses the AVL to identify approved suppliers for a material. Linking replenishment_order to approved_vendor_list supports procurement compliance reporting,

attributes.name: asset_work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_work_order. Business justification: Emergency spare part replenishment triggered by a specific maintenance work order (breakdown or corrective maintenance) must be traceable back to the originating work order. This supports maintenance

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the cost center that will be charged for this replenishment, used for financial accounting and cost allocation.
attributes.references: SAP CO Controlling Cost Center Accounting

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replenishment orders in manufacturing trigger inventory cost postings to GL accounts (stock replenishment expense, kanban cost allocation). Domain experts expect this FK for budget-to-actual cost trac

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: replenishment_order has a batch_number STRING column for batch-specific replenishment requests (e.g., replenishing a specific batch for quality or expiry reasons, or kanban-triggered batch replenishme

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material or SKU being replenished. Links to the material master record.

attributes.name: material_requirement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: Replenishment orders fulfill specific MRP material requirements. This FK enables requirement pegging — a core MRP process in industrial manufacturing — allowing planners to trace which material requir

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: MRP ORDER CONVERSION: replenishment orders are created from MRP planned orders; linking enables traceability.

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Replenishment orders are directly triggered by plant_data MRP parameters (lot_size_procedure, reorder_point, safety_stock_quantity, mrp_type). MRP traceability audits in industrial manufacturing requi

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Kanban and lean replenishment orders in industrial manufacturing are triggered by consumption at a specific production line. kanban_card_number on replenishment_order confirms lean context. Line-level

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_info_record. Business justification: Purchase info records provide the pricing, lead time, and sourcing conditions used when converting a replenishment order to a PO. This link supports price determination, lead time accuracy validation,

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: A replenishment order is the internal demand signal that results in a purchase order being raised. Linking replenishment_order to the resulting purchase_order supports procurement execution tracking,

attributes.name: safety_stock_policy_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supply Safety Stock Policy Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_safety_stock_policy. Business justification: Replenishment orders are triggered when stock falls below safety stock thresholds defined in supply_safety_stock_policy. This FK enables policy effectiveness analysis — tracking how often each policy

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: VMI replenishment orders for industrial customers are governed by SLA agreements specifying fill rates, lead times, and delivery frequency. SLA compliance reporting, penalty calculation, and contract

attributes.name: sourcing_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_rule. Business justification: Replenishment orders are created following sourcing rules (preferred supplier, MOQ, lead time). Linking replenishment_order to the governing sourcing_rule enables sourcing compliance audits, rule effe

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location or warehouse bin where the replenished material will be stored.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the external supplier or vendor from whom the material will be procured, applicable when source type is external procurement.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this replenishment order was approved for execution, marking transition from draft to active status.

attributes.name: closed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this replenishment order was closed after complete fulfillment or cancellation.

attributes.name: confirmed_delivery_date
attributes.description: Actual confirmed delivery date provided by the supplier or internal source, may differ from requested date based on availability and lead time.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this replenishment order record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost and financial transactions related to this replenishment order.

attributes.name: estimated_cost
attributes.description: Estimated total cost for fulfilling this replenishment order, including material cost, freight, and handling charges.

attributes.name: fulfilled_quantity
attributes.description: Actual quantity of material that has been delivered and received against this replenishment order.

attributes.name: inspection_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether incoming quality inspection is required before the replenished material can be released for use.

attributes.name: kanban_card_number
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the kanban card that triggered this replenishment, applicable when replenishment type is kanban-based pull system.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Expected number of calendar days from order placement to material availability at the storage location, used for planning and scheduling.
attributes.references: SAP MM Material Master Lead Time

attributes.name: lot_size_quantity
attributes.description: Standard or economic order quantity used for replenishment, may be based on MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity), EOQ (Economic Order Quantity), or fixed lot size rules.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this replenishment order record was last modified or updated.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information related to this replenishment order.

attributes.name: order_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique identifier for the replenishment order, used for tracking and reference across systems and documents.

attributes.name: order_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the replenishment order indicating its progress through the fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|in_progress|partially_fulfilled|fulfilled|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to this replenishment order, used for scheduling and allocation decisions when material is constrained.

attributes.name: reference_order_number
attributes.description: Reference to the consuming order that requires this replenishment (e.g., production order, sales order, maintenance order). Used to link demand to supply.

attributes.name: reference_order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the reference order type that is consuming or requiring this replenishment.

attributes.name: reorder_point_quantity
attributes.description: Inventory level threshold that triggers automatic replenishment when stock falls below this point, used in min-max and reorder point planning strategies.

attributes.name: replenishment_type
attributes.description: Classification of the replenishment trigger mechanism: planning-driven (MRP-based), kanban (pull-based), min-max (reorder point), JIT (Just In Time), manual reservation, or safety stock replenishment.
attributes.references: SAP PP MRP Material Requirements Planning

attributes.name: requested_delivery_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the replenished material is required to be available at the storage location to meet production or demand requirements.

attributes.name: required_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity of material required to meet the replenishment target or reservation commitment, expressed in the base unit of measure.

attributes.name: reserved_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of material currently reserved or committed against this replenishment order, used for available-to-promise (ATP) calculation.
attributes.references: SAP SD Sales and Distribution ATP Logic

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum buffer stock quantity that should be maintained at the storage location to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions.

attributes.name: serial_number_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether individual serial number tracking is required for the replenished material items.

attributes.name: source_document_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the originating document that triggered or fulfills this replenishment order (e.g., purchase requisition number, production order number, stock transfer order number).

attributes.name: source_type
attributes.description: Classification of the source from which the material will be replenished: purchase requisition (external procurement), production order (internal manufacturing), stock transfer (inter-plant), external procurement, or internal transfer.

attributes.name: special_procurement_type
attributes.description: Classification of special procurement arrangements such as consignment stock, subcontracting, pipeline materials, or inter-plant stock transfers.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Standard unit of measure for the material quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, M for meter).

products.name: serialized_unit
products.description: Individual unit tracking record for serialized components, assemblies, finished goods, and handling units (pallets, containers, totes, drums, reels) managed by unique identifier throughout their inventory lifecycle. Captures serial/unit number, material, unit type (serialized part, pallet, container, tote), contents (for handling units), batch, quantity, gross/net weight, dimensions, current plant and storage location, status (in stock, in production, shipped, returned, scrapped), seal number, and last movement timestamp. Provides pallet-level and serial-level traceability for warehouse operations, field service, and regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the serialized unit record. Primary key for individual unit tracking across inventory lifecycle.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account that owns or has been allocated this serialized unit. Used for customer-specific inventory, consignment stock, and field service tracking.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Serialized units (installed automation systems, drives, motors) are physically located at customer sites. Asset lifecycle tracking, field service dispatch, and warranty management all require knowing

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing (automation systems, drives, switchgear), each serialized unit is built to a specific product configuration (voltage rating, power rating, selected options). Field service,

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Each serialized physical unit is built to a specific engineering revision. Field service, warranty claims, and product recall management in industrial manufacturing require knowing the exact revision

attributes.name: fixed_asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: High-value serialized units in industrial manufacturing (automation systems, electrification components) are capitalized as fixed assets. Domain experts expect serialized_unit.fixed_asset_id to link p

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Reference to the sales order for which this serialized unit has been allocated or shipped. Links inventory to order fulfillment and revenue recognition.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Order Management

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Reference to the lot or batch record from which this serialized unit originated. Links unit-level traceability to batch-level quality and production data.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record that defines the product specification, Bill of Materials (BOM), and material characteristics for this serialized unit.

attributes.name: parent_unit_serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent handling unit (pallet, container, tote) that contains this serialized unit. Enables hierarchical packing structure and nested unit tracking for warehouse operations.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA WM Handling Unit Hierarchy

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, serialized units (automation systems, electrification components) are produced against specific planned orders. This FK enables end-to-end production traceability from pla

attributes.name: procurement_goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Serialized units received from suppliers must reference the procurement_goods_receipt that brought them into inventory. Critical for serial number traceability, warranty claim processing, and field se

attributes.name: production_work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production order that manufactured or assembled this serialized unit. Links unit traceability to production execution and Manufacturing Execution System (MES) data.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order Management

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the purchase order through which this serialized unit was procured. Enables procurement traceability and supplier performance analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Order Management

attributes.name: run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Serialized finished goods (automation systems, drives) in industrial manufacturing are produced during a specific production run. Serial number traceability, warranty claims, and field service require

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Installed serialized units (e.g., automation controllers, drives) are covered by specific SLA agreements governing uptime, maintenance response, and spare parts. Warranty claims, preventive maintenanc

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: serialized_unit tracks the physical location of individual serialized items via storage_location_code (STRING) and bin_location (STRING). The stock_location table is the authoritative master for all p

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor or supplier that provided this serialized unit. Links to procurement data and supplier quality management.

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Reference to the warehouse master record where this serialized unit is currently stored. Provides detailed warehouse attributes and operational parameters.

attributes.name: bin_location
attributes.description: Specific bin, rack, or storage position identifier within the warehouse where this serialized unit is physically located. Enables precise picking and putaway operations.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA WM Bin Location Management

attributes.name: country_of_origin
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code indicating where this serialized unit was manufactured or assembled. Required for customs declarations and trade compliance.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or username of the person who created this serialized unit record. Supports audit trail and data governance requirements.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this serialized unit record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record creation and data lineage.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the valuation amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Enables multi-currency inventory valuation and financial consolidation.

attributes.name: customs_tariff_code
attributes.description: Harmonized System (HS) code or Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) code for customs classification and duty calculation. Required for international shipments.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when this serialized unit reaches end of shelf life or warranty expiration. Used for First Expired First Out (FEFO) inventory management and compliance reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Shelf Life Management

attributes.name: gross_weight_kg
attributes.description: Total gross weight of the serialized unit including packaging and contents, measured in kilograms. Used for logistics planning and transportation compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 28640 Freight Containers - Coding, Identification and Marking

attributes.name: hazmat_class
attributes.description: United Nations (UN) hazard classification code for hazardous materials (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives). Required for transportation and storage compliance.
attributes.references: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

attributes.name: hazmat_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator whether this serialized unit contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, and transportation compliance.

attributes.name: height_cm
attributes.description: Height dimension of the serialized unit or handling unit in centimeters. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization.
attributes.references: ISO 3394 Packaging - Dimensions of Transport Packages

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or username of the person who last modified this serialized unit record. Supports audit trail and data governance requirements.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this serialized unit record was last updated. Enables change tracking and data synchronization across systems.

attributes.name: last_movement_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent inventory movement transaction for this serialized unit. Used for inventory aging analysis and cycle count prioritization.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Inventory Movement History

attributes.name: last_movement_type
attributes.description: Type of the most recent inventory movement transaction for this serialized unit (e.g., goods receipt, goods issue, transfer posting, physical inventory). Provides audit trail context.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Movement Type Configuration

attributes.name: length_cm
attributes.description: Length dimension of the serialized unit or handling unit in centimeters. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization.
attributes.references: ISO 3394 Packaging - Dimensions of Transport Packages

attributes.name: manufacturing_date
attributes.description: Date when this serialized unit was manufactured or assembled. Critical for warranty tracking, shelf life management, and regulatory compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Product Identification and Traceability

attributes.name: net_weight_kg
attributes.description: Net weight of the material contents excluding packaging, measured in kilograms. Used for material valuation and yield calculations.
attributes.references: ISO 28640 Freight Containers - Coding, Identification and Marking

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes and comments about this serialized unit. Used for special handling instructions, quality observations, or operational remarks.

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Indicates the legal ownership of the serialized unit. Distinguishes between company-owned inventory, customer consignment, vendor consignment, and third-party logistics (3PL) owned stock.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers - Consignment Inventory

attributes.name: quality_certificate_number
attributes.description: Certificate of Conformance (CoC) or quality certificate number issued for this serialized unit. Required for regulated industries and customer quality requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Certificate of Conformance

attributes.name: quality_inspection_status
attributes.description: Current quality inspection status of the serialized unit. Indicates whether the unit has passed quality checks and is approved for use or shipment.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Inspection and Testing

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of material contained in this serialized unit. For serialized parts typically 1; for handling units represents the aggregate quantity of contents.

attributes.name: seal_number
attributes.description: Security seal number applied to handling units (containers, pallets) for tamper detection and customs compliance. Required for international shipments and high-value goods.

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: Unique serial number or unit identifier assigned to this individual component, assembly, or finished good for traceability throughout its lifecycle. Enables field service tracking and warranty management.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management - Product Identification and Traceability

attributes.name: stock_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the serialized unit indicating its position in the inventory and production workflow. Drives availability for Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and order fulfillment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_stock|in_production|in_transit|shipped|delivered|returned|blocked|scrapped|consumed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Status Management

attributes.name: stock_type
attributes.description: Classification of stock availability indicating whether the unit is unrestricted for use, under quality inspection, blocked, or held as consignment inventory.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Type Classification

attributes.name: un_number
attributes.description: Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous substance for transportation and emergency response (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for hazmat shipping documentation.
attributes.references: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters). Aligns with material master UOM definition.

attributes.name: unit_type
attributes.description: Classification of the serialized unit indicating whether it is a serialized component part, assembly, finished good, or handling unit (pallet, container, tote, drum, reel) used for material handling and logistics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: serialized_part|assembly|finished_good|pallet|container|tote|drum|reel|handling_unit — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA WM Handling Unit Management

attributes.name: valuation_amount
attributes.description: Monetary valuation of this serialized unit for inventory accounting and financial reporting. Represents standard cost, moving average price, or actual cost depending on valuation method.

attributes.name: volume_cubic_meters
attributes.description: Total volume of the serialized unit or handling unit in cubic meters. Used for warehouse capacity planning and freight cost calculation.

attributes.name: width_cm
attributes.description: Width dimension of the serialized unit or handling unit in centimeters. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization.
attributes.references: ISO 3394 Packaging - Dimensions of Transport Packages

domains.name: supply
domains.description: Supply chain planning and demand management domain covering MRP/MRP II runs, demand forecasting, capacity planning, APS scheduling, supplier lead times, MOQ management, supply risk, multi-tier supplier coordination, and supply network optimization. Integrates Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM and SAP PP/MM for end-to-end supply network visibility.

products.name: mrp_run
products.description: Captures each MRP/MRP II planning run execution, recording planning horizon, run type (regenerative vs. net change), plant scope, planning parameters, BOM/routing explosion level, and completion status. Serves as the authoritative audit record of when and how material requirements were calculated, enabling traceability of all planned orders and material requirements back to their originating MRP execution in SAP PP/MM or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.

attributes.name: mrp_run_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the MRP/MRP II planning run execution. Primary key.

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: An MRP run is executed within the context of a master supply plan. The plan is the parent entity representing the approved supply response; mrp_run records each execution (regenerative, net change, et

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: MRP runs are executed per plant. mrp_run has plant_code as a plain denormalized attribute. A proper FK to production.plant enables plant-level MRP run management, multi-plant MRP comparison, and repla

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: MRP runs are executed at plant/warehouse scope in industrial manufacturing. plant_code on mrp_run is a denormalized reference to warehouse (which carries erp_plant_code). This FK enables plant-level M

attributes.name: actual_end_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual date and time when this MRP run completed or terminated. Used to calculate run duration and identify performance bottlenecks.

attributes.name: actual_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual date and time when this MRP run began execution. Key audit field for tracking planning run performance and compliance with scheduling windows.

attributes.name: bom_explosion_level
attributes.description: Maximum depth of BOM explosion performed during this MRP run. Indicates how many levels down the product structure the planning calculation extended (e.g., 1 for single-level, 99 for full multi-level explosion).

attributes.name: completion_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments entered by planners or system administrators regarding the outcome, issues, or special circumstances of this MRP run.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this MRP run record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: demand_time_fence_days
attributes.description: Number of days from the run date within which the MRP system does not automatically create, reschedule, or cancel planned orders. Defines the frozen zone where manual intervention is required.

attributes.name: error_messages_count
attributes.description: Total number of error messages generated during this MRP run that prevented successful completion or caused partial failures.

attributes.name: exception_messages_count
attributes.description: Total number of exception or warning messages generated during this MRP run, indicating planning issues such as material shortages, capacity overloads, or invalid master data.

attributes.name: include_forecast_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether demand forecast data was included in the MRP calculation (True) or only firm customer orders were considered (False).

attributes.name: include_safety_stock_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether safety stock requirements were included in the MRP calculation (True) or excluded (False).

attributes.name: include_wip_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether existing work-in-progress inventory was considered as available supply during this MRP run (True) or excluded (False).

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this MRP run record was last updated. Audit field for tracking changes to run status or metadata.

attributes.name: lead_time_offset_days
attributes.description: Global lead time offset (in days) applied to all planned orders during this MRP run to account for additional buffer time beyond standard procurement or production lead times.

attributes.name: lot_sizing_rule
attributes.description: Default lot sizing method applied during this MRP run for planned order quantity calculation: lot-for-lot (LFL), fixed lot size, economic order quantity (EOQ), or period order quantity (POQ).

attributes.name: materials_processed_count
attributes.description: Total number of material master records processed during this MRP run. Indicates the scope and scale of the planning calculation.

attributes.name: planned_orders_cancelled_count
attributes.description: Total number of existing planned orders that were cancelled by this MRP run because they were no longer needed to satisfy material requirements.

attributes.name: planned_orders_created_count
attributes.description: Total number of new planned orders (purchase requisitions or production orders) created by this MRP run to satisfy material requirements.

attributes.name: planned_orders_rescheduled_count
attributes.description: Total number of existing planned orders that were rescheduled (date or quantity changed) by this MRP run due to changes in demand or supply.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_days
attributes.description: Total number of days covered by the planning horizon for this MRP run, calculated from start to end date.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the planning horizon for this MRP run. Defines the end of the period for which material requirements are calculated.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the planning horizon for this MRP run. Defines the beginning of the period for which material requirements are calculated.

attributes.name: planning_mode
attributes.description: Manufacturing planning strategy applied during this MRP run: make-to-stock (MTS), make-to-order (MTO), assemble-to-order (ATO), or engineer-to-order (ETO).

attributes.name: planning_time_fence_days
attributes.description: Number of days from the run date within which the MRP system can reschedule but not create new planned orders. Defines the slushy zone between frozen and free planning periods.

attributes.name: routing_explosion_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether production routing and work center capacity requirements were calculated during this MRP run (True) or only material requirements were computed (False).

attributes.name: run_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Total elapsed time in minutes from actual start to actual end of the MRP run. Key performance indicator for planning system efficiency.
attributes.references: APICS CPIM - Planning System Performance Metrics

attributes.name: run_number
attributes.description: Business-facing identifier for the MRP run, typically system-generated or user-assigned for tracking and audit purposes.

attributes.name: run_parameters_json
attributes.description: JSON-formatted string containing additional MRP run configuration parameters and settings not captured in dedicated columns. Enables flexible storage of system-specific or advanced planning parameters.

attributes.name: run_status
attributes.description: Current execution status of the MRP run: scheduled (queued for execution), running (in progress), completed (successfully finished), failed (terminated with errors), or cancelled (manually aborted).
attributes.references: SAP PP/MM MRP Run Status Codes

attributes.name: run_type
attributes.description: Type of MRP planning run executed: regenerative (full recalculation of all materials), net_change (incremental update based on changes since last run), single_level (one BOM level), or multi_level (full BOM explosion).

attributes.name: safety_stock_method
attributes.description: Safety stock calculation method applied during this MRP run: fixed quantity, days of supply, statistical (based on demand variability), or none.

attributes.name: scheduled_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Planned date and time when this MRP run was scheduled to begin execution.

attributes.name: scheduling_method
attributes.description: Scheduling algorithm applied during this MRP run: forward (schedule from earliest start), backward (schedule from due date), finite (capacity-constrained), or infinite (no capacity constraints).

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the source ERP or planning system that executed this MRP run (e.g., SAP_PP, DYNAMICS_SCM, OPCENTER_APS).

products.name: planned_order
products.description: Represents a planned production, procurement, or stock transfer order generated by MRP/APS to satisfy a material requirement. Consolidates all replenishment proposals awaiting planner review through to firmed orders ready for conversion. Tracks planned quantity, scheduled start/finish dates, order type (production order, purchase requisition, stock transfer), priority, firming status, proposal lifecycle (open, under review, firmed, converted, rejected), planner override details, converting planner ID, and the material/plant combination. Serves as the single actionable output of MRP runs requiring human review before release to procurement or production. Sourced from SAP PP/MM and Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM planning outputs.

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the planned order generated by MRP/APS system. Primary key.
attributes.references: SAP PP/MM Planning Module, Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Make‑to‑order production planning requires linking each planned order to the originating customer account for order tracking and delivery scheduling.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Planned orders for external procurement are pegged to scheduling agreements or outline agreements governing price and delivery terms. This is standard SAP MM behavior: MRP references the agreement dur

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: In regulated industrial manufacturing (automotive IATF, aerospace AS9100), planned orders for externally procured parts must be traceable to an AVL entry for procurement compliance audits. This link e

attributes.name: bom_header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.bom_header. Business justification: Planned orders reference the manufacturing BOM header (product.bom_header) to determine component requirements during production. This is distinct from the engineering BOM (already linked via bom_id→e

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Reference to the bill of materials that defines the component requirements for this planned production order.
attributes.references: SAP PP BOM (Bill of Materials), Siemens Teamcenter PLM

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Supply proposal to component design traceability: planned orders are generated for specific components. Linking to engineering.component allows supply planners to validate make-or-buy decisions, lead

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: In CTO industrial manufacturing, planned orders are generated for specific product configurations (e.g., a configured automation panel). The configuration determines the BOM and routing used; planners

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Planned orders under blanket/framework contracts in industrial manufacturing must reference the governing sales contract to enforce contractual delivery schedules, call-off quantities, and pricing ter

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Procurement Cost Allocation Report assigns each planned order to a cost center for budgeting and cost accounting.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Planned orders in make‑to‑order environments originate from a specific sales order; linking enables the 'Planned Order to Sales Order Traceability' process.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record for which this planned order was generated.

attributes.name: material_requirement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: A planned order is generated by MRP to satisfy a specific material requirement — this is the core MRP pegging relationship. planned_order already has source_requirement_number and source_requirement_t

attributes.name: mrp_run_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the MRP or APS planning execution that generated this planned order.

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Planned orders are the primary output of a supply plan — each planned order represents a proposed production, procurement, or stock transfer action within the plan's horizon. While planned_order alrea

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Planned orders are generated using plant_data MRP parameters (lot sizes, lead times, procurement type, mrp_type). Linking planned_order to plant_data enables MRP audit traceability — planners can veri

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Planned orders are plant-specific in MRP. planned_order has plant_code as a plain denormalized attribute. A proper FK to production.plant enables plant-level planned order management and multi-plant s

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Contract-Governed Order Planning: Planned purchase orders must reference the governing procurement contract for price validation, contract utilization tracking, and compliance reporting. Manufacturing

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: Planned orders must reference the product revision they are manufacturing against for ECO traceability and regulatory compliance. In industrial manufacturing, producing against the wrong revision caus

attributes.name: production_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Planned orders in manufacturing are assigned to specific production lines for capacity loading and scheduling. MRP capacity planning requires line-level assignment to calculate available vs. required

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Reference to the production routing that defines the sequence of operations and work centers for manufacturing this material.

attributes.name: sales_order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Planned orders are created from confirmed order intake; linking enables order‑to‑production tracking.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Production planning ties each planned order to the SKU master for cost, compliance, and reporting of the manufactured product.

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: MRP planners in industrial manufacturing must schedule planned orders to meet contractual SLA delivery commitments. Linking planned_order to sla_agreement enables SLA-driven prioritization, on-time de

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the preferred supplier for this material when the order type is purchase requisition or subcontract order.
attributes.references: SAP MM Vendor Master, SAP Ariba Supplier Network

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Planned orders for external procurement specify the source supplier site for delivery scheduling. Site-level lead times, capacity, and quality scores are required for accurate scheduled_start_date and

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Planned orders are created for a specific manufacturing plant/warehouse. plant_code on planned_order is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level planned order repo

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Planned orders are assigned to work centers for capacity requirements planning (CRP). MRP generates work center load profiles from planned orders to identify overloads and underloads. No existing FK c

attributes.name: available_capacity_hours
attributes.description: Production capacity in hours available at the work center or plant for executing this planned order, used for capacity planning validation.
attributes.references: APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) Capacity Planning

attributes.name: converted_order_number
attributes.description: Document number of the production order, purchase requisition, or stock transfer order created when this planned order was converted to an executable order.
attributes.references: SAP PP Production Order, SAP MM Purchase Requisition

attributes.name: converted_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the planned order was converted to an executable order.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this planned order record was first created in the system by the planning run.

attributes.name: deletion_flag
attributes.description: Indicator that this planned order has been marked for deletion by a subsequent planning run because the requirement no longer exists or has been satisfied by other means.

attributes.name: exception_code
attributes.description: Code identifying planning exceptions or issues requiring planner intervention (e.g., capacity overload, material shortage, lead time violation, MOQ conflict).

attributes.name: exception_message
attributes.description: Detailed description of the planning exception or constraint violation that requires planner review and resolution.

attributes.name: firmed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the planned order was firmed by the planner.

attributes.name: firming_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this planned order has been firmed by the planner, preventing it from being automatically changed or deleted by subsequent planning runs.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this planned order record was last updated, either by a subsequent planning run or by manual planner intervention.

attributes.name: lot_size_rule
attributes.description: Lot sizing method applied by the planning system: exact_quantity (order exact requirement), moq (minimum order quantity), fixed_lot (standard batch size), or rounding (round up to packaging unit).

attributes.name: moq_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum order quantity constraint from the supplier or production process that was applied during lot sizing.
attributes.references: SAP MM Purchasing Info Record, MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) Management

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: Code identifying the planner or planning group responsible for reviewing and converting this planned order.
attributes.references: SAP PP MRP Controller Master Data

attributes.name: multi_tier_supplier_flag
attributes.description: Indicator that this planned order requires coordination across multiple tiers of the supply network (e.g., raw material supplier, component manufacturer, final assembly).
attributes.references: Supply Network Optimization, Multi-Tier Supply Chain Management

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Type of replenishment action proposed by the planning system: production order for in-house manufacturing, purchase requisition for external procurement, stock transfer for inter-plant movement, or subcontract order for external processing.
attributes.references: SAP PP Order Types, MRP II Planning Logic

attributes.name: planned_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of material proposed for replenishment by the planning run, expressed in base unit of measure.

attributes.name: planner_notes
attributes.description: Free-text comments entered by the planner documenting decisions, constraints, or coordination details related to this planned order.

attributes.name: planner_override_date
attributes.description: Manually adjusted scheduled finish date entered by the planner to override the system-calculated date based on capacity constraints or customer negotiation.

attributes.name: planner_override_quantity
attributes.description: Manually adjusted quantity entered by the planner to override the system-calculated planned quantity based on business judgment or known constraints.

attributes.name: planning_run_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the planning run that created this planned order was executed.

attributes.name: priority_code
attributes.description: Planning priority assigned to this order based on demand urgency, customer importance, or supply risk. Critical orders require immediate planner attention.
attributes.references: APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) Priority Logic

attributes.name: procurement_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Number of days required to procure or produce the material, used by the planning system to calculate scheduled start date from requirement date.
attributes.references: SAP MM Material Master Lead Time, MRP II Lead Time Logic

attributes.name: proposal_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the planned order proposal: open (awaiting planner review), under_review (being evaluated), firmed (approved for conversion), converted (released to execution), rejected (not needed), or cancelled (superseded by new planning run).
attributes.references: SAP PP Planned Order Status, MRP II Workflow

attributes.name: required_capacity_hours
attributes.description: Production capacity in hours required to complete this planned order based on routing and quantity.
attributes.references: SAP PP Routing, APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) Capacity Planning

attributes.name: requirement_date
attributes.description: Date when the material is needed to fulfill a sales order, production order component, or safety stock replenishment.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: Buffer stock level maintained to protect against demand variability or supply disruptions, factored into the planning calculation.
attributes.references: MRP (Material Requirements Planning) Safety Stock Logic

attributes.name: scheduled_finish_date
attributes.description: Planned date when the material should be available to satisfy the downstream requirement or customer demand.
attributes.references: MRP II Scheduling Logic, APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling)

attributes.name: scheduled_start_date
attributes.description: Planned date when production or procurement activities should begin to meet the requirement date, calculated backward from finish date using lead time.
attributes.references: MRP II Scheduling Logic, APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling)

attributes.name: source_requirement_number
attributes.description: Document number of the originating requirement (sales order, production order, forecast entry) that drove the creation of this planned order.
attributes.references: SAP SD Sales Order, SAP PP Production Order

attributes.name: source_requirement_type
attributes.description: Type of demand that triggered the creation of this planned order: sales order (customer demand), production order (component requirement), safety stock (replenishment), forecast (anticipated demand), or service order (spare parts).

attributes.name: supply_risk_score
attributes.description: Quantitative risk assessment score (0-100) indicating the likelihood of supply disruption for this planned order based on supplier performance, lead time volatility, and geopolitical factors.
attributes.references: Supply Chain Risk Management, Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM Risk Analytics

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Base unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter).

products.name: demand_forecast
products.description: Stores version-aware demand forecast records supporting scenario comparison and consensus planning. Captures statistical baseline forecasts, sales-adjusted forecasts, consensus forecasts, and approved forecasts with full version lineage. Each record includes forecast quantity, forecast horizon, forecast model used, confidence interval, demand class (independent vs. dependent), item/location combination, version type, version approval workflow status, and planning period. Supports S&OP processes by maintaining multiple demand scenarios for supply planning sensitivity analysis. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM demand planning module.

attributes.name: demand_forecast_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the demand forecast record. Primary key for the demand forecast entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑specific demand forecasts are generated per account to support sales‑aligned planning and KPI reporting.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Industrial manufacturers forecast demand at the customer ship-to site level for large accounts with multiple plants. Site-level demand forecasting drives logistics capacity planning and inventory posi

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: In configure-to-order industrial manufacturing, demand forecasts are created at the configuration level (e.g., specific voltage/power variant of a drive system) to support S&OP and ATP processes. Plan

attributes.name: demand_plan_version_id
attributes.description: Reference to the forecast version record enabling scenario comparison and version lineage tracking.

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Demand forecasts in industrial manufacturing are generated at the product family level using statistical models, then disaggregated to SKU. This FK supports family-level forecast accuracy reporting, S

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material or item being forecasted. Links to the material master record in inventory domain.

attributes.name: opportunity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: S&OP process in industrial manufacturing explicitly reconciles demand forecasts against the sales opportunity pipeline. Planners need to trace which opportunity seeded a forecast entry for pipeline-ba

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Demand forecasting in industrial manufacturing is structured by customer segment (OEM, distributor, process industry). Planners generate and review segment-level forecasts for capacity and supply plan

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Forecasting uses the SKU master to align demand quantities with product specifications and pricing.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the planning location, warehouse, or distribution center for which demand is forecasted.

attributes.name: approval_comments
attributes.description: Comments or notes provided by the approver during the forecast approval workflow, documenting decision rationale or conditions.

attributes.name: bias_percent
attributes.description: Systematic forecast bias expressed as percentage, indicating tendency to over-forecast (positive) or under-forecast (negative). Used to detect and correct systematic errors.

attributes.name: confidence_interval_lower
attributes.description: Lower bound of the forecast confidence interval, representing the pessimistic demand scenario for risk analysis and safety stock planning.

attributes.name: confidence_interval_upper
attributes.description: Upper bound of the forecast confidence interval, representing the optimistic demand scenario for capacity planning and supply risk assessment.

attributes.name: confidence_level_percent
attributes.description: Statistical confidence level of the forecast interval expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 for 95% confidence). Used for supply planning sensitivity analysis.

attributes.name: consensus_approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the forecast achieved consensus approval in the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) process, marking it as the agreed demand plan.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: demand_class
attributes.description: Classification of demand type: independent (end-customer driven, forecasted) or dependent (derived from BOM and parent item demand via MRP explosion).

attributes.name: demand_pattern
attributes.description: Characterization of the historical demand behavior pattern used to select appropriate forecasting models and safety stock strategies.

attributes.name: forecast_accuracy_percent
attributes.description: Historical accuracy percentage of the forecast model for this material-location combination, calculated as (1 - MAPE) * 100. Used for model selection and tuning.

attributes.name: forecast_consumption_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this forecast is subject to consumption by actual customer orders in the MRP logic. Controls forecast vs. order-driven planning behavior.

attributes.name: forecast_generation_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the forecast was generated by the forecasting engine or planning system. Represents the business event time of forecast creation.

attributes.name: forecast_horizon_days
attributes.description: Number of days into the future that this forecast covers, calculated from the planning period start date. Used for lead time coverage analysis.
