attributes.name: forecast_model_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the statistical or algorithmic model used to generate the forecast (e.g., ARIMA, EXP_SMOOTH, MOVING_AVG, ML_ENSEMBLE).

attributes.name: forecast_model_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the forecasting model or algorithm applied to generate this forecast.

attributes.name: forecast_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the forecast record, externally visible and used for tracking and reference in planning meetings.

attributes.name: forecast_quantity
attributes.description: Predicted demand quantity for the material at the specified location during the planning period. Core forecast value used for MRP and capacity planning.

attributes.name: forecast_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the forecast record in the approval workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval or rejection.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this forecast record, tracking manual adjustments or workflow updates.

attributes.name: mean_absolute_percentage_error
attributes.description: MAPE metric measuring forecast error as a percentage of actual demand. Lower values indicate better forecast accuracy. Key KPI for demand planning performance.

attributes.name: mrp_area_code
attributes.description: MRP area code defining the planning scope and organizational unit for material requirements planning execution.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about this forecast record, capturing planner insights, assumptions, or special considerations.

attributes.name: outlier_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this forecast was flagged as a statistical outlier requiring review. Used for data quality monitoring and exception management.

attributes.name: outlier_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for why this forecast was flagged as an outlier, documenting the business or statistical reason for the exception.

attributes.name: planning_group_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the planning group or demand planner responsible for this forecast. Used for workload distribution and accountability.

attributes.name: planning_period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the planning period for which this forecast applies. Defines the end of the demand horizon.

attributes.name: planning_period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the planning period for which this forecast applies. Defines the beginning of the demand horizon.

attributes.name: product_lifecycle_stage
attributes.description: Current stage of the product in its lifecycle. Influences forecasting model selection and demand pattern expectations.

attributes.name: promotional_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this forecast includes promotional or campaign-driven demand uplift. Used to separate base demand from promotional spikes.

attributes.name: promotional_uplift_percent
attributes.description: Expected percentage increase in demand due to promotional activities during the planning period. Used for promotional planning and inventory pre-positioning.

attributes.name: sales_adjustment_quantity
attributes.description: Manual adjustment quantity applied by sales team to the statistical baseline forecast, reflecting market intelligence and customer commitments.

attributes.name: sales_adjustment_reason
attributes.description: Business justification for the sales adjustment, documenting market intelligence, customer commitments, or promotional activities driving the override.

attributes.name: scenario_name
attributes.description: Name of the planning scenario this forecast belongs to (e.g., Base Case, Optimistic, Pessimistic, New Product Launch). Enables scenario comparison and sensitivity analysis.

attributes.name: seasonality_index
attributes.description: Seasonal adjustment factor applied to the forecast for this planning period. Values above 1.0 indicate peak season, below 1.0 indicate off-season.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the source system that generated or provided this forecast record (e.g., D365_SCM, SAP_APO, CUSTOM_MODEL).

attributes.name: trend_coefficient
attributes.description: Trend component coefficient from the forecasting model, indicating the rate of demand growth or decline over time.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the forecast quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Must align with material master base UOM.

attributes.name: version_type
attributes.description: Classification of the forecast version: baseline (statistical model output), sales_adjusted (sales team input), consensus (S&OP aligned), approved (final for MRP), or working (draft).

products.name: plan
products.description: Master supply plan record representing the approved supply response to demand for a given planning period, plant, and material group. Captures planned supply quantities by time bucket, planning method (MRP, APS, manual), plan version, approval status, planning parameters (strategy, horizon, time fences, lot-sizing procedure, MRP controller), and variance against demand forecast. Integrates outputs from SAP PP and Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM APS scheduling into a unified supply position for S&OP review.
products.reference: APS Supply Plan / SAP PP - Planning Table

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Primary key for plan

attributes.name: budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: S&OP Budget Alignment: Supply plans must reference the finance budget they operate under for budget vs. actual variance reporting in S&OP. Manufacturing finance controllers require this link to valida

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Long-term framework contracts in industrial manufacturing define committed supply volumes that directly drive supply plans. Planners reference the governing contract to enforce contractual delivery sc

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supply Plan vs Budget variance analysis requires linking each supply plan to its responsible cost center.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Specific material or SKU for which this supply plan is created. May be null for aggregate material group plans.

attributes.name: opportunity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Large strategic opportunities (e.g., major automation system projects) trigger dedicated supply plans in industrial manufacturing. Supply planners must trace which opportunity drove a plan for S&OP fe

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Supply plans are created for a specific material-plant combination; plant_data holds the MRP parameters (lot_size_procedure, planned_delivery_time_days, procurement_type) the plan is based on. This FK

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Supply plans are plant-specific in manufacturing. plan has plant_code as a plain denormalized attribute. A proper FK to production.plant enables plant-level supply plan filtering, multi-plant planning

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: Supply plans in industrial manufacturing are revision-sensitive — an ECO-driven product revision may invalidate existing plans (BOM changes, process changes). Linking plan to product_revision enables

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Supply plans reference the SKU master to allocate production capacity and material supply per product.

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Supply plans are created at plant/warehouse level in manufacturing. plant_code on plan is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level supply plan reporting, warehouse

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the supply plan was approved, marking the transition from planning to execution phase.

attributes.name: capacity_utilization_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of available production or procurement capacity utilized by this supply plan, indicating capacity constraints.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this supply plan record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: demand_forecast_quantity
attributes.description: Total forecasted demand quantity for the planning period, serving as the primary input to the supply plan.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this supply plan record was last updated, tracking the most recent change to planning parameters or status.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Total lead time in days from order placement to material availability, including procurement, production, and transportation time.

attributes.name: lot_sizing_procedure
attributes.description: Lot-sizing rule used to determine planned order quantities: LOT_FOR_LOT (exact requirement), FIXED_LOT (fixed quantity), EOQ (Economic Order Quantity), POQ (Periodic Order Quantity), or PERIOD_LOT (period-based aggregation).

attributes.name: material_group_code
attributes.description: Material group or product family covered by this supply plan, enabling aggregated planning at the product category level.

attributes.name: maximum_lot_size
attributes.description: Maximum order quantity constraint applied during supply planning, reflecting capacity or storage limitations.

attributes.name: minimum_lot_size
attributes.description: Minimum order quantity (MOQ) constraint applied during supply planning, reflecting supplier or production constraints.

attributes.name: mrp_controller_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the MRP controller or planner responsible for this supply plan, enabling workload distribution and accountability.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes and comments from planners regarding assumptions, constraints, or special considerations for this supply plan.

attributes.name: plan_number
attributes.description: Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan, used for cross-system reference and S&OP review documentation.

attributes.name: plan_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the supply plan: DRAFT (under development), PENDING_REVIEW (awaiting S&OP approval), APPROVED (approved for execution), ACTIVE (currently executing), SUPERSEDED (replaced by newer version), or CANCELLED (voided).

attributes.name: planned_supply_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity of material planned to be supplied during the planning period, aggregated across all time buckets.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_days
attributes.description: Number of days into the future covered by this supply plan, defining the forward-looking planning window.

attributes.name: planning_method
attributes.description: Method used to generate the supply plan: MRP (Material Requirements Planning), MRP II (Manufacturing Resource Planning), APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling), MANUAL (manual planning), JIT (Just In Time), or KANBAN (pull-based replenishment).

attributes.name: planning_period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan.

attributes.name: planning_period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan.

attributes.name: planning_run_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the MRP/APS planning run was executed to generate this supply plan.

attributes.name: planning_strategy
attributes.description: Supply planning strategy: MTS (Make to Stock), MTO (Make to Order), ATO (Assemble to Order), or ETO (Engineer to Order).

attributes.name: planning_time_fence_days
attributes.description: Number of days from today within which the planning system will not automatically change planned orders, protecting near-term execution stability.

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Source of supply: IN_HOUSE (internal production), EXTERNAL (external procurement from suppliers), or BOTH (mixed sourcing).

attributes.name: reorder_point_quantity
attributes.description: Reorder point quantity that triggers replenishment planning when stock falls below this threshold.

attributes.name: rounding_value
attributes.description: Rounding increment for planned order quantities, ensuring orders align with packaging or production batch multiples.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: Safety stock level maintained as buffer against demand variability and supply uncertainty, factored into the supply plan.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the source system that generated this supply plan: SAP_PP (SAP Production Planning), DYNAMICS_SCM (Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management), OPCENTER (Siemens Opcenter APS), or MANUAL (manually created).

attributes.name: supply_risk_level
attributes.description: Assessed risk level for supply availability: LOW (stable supply), MEDIUM (minor risks), HIGH (significant risks), or CRITICAL (severe supply constraints).

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the planned supply quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, M for meter).

attributes.name: variance_percentage
attributes.description: Variance expressed as a percentage of demand forecast, enabling normalized comparison across materials.

attributes.name: variance_quantity
attributes.description: Variance between planned supply quantity and demand forecast quantity, indicating over-planning or under-planning.

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version identifier for the supply plan, enabling comparison of multiple planning scenarios and what-if analysis.

products.name: capacity_plan
products.description: Records capacity planning data for work centers and production resources, including available capacity, required capacity derived from planned orders, utilization rate, bottleneck flags, and capacity leveling adjustments. Supports rough-cut capacity planning (RCCP) and detailed capacity planning (CRP) as executed in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter APS. Enables identification of capacity constraints before production scheduling.

attributes.name: capacity_plan_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the capacity plan record. Primary key for capacity planning analysis.

attributes.name: budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Capacity Budget Authorization: Capacity plans for overtime, tooling, and resource acquisition require authorization against a finance budget. Industrial manufacturing operations require this link for

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capacity Cost Allocation process charges capacity usage to cost centers for internal cost tracking.

attributes.name: mrp_run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.mrp_run. Business justification: A capacity plan is produced by a specific MRP run — the run triggers BOM explosion, routing explosion, and capacity load calculation. capacity_plan already carries last_mrp_run_date as a denormalized

attributes.name: opportunity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, large opportunities (e.g., factory automation projects) trigger dedicated capacity plans to assess production feasibility before committing. Capacity planners need to link

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: A capacity plan is a sub-component of the master supply plan, recording work center and resource capacity data in support of the overall supply response. The plan entity is the parent; capacity_plan i

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the work center is located. Enables multi-plant capacity planning.

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: Capacity plans use routing data to calculate required capacity hours per work center. The routing defines operation sequences and standard times that feed directly into capacity requirements planning.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Capacity plans in industrial manufacturing include subcontracted and outsourced work center capacity. When a work center is operated by an external supplier, the capacity plan must reference the suppl

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the work center or production resource for which capacity is being planned. Links to the production facility or machine group.

attributes.name: available_capacity_hours
attributes.description: Total available capacity in hours for the work center during the planning period. Calculated based on shifts, calendar days, and resource availability.

attributes.name: capacity_buffer_hours
attributes.description: Safety buffer or protective capacity reserved to absorb variability and prevent schedule disruptions. Part of Theory of Constraints buffer management.

attributes.name: capacity_category
attributes.description: Classification of the capacity resource based on criticality to production flow. Critical resources are closely monitored for bottlenecks.
attributes.references: Theory of Constraints, Siemens Opcenter APS

attributes.name: capacity_load_profile
attributes.description: Pattern of capacity demand over the planning period. Indicates whether load is evenly distributed, concentrated in peaks, or highly variable.

attributes.name: capacity_source_system
attributes.description: Source system that generated the capacity plan data. Typically SAP PP, Siemens Opcenter APS, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.

attributes.name: capacity_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for capacity planning. Typically hours for time-based capacity or units/pieces for output-based capacity.

attributes.name: capacity_utilization_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of available capacity that is utilized by required capacity. Calculated as (required_capacity_hours / available_capacity_hours) * 100. Key metric for identifying overload or underutilization.
attributes.references: ISO 22400 KPI for Manufacturing, OEE Standards

attributes.name: critical_ratio
attributes.description: Ratio of time remaining until due date to work time remaining. Used for priority scheduling and capacity allocation. Values less than 1.0 indicate behind schedule.

attributes.name: efficiency_rate
attributes.description: Efficiency factor applied to available capacity to account for actual performance versus standard. Expressed as percentage. Used to adjust theoretical capacity to realistic capacity.

attributes.name: is_bottleneck
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this work center is identified as a bottleneck constraint in the production schedule. True if utilization exceeds threshold or if this resource limits throughput.
attributes.references: Theory of Constraints, Siemens Opcenter APS

attributes.name: leveling_adjustment_hours
attributes.description: Capacity adjustment applied through leveling actions such as overtime, shift changes, subcontracting, or order rescheduling to balance load.
attributes.references: SAP PP Capacity Leveling, Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM

attributes.name: leveling_strategy
attributes.description: Strategy applied to resolve capacity overload. Indicates the method used to balance capacity constraints.

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: MRP controller code responsible for the materials and capacity planning for this work center. Links capacity planning to material planning.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments from the planner regarding capacity constraints, assumptions, or leveling decisions.

attributes.name: overload_hours
attributes.description: Number of hours by which required capacity exceeds available capacity. Positive value indicates capacity shortage requiring leveling or additional resources.

attributes.name: plan_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the capacity plan record was created in the system.

attributes.name: plan_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the capacity plan. Indicates whether the plan is in draft, approved for execution, actively used, or archived.

attributes.name: plan_type
attributes.description: Type of capacity planning method applied. RCCP (Rough-Cut Capacity Planning) for aggregate planning, CRP (Capacity Requirements Planning) for detailed planning, infinite for unconstrained, finite for constrained scheduling.
attributes.references: SAP PP Capacity Planning, APICS Dictionary

attributes.name: plan_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the capacity plan record was last updated or recalculated.

attributes.name: planned_downtime_hours
attributes.description: Hours of planned maintenance, changeovers, or scheduled downtime that reduce available capacity during the planning period.
attributes.references: TPM Total Productive Maintenance, SAP PM

attributes.name: planning_horizon
attributes.description: Time granularity of the capacity plan. Indicates whether this is a short-term, medium-term, or long-term capacity plan.

attributes.name: planning_period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the capacity planning period. Defines the end of the time bucket for capacity analysis.

attributes.name: planning_period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the capacity planning period. Defines the beginning of the time bucket for capacity analysis.

attributes.name: planning_version
attributes.description: Version identifier for the capacity plan. Supports scenario planning and what-if analysis by maintaining multiple plan versions.

attributes.name: queue_time_hours
attributes.description: Average queue time or wait time for jobs at this work center during the planning period. Indicates congestion and scheduling delays.

attributes.name: required_capacity_hours
attributes.description: Total required capacity in hours derived from planned orders, production orders, and demand forecasts for the planning period.

attributes.name: resource_type
attributes.description: Type of production resource being planned. Distinguishes between machine capacity, labor capacity, tooling, or other equipment.

attributes.name: run_time_hours
attributes.description: Total productive run time or processing time required for planned orders during the planning period.

attributes.name: setup_time_hours
attributes.description: Total setup or changeover time required during the planning period. Reduces available productive capacity.
attributes.references: SMED Single-Minute Exchange of Die, Lean Manufacturing

attributes.name: shift_count
attributes.description: Number of production shifts scheduled for the work center during the planning period. Used to calculate available capacity.

attributes.name: shift_hours_per_day
attributes.description: Total productive hours per day across all shifts. Excludes breaks and non-productive time.

attributes.name: underload_hours
attributes.description: Number of hours by which available capacity exceeds required capacity. Positive value indicates spare capacity available for additional orders or maintenance.

attributes.name: unplanned_downtime_hours
attributes.description: Estimated or historical hours of unplanned downtime (breakdowns, failures) factored into capacity planning to provide realistic capacity estimates.
attributes.references: MTBF Mean Time Between Failures, MTTR Mean Time To Repair

products.name: material_requirement
products.description: Individual material requirement record produced by MRP, representing the net requirement for a specific material at a specific plant and date. Captures gross requirement, scheduled receipts, projected available balance, net requirement, lot-size adjusted requirement, and the requirement source (sales order, forecast, safety stock). Core output of SAP PP MRP runs used to drive procurement and production planning.

attributes.name: material_requirement_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the material requirement record generated by MRP run.

attributes.name: bom_header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.bom_header. Business justification: Material requirements are generated via BOM explosion from a specific manufacturing BOM header. Linking material_requirement to bom_header enables MRP audit traceability — planners and quality enginee

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: MRP BOM explosion traceability: each material_requirement is generated by exploding a specific BOM. Planners and engineers need this link to perform ECO impact analysis — identifying which open requir

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: BOM explosion component traceability: material_requirement records are the direct output of BOM explosion per component. Linking to engineering.component enables ECO impact analysis — planners identif

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Material Requirement Cost Tracking records which cost center bears the expense of each material requirement.

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Engineering change impact on open requirements: when an ECO changes a component revision, supply planners must identify all material_requirements generated against the superseded revision. This link e

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: MRP demand pegging: material requirements are directly traceable to the sales order that created the demand. ATP (Available-to-Promise) checks, demand pegging reports, and exception management in indu

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record for which this requirement was generated.

attributes.name: mrp_run_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific MRP execution run that generated this requirement record.

attributes.name: order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: MRP pegging in industrial manufacturing traces material requirements directly to the sales order (order_intake) that created the demand. This pegging traceability is essential for order-level ATP (Ava

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Individual material requirements are produced as outputs of a supply plan's MRP execution. The plan is the governing parent record; material_requirement records are the net requirement lines generated

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Material requirements are calculated using plant_data parameters (reorder_point, safety_stock_quantity, lot_size_procedure, mrp_type). This FK enables MRP audit reports to verify which plant-material

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Material requirements are plant-specific in MRP. material_requirement has plant_code as a plain denormalized attribute. A proper FK to production.plant enables plant-level requirement reporting and mu

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Contract-Driven MRP: Material requirements must reference the procurement contract governing supply to ensure MRP respects contracted lead times, MOQs, and pricing. Industrial manufacturing contract c

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Material requirements are linked to the SKU master to calculate net requirements for the exact product.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: MRP material requirements are pegged to specific storage locations in manufacturing plants. storage_location_code on material_requirement is a denormalized plain-text reference. This FK enables requir

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Material requirement records assign a preferred supplier for each requirement to drive procurement planning and lead‑time estimation.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: MRP material requirements for externally procured items must identify the specific supplier site for delivery scheduling. Site-level lead times, MOQ, and capacity constrain the requirement date and pl

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Material requirements in MRP are plant-scoped. plant_code on material_requirement is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level material requirement analysis and cro

attributes.name: abc_indicator
attributes.description: Classification of material importance based on consumption value: A (high value, tight control), B (moderate value), C (low value, loose control).

attributes.name: bom_explosion_date
attributes.description: The date on which the BOM was exploded to generate dependent requirements for component materials.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this material requirement record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: exception_message_code
attributes.description: SAP exception code indicating planning issues such as late orders, missing parts, excess stock, or capacity overload.

attributes.name: exception_message_text
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the planning exception or issue requiring planner attention.
attributes.references: SAP PP MRP Exception Message Catalog

attributes.name: firming_date
attributes.description: Date after which this planned order is frozen and will not be automatically rescheduled or changed by subsequent MRP runs.

attributes.name: goods_receipt_processing_time_days
attributes.description: Number of days required for receiving, inspecting, and putting away material after physical arrival.
attributes.references: SAP MM Material Master MRP View

attributes.name: gross_requirement_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity of material required before considering available stock or scheduled receipts.

attributes.name: in_house_production_time_days
attributes.description: Total manufacturing lead time in days from production order release to finished goods availability.
attributes.references: SAP PP Routing and Work Center Data

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this material requirement record was last updated or recalculated.

attributes.name: lot_size_key
attributes.description: SAP lot-sizing procedure code applied to this requirement: EX (lot-for-lot), HB (replenish to maximum), FX (fixed lot size), WB (weekly lot size), PD (period lot size).

attributes.name: maximum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Largest quantity that can be ordered or produced in a single transaction due to capacity, storage, or supplier constraints.
attributes.references: SAP PP Material Master MRP View

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Smallest quantity that can be ordered from supplier or produced in a single production run.

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: Three-character code identifying the planner or buyer responsible for managing this material requirement.
attributes.references: SAP PP MRP Controller Master Data

attributes.name: mrp_element_type
attributes.description: Classification of the MRP element that created this requirement: sales order, forecast, safety stock replenishment, dependent requirement from BOM explosion, planned order, or purchase requisition.

attributes.name: mrp_run_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the MRP run that generated this requirement record was executed.

attributes.name: net_requirement_quantity
attributes.description: The actual shortage quantity that must be procured or produced, calculated as gross requirement minus available stock and scheduled receipts.
attributes.references: SAP PP MRP Net Requirement Calculation

attributes.name: pegging_requirement_reference
attributes.description: Reference to the parent requirement that triggered this dependent requirement through BOM explosion (used for requirement traceability).
attributes.references: SAP PP Pegging and Where-Used Analysis

attributes.name: planned_delivery_time_days
attributes.description: Expected lead time in days from order placement to material receipt, used for backward scheduling.

attributes.name: planned_order_quantity
attributes.description: The lot-size adjusted quantity that MRP proposes to procure or produce, after applying lot-sizing rules (MOQ, rounding, fixed lot size).

attributes.name: planning_time_fence_days
attributes.description: Number of days into the future during which MRP will not automatically create or change planned orders without planner approval.
attributes.references: SAP PP Planning Time Fence Configuration

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Indicates how the material is procured: E (in-house production), F (external procurement), X (both).
attributes.references: SAP MM Material Master Procurement View

attributes.name: projected_available_balance
attributes.description: Projected on-hand inventory balance after accounting for gross requirements and scheduled receipts up to this requirement date.

attributes.name: reorder_point_quantity
attributes.description: Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered to avoid stockout.

attributes.name: requirement_date
attributes.description: The date on which the material is needed to meet demand or production schedule.

attributes.name: requirement_priority
attributes.description: Numeric priority ranking used to sequence requirements when allocating limited supply or production capacity (lower number = higher priority).

attributes.name: requirement_source_item
attributes.description: The line item number within the source document that triggered this requirement.

attributes.name: requirement_source_number
attributes.description: The document number of the source that triggered this requirement (e.g., sales order number, forecast ID, planned order number).

attributes.name: requirement_status
attributes.description: Current fulfillment status of the material requirement: open (not yet covered), partially covered (some supply allocated), fully covered (sufficient supply allocated), cancelled, or expired.

attributes.name: rounding_value
attributes.description: Quantity increment to which planned orders are rounded up (e.g., round to nearest pallet quantity or container size).
attributes.references: SAP PP Lot Sizing Rounding Profile

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum inventory level maintained as buffer against demand variability and supply uncertainty.

attributes.name: scheduled_receipt_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of material expected to be received from open purchase orders, production orders, or transfer orders by the requirement date.

attributes.name: special_procurement_type
attributes.description: Code indicating special procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or third-party order processing.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit in which material quantities are expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter).

products.name: sourcing_rule
products.description: Defines the complete sourcing logic, lot-sizing constraints, and MOQ parameters for each material-plant-supplier combination. Specifies preferred suppliers, make-vs-buy decisions, split sourcing percentages, sourcing priority, applicable date ranges, Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ), maximum order quantity, lot size rounding values, fixed lot sizes, order quantity constraints, MOQ negotiation history, and planner-approved exceptions. These rules directly govern how MRP generates planned purchase requisitions or production orders and enforce procurement cost optimization through lot-size calculations. Managed in SAP MM and Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.
products.reference: SAP MM - Source List / Quota Arrangement

attributes.name: sourcing_rule_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the sourcing rule record. Primary key.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Sourcing rules in ERP (SAP MM) reference scheduling agreements or outline agreements that govern price, delivery terms, and volume commitments. MRP source determination uses this link to apply correct

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: Procurement compliance in industrial manufacturing requires sourcing rules to be traceable to an AVL entry. Purchasing governance audits verify that every active sourcing rule references a qualified,

attributes.name: blanket_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: Procurement sourcing rules in industrial manufacturing reference the blanket order/scheduling agreement that governs supply execution terms. Supply planners use this link to validate that sourcing rul

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Commodity-Based Sourcing Strategy: Sourcing rules in industrial manufacturing are governed by commodity categories that define pricing strategy, preferred suppliers, and sourcing approach. Category ma

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Inter-Company Sourcing Governance: Sourcing rules in multi-entity industrial manufacturing organizations are scoped to company codes. This link enables intercompany procurement compliance, legal entit

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Make-or-buy sourcing rule definition: sourcing rules in industrial manufacturing are established per component based on engineering make_or_buy designation, commodity_code, and supplier qualification

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record that this sourcing rule applies to. Links to the material being sourced.

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Sourcing rules in industrial manufacturing ERP are scoped to a material-plant combination. plant_data holds procurement_type and special_procurement_type that govern which sourcing rules apply. This F

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Sourcing rules (make-or-buy decisions, supplier allocation) are plant-specific in manufacturing. Each plant has distinct sourcing strategies. A plant FK enables plant-level sourcing rule management an

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Contract-Governed Sourcing: MRP sourcing rules must reference the procurement contract that governs supplier selection, pricing, and MOQ. Industrial manufacturing planners use this link to enforce con

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_info_record. Business justification: Purchase info records (PIRs) are the master data source for price, lead time, and MOQ used in sourcing rules during MRP source determination. In SAP MM, sourcing rules reference PIRs directly. Industr

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Sourcing rules are defined per SKU to manage make‑or‑buy, allocation percentages and supplier contracts.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier or vendor from whom the material is sourced under this rule. Links to the supplier master.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Sourcing rules specify which supplier SITE fulfills a material, not just the supplier. Site-level lead times, MOQ, and capacity are used in MRP source determination. Industrial manufacturing planners

attributes.name: allocation_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of total demand allocated to this sourcing rule when split sourcing is configured. Used for quota arrangements and multi-supplier strategies.

attributes.name: automatic_po_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether MRP is authorized to automatically convert planned orders to purchase orders without manual intervention for this sourcing rule.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who created this sourcing rule record.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this sourcing rule record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard price and other monetary values in this sourcing rule.

attributes.name: exception_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation for any exceptions or deviations from standard sourcing policies, such as emergency procurement, single-source justification, or temporary supplier changes.

attributes.name: fixed_lot_size
attributes.description: Fixed quantity that must be ordered when this sourcing rule is applied. MRP will generate orders in exact multiples of this lot size.

attributes.name: gr_processing_time_days
attributes.business_glossary_term: Goods Receipt (GR) Processing Time in Days
attributes.description: Number of days required for goods receipt processing, inspection, and put-away after material arrives at the plant. Included in total procurement lead time.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: Standard trade terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, insurance, and risk transfer in international transactions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: incoterms_location
attributes.description: Specific location or port associated with the Incoterms designation, defining the point of delivery or risk transfer.

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this sourcing rule record.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this sourcing rule record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: last_moq_negotiation_date
attributes.business_glossary_term: Last Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) Negotiation Date
attributes.description: Date when the MOQ was last negotiated or reviewed with the supplier, used to track contract refresh cycles and renegotiation schedules.

attributes.name: lot_size_rounding_value
attributes.description: Rounding increment for order quantities. MRP will round calculated requirements up to the nearest multiple of this value to align with packaging or production batch constraints.

attributes.name: lot_sizing_procedure
attributes.description: SAP lot-sizing procedure code defining how MRP calculates order quantities: EX (lot-for-lot), HB (fixed lot size), FB (replenish to maximum), PD (period lot size), WM (weekly lot size), MB (monthly lot size).

attributes.name: make_or_buy_indicator
attributes.description: Strategic decision indicator specifying whether the material is manufactured in-house (make), procured externally (buy), or both options are available.

attributes.name: maximum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase requisition or production order under this sourcing rule, based on supplier capacity or production constraints.

attributes.name: moq
attributes.description: Minimum Order Quantity negotiated with the supplier or defined by production constraints. MRP will not generate orders below this threshold.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-text notes or comments providing context, special instructions, or historical information about this sourcing rule.

attributes.name: order_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure in which orders are placed with the supplier (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Must be convertible to base UOM.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Code representing the negotiated payment terms with the supplier, defining due dates, discounts, and payment schedules.

attributes.name: planned_delivery_time_days
attributes.description: Expected lead time in calendar days from order placement to goods receipt for this material-plant-supplier combination. Used by MRP for backward scheduling.

attributes.name: planner_approved_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the sourcing rule has been reviewed and approved by the material planner or supply chain manager for use in MRP runs.

attributes.name: preferred_supplier_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator marking this supplier as the preferred source for the material-plant combination. Used to prioritize sourcing decisions when multiple suppliers are available.

attributes.name: price_unit
attributes.description: Quantity unit to which the standard price applies. For example, if price_unit is 100, the standard_price represents the cost per 100 units.

attributes.name: purchasing_group
attributes.description: Code identifying the purchasing group or buyer responsible for managing procurement activities under this sourcing rule.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization
attributes.description: Code identifying the purchasing organization responsible for negotiating and executing procurement under this sourcing rule.

attributes.name: quota_arrangement_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this sourcing rule is part of a quota arrangement where demand is split across multiple suppliers based on allocation percentages.

attributes.name: rule_code
attributes.description: Business-assigned unique code identifying this sourcing rule for operational reference and reporting.

attributes.name: rule_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the sourcing rule for business user identification and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: rule_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the sourcing rule indicating whether it is active and available for MRP, temporarily blocked, awaiting approval, or expired.

attributes.name: sourcing_priority
attributes.description: Numeric ranking indicating the preference order when multiple sourcing rules exist for the same material-plant combination. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.

attributes.name: sourcing_type
attributes.description: Classification of the sourcing method: external procurement from supplier, in-house production, subcontracting, stock transfer between plants, or consignment arrangement.

attributes.name: standard_price
attributes.description: Standard negotiated unit price for the material from this supplier, used for cost estimation and procurement planning. Excludes taxes and freight.

attributes.name: supply_risk_level
attributes.description: Assessment of supply chain risk associated with this sourcing rule, considering supplier reliability, geopolitical factors, lead time variability, and single-source dependency.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: Start date from which this sourcing rule becomes effective and can be used by MRP for procurement planning.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: End date after which this sourcing rule is no longer valid and will not be considered by MRP. Null indicates indefinite validity.

products.name: safety_stock_policy
products.description: Defines safety stock parameters and reorder point policies for each material-plant combination, including safety stock quantity, safety days of supply, service level target, reorder point, maximum stock level, and the calculation method (static, dynamic, statistical). Captures the business rules governing buffer stock levels to protect against demand variability and supplier lead time uncertainty. Managed in SAP MM material master planning data.
products.reference: SAP MM - Material Master MRP Data (MM02)

attributes.name: safety_stock_policy_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the safety stock policy record.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Critical component safety stock governance: safety stock policies for industrial manufacturing components are driven by engineering attributes — lead_time_days, abc_classification, hazardous_material_

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory Holding Cost Allocation: Safety stock policies incur holding costs (already tracked as holding_cost_percent_annual) that must be allocated to a cost center. Manufacturing finance teams requi

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record for which this safety stock policy is defined.

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Safety stock policies are defined per material-plant combination and directly govern the plant_data safety_stock_quantity and reorder_point fields. This FK links the policy to the plant_data record it

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Safety stock policies are plant-specific in manufacturing. supply_safety_stock_policy has plant_code as a plain denormalized attribute. A proper FK to production.plant enables plant-level safety stock

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Safety‑stock policies are applied per SKU to meet service‑level targets and calculate reorder points.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Safety stock policies in industrial manufacturing are defined at the storage location level. storage_location_code on supply_safety_stock_policy is a denormalized plain-text reference to stock_locatio

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Safety‑stock policies are often defined per supplier to reflect reliability and lead‑time variability, required for accurate buffer calculations.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Safety stock policies are site-specific: a supplier site's lead time variability and capacity directly drive safety stock calculations. Planners need to reference the specific supplier site to apply c

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Safety stock policies are plant-scoped in industrial manufacturing. plant_code on supply_safety_stock_policy is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level safety sto

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: Inventory classification based on value and criticality: A (high-value, tight control), B (moderate-value), C (low-value, loose control).

attributes.name: average_daily_demand
attributes.description: Mean daily consumption or usage rate of the material, used as input for reorder point and safety stock calculations.

attributes.name: calculation_method
attributes.description: The methodology used to determine safety stock levels: static (fixed quantity), dynamic (demand-driven), statistical (based on demand variability and service level), or manual (user-defined).

attributes.name: coverage_profile
attributes.description: Six-character code referencing a time-phased coverage profile used in advanced planning systems for dynamic safety stock calculation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this safety stock policy record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: criticality_code
attributes.description: Business criticality rating indicating the impact of stockout on operations: critical (production stoppage), high (significant disruption), medium (moderate impact), low (minimal impact).

attributes.name: demand_variability_coefficient
attributes.description: Statistical measure of demand fluctuation (coefficient of variation or standard deviation) used in statistical safety stock calculations.

attributes.name: effective_from_date
attributes.description: The date from which this safety stock policy becomes active and is used in MRP calculations.

attributes.name: effective_to_date
attributes.description: The date on which this safety stock policy expires or is superseded by a new policy. Null indicates an open-ended policy.

attributes.name: holding_cost_percent_annual
attributes.description: Annual inventory carrying cost as a percentage of material value, including warehousing, insurance, obsolescence, and cost of capital.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this safety stock policy record was most recently updated.

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: The most recent date on which this safety stock policy was reviewed and validated by supply chain planning.

attributes.name: lead_time_variability_days
attributes.description: Standard deviation of supplier lead time in days, used to account for supply uncertainty in safety stock calculations.

attributes.name: lot_size_rule
attributes.description: SAP lot-sizing procedure code: EX (Lot-for-Lot), HB (Replenish to Maximum), FX (Fixed Lot Size), WB (Weekly Lot Size), MB (Monthly Lot Size).

attributes.name: maximum_stock_level
attributes.description: The upper inventory limit for this material-plant combination, used to prevent overstocking and optimize working capital.

attributes.name: minimum_stock_level
attributes.description: The lower inventory threshold below which stock should not fall under normal operating conditions.

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this safety stock policy record.

attributes.name: moq_minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: The smallest order quantity that can be procured from the supplier, constraining replenishment order sizes.

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: Three-character code identifying the MRP controller responsible for exception management and planning parameter maintenance.

attributes.name: mrp_type
attributes.description: MRP procedure type that determines how material requirements are planned: PD (MRP), VB (Manual Reorder Point), VM (Forecast-Based Planning), VV (Time-Phased Planning), R1 (Reorder Point Planning), R2 (Replenishment Planning).

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next review of this safety stock policy.

attributes.name: planned_delivery_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Expected number of calendar days from order placement to material receipt, used in reorder point calculation.

attributes.name: planner_code
attributes.description: Three-character code identifying the supply chain planner or buyer responsible for managing this material-plant combination.

attributes.name: policy_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for planners to document special considerations, assumptions, or business rules governing this safety stock policy.

attributes.name: policy_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the safety stock policy: active (in use), inactive (not applied), suspended (temporarily disabled), under_review (pending approval).

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: SAP procurement type indicator: E (In-house production), F (External procurement), X (Both).

attributes.name: reorder_point_quantity
attributes.description: The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered, calculated as safety stock plus demand during lead time.

attributes.name: review_frequency_days
attributes.description: Number of days between scheduled reviews of this safety stock policy to ensure continued relevance and accuracy.

attributes.name: rounding_value
attributes.description: Quantity increment to which replenishment orders are rounded (e.g., round to nearest pallet quantity).

attributes.name: safety_days_supply
attributes.description: Number of days of average demand that the safety stock quantity is intended to cover.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: The buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty, expressed in base unit of measure.

attributes.name: safety_time_days
attributes.description: Number of days added to the planned delivery lead time as a time buffer to account for supply uncertainty.

attributes.name: service_level_target_percent
attributes.description: The target probability of not experiencing a stockout during the lead time, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 for 95% service level).

attributes.name: special_procurement_key
attributes.description: Two-digit SAP code indicating special procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or third-party processing.

attributes.name: stockout_cost_per_unit
attributes.description: Estimated cost impact per unit of stockout, including lost sales, expediting costs, and production disruption, used in economic safety stock optimization.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The base unit of measure in which safety stock quantities are expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter).

attributes.name: xyz_classification
attributes.description: Demand variability classification: X (stable demand), Y (moderate variability), Z (highly variable or sporadic demand).

products.name: demand_plan_version
products.description: Manages versioned demand plan records enabling scenario comparison and consensus planning. Captures plan version identifier, version type (baseline statistical, sales-adjusted, consensus, approved), creation date, planning horizon, total planned demand quantity, and version approval workflow status. Supports S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) processes by maintaining multiple demand scenarios for supply planning sensitivity analysis in Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.
products.reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM - Demand Planning Versions

attributes.name: demand_plan_version_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the demand plan version record. Primary key for the demand plan version entity.

attributes.name: demand_plan_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent demand plan that this version belongs to. Links to the master demand plan entity.

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Demand plan versions in industrial manufacturing S&OP are created and managed at the product family level (family-level statistical forecasting is standard). This FK enables S&OP teams to aggregate de

attributes.name: superseded_by_version_demand_plan_version_id
attributes.description: Reference to the newer demand plan version that replaces this version. Null if this is the current active version. Maintains version lineage and audit trail for plan evolution tracking.

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Demand plan versions are plant-scoped in industrial manufacturing. plant_code on demand_plan_version is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level demand planning ve

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when this demand plan version was formally approved by authorized stakeholders. Null if version has not yet been approved. Marks the transition to executable plan status.

attributes.name: approved_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this demand plan version has received formal approval through the S&OP governance process. True if approved for execution, false if still in draft or review.

attributes.name: baseline_version_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this version serves as the baseline reference for variance analysis and scenario comparison. True if this is the authoritative baseline, false for alternative scenarios or working versions.

attributes.name: bias_percentage
attributes.description: Systematic tendency of this demand plan version to over-forecast (positive bias) or under-forecast (negative bias) actual demand. Calculated as the sum of forecast errors divided by sum of actuals, used to detect and correct persistent forecasting errors.

attributes.name: confidence_level_percentage
attributes.description: Statistical confidence level associated with the demand forecast in this version. Represents the probability that actual demand will fall within the forecasted range, typically 80%, 90%, or 95% confidence intervals used for safety stock calculations.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when this demand plan version record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: demand_time_fence_days
attributes.description: Number of days from the current date within which demand changes are frozen or restricted. Defines the period where MRP (Material Requirements Planning) will not automatically adjust planned orders based on forecast changes, protecting near-term production stability.

attributes.name: forecast_accuracy_percentage
attributes.description: Measured accuracy of this demand plan version compared to actual demand realization. Calculated as 100 minus the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), used for continuous improvement of forecasting methods and model selection.

attributes.name: forecast_model_code
attributes.description: Identifier for the statistical or algorithmic model used to generate demand forecasts in this version (e.g., ARIMA, exponential smoothing, machine learning model). Applicable when planning method is statistical or APS.
attributes.references: Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM - Demand Forecasting Models

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when this demand plan version record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.

attributes.name: material_group_code
attributes.description: Product family or material group classification for demand aggregation. Used to organize demand planning at the product category level before disaggregation to individual SKUs (Stock Keeping Units).

attributes.name: planning_horizon_days
attributes.description: Total number of calendar days covered by this demand plan version. Calculated as the difference between planning horizon end and start dates, used for capacity planning and lead time analysis.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_end_date
attributes.description: Last date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the end of the forecast window, typically aligned with fiscal quarters or annual planning cycles.

attributes.name: planning_horizon_start_date
attributes.description: First date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the beginning of the forecast window for supply planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) execution.

attributes.name: planning_method
attributes.description: Methodology used to generate this demand plan version. Statistical uses algorithmic forecasting, collaborative incorporates cross-functional input, consensus reflects S&OP agreement, APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) uses optimization engines, and manual indicates planner-driven creation.

attributes.name: planning_time_fence_days
attributes.description: Number of days from the current date within which supply planning changes require manual approval. Defines the period where automated MRP adjustments are restricted to prevent disruption to committed production schedules.

attributes.name: scenario_name
attributes.description: Business name for the planning scenario represented by this version. Used for what-if analysis and sensitivity testing in S&OP processes (e.g., Optimistic Growth, Conservative Base, Supply Constrained).

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Identifier for the operational system that created or owns this demand plan version record. Typically Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM, SAP APO (Advanced Planning and Optimization), or specialized demand planning applications for data lineage and integration traceability.

attributes.name: total_planned_demand_quantity
attributes.description: Aggregate demand quantity across all materials and time buckets within this plan version. Represents the total forecasted demand volume for supply network capacity planning and scenario comparison.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Standard unit of measure for the total planned demand quantity. Typically base inventory unit (EA, KG, L, M) aligned with material master UOM definitions in SAP MM or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.

attributes.name: version_notes
attributes.description: Free-text commentary and business context for this demand plan version. Captures assumptions, market conditions, promotional events, supply constraints, or other factors influencing the forecast that are relevant for scenario interpretation and decision-making.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the demand plan version. Human-readable version code used for tracking and referencing specific plan iterations (e.g., V1.0, V2.1, BASELINE-2024Q1).

attributes.name: version_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the demand plan version. Tracks the approval workflow state from initial draft through review, approval, and eventual archival or supersession by newer versions.

attributes.name: version_type
attributes.description: Classification of the demand plan version indicating its purpose and stage in the S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) process. Baseline represents initial statistical forecast, sales-adjusted includes sales team input, consensus reflects cross-functional agreement, approved is the final authorized plan, and working is an in-progress draft.

attributes.name: creation_date
attributes.description: Date when this demand plan version was created. Represents the business event timestamp for version instantiation in the S&OP cycle.

domains.name: customer
domains.description: Customer identity and account management domain serving as the SSOT for all B2B industrial customers, OEM accounts, distributors, and end-user organizations. Manages customer profiles, contacts, account hierarchies, segmentation, credit terms, relationship history, and SLA agreements via Salesforce CRM.

products.name: account
products.description: Master record for all B2B industrial customers, OEM accounts, distributors, and end-user organizations. Serves as the SSOT for customer identity in Salesforce CRM (Account object) and SAP Business Partner. Captures legal entity name, industry classification (SIC/NAICS), account type (OEM, distributor, system integrator, end-user, EPC contractor), account status with full lifecycle history, annual revenue, employee count, DUNS number, tax ID, VAT registration, credit rating, assigned account manager, parent account reference, and strategic account flag. Foundation entity referenced by all other products in this domain and cross-referenced by sales, service, order, and billing domains. Corporate hierarchy and commercial relationships are managed in account_relationship.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the customer_account master data product. Serves as the SSOT reference key for all downstream domains including sales, service, order, and billing.

attributes.name: business_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.business_partner. Business justification: In SAP-based industrial manufacturing ERP, every customer account maps to a business partner master record for unified MDM, payment terms, and compliance management. customer_account.sap_business_part

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Legal‑entity invoicing and tax reporting assign each customer to a company code for statutory compliance.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost Allocation Report requires mapping each customer account to a cost center for internal expense tracking per strategic account.

attributes.name: parent_account_customer_account_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent customer_account record in the corporate hierarchy. Enables multi-level account hierarchy modeling (e.g., subsidiary → division → global parent). Null for top-level accounts.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Hierarchy; Informatica MDM — Corporate Hierarchy

attributes.name: price_book_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the SAP S/4HANA pricing condition group or price list assigned to this customer. Determines applicable pricing, discounts, and surcharges during sales order creation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — SD Pricing Conditions; Condition Technique

attributes.name: primary_sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Primary sales rep on an account drives quota allocation, forecasting, and territory performance metrics in industrial manufacturing.

attributes.name: primary_ultimate_parent_account_customer_account_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the topmost entity in the corporate hierarchy for this customer account. Supports global account consolidation, enterprise-level revenue reporting, and strategic account management.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Hierarchy; Dun & Bradstreet Corporate Linkage

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis per customer uses profit_center_id on the account to attribute revenue and margin to the correct profit center.

attributes.name: sales_territory_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Direct FK to sales territory replaces the denormalized code, supporting territory‑based reporting and incentive calculations.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Linking account to its segment enables normalization of segment data and removes redundant string field.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Procurement planning and supplier performance dashboards require each account to designate a primary supplier.

attributes.name: account_source
attributes.description: Channel or method through which this customer account was originally acquired. Used for marketing attribution, channel effectiveness analysis, and sales pipeline reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_sales|referral|trade_show|inbound_marketing|partner|acquisition|existing_relationship — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Source; Marketing Attribution Framework

attributes.name: account_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the customer account. Controls whether transactions, orders, and service requests can be processed against this account. Managed in Salesforce CRM and synchronized to SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD — Customer Master Status

attributes.name: account_type
attributes.description: Classification of the customer's commercial role in the industrial supply chain. Drives pricing tiers, contract templates, and service entitlements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OEM|distributor|system_integrator|end_user|EPC_contractor|reseller|value_added_reseller|direct — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Account Type; Industrial Manufacturing Commercial Segmentation

attributes.name: annual_revenue
attributes.description: Customer organization's most recently reported annual revenue in the account's local currency. Used for customer segmentation, credit risk assessment, and strategic account classification. Currency indicated by revenue_currency_code.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Annual Revenue; IFRS/GAAP — Revenue Reporting

attributes.name: billing_city
attributes.description: City of the customer's primary billing address. Used for geographic segmentation, logistics planning, and tax jurisdiction determination.

attributes.name: billing_country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the customer's primary billing address country. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory compliance, and geographic segmentation.

attributes.name: billing_postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code of the customer's primary billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, logistics routing, and geographic analytics.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Billing Address; Universal Postal Union (UPU)

attributes.name: billing_state_province
attributes.description: State or province of the customer's primary billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, sales territory assignment, and regional reporting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Billing Address; ISO 3166-2

attributes.name: close_date
attributes.description: Date on which the customer account was formally closed or terminated. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis, lifecycle reporting, and regulatory record retention.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer account record was first created in the source system of record (Salesforce CRM). Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 — Date and Time; ISO 9001 — Record Management; GDPR Article 30

attributes.name: credit_limit
attributes.description: Maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance permitted for this customer account, denominated in the account's billing currency. Enforced in SAP S/4HANA SD order processing and credit management workflows.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — SD/FI Credit Management; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: credit_limit_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit_limit field. Ensures correct interpretation of the credit limit in multi-currency environments.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: credit_rating
attributes.description: Credit rating assigned to the customer by an internal credit team or external agency (e.g., Dun & Bradstreet, Moody's). Used to determine credit limits, payment terms, and order approval workflows in SAP S/4HANA FI.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — FI Credit Management; Dun & Bradstreet; Moody's

attributes.name: crm_account_code
attributes.description: Native Account record ID from Salesforce CRM (Account object). Used to cross-reference the master record back to the operational source system for reconciliation and lineage tracing.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Account Object

attributes.name: data_quality_score
attributes.description: Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the completeness and accuracy of the customer account record as assessed by Informatica MDM data quality rules. Used to prioritize data stewardship activities and MDM enrichment workflows.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM — Data Quality; ISO 8000 — Data Quality

attributes.name: distribution_channel_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA distribution channel code indicating how products are sold to this customer (e.g., direct sales, wholesale, OEM channel). Part of the SAP SD organizational assignment.

attributes.name: duns_number
attributes.description: Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number uniquely identifying the customer's legal entity globally. Used for credit risk assessment, supplier/customer verification, and corporate hierarchy resolution.
attributes.references: Dun & Bradstreet DUNS Standard; SAP S/4HANA — Business Partner

attributes.name: employee_count
attributes.description: Total number of employees at the customer organization as reported or estimated. Used for customer segmentation (SMB vs. enterprise), credit risk scoring, and market sizing analytics.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Number of Employees; Dun & Bradstreet

attributes.name: incoterms_code
