attributes.description: ICC Incoterms code defining the agreed delivery and risk transfer conditions for shipments to this customer. Used in SAP S/4HANA SD sales order processing and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA — SD Delivery Terms

attributes.name: industry_naics_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code
attributes.description: Six-digit NAICS code identifying the customer's primary industry sector per North American classification standards. Complements SIC code for more granular industry segmentation and analytics.
attributes.references: ANSI — NAICS Standard; US Census Bureau NAICS

attributes.name: industry_sic_code
attributes.description: Four-digit Standard Industrial Classification code identifying the customer's primary industry sector. Used for market segmentation, regulatory reporting, and credit risk assessment.
attributes.references: ANSI — SIC Code Standard; US SEC SIC Classification

attributes.name: is_global_account
attributes.description: Indicates whether this customer operates across multiple countries and is managed under a global account management program with consolidated commercial terms and cross-regional coordination.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud; Global Account Management Framework

attributes.name: is_strategic_account
attributes.description: Indicates whether this customer is designated as a strategic account requiring executive-level relationship management and customized service agreements. Strategic accounts receive dedicated account teams and priority support.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud; Key Account Management Framework

attributes.name: last_activity_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent commercial activity (order, service request, or interaction) recorded against this customer account. Used for account health monitoring, dormancy detection, and re-engagement campaigns.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer account record in the source system of record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 8601 — Date and Time; ISO 9001 — Record Management; GDPR Article 30

attributes.name: legal_name
attributes.description: Full registered legal name of the B2B customer organization as recorded in official corporate filings. Used for invoicing, contracts, and regulatory compliance. Must match the name on file with the relevant tax authority.
attributes.references: IFRS/GAAP — Entity Identification; ISO 9001 — Customer Records

attributes.name: mdm_golden_record_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Master Data Management (MDM) Golden Record Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this record has been designated as the MDM golden (master) record for the customer entity after deduplication and survivorship processing in Informatica MDM. False indicates a candidate or survivor record pending promotion.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM — Golden Record Survivorship; ISO 8000

attributes.name: open_date
attributes.description: Date on which the customer account was formally opened and activated in the system of record. Marks the start of the commercial relationship. Used for customer tenure calculations and cohort analysis.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Created Date; SAP S/4HANA SD

attributes.name: phone
attributes.description: Primary business telephone number for the customer organization. Used for account management communications and service escalation. Stored in E.164-compatible format.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Phone; ITU-T E.164

attributes.name: preferred_language_code
attributes.description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region suffix) indicating the customer's preferred language for communications, documents, and correspondence (e.g., en-US, de-DE, fr-FR).
attributes.references: ISO 639-1 — Language Codes; ISO 3166-1; Salesforce CRM

attributes.name: revenue_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual_revenue field (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required to correctly interpret the revenue figure for multi-currency global accounts.

attributes.name: sales_organization_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA sales organization code responsible for selling to this customer. Determines the legal entity, currency, and pricing procedures applicable to sales transactions.

attributes.name: sla_tier
attributes.description: SLA tier assigned to this customer account, defining the service response times, support priority, and escalation paths applicable to service requests and after-sales support. Managed in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Service Cloud SLA; ISO 9001 — Customer Service

attributes.name: status_date
attributes.description: Date on which the current account_status became effective. Supports lifecycle history tracking and audit compliance. Used to calculate duration in current status for account health reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 — Customer Record Lifecycle; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: tax_number
attributes.description: Government-issued tax identification number for the customer's legal entity (e.g., EIN in the US, CRN in the UK). Required for invoicing, tax reporting, and regulatory compliance under IFRS/GAAP.
attributes.references: IFRS/GAAP — Tax Reporting; IRS EIN; OSHA Regulatory Compliance

attributes.name: trading_name
attributes.description: Commercial or brand name under which the customer operates if different from the legal entity name. Used in sales communications, account management, and CRM displays.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Account Object

attributes.name: vat_registration_number
attributes.description: VAT registration number for the customer entity, required for EU and international tax compliance. Used in invoice generation and cross-border transaction reporting.
attributes.references: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; IFRS/GAAP — Tax Compliance

attributes.name: website
attributes.description: Official website URL of the customer organization. Used for account research, digital engagement tracking, and customer profile enrichment.

products.name: customer_contact
products.description: Individual person records associated with customer accounts representing key stakeholders at B2B industrial organizations — including design engineers, procurement managers, plant managers, maintenance supervisors, and executive sponsors. Maps to Salesforce CRM Contact object. Captures full name, job title, department, phone, email, preferred communication channel, language preference, marketing consent status, decision-making role (technical evaluator, commercial buyer, executive approver), and active/inactive status. Serves as the SSOT for person-level engagement across sales, service, and marketing.

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the contact record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent B2B customer account (OEM, distributor, or end-user organization) to which this contact belongs. Establishes the person-to-account relationship.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: reports_to_contact_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing identifier pointing to the contact's organizational superior within the same account. Enables modeling of the customer-side org chart for multi-stakeholder enterprise sales and service engagement strategies.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Assigning a sales rep to each contact enables contact‑level sales follow‑up and pipeline reporting, a standard practice in manufacturing account management.

attributes.name: account_site
attributes.description: Specific plant, facility, or site within the parent account organization where this contact is based. Critical for industrial manufacturing customers with multiple production sites, enabling site-level service and sales targeting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object; SAP S/4HANA SD — Customer Master

attributes.name: assistant_name
attributes.description: Name of the contact's executive assistant or administrative coordinator. Used to facilitate scheduling, correspondence routing, and executive-level engagement for senior stakeholders at key accounts.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: assistant_phone
attributes.description: Phone number of the contact's executive assistant. Enables sales and service teams to reach senior contacts through their administrative support channel.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: birthdate
attributes.description: Date of birth of the contact. Captured selectively for key account contacts where relationship management programs require it. Subject to strict GDPR data minimization principles — only collected when there is a documented lawful basis.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(1)(c) Data Minimization; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: consent_record_code
attributes.description: Reference identifier linking to the formal consent record in the consent management system. Provides an auditable trail of the lawful basis under which this contact's personal data is processed, as required by GDPR Article 7.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 & 7; Salesforce CRM — Marketing Cloud Consent Management

attributes.name: contact_type
attributes.description: Classification of the contact's decision-making role in the B2B buying and service process. Drives sales engagement strategy and marketing segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technical_evaluator|commercial_buyer|executive_approver|end_user|influencer|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object; APQP stakeholder classification

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED anchor for data lineage, GDPR data retention calculations, and contact age analytics.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(1)(e); Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: crm_contact_code
attributes.description: External identifier for this contact record as assigned by Salesforce CRM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and delta-load processing from the operational source of record.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: customer_contact_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Governs whether the contact is eligible for outreach, service engagement, and data processing activities. 'do_not_contact' enforces opt-out and regulatory suppression requirements.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 17; CCPA; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: data_quality_score
attributes.description: Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the completeness and accuracy of this contact record as assessed by Informatica MDM data quality rules. Drives data stewardship prioritization and master data governance workflows.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM — Data Quality; ISO 8000 Data Quality

attributes.name: department
attributes.description: Organizational department or functional unit to which the contact belongs at their employer (e.g., Engineering, Procurement, Manufacturing, Maintenance, Finance, Executive). Supports segmentation for targeted outreach.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: do_not_call
attributes.description: Indicates whether the contact has requested to be excluded from telephone outreach. True = phone contact suppressed. Enforced in compliance with national do-not-call registries and GDPR consent requirements.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 17; FTC Do Not Call Registry; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: email
attributes.description: Primary business email address of the contact. Used as the principal digital communication channel for sales outreach, service notifications, and marketing campaigns. Governed under GDPR and applicable privacy regulations.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: email_opt_out
attributes.description: Indicates whether the contact has explicitly opted out of email communications. True = email suppressed; False = email permitted. Distinct from marketing_opt_in as it applies specifically to all email types including transactional. Enforced by Salesforce CRM email suppression lists.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 17; CAN-SPAM Act; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: fax
attributes.description: Fax number associated with the contact or their office location. Retained for legacy B2B industrial communication workflows and regulatory document exchange in certain jurisdictions.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: first_name
attributes.description: Given name of the individual contact as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Used for personalized communication across sales, service, and marketing channels.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: gdpr_data_subject_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Subject ID
attributes.description: Unique identifier assigned to this contact as a GDPR data subject. Used to process data subject rights requests (access, erasure, portability) and maintain the record of processing activities (ROPA) as required under GDPR Article 30.

attributes.name: is_decision_maker
attributes.description: Indicates whether this contact holds formal decision-making authority for purchasing or contracting at their organization. True = authorized decision maker. Used to prioritize sales engagement and opportunity progression in the CRM pipeline.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: is_primary_contact
attributes.description: Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for their account. True = primary contact for account-level communications and escalations. Only one contact per account should hold this designation.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object; SAP S/4HANA SD — Customer Master

attributes.name: job_title
attributes.description: Official job title of the contact at their organization (e.g., Plant Manager, Procurement Manager, Design Engineer, Maintenance Supervisor, VP of Operations). Critical for routing sales and service engagements to the appropriate stakeholder.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the contact's preferred language for communications and documentation (e.g., en, de, zh, fr, ja). Drives localization of marketing materials, service documents, and product manuals.
attributes.references: ISO 639-1; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: last_activity_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent logged activity (call, email, meeting, task) associated with this contact in Salesforce CRM. Used to identify dormant contacts, trigger re-engagement workflows, and measure sales cadence effectiveness.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this contact record in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for incremental data extraction, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: last_name
attributes.description: Family name or surname of the individual contact. Combined with first_name to form the full display name used in correspondence and CRM records.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: lead_source
attributes.description: Channel or origin through which this contact was first acquired or identified. Used for marketing attribution, ROI analysis of demand generation programs, and sales pipeline reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade_show|web_inquiry|referral|partner|direct_sales|marketing_campaign|distributor|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: linkedin_url
attributes.description: URL of the contact's LinkedIn professional profile. Used by sales and marketing teams for social selling, account-based marketing (ABM), and contact enrichment workflows.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object; GDPR Article 6

attributes.name: mailing_city
attributes.description: City component of the contact's mailing address. Used in geographic segmentation, territory assignment, and regional sales reporting.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: mailing_country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the contact's mailing address (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Drives multi-national compliance, language preference defaults, and export control screening.

attributes.name: mailing_postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code component of the contact's mailing address. Used for geographic routing, logistics planning, and regional analytics.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: mailing_state
attributes.description: State or province component of the contact's mailing address. Supports territory management, tax jurisdiction determination, and regional compliance reporting.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: mailing_street
attributes.description: Street address line for the contact's mailing address. Used for physical correspondence, contract delivery, and regulatory document dispatch.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: marketing_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the contact has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications. True = opted in; False = opted out or consent not obtained. Mandatory for GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance. Must be False by default until affirmative consent is recorded.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6 & 7; CAN-SPAM Act; CASL; Salesforce CRM — Marketing Cloud

attributes.name: marketing_opt_in_date
attributes.description: Date on which the contact provided or last renewed their marketing consent. Required for GDPR audit trail demonstrating lawful basis for marketing data processing.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 7; CAN-SPAM Act; Salesforce CRM — Marketing Cloud

attributes.name: mdm_golden_record_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: Master Data Management (MDM) Golden Record ID
attributes.description: Identifier of the golden (master) record in Informatica MDM to which this contact has been matched and merged. Enables cross-system deduplication and single-view-of-contact analytics across Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA, and other operational systems.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM — Master Data Management; ISO 8000 Data Quality

attributes.name: mobile_phone
attributes.description: Mobile or cellular phone number for the contact. Used for SMS notifications, urgent service alerts, and field engineer coordination. Distinct from the primary business phone line.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: persona
attributes.description: Functional persona label describing the contact's primary business role in the industrial context (e.g., Design Engineer, Procurement Manager, Plant Manager, Maintenance Supervisor, Executive Sponsor, Quality Engineer). Used for targeted content delivery and sales play alignment.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud; Industrial Manufacturing Sales Methodology

attributes.name: phone
attributes.description: Primary business phone number for the contact including country code where applicable. Used for direct voice communication by field sales, inside sales, and customer service teams.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: preferred_communication_channel
attributes.description: The contact's stated preferred channel for receiving communications from the company. Drives outreach strategy for sales, service, and marketing teams to maximize engagement effectiveness.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object; GDPR Article 7

attributes.name: salutation
attributes.description: Formal honorific or title prefix used when addressing the contact in written and verbal communication (e.g., Mr., Dr., Prof.).
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Sales Cloud Contact Object

attributes.name: sla_tier
attributes.description: Service Level Agreement tier associated with this contact's account, determining response time commitments and support priority for after-sales service requests. Aligned with the SLA agreements managed in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Service Cloud; ISO 9001 Quality Management

attributes.name: technical_contact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this contact serves as a technical point of contact for product support, engineering queries, or MES/PLC/SCADA integration activities. True = designated technical contact. Drives routing of technical service cases and engineering change notifications (ECN).
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Service Cloud; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity

products.name: segment
products.description: Market segmentation classification for customer accounts used in sales strategy, pricing tiers, and service prioritization. Captures segment name (Strategic, Key Account, Growth, Transactional), segment criteria, revenue band, industry vertical, geographic region, assigned segment manager, and review cycle. Enables targeted commercial strategies for OEMs, distributors, and end-users.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer segment record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and references.

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Customer segments in industrial manufacturing are defined and governed per legal entity (company code). Segment-level financial reporting — revenue bands, credit limits, target gross margins — must be

attributes.name: price_book_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: Customer segments in industrial manufacturing are mapped to specific price books governing discount tiers and pricing strategy. This link drives segment-based pricing governance, enabling sales ops to

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the segment definition was formally approved by the designated commercial authority. Used for governance audit trails and to establish the authoritative effective date of the segment.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; Salesforce CRM Approval Process

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or employee identifier of the commercial leadership authority (e.g., VP Sales, Regional Sales Director) who formally approved this segment definition and its commercial parameters. Sourced from Salesforce CRM approval workflow records.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; Salesforce CRM Approval Process

attributes.name: assigned_segment_manager
attributes.description: Full name or employee identifier of the commercial manager responsible for owning the segment strategy, reviewing segment performance, and approving account assignments. Sourced from Salesforce CRM user records and Workday HCM.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; Workday HCM

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: Indicates the go-to-market channel model applicable to this segment: Direct (manufacturer sells directly to end customer), Indirect (sales through distributors or channel partners), or Hybrid (combination of direct and indirect). Drives commercial policy and commission structures.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD Distribution Channel

attributes.name: contract_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a formal master supply agreement or framework contract is mandatory for customer accounts in this segment before order processing can commence. Enforced in SAP S/4HANA SD order entry validation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and governance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.

attributes.name: credit_limit_usd
attributes.description: Standard credit limit in USD applied to customer accounts assigned to this segment. Used as the default credit exposure ceiling in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR credit management for new account onboarding within the segment.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments

attributes.name: criteria
attributes.description: Structured definition of the qualifying rules and thresholds used to assign a customer account to this segment (e.g., annual revenue > $10M, OEM classification, multi-site deployment). Supports automated segmentation logic in Salesforce CRM and Informatica MDM.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: crm_segment_record_code
attributes.description: Native Salesforce CRM record identifier for the corresponding segment or account group object in Salesforce Sales Cloud. Used for bi-directional synchronization between the Databricks Silver Layer and the CRM system of record.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: customer_account_type
attributes.description: Classification of the customer relationship type that this segment targets. OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) accounts receive differentiated pricing and co-development support; Distributors receive channel margin structures; End Users receive direct service SLAs; System Integrators and EPCs (Engineering, Procurement & Construction) receive project-based commercial terms.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Account Group

attributes.name: dedicated_account_manager_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether customer accounts in this segment require a dedicated named account manager assignment in Salesforce CRM. Strategic and Key Account segments typically require dedicated coverage; Transactional segments may use pooled inside sales resources.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: discount_rate_pct
attributes.description: Standard discount percentage applied to list prices for customers in this segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 12.50 = 12.5%). Confidential as it reflects commercial pricing strategy. Used in SAP S/4HANA SD pricing condition records.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: effective_from_date
attributes.description: Date from which this segment definition became commercially active and applicable to account assignments. Supports temporal validity tracking for segment versioning and historical analysis of commercial strategy changes.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; ISO/IEC 11179

attributes.name: effective_until_date
attributes.description: Date on which this segment definition ceases to be commercially active. Null indicates the segment is open-ended with no planned expiry. Used for segment versioning, sunset planning, and historical reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; ISO/IEC 11179

attributes.name: erp_customer_group_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) Customer Group Code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA SD customer group code (Kundengruppe) mapped to this segment. Used to apply segment-specific pricing conditions, output determination, and reporting groupings in the ERP system of record.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Group; ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning)

attributes.name: geographic_region
attributes.description: Primary geographic sales region associated with this segment definition (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, LATAM). Used to align segment management responsibilities with regional sales organizations and territory planning in Salesforce CRM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: North America|EMEA|APAC|LATAM|Greater China|South Asia|Middle East & Africa — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Organization

attributes.name: industry_vertical
attributes.description: The primary industry vertical classification of customers within this segment (e.g., Automotive OEM, Aerospace & Defense, Food & Beverage, Energy & Utilities, Building Automation, Transportation). Aligns with NAICS/SIC industry classification and Salesforce CRM account industry field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Automotive OEM|Aerospace & Defense|Food & Beverage|Energy & Utilities|Building Automation|Transportation|Semiconductor|Pharmaceuticals|Oil & Gas|General Manufacturing — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: NAICS Industry Classification; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; ISO 9001 Clause 4.1

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date on which the most recent formal review of this segment's criteria, account assignments, and commercial performance was completed. Used to trigger the next review cycle and track governance compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Clause 9.1; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: max_account_count
attributes.description: Maximum number of customer accounts permitted in this segment before a split or restructuring review is triggered. Ensures segment managers maintain adequate coverage quality. Null indicates no upper cap.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD

attributes.name: mdm_segment_code
attributes.description: Informatica MDM golden record identifier for this segment definition. Ensures cross-system consistency of segment master data across Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management.

attributes.name: min_account_count
attributes.description: Minimum number of customer accounts that should be assigned to this segment to maintain commercial viability and justify dedicated segment management resources. Used in segment health monitoring and portfolio planning.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD

attributes.name: moq_applicable
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) policy applies to customer accounts in this segment. When True, the MOQ defined in SAP S/4HANA SD condition records is enforced during order entry for accounts in this segment.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Order Management; MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity)

attributes.name: nda_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is required for customer accounts in this segment before sharing product roadmaps, engineering specifications, or pricing strategies. Relevant for Strategic and OEM segments involved in co-development programs.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; ISO/IEC 27001 Information Security

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next formal review of this segment definition. Calculated from last_review_date plus review_cycle_months. Used by the segment manager and commercial governance team to plan review activities.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Clause 9.1; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: owner_email
attributes.description: Business email address of the assigned segment manager. Used for automated notifications, review cycle reminders, and escalation routing in Salesforce CRM workflows. Classified confidential as it is an internal business contact.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: payment_terms_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA payment terms code (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NT30) assigned as the default for customer accounts in this segment. Defines invoice due dates, early payment discount eligibility, and cash flow planning inputs.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Payment Terms; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: pricing_tier_code
attributes.description: Code referencing the applicable pricing tier in SAP S/4HANA SD condition records for customers assigned to this segment. Drives list price, discount matrix, and rebate eligibility. Confidential as it encodes commercial pricing strategy.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions; IFRS 15

attributes.name: rebate_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether customer accounts in this segment are eligible for volume-based rebate programs. When True, rebate agreements are activated in SAP S/4HANA SD rebate processing for qualifying accounts.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Rebate Management; IFRS 15

attributes.name: revenue_band_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the revenue band thresholds (revenue_band_min and revenue_band_max) are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent multi-currency interpretation across global sales regions.

attributes.name: revenue_band_max
attributes.description: Maximum annual revenue threshold (in USD) that defines the upper boundary of the revenue band for this segment. Null indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., for the Strategic tier). Used in pricing tier assignment and SLA prioritization.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing

attributes.name: revenue_band_min
attributes.description: Minimum annual revenue threshold (in USD) that a customer account must meet to qualify for this segment. Used in conjunction with revenue_band_max to define the revenue band boundary for segmentation scoring.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing

attributes.name: review_cycle_months
attributes.description: Frequency in months at which the segment definition, criteria, and account assignments are formally reviewed and validated by the segment manager and commercial leadership (e.g., 3 = quarterly, 6 = semi-annual, 12 = annual).
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Clause 9.1 Monitoring and Measurement; Salesforce CRM

attributes.name: segment_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment across systems (e.g., STRAT-001, KEY-002). Used as the business key in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA SD module for cross-system reconciliation.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; SAP S/4HANA SD

attributes.name: segment_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the segment's commercial purpose, qualifying criteria, and strategic intent. Used by sales managers and account teams to understand segment boundaries and apply correct commercial policies.

attributes.name: segment_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the customer segment (e.g., Strategic, Key Account, Growth, Transactional). Used in sales strategy, pricing tier assignment, and service prioritization workflows.

attributes.name: segment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the segment definition. Active segments are applied in commercial operations; Under Review segments are being reassessed during periodic review cycles; Archived segments are retired and no longer assigned to new accounts.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; ISO 9001 Clause 9.1

attributes.name: segment_type
attributes.description: Classification of the segment into one of the four standard commercial tiers used by the industrial manufacturing sales organization: Strategic (top-tier OEM/enterprise accounts), Key Account (high-value recurring customers), Growth (emerging or expanding accounts), Transactional (low-frequency, price-driven buyers).
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; ISO 9001 Customer Focus Clause 5.1.2

attributes.name: service_priority_level
attributes.description: Service prioritization level for after-sales support and field service dispatch for customers in this segment. Critical and High priority segments receive preferential scheduling in Salesforce Service Cloud and Maximo work order queues.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

attributes.name: sla_tier_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the SLA tier assigned to customers in this segment, referencing the SLA agreement product. Determines response time commitments, field service priority, spare parts availability guarantees, and escalation paths in Salesforce Service Cloud and Maximo Asset Management.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: strategic_account_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this segment is classified as a strategic segment requiring executive sponsorship, board-level visibility, and dedicated cross-functional account teams. Drives escalation routing and executive review cadence in Salesforce CRM.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; ISO 9001 Clause 5.1.2

attributes.name: target_gross_margin_pct
attributes.description: Target gross margin percentage for the segment, used in pricing strategy and commercial deal approval thresholds. Deals below this margin threshold require escalated approval for accounts in this segment. Confidential as it encodes commercial profitability strategy.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; IFRS/GAAP; Salesforce CRM

attributes.name: target_revenue_usd
attributes.description: Annual revenue target in USD for the aggregate of all customer accounts assigned to this segment. Used in commercial planning, sales quota allocation, and KPI tracking against actual segment revenue performance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA Profitability Analysis; IFRS 15; Salesforce CRM

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this segment record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Monotonically incrementing version number tracking revisions to the segment definition, criteria, or commercial parameters. Supports audit trail and change history for governance and compliance reviews.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; Informatica MDM

products.name: address
products.description: Physical and mailing address records for customer accounts and contacts, including headquarters, billing address, shipping/delivery address, and plant site addresses. Captures address type, street lines, city, state/province, postal code, country, geocoordinates, validated flag, and primary address indicator. Supports logistics, invoicing, and field service dispatch.

attributes.name: address_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each address record in the customer domain. Serves as the primary key for the address data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account that owns this address record. Links the address to the parent B2B industrial customer, OEM account, distributor, or end-user organization in the customer master.

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Reference to the individual contact person associated with this address, when the address belongs to a specific contact rather than the account-level organization. Nullable for account-level addresses.

attributes.name: address_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active addresses are in current use; inactive addresses are no longer used but retained for historical reference; pending addresses are awaiting validation or approval; archived addresses are soft-deleted for audit trail purposes.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Record Status; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master Lifecycle

attributes.name: address_type
attributes.description: Functional classification of the address indicating its business purpose. Billing addresses are used for invoicing (SAP SD), shipping/delivery addresses for logistics dispatch (SAP WM/TMS), headquarters for corporate correspondence, plant_site for manufacturing facility locations, mailing for postal communications, and field_service for on-site service dispatch. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|shipping|headquarters|plant_site|mailing|field_service|registered|returns — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Address; Salesforce CRM - Address Type

attributes.name: attention_line
attributes.description: Addressee attention line specifying the department, role, or individual to whom correspondence or deliveries should be directed at this address (e.g., 'Attn: Procurement Department', 'Attn: Receiving Dock'). Common in B2B industrial shipping.
attributes.references: USPS Publication 28 - Attention Line; SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Address

attributes.name: building_name
attributes.description: Name of the building or facility at this address (e.g., 'Tower A', 'Building 7 - Final Assembly', 'North Warehouse'). Common in large industrial campuses and multi-building manufacturing complexes. Supplements street address for precise delivery and field service dispatch.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City, municipality, or locality name of the address. Used for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and field service territory assignment.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code for the address (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN, GBR). Used for international logistics, customs documentation, export compliance, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; SAP S/4HANA - Country Field (LAND1 mapped to Alpha-3)

attributes.name: county_district
attributes.description: County, district, or sub-regional administrative unit of the address. Used for local tax jurisdiction determination, permit compliance, and field service territory mapping in industrial manufacturing contexts.
attributes.references: ISO 19160-1 Addressing; SAP S/4HANA - District Field (BEZEI)

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this address record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and compliance with ISO 9001 quality management record-keeping requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Quality Management Records; SAP S/4HANA - Created On (ERDAT); Salesforce CRM - CreatedDate

attributes.name: delivery_instructions
attributes.description: Free-text special instructions for deliveries to this address, such as dock access hours, security clearance requirements, forklift availability, hazardous material handling notes, or contact person for receiving. Critical for industrial manufacturing shipments to plant sites.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Note Text; WMS Receiving Instructions

attributes.name: effective_from
attributes.description: Date from which this address record is valid and in effect for business operations. Supports bi-temporal address management for customers who relocate or open new facilities, ensuring correct address is used for historical invoicing and logistics.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - Address Validity Start (DATE_FROM); ISO 8601 Date

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date until which this address record is valid. Null indicates the address is open-ended and currently active. Used for managing address changes over time, particularly for plant site relocations and facility closures.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - Address Validity End (DATE_TO); ISO 8601 Date

attributes.name: floor_number
attributes.description: Floor or level number within a building at this address. Used for precise field service dispatch and delivery routing within multi-story industrial facilities, office buildings, and distribution centers.

attributes.name: geocoding_accuracy
attributes.description: Precision level of the geocoordinates assigned to this address. Rooftop indicates exact building-level precision; range_interpolated is street-level; geometric_center is centroid of a region; approximate is low-precision; ungeocoded means no coordinates assigned. Informs reliability of geospatial analytics.
attributes.references: Google Maps Geocoding API - Location Type; HERE Geocoding API

attributes.name: hazmat_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether this address/facility is certified to receive hazardous materials shipments. Relevant for industrial manufacturing customers receiving chemicals, electrification components, or other regulated materials. Supports EPA and OSHA compliance.
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 - Process Safety Management; EPA RCRA; DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials

attributes.name: is_primary
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this is the primary address for the associated customer account or contact. Only one address per customer per address_type should be marked as primary. Used to determine the default address for invoicing, shipping, and correspondence.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Primary Address Flag; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master

attributes.name: is_validated
attributes.description: Indicates whether the address has been validated against a postal authority or address verification service (e.g., USPS CASS, Informatica MDM address cleansing). Validated addresses reduce delivery failures and logistics errors.
attributes.references: USPS CASS (Coding Accuracy Support System); Informatica MDM - Address Validation

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Human-readable descriptive label or nickname for the address record (e.g., 'Stuttgart HQ', 'Chicago Distribution Center', 'Plant 3 - Assembly Line'). Facilitates quick identification in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA user interfaces.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Address Label; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: ISO 639-1 language code for the preferred communication language at this address (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'zh-CN', 'fr'). Used to determine document language for invoices, shipping labels, and field service work orders dispatched to this location.
attributes.references: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; SAP S/4HANA - Language Key (SPRAS)

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this address record was last updated in the source system. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental data loading, and audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 - Quality Management Records; SAP S/4HANA - Changed On (AEDAT); Salesforce CRM - LastModifiedDate

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS 84 datum). Precision to 7 decimal places (~1 cm accuracy). Used for field service dispatch routing, logistics optimization, and geospatial analytics in industrial manufacturing.
attributes.references: WGS 84 (World Geodetic System 1984); ISO 6709 Geographic Point Location

attributes.name: line_1
attributes.description: Primary street address line including building number, street name, and any directional qualifiers. This is the first and mandatory line of the physical or mailing address as captured in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA customer master.
attributes.references: USPS Publication 28 - Postal Addressing Standards; ISO 19160-1 Addressing

attributes.name: line_2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for suite, floor, unit, building name, mail stop, or additional location qualifier. Optional field used when the primary address line is insufficient to fully identify the location.
attributes.references: USPS Publication 28 - Postal Addressing Standards; ISO 19160-1 Addressing

attributes.name: line_3
attributes.description: Tertiary address line for additional location details such as industrial park name, campus identifier, or zone designation. Used primarily for complex plant site and industrial facility addresses common in manufacturing environments.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - Customer Master Address (ADRC); ISO 19160-1 Addressing

attributes.name: loading_dock_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether a loading dock is available at this address for receiving industrial equipment, automation systems, and bulk material shipments. Affects carrier selection (LTL vs FTL) and delivery scheduling in the Transportation Management System (TMS).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Conditions; TMS Facility Attributes

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS 84 datum). Precision to 7 decimal places (~1 cm accuracy). Used alongside latitude for geospatial analytics, field service routing, and logistics network optimization.
attributes.references: WGS 84 (World Geodetic System 1984); ISO 6709 Geographic Point Location

attributes.name: mdm_address_key
attributes.description: Unique address identifier assigned by Informatica MDM during master data consolidation and deduplication. Enables cross-system address matching and golden record linkage across SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce CRM, and other operational systems.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM - Master Data Management; ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA plant code associated with this address when the address represents a customer's manufacturing plant or production facility. Enables direct linkage between customer delivery addresses and SAP plant master data for production planning (MRP/MRP II) and goods receipt processing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant Master (T001W); SAP PP - Production Planning

attributes.name: po_box
attributes.description: Post Office (PO) Box number for mailing addresses where physical street delivery is not applicable. Used for billing correspondence and formal legal notices to corporate customers. Stored separately from street address lines.
attributes.references: USPS Publication 28 - PO Box Addressing; SAP S/4HANA - PO Box (PFACH)

attributes.name: po_box_postal_code
attributes.description: Postal code specific to the PO Box address, which may differ from the street address postal code. Required for accurate mail routing when a PO Box is used for billing or correspondence.
attributes.references: USPS Publication 28; SAP S/4HANA - PO Box Postal Code (PSTL2)

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the address. Format varies by country (e.g., 5-digit US ZIP, alphanumeric UK postcode). Used for mail delivery, logistics carrier routing (LTL/FTL), and geographic segmentation.
attributes.references: USPS Publication 28; Universal Postal Union (UPU) S42 Standard

attributes.name: postal_code_extension
attributes.description: Extended postal code suffix providing finer delivery point precision (e.g., ZIP+4 in the US). Improves mail delivery accuracy and carrier routing for industrial shipments.

attributes.name: room_number
attributes.description: Room, suite, or unit number within a building at this address. Provides the most granular level of location detail for field service technician dispatch and internal mail routing at customer facilities.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - Room Number (ROOMNUMBER); ISO 19160-1 Addressing

attributes.name: sales_region_code
attributes.description: Sales region or territory code associated with this address, used for sales force assignment, revenue reporting, and quota management. Derived from geographic location and aligned with SAP S/4HANA SD sales organization structure.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Region (REGIO); Salesforce CRM - Sales Territory

attributes.name: service_territory_code
attributes.description: Field service territory code assigned to this address, used to route field service engineers and technicians for equipment maintenance, installation, and after-sales support. Aligns with Salesforce Service Cloud territory management and Maximo Asset Management work order dispatch.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Service Cloud Territory Management; Maximo Asset Management - Service Territory

attributes.name: shipping_condition
attributes.description: Default shipping condition or delivery method applicable to this address, indicating the preferred logistics mode. LTL (Less Than Truckload) and FTL (Full Truckload) are standard industrial shipping terms. Drives carrier selection and Transportation Management System (TMS) routing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD - Shipping Conditions (VSBED); TMS Routing Rules

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Operational system of record from which this address record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during MDM consolidation across Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA, and Informatica MDM.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM - Data Lineage; DAMA DMBOK Data Governance

attributes.name: source_system_address_code
attributes.description: The native address identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce CRM Address ID, SAP ADRC ADDRNUMBER). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and operational systems during ETL and data quality processes.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - Address Number (ADRNR/ADDRNUMBER in ADRC table); Salesforce CRM - Address ID

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: State, province, or regional administrative division of the address. Stored as ISO 3166-2 subdivision code (e.g., US-CA, DE-BY). Used for tax jurisdiction, regulatory compliance (OSHA, EPA), and logistics zone determination.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-2 Country Subdivision Codes; SAP S/4HANA - Region Field (REGIO)

attributes.name: tax_jurisdiction_code
attributes.description: Tax jurisdiction identifier for the address location, used to determine applicable sales tax, VAT, or GST rates for invoicing. Typically derived from postal code and state/province. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA tax determination and IFRS/GAAP revenue recognition requirements.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - Tax Jurisdiction Code (TXJCD); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; GAAP ASC 606

attributes.name: time_zone
attributes.description: IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'Europe/Berlin', 'Asia/Shanghai'). Used for scheduling field service visits, coordinating production planning across global manufacturing sites, and SLA response time calculations.
attributes.references: IANA Time Zone Database (TZDB); SAP S/4HANA - Time Zone (TZONE)

attributes.name: validation_source
attributes.description: The system or method used to validate the address. Tracks whether validation was performed by USPS CASS certification, a geocoding API (Google Maps, HERE), Informatica MDM address cleansing, manual review, or not yet validated. Supports data quality governance.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM - Data Quality; USPS CASS Certification

attributes.name: validation_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the address was last validated against a postal authority or address verification service. Used to determine if re-validation is required based on data quality policies.
attributes.references: Informatica MDM - Data Quality; ISO 8601 Timestamp

attributes.name: vat_registration_number
attributes.description: Value Added Tax (VAT) registration number associated with this address, particularly for EU and international billing addresses. Required for cross-border B2B invoicing compliance and VAT reclaim processes in industrial manufacturing.
attributes.references: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; SAP S/4HANA - VAT Registration Number (STCEG); IFRS/GAAP

products.name: credit_profile
products.description: Customer credit assessment and terms management record. Captures credit limit, credit rating, payment terms (Net 30, Net 60, Net 90), currency, credit hold status, credit hold reason, last credit review date, next review date, credit insurance policy reference, outstanding balance, and days sales outstanding (DSO). Managed in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR and synchronized with Salesforce CRM.

attributes.name: credit_profile_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer credit profile record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the B2B industrial customer, OEM account, distributor, or end-user organization for whom this credit profile is maintained. Links to the customer master record.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Credit management in industrial manufacturing is administered per legal entity (company code). Credit control areas, dunning procedures, and bad-debt provisions are company-code-specific in SAP ERP. F

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the current credit limit and terms were formally approved by the authorized credit manager. Part of the credit governance audit trail required for SOX and IFRS compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; SOX; IFRS/GAAP; ISO 9001

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or employee ID of the credit manager or authorized approver who approved the current credit limit and terms. Required for segregation of duties (SoD) compliance and audit trail in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; ISO 9001; IFRS/GAAP; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act)

attributes.name: bad_debt_provision_amount
attributes.description: Amount provisioned for potential bad debt loss on this customer's receivables under the IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss (ECL) model or GAAP ASC 310 allowance method. Maintained in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9 ECL; GAAP ASC 310

attributes.name: bank_account_verified_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer's bank account details on file have been verified through the company's bank account validation process. True = verified; False = unverified. Required for direct debit and bank transfer payment methods.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS/GAAP; Anti-Money Laundering (AML) compliance

attributes.name: collection_strategy_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the dunning and collections strategy assigned to this customer in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR (e.g., standard dunning, escalated collections, legal action track). Drives automated dunning notice generation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this credit profile record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and IFRS/GAAP record-keeping requirements.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS/GAAP; ISO 9001; GDPR Article 5

attributes.name: credit_account_type
attributes.description: Classification of the credit account based on the nature of the customer relationship. Determines applicable credit policies and risk frameworks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: domestic|export|intercompany|distributor|oem|government — promote to reference product if additional types are required]

attributes.name: credit_control_area
attributes.description: SAP organizational unit (credit control area code) responsible for managing and monitoring this customer's credit. Defines the credit management scope and currency for credit limit enforcement in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR)

attributes.name: credit_hold_date
attributes.description: Date on which the credit hold was applied to the customer account. Used for aging analysis of credit holds and SLA compliance tracking for credit resolution processes.

attributes.name: credit_hold_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer account is currently placed on credit hold, blocking new order processing in SAP S/4HANA SD and Siemens Opcenter MES. True = credit hold active; False = no hold. Synchronized with Salesforce CRM account status.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001

attributes.name: credit_hold_reason
attributes.description: Reason code explaining why the customer account has been placed on credit hold. Required for audit trail and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) processes. Null when credit_hold_flag is False.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; ISO 9001 CAPA; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: credit_hold_released_date
attributes.description: Date on which the credit hold was lifted and normal order processing was reinstated. Null if the credit hold is still active. Used for credit resolution cycle time analysis.

attributes.name: credit_insurance_coverage_limit
attributes.description: Maximum insured amount under the trade credit insurance policy for this customer, in the account currency. Represents the maximum receivable amount recoverable in case of customer default.

attributes.name: credit_insurance_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which the trade credit insurance policy for this customer expires. Triggers renewal workflow alerts to prevent uninsured credit exposure.

attributes.name: credit_insurance_policy_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the trade credit insurance policy (e.g., Euler Hermes, Coface, Atradius) covering this customer's receivables. Null if no credit insurance is in place. Used for insurance claim processing and risk mitigation reporting.

attributes.name: credit_insurance_provider
attributes.description: Name of the trade credit insurance provider covering this customer's receivables (e.g., Euler Hermes, Coface, Atradius, Credendo). Null if no credit insurance is in place.

attributes.name: credit_limit
attributes.description: Maximum approved credit exposure (in the account currency) that can be extended to the customer at any point in time. Set during credit review and enforced in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR credit management.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

attributes.name: credit_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for credit analyst notes, special conditions, exceptions, or contextual information relevant to this customer's credit profile. Supports credit review documentation and exception management.

attributes.name: credit_rating
attributes.description: Internal or external credit risk rating assigned to the customer (e.g., A, B, C, D or numeric score). Used to classify credit risk tier and determine applicable credit terms, insurance requirements, and collection priority. May align with Dun & Bradstreet or internal scoring models.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; Basel III Credit Risk Framework

attributes.name: credit_rating_agency
attributes.description: Source or agency that issued the credit rating for this customer. Distinguishes between internally-derived ratings and third-party agency assessments used for credit risk management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal|dun_bradstreet|moody|sp|fitch|coface|euler_hermes — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: credit_review_frequency
attributes.description: Frequency at which formal credit reviews are scheduled for this customer. Determined by credit risk tier and customer segment. Higher-risk customers require more frequent reviews.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; Basel III

attributes.name: credit_segment
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA credit segment identifier that groups customers under a common credit management policy and credit limit pool. Enables multi-segment credit management for customers operating across multiple business units or regions.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR)

attributes.name: credit_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the customer credit profile. Drives order release decisions in SAP S/4HANA SD and MES order management. 'on_hold' and 'blocked' prevent new order processing until resolved.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001

attributes.name: credit_utilization_pct
attributes.description: Percentage of the approved credit limit currently consumed by the outstanding balance (outstanding_balance / credit_limit × 100). Triggers credit hold alerts when threshold is breached. Synchronized from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR credit exposure calculation.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit, outstanding balance, and financial terms are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the customer's billing currency in SAP S/4HANA.

attributes.name: dso_days
attributes.description: Days Sales Outstanding — the average number of days it takes the customer to pay invoices, calculated as (outstanding_balance / total_credit_sales) × number_of_days. A key KPI for accounts receivable performance and cash flow management per IFRS/GAAP reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS/GAAP; EBITDA reporting

attributes.name: dunning_level
attributes.description: Current dunning level (1–4) indicating the escalation stage of overdue payment notices sent to the customer. Level 1 = first reminder; Level 4 = final notice before legal action. Managed in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR dunning program.

attributes.name: early_payment_discount_days
attributes.description: Number of days from invoice date within which the customer must pay to qualify for the early payment discount. Null if no early payment discount applies.

attributes.name: early_payment_discount_pct
attributes.description: Percentage discount offered to the customer for early payment within the discount period defined in the payment terms (e.g., 2.00 for a 2% early payment discount). Null if no early payment discount applies.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date from which the current credit terms, credit limit, and risk classification became effective for this customer. Supports temporal credit management and audit trail requirements.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which the current credit terms and credit limit are scheduled to expire and require renewal or re-assessment. Null for open-ended credit agreements. Triggers next_credit_review_date workflow.

attributes.name: last_credit_review_date
attributes.description: Date on which the most recent formal credit assessment was completed for this customer. Triggers next review scheduling and is used for credit governance compliance reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; Basel III

attributes.name: last_dunning_date
attributes.description: Date on which the most recent dunning notice was issued to the customer for overdue invoices. Used to track collections activity and enforce dunning intervals in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credit profile record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in Databricks Silver Layer ingestion and audit compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Informatica MDM; IFRS/GAAP; ISO 9001

attributes.name: next_credit_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next formal credit review of this customer. Drives workflow alerts in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR and Salesforce CRM for proactive credit management.

attributes.name: oldest_overdue_days
attributes.description: Number of days since the oldest unpaid overdue invoice became due. Used for collections aging bucket analysis and bad debt provisioning triggers in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

attributes.name: outstanding_balance
attributes.description: Total open accounts receivable balance currently owed by the customer in the account currency, as synchronized from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR. Includes all open invoices, debit memos, and unapplied credits. Used for credit utilization monitoring.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

attributes.name: overdue_amount
attributes.description: Total value of invoices that have exceeded their due date and remain unpaid, in the account currency. A subset of outstanding_balance. Used for collections prioritization and bad debt provisioning per IFRS 9 expected credit loss model.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

attributes.name: payment_behavior_score
attributes.description: Internal scoring metric (0–100) reflecting the customer's historical payment behavior, including on-time payment rate, frequency of late payments, and average days late. Inputs into credit rating and risk category determination.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: Agreed payment instrument used by the customer for settling invoices (e.g., bank transfer, letter of credit for export customers, direct debit). Configured in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR payment method settings.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; SAP S/4HANA SD; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: payment_terms_code
attributes.description: SAP payment terms key defining the agreed payment schedule for this customer (e.g., NT30 = Net 30, NT60 = Net 60, NT90 = Net 90, 2/10NT30 = 2% discount if paid within 10 days, net 30). Drives invoice due date calculation in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; SAP S/4HANA SD; IFRS/GAAP

attributes.name: payment_terms_days
attributes.description: Number of calendar days from invoice date within which payment is due under the agreed payment terms (e.g., 30 for Net 30, 60 for Net 60, 90 for Net 90). Used for DSO calculation and cash flow forecasting.

attributes.name: risk_category
attributes.description: Categorical risk classification assigned to the customer based on credit rating, payment history, and financial health assessment. Drives credit limit setting, insurance requirements, and collection strategy.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; IFRS 9; Basel III Credit Risk Framework

attributes.name: salesforce_account_code
attributes.description: Salesforce CRM Account record ID for this customer, used for bi-directional synchronization of credit status and credit hold flags between SAP S/4HANA FI-AR and Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: sap_customer_account_number
attributes.description: The externally-known SAP S/4HANA FI-AR customer account number (debtor number) used to uniquely identify the customer in the ERP system. Serves as the business identifier for cross-system reconciliation.

products.name: sla_agreement
products.description: Service Level Agreement records defining contractual service commitments to industrial customers, including response time SLAs, uptime guarantees, on-time delivery targets, and support tier entitlements. Captures SLA type, tier (Platinum, Gold, Silver, Bronze), response time thresholds, resolution time thresholds, penalty clauses, effective date, expiry date, and associated account. Managed via Salesforce Service Cloud.

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the SLA agreement record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the B2B industrial customer, OEM account, distributor, or end-user organization account to which this SLA agreement applies. Links to the customer account master in the customer domain.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, SLA agreements are frequently scoped to specific physical plant or factory locations (account_sites) rather than just the parent customer account. For example, a factory r

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: SLA service contracts in multi-entity industrial manufacturers are legally bound to a specific company code for revenue recognition, contract liability reporting, and governing-law compliance. Finance

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, SLA agreements are formalized within or attached to a sales contract. Linking sla_agreement to sales_contract enables contract-SLA traceability for compliance audits, rene

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Reference to the designated customer contact person who receives escalation notifications when SLA thresholds are at risk of breach or have been breached. Links to the contact master in the customer domain.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Contact; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Service Contract Management: SLA agreements in industrial manufacturing are scoped to specific product SKUs (e.g., a maintenance contract for a specific automation controller). Field service dispatch,

attributes.name: agreement_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the SLA agreement as displayed in Salesforce Service Cloud and customer-facing documents (e.g., 'Platinum Support SLA — Acme Automation 2024'). Used for identification and reporting.

attributes.name: agreement_number
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number for the SLA agreement as assigned in Salesforce Service Cloud (e.g., SLA-2024-00123). Used in customer communications, contract documents, and cross-system references.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement Object; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: agreement_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the SLA agreement: draft (under negotiation), active (in force), suspended (temporarily paused), expired (past end date without renewal), or terminated (cancelled before end date). Governs whether SLA commitments are enforceable.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement Status; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: amendment_date
attributes.description: The date on which the most recent amendment or revision to the SLA agreement was executed and became effective. Null for the original version. Supports change management and audit compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement

attributes.name: annual_fee
attributes.description: The recurring annual fee charged to the customer for the SLA agreement, in the agreement currency. Used for revenue recognition, billing scheduling in SAP S/4HANA SD, and profitability analysis.
attributes.references: IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA SD

attributes.name: auto_renewal
attributes.description: Indicates whether the SLA agreement automatically renews upon expiry without requiring a new contract signature. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action. Drives renewal workflow automation in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: billing_frequency
attributes.description: The frequency at which the customer is invoiced for the SLA agreement fee (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual). Drives billing schedule creation in SAP S/4HANA SD and revenue recognition cadence.
attributes.references: IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA SD; GAAP ASC 606

attributes.name: contract_document_ref
attributes.description: Reference identifier or URL to the signed SLA contract document stored in the document management system (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM or SharePoint). Enables traceability to the original legal document.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM — Document Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

attributes.name: contract_value
attributes.description: The total monetary value of the SLA agreement over its full term, expressed in the agreement currency. Used for financial reporting, revenue recognition under IFRS 15/ASC 606, and penalty cap calculations.
attributes.references: IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO

attributes.name: covered_asset_count
attributes.description: The number of individual equipment units, machines, or installed assets covered under this SLA agreement. Used for capacity planning of field service resources and Maximo Asset Management integration.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the SLA agreement record was first created in the source system (Salesforce Service Cloud). Represents the record audit creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
attributes.references: ISO 8601; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Salesforce Service Cloud

attributes.name: currency_code
