attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value and penalty amounts in this SLA agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency financial reporting and consolidation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the SLA agreement becomes contractually binding and service commitments begin. Corresponds to the contract start date in Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement Start Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IFRS 15

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the SLA agreement ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended agreements. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance reporting in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement End Date; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IFRS 15

attributes.name: field_service_included
attributes.description: Indicates whether on-site field service visits are included in the SLA agreement. True = field service dispatches are covered; False = remote support only. Drives field service scheduling and resource allocation in Salesforce Field Service.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: governing_law_country
attributes.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this SLA agreement (e.g., USA, DEU). Determines applicable legal and regulatory frameworks for dispute resolution.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1; IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: initial_response_time_hours
attributes.description: The maximum number of hours within which the service team must provide an initial acknowledgment or response to a customer-reported incident or service request under this SLA. A core contractual KPI metric tracked in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Milestone; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; ANSI/ASQ Q9001

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the SLA agreement record was most recently updated in the source system (Salesforce Service Cloud). Used for incremental data ingestion, change detection, and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 8601; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; Salesforce Service Cloud; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: max_annual_service_visits
attributes.description: The maximum number of on-site service visits (preventive or corrective) included per year under this SLA agreement. Null if unlimited or not applicable. Used for field service capacity planning and entitlement consumption tracking.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for capturing additional contractual terms, special conditions, exclusions, or operational notes associated with the SLA agreement that are not captured in structured fields. Sourced from Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement description.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

attributes.name: on_time_delivery_target_pct
attributes.description: The contractually committed minimum percentage of orders or shipments that must be delivered on or before the promised delivery date under this SLA. Applicable for on_time_delivery SLA types. Tracked against SAP S/4HANA SD delivery performance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; ANSI/ASQ Q9001

attributes.name: parent_contract_ref
attributes.description: Reference number of the master or parent commercial contract (e.g., Master Service Agreement or Framework Agreement) under which this SLA agreement is issued. Enables hierarchical contract traceability in SAP S/4HANA SD.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IFRS 15

attributes.name: penalty_cap_pct
attributes.description: The maximum cumulative penalty or service credit that can be applied over the agreement period, expressed as a percentage of the total contract value. Limits the company's financial liability exposure from SLA breaches.

attributes.name: penalty_clause_applicable
attributes.description: Indicates whether financial or service credit penalties apply to this SLA agreement when contractual thresholds are breached. True = penalty clauses are active; False = no penalty provisions. Drives financial exposure tracking and CAPA processes.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IFRS 15; Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement

attributes.name: penalty_credit_pct
attributes.description: The percentage of the contracted service fee or invoice value that is credited or refunded to the customer for each SLA breach event, as defined in the penalty clause. Null when penalty_clause_applicable is False.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 — Revenue Recognition; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce Service Cloud

attributes.name: preventive_maintenance_included
attributes.description: Indicates whether scheduled preventive maintenance visits are included as part of the SLA agreement entitlement. True = preventive maintenance is covered; False = corrective/reactive only. Linked to TPM programs managed in Maximo.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; ISO 45001

attributes.name: remote_monitoring_included
attributes.description: Indicates whether IIoT-based remote monitoring and diagnostics of covered assets is included in the SLA agreement. True = remote monitoring via Aveva SCADA or IIoT platform is active; False = not included.

attributes.name: renewal_notice_days
attributes.description: The number of calendar days before the expiry date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate the SLA agreement. Triggers automated renewal alerts in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: resolution_time_hours
attributes.description: The maximum number of hours within which a reported incident or service request must be fully resolved under this SLA agreement. Drives escalation rules and penalty clause triggers in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Milestone; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; ANSI/ASQ Q9001

attributes.name: service_region
attributes.description: The primary geographic region where the SLA service commitments apply, expressed as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR). Determines applicable regulatory requirements, support staffing, and logistics SLA parameters.

attributes.name: service_tier
attributes.description: The contractual service tier level assigned to this SLA agreement, determining the priority, response speed, and entitlements available to the customer. Platinum is the highest tier; Bronze is the base tier. Drives case prioritization in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: sla_type
attributes.description: Classification of the SLA agreement by the primary service commitment it governs: response_time (initial response to incidents), uptime_guarantee (system/equipment availability), on_time_delivery (order fulfillment punctuality), support_entitlement (support tier access), or maintenance_coverage (preventive/corrective maintenance). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: response_time|uptime_guarantee|on_time_delivery|support_entitlement|maintenance_coverage|field_service — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement Type; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; ANSI/ASQ Q9001

attributes.name: spare_parts_included
attributes.description: Indicates whether the cost of spare parts and replacement components is covered under this SLA agreement. True = parts included; False = parts billed separately. Impacts inventory planning in SAP S/4HANA MM and WMS.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM; Maximo Asset Management; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: support_hours_coverage
attributes.description: The contracted support availability window defining hours per day and days per week during which the service team is obligated to respond (e.g., 8x5 = 8 hours/day, 5 days/week; 24x7 = round-the-clock). Determines staffing and escalation routing in Salesforce Service Cloud.
attributes.references: Salesforce Service Cloud — Business Hours; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: termination_date
attributes.description: The actual date on which the SLA agreement was terminated prior to its scheduled expiry date. Null for agreements that are active or expired naturally. Required for financial accrual reversal and CAPA closure in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: IFRS 15; GAAP ASC 606; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: termination_reason
attributes.description: The reason code for early termination of the SLA agreement. Null when the agreement is active or expired naturally. Used for churn analysis, CAPA processes, and financial reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; Salesforce CRM — Churn Analysis; IFRS 15

attributes.name: uptime_target_pct
attributes.description: The contractually committed minimum system or equipment availability expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.5 for 99.5% uptime). Applicable for uptime_guarantee SLA types. Used in OEE and availability reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.2; IEC 62443; Salesforce Service Cloud — Entitlement

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Sequential version number of the SLA agreement, incremented each time the agreement is amended or renegotiated. Version 1 is the original agreement. Supports audit trail and change history tracking per ISO 9001 document control requirements.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 — Document Control; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

products.name: account_site
products.description: Physical plant, factory, or facility locations associated with a customer account where Manufacturing products are installed, delivered, or serviced. Captures site name, site type (manufacturing plant, warehouse, distribution center, data center, commercial building, substation), address reference, site contact, installed product count, site criticality rating, site status (active, decommissioned, under construction), operational hours, and environmental/safety classification. Enables field service dispatch, logistics planning, installed base tracking, and preventive maintenance scheduling at customer locations.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account site record in the Manufacturing lakehouse. Primary key for the account_site entity.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM (Account Site Object); Informatica MDM

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent customer account to which this site belongs. Links the physical site to the B2B customer, OEM, distributor, or end-user organization in the account master.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM (Account Object); SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

attributes.name: address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: account_site stores a physical plant or factory location and currently duplicates address fields (address_line1, address_line2, city, state_province, postal_code, country_code, latitude, longitude) th

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Customer sites in industrial manufacturing are serviced by a specific legal entity (company code) for invoicing, VAT/tax compliance, and revenue recognition. The account_site has erp_plant_code which

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Site‑level cost tracking links each plant/site to a cost center for detailed operational expense reporting.

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Reference to the primary contact person at this site responsible for coordinating field service visits, deliveries, and maintenance activities. Links to the contact master record.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Contact Person

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center reporting per manufacturing site requires a profit_center_id on the site record.

attributes.name: site_sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Site‑level sales rep ownership is required for site‑specific revenue tracking and service contract management.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: Logistics scheduling links each customer site to the supplier site that delivers goods, enabling inbound receipt matching.

attributes.name: access_restriction_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes describing access restrictions, security clearance requirements, visitor registration procedures, or special entry protocols for field service engineers and delivery personnel at this site.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site Notes; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 45001

attributes.name: commissioning_date
attributes.description: Date on which the site was formally commissioned and became operational for Manufacturing product installations and services. Used to calculate site age, warranty period tracking, and lifecycle analytics.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site; SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the account site record was first created in the system of record (Salesforce CRM). Provides audit trail for data governance and record lineage tracking per ISO 9001 documentation requirements.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM System Fields; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

attributes.name: crm_site_code
attributes.description: Salesforce CRM native identifier for this account site record. Used for cross-system data lineage, reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Salesforce source system, and audit traceability.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site Object; Informatica MDM Cross-Reference

attributes.name: decommission_date
attributes.description: Date on which the site was or is planned to be decommissioned. Populated when site_status transitions to 'decommissioned'. Used for installed base retirement tracking and asset lifecycle reporting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site; SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location; ISO 55001

attributes.name: environmental_classification
attributes.description: Environmental condition classification of the site affecting product installation requirements, equipment ratings, and maintenance intervals. Informs selection of appropriate equipment IP ratings and material specifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|controlled|outdoor|corrosive|high_temperature|high_humidity — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IEC 60529 (IP Rating); ISO 14001; IEC 62443; NEMA Enclosure Standards

attributes.name: erp_plant_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA plant code corresponding to this customer site. Used for cross-system reconciliation of delivery addresses, service orders, and inventory movements associated with this site.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Plant Master; Informatica MDM Cross-Reference

attributes.name: industry_segment
attributes.description: Industry vertical or market segment classification of the customer site (e.g., Automotive, Food & Beverage, Oil & Gas, Utilities, Pharmaceuticals). Used for market analytics, product compatibility assessment, and regulatory compliance framework selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full industry taxonomy]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Industry Field; NAICS Industry Classification; SIC Codes

attributes.name: installed_product_count
attributes.description: Total number of Manufacturing products currently installed and tracked at this site. Drives installed base analytics, preventive maintenance scheduling volume, and spare parts demand forecasting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Installed Products; SAP S/4HANA PM Equipment Master; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: is_headquarters
attributes.description: Indicates whether this site is the primary headquarters or main facility of the customer account. Used for account hierarchy reporting, executive relationship management, and primary billing address determination.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the account site record in the system of record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM System Fields; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; Informatica MDM Data Governance

attributes.name: last_site_visit_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent field service engineer or account manager visit to the site. Used for relationship management, service frequency analysis, and identifying sites overdue for preventive maintenance visits.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Activity History; Maximo Asset Management Work Order; SAP S/4HANA PM

attributes.name: maximo_location_code
attributes.description: IBM Maximo Asset Management location identifier for this site. Used to link account site records to Maximo work orders, preventive maintenance plans, and asset hierarchies for field service execution.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management Location Object; ISO 55001 Asset Management

attributes.name: mes_system_present
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Manufacturing Execution System (MES) is installed at the site. Used to assess integration readiness for Siemens Opcenter MES connectivity and shop floor data exchange.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES; ISA-95 Manufacturing Operations Management; ISO 9001

attributes.name: network_connectivity_type
attributes.description: Primary network connectivity type available at the site for IIoT device integration, remote monitoring, and SCADA/MES connectivity. Determines feasibility of remote diagnostics and predictive maintenance services.
attributes.references: IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity; NIST Cybersecurity Framework; IIoT Architecture Standards

attributes.name: next_scheduled_maintenance_date
attributes.description: Date of the next planned preventive maintenance visit at this site. Drives field service dispatch scheduling, spare parts pre-positioning, and resource capacity planning for the service organization.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management PM Schedule; SAP S/4HANA PM Maintenance Plan; ISO 55001; TPM Methodology

attributes.name: number_of_production_lines
attributes.description: Count of active production lines at the manufacturing site. Used to estimate installed base potential, maintenance workload, and spare parts demand. Relevant for manufacturing plant site types.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES Production Line Configuration; SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center

attributes.name: operates_24x7
attributes.description: Indicates whether the site operates continuously 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. When true, overrides operational_hours_start/end for scheduling purposes and may trigger higher SLA tier assignment.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site; Maximo Asset Management Work Order Scheduling

attributes.name: operational_hours_end
attributes.description: Daily operational end time of the site in HH:MM (24-hour) format in the site's local time zone. Used to constrain field service scheduling, maintenance windows, and delivery cutoff times.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site Hours; ISO 8601; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

attributes.name: operational_hours_start
attributes.description: Daily operational start time of the site in HH:MM (24-hour) format in the site's local time zone. Used to schedule field service visits, preventive maintenance windows, and delivery time slots within permitted hours.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site Hours; ISO 8601; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

attributes.name: plant_floor_area_sqm
attributes.description: Total operational floor area of the site in square meters. Used for capacity planning, equipment density analysis, and logistics resource estimation for large-scale installations.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Clause 7.1; Salesforce CRM Account Site; Maximo Asset Management

attributes.name: power_supply_frequency_hz
attributes.description: Electrical supply frequency at the site in Hertz (50 or 60 Hz). Required for equipment compatibility validation for automation systems, drives, and electrification products.
attributes.references: IEC 60038; ANSI C84.1; IEC 61131; UL Certification

attributes.name: power_supply_voltage
attributes.description: Primary electrical supply voltage standard at the site. Critical for ensuring compatibility of electrification solutions and automation equipment installed or to be installed at the site. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 110V|220V|380V|400V|480V|600V|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IEC 60038 Standard Voltages; IEC 61131; ANSI C84.1; UL Certification

attributes.name: safety_classification
attributes.description: Safety and environmental classification of the site indicating special handling requirements for field service engineers and delivery personnel. Drives PPE requirements, access protocols, and compliance documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hazardous|non_hazardous|restricted_access|cleanroom|explosion_proof — promote to reference product if additional classifications are required]
attributes.references: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; IEC 62443; ISO 45001; EPA; NFPA 70E

attributes.name: scada_system_present
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) System Present Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a SCADA system is installed and operational at the site. Relevant for integration planning with Aveva SCADA and for determining remote monitoring service eligibility.
attributes.references: Aveva SCADA; IEC 62443; NIST SP 800-82 (ICS Security)

attributes.name: site_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the site across operational systems (SAP, Salesforce, Maximo). Used as the cross-system business key for site-level transactions, work orders, and shipments.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Delivery Plant Code; Salesforce CRM Site Code; Informatica MDM

attributes.name: site_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the primary on-site contact for service notifications, maintenance scheduling confirmations, and delivery alerts.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Contact Email; GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: site_contact_name
attributes.description: Full name of the primary on-site contact person for field service coordination, delivery acceptance, and maintenance scheduling. Captured directly for operational use when the contact master record may not yet be established.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Contact; GDPR Article 4; SAP S/4HANA SD

attributes.name: site_contact_phone
attributes.description: Direct phone number for the primary on-site contact. Used by field service engineers and logistics teams for on-site coordination and delivery confirmation.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Contact Phone; GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: site_criticality_rating
attributes.description: Business-assigned criticality level of the site reflecting the operational impact of downtime or service disruption. Drives SLA priority, spare parts stocking strategy, and preventive maintenance frequency. Aligned with FMEA risk classification.
attributes.references: Maximo Asset Management Criticality; FMEA Risk Priority; ISO 9001 Clause 6.1

attributes.name: site_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the physical plant, factory, or facility location (e.g., 'Chicago North Assembly Plant', 'Frankfurt Distribution Hub'). Used in field service dispatch, logistics planning, and installed base reporting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM (Account Site Name); ISO 9001 Clause 4.1

attributes.name: site_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the customer site. Determines eligibility for field service dispatch, new product installations, and preventive maintenance scheduling. 'decommissioned' sites are retained for historical installed base tracking.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site Status; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1

attributes.name: site_type
attributes.description: Classification of the facility type indicating the primary operational purpose of the site. Drives field service routing, SLA tier assignment, and preventive maintenance scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_plant|warehouse|distribution_center|data_center|commercial_building|substation — promote to reference product if additional types are required]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Account Site Type; ISO 9001 Clause 4.1; IEC 62443

attributes.name: sla_tier
attributes.description: SLA service tier assigned to this site, determining response time commitments, on-site support priority, and spare parts availability guarantees for field service and maintenance activities.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Service Cloud SLA; SAP S/4HANA SD Service Contract; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

attributes.name: time_zone
attributes.description: IANA time zone identifier for the site location (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'Europe/Berlin'). Used for scheduling preventive maintenance windows, field service appointments, and operational hours alignment.
attributes.references: IANA Time Zone Database; Salesforce CRM Time Zone; ISO 8601

domains.name: product
domains.description: Product catalog and offering management domain serving as the SSOT for all manufactured products, automation systems, electrification solutions, and smart infrastructure components. Manages SKU master data, product families, configurations, pricing structures, product lifecycle stages from NPI to end-of-life, and product portfolio management.

products.name: sku_master
products.description: Single source of truth for all Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) across the manufactured product portfolio. Captures the authoritative SKU identifier, description, unit of measure, weight, dimensions, hazardous material classification (UN number, hazard class, packing group, SDS reference), export control classification (ECCN), and lifecycle status. Serves as the foundational master record referenced by BOM, MES, ERP (SAP S/4HANA MM), and PLM (Siemens Teamcenter) systems. Every manufactured item, automation system component, electrification solution, and smart infrastructure element is registered here before it can be produced, sold, or shipped. Includes base/sales/production UoM with conversion factors and multi-language commercial descriptions. Note: product pricing structures are owned by the sales domain; this domain owns product identity, structure, and commercial catalog definition only.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the SKU master record. Primary key for the SKU master data product.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost Allocation Report requires each SKU to be charged to a cost center for manufacturing cost tracking.

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: sku_master.product_family_code is a denormalized string reference to the product family. Adding a proper FK sku_master.family_id -> product.family.family_id normalizes this relationship, enabling refe

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: NPI traceability: in industrial manufacturing, each SKU is developed through a New Product Introduction engineering project. Product managers and program managers need to trace which engineering proje

attributes.name: revenue_gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Financial reporting of sales per SKU needs a dedicated revenue GL account for posting invoices.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Primary Supplier Assignment report requires each SKU to reference its main vendor for cost, lead‑time, and compliance decisions.

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: Inventory classification based on value and consumption: A (high value, tight control), B (moderate value), C (low value, relaxed control). Used for inventory policy and cycle counting frequency.

attributes.name: base_uom
attributes.description: The fundamental unit of measure for inventory tracking and stock keeping. All other UoM conversions are calculated relative to this base unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|FT|L|GAL|M2|FT2|M3|FT3|SET|KIT — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: commercial_description
attributes.description: Marketing-optimized product description for sales and customer communication. Multi-language support maintained in PLM system.

attributes.name: cost_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard cost. Typically the company's base or functional currency for financial reporting.

attributes.name: country_of_origin
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product is manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs declarations and trade compliance.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the SKU master record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance and compliance.

attributes.name: dimension_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure for length, width, and height dimensions. Standardized for consistent warehouse and logistics operations.

attributes.name: discontinuation_date
attributes.description: Date when the SKU is discontinued and no longer available for new orders. Supports end-of-life planning and inventory liquidation strategies.

attributes.name: eccn_code
attributes.description: Export Control Classification Number assigned under the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR). Determines export licensing requirements and trade compliance obligations.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the SKU becomes active and available for transactions. Used for new product introductions and lifecycle transitions.

attributes.name: gross_weight
attributes.description: Total weight of the product including packaging, measured in the weight UoM. Used for logistics planning, freight calculation, and warehouse capacity management.

attributes.name: hazard_class
attributes.description: UN hazard classification code indicating the primary hazard type (e.g., 3 for flammable liquids, 8 for corrosive substances). Used for transportation and storage compliance.
attributes.references: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

attributes.name: hazmat_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the SKU is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and transportation procedures per regulatory requirements.

attributes.name: height
attributes.description: Height dimension of the product or its packaging, measured in the dimension UoM. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.

attributes.name: hts_code
attributes.description: Harmonized Tariff Schedule classification code for customs and international trade. Used to determine import duties, tariffs, and trade statistics reporting.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the SKU master record. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.

attributes.name: length
attributes.description: Length dimension of the product or its packaging, measured in the dimension UoM. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.

attributes.name: lifecycle_status
attributes.description: Current stage in the product lifecycle from New Product Introduction (NPI) through end-of-life. Governs availability for new orders, production scheduling, and inventory management decisions.

attributes.name: long_description
attributes.description: Detailed product description for technical documentation, sales catalogs, and customer-facing materials. Includes full technical specifications and feature details.

attributes.name: lot_control_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the SKU requires lot or batch number tracking for traceability, quality control, and recall management purposes.

attributes.name: make_or_buy_code
attributes.description: Procurement strategy indicator: make (manufactured in-house), buy (purchased from suppliers), or both (dual-sourced). Drives MRP planning logic and sourcing decisions.

attributes.name: material_number
attributes.description: SAP material number from S/4HANA MM module. Legacy identifier maintained for ERP integration and cross-system traceability.

attributes.name: net_weight
attributes.description: Weight of the product excluding packaging, measured in the weight UoM. Used for material content reporting and customs declarations.

attributes.name: packing_group
attributes.description: UN packing group classification indicating the degree of danger: I (high danger), II (medium danger), III (low danger). Determines packaging and handling requirements.
attributes.references: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

attributes.name: plm_item_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier from Siemens Teamcenter PLM system. Links the SKU master record to engineering design data, CAD models, BOMs, and technical documentation.

attributes.name: product_type
attributes.description: Classification of the SKU by manufacturing and inventory treatment. Determines BOM structure, production planning, and inventory valuation methods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: finished_good|semi_finished|raw_material|component|assembly|service_part|consumable|tool — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: production_to_base_conversion
attributes.business_glossary_term: Production to Base Unit of Measure (UoM) Conversion Factor
attributes.description: Conversion factor to translate production UoM quantities to base UoM. Used for MRP calculations and production planning.

attributes.name: production_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure used in manufacturing execution and shop floor control. Aligns with MES work order quantities and production reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|FT|L|GAL|M2|FT2|M3|FT3|SET|KIT|BATCH|LOT — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: revision_level
attributes.description: Current engineering revision or version of the product design. Managed through ECO/ECN processes in PLM. Critical for configuration management and change control.

attributes.name: sales_to_base_conversion
attributes.business_glossary_term: Sales to Base Unit of Measure (UoM) Conversion Factor
attributes.description: Conversion factor to translate sales UoM quantities to base UoM. Example: if sales UoM is BOX and base UoM is EA, and one box contains 12 pieces, the factor is 12.000000.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure Conversion

attributes.name: sales_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure used for customer orders and sales transactions. May differ from base UoM to accommodate customer ordering preferences. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|FT|L|GAL|M2|FT2|M3|FT3|SET|KIT|BOX|PALLET — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Order Management

attributes.name: sds_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or document number for the Safety Data Sheet (formerly MSDS) containing detailed hazard information, handling instructions, and emergency procedures.

attributes.name: serial_control_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the SKU requires individual serial number tracking for warranty management, asset tracking, and field service support.

attributes.name: shelf_life_days
attributes.description: Number of days the product remains usable or saleable from the date of manufacture or receipt. Used for inventory rotation, expiration management, and quality control.

attributes.name: short_description
attributes.description: Concise product description for internal operational use, typically 40 characters or less. Used in MES shop floor displays and warehouse picking lists.

attributes.name: sku_code
attributes.description: The authoritative, externally-known unique identifier for the SKU. Used across all systems including SAP S/4HANA MM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM, and Siemens Opcenter MES. This is the business key for the product.

attributes.name: standard_cost
attributes.description: Standard unit cost for inventory valuation and cost accounting. Maintained in the base currency and updated periodically through cost roll-up processes. Note: pricing structures are owned by the sales domain.

attributes.name: un_number
attributes.description: Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials as defined by the UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods. Required for shipping documentation and regulatory compliance.
attributes.references: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

attributes.name: volume
attributes.description: Volumetric measurement of the product or its packaging, measured in the volume UoM. Used for warehouse capacity planning and freight optimization.

attributes.name: volume_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure for volume. Used in logistics and warehouse management calculations.

attributes.name: weight_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure for gross and net weight values. Standardized across the enterprise for consistent logistics and shipping calculations.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: Width dimension of the product or its packaging, measured in the dimension UoM. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.

products.name: family
products.description: Defines the hierarchical grouping of related manufactured products into product families and sub-families (e.g., Industrial Drives, Low-Voltage Switchgear, Smart Building Controllers). Manages the product portfolio taxonomy used for planning, pricing, and go-to-market segmentation. Supports multi-level hierarchy with parent-child relationships, market segment alignment, and product line ownership. Referenced by PLM (Siemens Teamcenter) and SAP S/4HANA SD for catalog navigation and revenue reporting.

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Primary key for family

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Product families are mapped to inventory and revenue GL accounts for product-line financial segment reporting. family already has profit_center_id but no GL link. Industrial manufacturing controllers

attributes.name: parent_family_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent product family in the multi-level hierarchy. Enables nested family structures (e.g., Industrial Automation > Drives > Low Voltage Drives). Null for top-level families.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit Center Performance Report groups families to evaluate profitability by product line.

attributes.name: business_unit
attributes.description: Name or code of the business unit or division responsible for this product family. Defines ownership for P&L accountability, product management, and strategic roadmap decisions.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure (Profit Center, Division)

attributes.name: certification_requirements
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of required certifications and compliance standards for products in this family (e.g., UL, CE, IEC 61131, ISO 9001, ATEX, IECEx). Defines regulatory and safety compliance scope.
attributes.references: UL (Underwriters Laboratories), CE Marking, IEC Standards, ISO 9001

attributes.name: competitive_positioning
attributes.description: Strategic positioning statement describing how this product family competes in the market (e.g., technology leader, cost leader, niche specialist). Used for marketing strategy and sales enablement.
attributes.references: Porter's Competitive Strategy Framework, Product Marketing Best Practices

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this product family record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
attributes.references: Data Governance Best Practices, ISO 8601 Timestamp Format

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for standard cost and list price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Defines the monetary unit for financial attributes.

attributes.name: cybersecurity_classification
attributes.description: Cybersecurity risk classification for products in this family based on connectivity, data handling, and criticality to customer operations. Drives security testing, vulnerability management, and compliance with IEC 62443 and NIST Cybersecurity Framework.
attributes.references: IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity, NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: data_source_system
attributes.description: Name or code of the source system from which this product family record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Siemens Teamcenter PLM, Informatica MDM). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.
attributes.references: Data Governance Best Practices, Informatica MDM (Master Data Management)

attributes.name: distribution_channel
attributes.description: Primary distribution channel for products in this family (e.g., direct sales, distributor, OEM, e-commerce). Influences pricing, logistics, and customer engagement strategy.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution (SD) Organizational Structure

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this product family was or will be discontinued or retired from the catalog. Null for active families. Used for phase-out planning and end-of-life management.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Material Master Valid To Date, PLM Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this product family became active and available for sale or production. Marks the beginning of the family's lifecycle in the product catalog.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Material Master Valid From Date, PLM Lifecycle Management

attributes.name: environmental_compliance
attributes.description: Environmental and sustainability compliance standards applicable to this family (e.g., RoHS, REACH, WEEE, ISO 14001, Energy Star). Ensures adherence to environmental regulations and corporate sustainability commitments.
attributes.references: ISO 14001 Environmental Management, RoHS Directive, REACH Regulation, EPA Standards

attributes.name: erp_material_group
attributes.description: Material group code assigned in SAP S/4HANA (MATKL). Used for procurement, inventory management, and financial reporting. Links to purchasing conditions and valuation classes.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Material Master (MATKL), SAP Materials Management (MM)

attributes.name: family_code
attributes.description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the product family. Used in SAP S/4HANA SD for catalog navigation and PLM (Siemens Teamcenter) for product classification. Serves as the business identifier for cross-system integration.

attributes.name: family_description
attributes.description: Detailed business description of the product family, including its purpose, key characteristics, typical applications, and differentiating features. Used for marketing collateral, sales enablement, and product documentation.

attributes.name: family_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the product family (e.g., Industrial Drives, Low-Voltage Switchgear, Smart Building Controllers, Programmable Logic Controllers). Primary display label for catalog navigation and reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Material Master, Siemens Teamcenter PLM

attributes.name: family_type
attributes.description: Classification of the product family by offering type. Distinguishes between finished goods (end products), components (parts), assemblies (sub-systems), services (maintenance/support), software (embedded/standalone), systems (integrated solutions), and spare parts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: finished_goods|components|assemblies|services|software|systems|spare_parts — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: geographic_availability
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of geographic regions or countries where products in this family are available for sale (e.g., EMEA, APAC, Americas, USA, DEU, CHN). Reflects regulatory approvals, distribution network, and go-to-market scope.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Sales Organization, ISO 3166 Country Codes

attributes.name: hazardous_material_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether products in this family contain or are classified as hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, or transportation (e.g., batteries, chemicals, flammable substances). Drives compliance with OSHA, EPA, and transportation regulations.
attributes.references: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard, EPA Regulations, UN Dangerous Goods Classification

attributes.name: hierarchy_level
attributes.description: Numeric level in the product family hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents top-level families (e.g., Automation Systems). Level 2 represents sub-families (e.g., Drives). Level 3+ represents further nested classifications. Used for catalog navigation depth and reporting rollups.

attributes.name: innovation_priority
attributes.description: Priority level for innovation and new product development within this family. High priority families receive accelerated R&D investment and technology roadmap focus.
attributes.references: Product Management Best Practices, R&D Portfolio Management

attributes.name: iot_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether products in this family have embedded IoT (Internet of Things) or IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) connectivity for remote monitoring, predictive maintenance, or data analytics. Supports digital services and smart infrastructure offerings.
attributes.references: IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity, IoT Architecture Best Practices

attributes.name: last_modified_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person or system that last modified this product family record. Supports audit trail and accountability for master data changes.
attributes.references: Data Governance Best Practices, SAP S/4HANA User Audit Trail

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this product family record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.
attributes.references: Data Governance Best Practices, ISO 8601 Timestamp Format

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Standard manufacturing or procurement lead time in days for products in this family. Used for order promising, production planning (MRP), and inventory policy setting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Production Planning (PP), MRP (Material Requirements Planning)

attributes.name: lifecycle_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle stage of the product family. Active families are available for sale and production. Discontinued families are no longer offered. Phase-out families are being retired. NPI (New Product Introduction) families are in launch phase. End-of-life families support existing installations only. Under-development families are pre-launch.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Material Status, PLM Lifecycle Management (ISO 10303 STEP)

attributes.name: list_price
attributes.description: Representative list price or price range midpoint for products in this family. Used for catalog display, quotation guidance, and revenue forecasting. Actual SKU prices may vary based on configuration and customer agreements.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution (SD) Pricing, ISO 4217 Currency Codes

attributes.name: manufacturing_strategy
attributes.description: Primary manufacturing strategy for products in this family. Make-to-stock (MTS) builds to forecast. Make-to-order (MTO) builds to customer orders. Engineer-to-order (ETO) customizes designs. Configure-to-order (CTO) assembles from standard modules. Assemble-to-order (ATO) final assembly from components.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Production Planning (PP), APICS Supply Chain Management

attributes.name: market_segment
attributes.description: Primary market segment or vertical industry that this product family serves. Aligns with go-to-market strategy and sales organization structure. Used for revenue reporting, market analysis, and strategic planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: industrial_automation|building_automation|energy_management|transportation|process_industries|discrete_manufacturing|infrastructure|oem_solutions — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Sales Organization Structure, Industry Classification (NAICS, SIC)

attributes.name: mean_time_between_failures
attributes.description: Average Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in hours for products in this family. Key reliability metric used for maintenance planning, service level agreements, and product quality benchmarking.
attributes.references: IEC 61508 Functional Safety, ISO 14224 Reliability Data, TPM (Total Productive Maintenance)

attributes.name: mean_time_to_repair
attributes.description: Average Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) in hours for products in this family. Indicates serviceability and downtime impact. Used for maintenance planning and SLA (Service Level Agreement) definition.
attributes.references: IEC 61508 Functional Safety, ISO 14224 Reliability Data, CMMS Best Practices

attributes.name: plm_category
attributes.description: Classification code or category assigned in the PLM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for document management, BOM (Bill of Materials) structure, and engineering change control (ECO/ECN).
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM, ISO 10303 STEP (Product Data Representation)

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether products in this family are manufactured in-house, purchased from suppliers, or a combination of both. Drives sourcing strategy and supply chain planning.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Materials Management (MM), SAP Ariba Procurement

attributes.name: product_line_owner
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the product manager or product line owner responsible for this family. Accountable for portfolio strategy, pricing, and lifecycle management.
attributes.references: Product Management Best Practices, SAP S/4HANA User Assignment

attributes.name: product_portfolio_strategy
attributes.description: Strategic classification of the product family in the corporate portfolio. Invest families receive R&D funding and growth initiatives. Maintain families sustain current market position. Harvest families maximize cash flow with minimal investment. Divest families are candidates for discontinuation or sale.
attributes.references: BCG Growth-Share Matrix, Product Portfolio Management Best Practices

attributes.name: record_status
attributes.description: Administrative status of this master data record. Active records are current and in use. Inactive records are deprecated but retained for historical reference. Pending approval records await governance review. Archived records are retained for compliance but not operational use.
attributes.references: Data Governance Best Practices, Informatica MDM (Master Data Management)

attributes.name: sales_organization
attributes.description: Primary sales organization code responsible for selling products in this family. Defines sales territory, pricing authority, and revenue attribution in SAP S/4HANA SD.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution (SD) Organizational Structure

attributes.name: service_level_tier
attributes.description: Default service level tier for products in this family. Defines response time commitments, spare parts availability, and support escalation paths. Influences service pricing and customer expectations.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Service Management, SLA (Service Level Agreement) Best Practices

attributes.name: standard_cost
attributes.description: Average standard cost per unit for products in this family, used for financial planning and margin analysis. Expressed in the company's base currency. Aggregated from individual SKU costs.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO), IFRS/GAAP Cost Accounting Standards

attributes.name: target_customer_segment
attributes.description: Primary customer segment or persona targeted by this product family (e.g., large industrial OEMs, mid-size system integrators, building owners, utilities). Guides marketing campaigns and sales territory planning.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Customer Segmentation, Market Segmentation Best Practices

attributes.name: target_margin_percent
attributes.description: Target gross margin percentage for products in this family, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 35.50 for 35.5%). Used for pricing strategy, product mix optimization, and profitability analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO), Financial Planning Best Practices

attributes.name: technology_platform
attributes.description: Underlying technology platform or architecture shared by products in this family (e.g., SIMATIC Platform, SINAMICS Drive Platform, Desigo Building Automation Platform). Enables commonality analysis and platform lifecycle planning.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM (Platform Management), ISO 10303 STEP

attributes.name: warranty_period_months
attributes.description: Standard warranty period in months offered for products in this family. Defines the baseline service commitment and influences pricing and service revenue forecasting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Service Management, ISO 9001 Quality Management

products.name: configuration
products.description: Manages configurable product variants and option sets for make-to-order and engineer-to-order automation systems and electrification solutions. Captures configuration rules, valid option combinations, constraint logic, base model references, and configured variant codes. Supports CPQ (Configure-Price-Quote) workflows in Salesforce CRM and variant configuration in SAP S/4HANA. Tracks which configuration options are available per product family, regional market, and customer segment.

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Primary key for configuration

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) that defines the components and assemblies required to manufacture this configuration.

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: configuration.product_family_code is a denormalized string reference to the product family. A configurable product variant belongs to a product family — this is essential for grouping configurations b

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Product configurations carry base_price and total_configuration_price requiring revenue GL account assignment for order-to-cash revenue recognition and configuration-level pricing analytics. Industria

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each configuration variant represents a distinct sellable offering; profit center assignment enables product-line P&L reporting at the configuration level. Industrial manufacturing controllers track m

attributes.name: routing_id
attributes.description: Identifier for the manufacturing routing or process plan used to produce this configuration. Defines the sequence of operations.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the base product or product family that this configuration is derived from. Links to the product master catalog.

attributes.name: superseded_by_configuration_id
attributes.description: Reference to the configuration that replaces this one when it becomes obsolete. Supports product migration and upgrade paths.

attributes.name: application_type
attributes.description: Intended application or use case for this configuration. Describes the operational context where the product will be deployed.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when this configuration was formally approved for use in quoting and order fulfillment. Part of the configuration governance process.

attributes.name: base_price
attributes.description: Starting price for the base product before configuration options are applied. Expressed in the default currency.

attributes.name: certification_requirements
attributes.description: List of required certifications and compliance standards for this configuration, such as CE, UL, ISO certifications. Varies by market and application.
attributes.references: ISO 9001, CE Marking, UL Certification Standards

attributes.name: configuration_code
attributes.description: Unique business identifier for the configured product variant. Used in CPQ (Configure-Price-Quote) workflows and order processing systems.

attributes.name: configuration_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the configured product variant, including key features, options selected, and intended use case.

attributes.name: configuration_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or title for the configured product variant. Describes the specific configuration in business terms.

attributes.name: configuration_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the product configuration. Indicates whether the configuration is available for quoting and ordering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|active|inactive|obsolete|pending_approval|approved|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: configuration_type
attributes.description: Classification of the configuration approach. Standard configurations are pre-defined, custom configurations are unique to customer requirements, engineer-to-order requires design work, make-to-order uses existing designs with custom parameters, configure-to-order selects from predefined options, and assemble-to-order combines standard modules.

attributes.name: constraint_rules
attributes.description: Business rules and constraints that govern valid option combinations for this configuration. Defines incompatibilities, dependencies, and mandatory selections.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this configuration record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for pricing fields. Indicates the currency in which prices are expressed.

attributes.name: customer_segment
attributes.description: Target customer segment or industry vertical for this configuration. Examples include automotive, energy, infrastructure, building automation.

attributes.name: dimensions_height_mm
attributes.description: Height dimension of the configured product in millimeters. Critical for packaging, shipping, and installation planning.

attributes.name: dimensions_length_mm
attributes.description: Length dimension of the configured product in millimeters. Critical for packaging, shipping, and installation planning.

attributes.name: dimensions_width_mm
attributes.description: Width dimension of the configured product in millimeters. Critical for packaging, shipping, and installation planning.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date after which this configuration is no longer available for new orders. Supports product phase-out and end-of-life management.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which this configuration becomes available for quoting and ordering. Supports phased product launches and market introductions.

attributes.name: is_orderable
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this configuration can currently be ordered. Used to control availability in CPQ (Configure-Price-Quote) and order management systems.

attributes.name: is_quotable
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this configuration can be included in customer quotes. May be quotable but not yet orderable during pre-launch phases.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this configuration record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Standard manufacturing and delivery lead time for this configuration in days. Used for order promising and scheduling.
attributes.references: MRP (Material Requirements Planning) Lead Time Standard

attributes.name: manufacturing_complexity
attributes.description: Assessment of the manufacturing complexity for this configuration. Impacts production planning, resource allocation, and lead time.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum number of units that must be ordered for this configuration. Used to enforce economic order quantities and production efficiency.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this configuration. Used for internal communication and documentation.

attributes.name: power_rating_kw
attributes.description: Electrical power rating of the configured product in kilowatts. Relevant for electrification solutions and automation systems.

attributes.name: price_adjustment
attributes.description: Total price adjustment resulting from selected configuration options. Can be positive (upcharge) or negative (discount).

attributes.name: pricing_model
attributes.description: Pricing approach used for this configuration. Determines how the final price is calculated based on base product and selected options.

attributes.name: regional_availability
attributes.description: Geographic regions or markets where this configuration is available for sale. Comma-separated list of region codes or market identifiers.

attributes.name: requires_engineering_review
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether orders for this configuration require engineering review and approval before production. Common for engineer-to-order configurations.

attributes.name: selected_options
attributes.description: Comma-separated or structured list of specific option codes selected for this configuration variant. Represents the actual choices made from the available option set.

attributes.name: technical_specification_document
attributes.description: Reference to the technical specification document or datasheet that provides detailed engineering specifications for this configuration.

attributes.name: total_configuration_price
attributes.description: Final calculated price for the configured product variant, including base price and all option adjustments.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for this configuration. Supports configuration change management and revision control.

attributes.name: voltage_rating
attributes.description: Operating voltage specification for the configured product. Critical for electrical compatibility and safety compliance.

attributes.name: weight_kg
attributes.description: Total weight of the configured product in kilograms. Used for shipping, logistics planning, and installation requirements.

products.name: bom_header
products.description: Bill of Materials (BOM) header record defining the top-level assembly structure for manufactured products, automation systems, and electrification solutions. Captures BOM type (engineering BOM, manufacturing BOM, sales BOM), BOM status, effective date range, plant assignment, base quantity, and revision level. Managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and synchronized to SAP S/4HANA PP for production planning. The BOM header is the authoritative structural definition of a manufactured product.
products.reference: SAP BOM Header (CS01/MM01), Siemens Teamcenter BOM

attributes.name: bom_header_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the BOM header record. Primary key for the BOM header entity.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Engineering cost allocation tracks BOM creation expenses against the responsible cost center.

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: ECN-to-BOM synchronization: the Engineering Change Notice is the formal release document that triggers BOM updates in ERP. MES/ERP synchronization workflows and supplier notification processes require

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: ECO-driven BOM change traceability: every product BOM revision in industrial manufacturing is authorized by an Engineering Change Order. Change impact reports and ERP audit trails require linking bom_

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility where this BOM is valid and used. BOMs can be plant-specific to accommodate local manufacturing processes and material availability.

attributes.name: product_family_id
attributes.description: FK to product.family

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: bom_header.revision_level is a denormalized string reference to the engineering revision. In manufacturing, a Bill of Materials is always associated with a specific engineering revision — the BOM defi

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufactured product, automation system, or electrification solution that this BOM defines. Links to the product master data.

attributes.name: alternative_bom_indicator
attributes.description: Identifier for alternative BOM versions for the same product. Allows multiple valid BOMs for a product to support different manufacturing methods, material substitutions, or regional variations.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when this BOM version was formally approved for production use. Marks the completion of engineering review and quality validation processes.

attributes.name: base_quantity
attributes.description: The reference quantity for which the BOM is defined. All component quantities in the BOM lines are specified relative to this base quantity. Typically 1 unit of the finished product.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP BOM Base Quantity

attributes.name: base_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the base quantity. Defines the measurement unit (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) for the finished product quantity.

attributes.name: bom_category
attributes.description: Structural classification of the BOM. Standard for fixed structures, Configurable for variant BOMs, Phantom for transient assemblies, Reference for documentation, Kit for bundled items.

attributes.name: bom_description
attributes.description: Textual description of the BOM purpose, scope, or special characteristics. Provides context for the BOM structure and its intended application.

attributes.name: bom_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the BOM. Externally-known unique code used across PLM and ERP systems for referencing the BOM structure.

attributes.name: bom_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the BOM header. Tracks progression from draft through approval to active production use and eventual obsolescence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|active|frozen|obsolete|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: bom_type
attributes.description: Classification of the BOM by its intended use. Engineering BOM for design, Manufacturing BOM for production, Sales BOM for customer-facing configurations, Service BOM for maintenance, Planning BOM for MRP, Costing BOM for financial analysis.

attributes.name: bom_usage
attributes.description: Intended application context for this BOM. Defines whether the BOM is used for production runs, prototyping, spare parts management, rework operations, simulation, or testing purposes.

attributes.name: configuration_profile
attributes.description: Reference to the variant configuration profile for configurable products. Links the BOM to the product configurator rules and constraints.

attributes.name: created_date
attributes.description: Timestamp when this BOM header record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field for record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date from which this BOM version becomes valid and can be used for production planning and manufacturing execution. Supports phased rollout of engineering changes.

attributes.name: environmental_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this BOM must comply with environmental regulations (RoHS, REACH, WEEE, ISO 14001). Used to enforce material restrictions and sustainability requirements.

attributes.name: erp_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier for this BOM in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA PP). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date after which this BOM version is no longer valid for production use. Nullable for BOMs without a planned end date. Used to phase out obsolete BOM structures.

attributes.name: explosion_type
attributes.description: Defines how the BOM structure is expanded in planning and costing. Single-level shows immediate components, Multi-level shows full hierarchy, Summarized aggregates across levels.

attributes.name: is_configurable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this BOM supports variant configuration. True for BOMs with configurable components or optional features that can be selected during order entry.

attributes.name: is_critical
attributes.description: Indicates whether this BOM is for a critical product requiring special handling, quality controls, or regulatory compliance. Used to trigger enhanced review and approval workflows.

attributes.name: is_phantom
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is a phantom BOM. Phantom BOMs represent transient assemblies that are not stocked but are consumed immediately in the parent assembly.

attributes.name: last_modified_date
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this BOM header record. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: lot_size
attributes.description: Standard production batch size for which this BOM is optimized. Used in Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and production scheduling to determine component requirements.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the BOM. Used for manufacturing notes, quality alerts, or design rationale.

attributes.name: plm_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier for this BOM in the source PLM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for synchronization and traceability between PLM and ERP systems.

attributes.name: production_version
attributes.description: Identifier linking the BOM to a specific production process and routing. Enables multiple production methods for the same product with different BOM and routing combinations.

attributes.name: regulatory_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this BOM is subject to regulatory compliance requirements (CE marking, UL certification, IEC standards, etc.). Triggers additional documentation and validation workflows.
attributes.references: IEC 61131 PLC Programming, CE Marking, UL Product Safety

products.name: product_bom_line
products.description: Individual component line items within a Bill of Materials (BOM), representing the parent-child assembly relationships for manufactured products. Captures component SKU reference, quantity per assembly, unit of measure, item category (stock, non-stock, phantom), scrap factor, lead time offset, alternative item group, and engineering change order (ECO) reference. Supports multi-level BOM explosion for MRP runs in SAP S/4HANA PP and production routing in Siemens Opcenter MES. Each bom_line belongs to exactly one bom_header and references exactly one component from sku_master.
products.reference: SAP BOM Item (CS01), Siemens Teamcenter BOM Line

attributes.name: product_bom_line_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Product Bill of Materials (BOM) Line ID
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the BOM line item. Primary key for the product_bom_line entity.

attributes.name: bom_header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the parent BOM header that this line belongs to. Each BOM line must belong to exactly one BOM header.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Production planning reports require each BOM line to reference the engineering component master to ensure design consistency and cost roll‑up.

attributes.name: drawing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: Manufacturing execution system uses the drawing ID per BOM line to fetch exact assembly drawings for work orders and quality inspections.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the component material or part from the SKU master data that is required for this assembly. Each BOM line references exactly one component SKU.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: BOM Supplier Allocation process tracks which vendor supplies each component, essential for production scheduling and quality audits.

attributes.name: work_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the work center or production resource where this component is consumed. Used for shop floor material staging and kitting.
attributes.references: Siemens Opcenter MES Shop Floor Control

attributes.name: alternative_item_group
attributes.description: Identifier for a group of interchangeable components that can substitute for each other in the assembly. Used for flexible BOM structures and supply chain resilience.

attributes.name: alternative_item_priority
attributes.description: Priority ranking for this component within its alternative item group. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for material selection during MRP and production execution.

attributes.name: assembly_level
attributes.description: The hierarchical level of this component in the multi-level BOM structure. Level 0 is the finished product, level 1 is direct sub-assembly, level 2 is sub-sub-assembly, etc. Used for BOM explosion and implosion.

attributes.name: backflush_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this component is consumed automatically via backflushing when the operation is confirmed (True) or requires manual goods issue (False). Used in lean manufacturing and MES integration.

attributes.name: bulk_material_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this component is a bulk material (liquids, powders, gases) that requires special handling, storage, and measurement during production.

attributes.name: change_number
attributes.description: Internal change control number tracking modifications to this BOM line. Used for audit trail and version control in engineering change management.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Engineering Change Management

attributes.name: component_height_mm
attributes.description: Height dimension of the component in millimeters. Used for variable-size materials and spatial planning in assembly.

attributes.name: component_length_mm
attributes.description: Length dimension of the component in millimeters. Used for variable-size materials and spatial planning in assembly.

attributes.name: component_origin
attributes.description: Procurement strategy for this component: make (manufactured in-house), buy (purchased from supplier), make_or_buy (flexible sourcing), subcontract (outsourced manufacturing).

attributes.name: component_weight_kg
attributes.description: Weight of the component in kilograms. Used for calculating total assembly weight, shipping weight, and material handling requirements.

attributes.name: component_width_mm
attributes.description: Width dimension of the component in millimeters. Used for variable-size materials and spatial planning in assembly.

attributes.name: cost_relevance_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this component should be included in product costing calculations. Text items and phantom assemblies are typically not cost-relevant.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Product Cost Controlling

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who created this BOM line record. Used for accountability and audit trail.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this BOM line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: critical_component_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this component is critical for production and requires special attention in material planning, procurement, and availability checking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP Material Requirements Planning (MRP)

attributes.name: ecn_number
attributes.description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice that authorized this BOM line change. Provides traceability to engineering change documentation.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Engineering Change Management
