attributes.name: eco_number
attributes.description: Reference to the Engineering Change Order that introduced or modified this BOM line. Links BOM changes to formal engineering change management processes.
attributes.references: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Engineering Change Management

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date after which this BOM line is no longer active and should not be used in new production orders. Null indicates no planned end date.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this BOM line becomes active and should be used in production planning and execution. Supports date-effectivity for phased component changes.

attributes.name: fixed_quantity_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether the component quantity is fixed regardless of parent assembly batch size (True) or scales proportionally with batch size (False). Used for setup materials and consumables.

attributes.name: installation_point
attributes.description: Physical location or position identifier where this component is installed in the parent assembly. Used for assembly instructions and service documentation.

attributes.name: item_category
attributes.description: Classification of the BOM line item type: stock (regular inventory item), non_stock (direct procurement), phantom (pass-through assembly with no inventory), text (documentation only), variable_size (length/weight variable), co_product (joint output), by_product (secondary output). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock|non_stock|phantom|text|variable_size|co_product|by_product — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: lead_time_offset_days
attributes.description: Number of days before the parent assembly start date that this component must be available. Used for backward scheduling in production planning and MRP runs.

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line item number within the BOM header, used for ordering and referencing specific components in the assembly structure.

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this BOM line record. Used for accountability and audit trail.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this BOM line record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional instructions, comments, or special handling requirements for this component in the assembly process.

attributes.name: operation_sequence
attributes.description: The routing operation number at which this component is consumed or installed in the manufacturing process. Links BOM to routing for integrated production planning.

attributes.name: product_bom_line_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this BOM line: active (in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), pending (awaiting approval), obsolete (phased out), blocked (quality hold), released (approved for production).

attributes.name: quantity_per_assembly
attributes.description: The required quantity of this component needed to produce one unit of the parent assembly. Supports up to 6 decimal places for precision in chemical, pharmaceutical, and electronics manufacturing.

attributes.name: revision_level
attributes.description: Engineering revision level or version of this BOM line. Tracks design iterations and ensures correct component versions are used in production.

attributes.name: scrap_factor_percent
attributes.description: Expected scrap or waste percentage for this component during the manufacturing process. Used by MRP to calculate additional material requirements. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP Material Requirements Planning (MRP)

attributes.name: spare_part_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether this component is also available as a spare part for after-sales service and maintenance. Used for service parts planning and documentation.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the component quantity (e.g., EA=Each, PC=Piece, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, M=Meter). Must align with the component SKU base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|G|L|ML|M|CM|FT|IN|LB|OZ|SET|BOX|ROLL|SHEET — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: product_revision
products.description: Manages engineering revision history for manufactured products, tracking each revision level, Engineering Change Order (ECO) and Engineering Change Notice (ECN) references, revision description, effectivity date, and approval status. Captures the delta between revisions including affected BOM lines, drawing changes, and specification updates. Ensures traceability from product design through manufacturing execution per ISO 9001 document control requirements. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter PLM.

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the product revision record. Primary key.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Engineering change orders (ECOs/ECNs) — evidenced by ecn_number and eco_number on product_revision — incur engineering labor, tooling, and testing costs charged to cost centers. Industrial manufacturi

attributes.name: ecn_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: ECN-to-product-revision traceability: the Engineering Change Notice is the formal release document that authorizes a product revision. Regulatory compliance and customer notification workflows require

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: Change management traceability: every product revision in industrial manufacturing is triggered by an Engineering Change Order. Auditors and PPAP documentation require tracing product_revision back to

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Engineering-to-product revision traceability: in industrial manufacturing, a product revision is directly derived from an engineering revision. PPAP submissions, regulatory filings, and interchangeabi

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent product for which this revision applies. Links to the product master record.

attributes.name: superseded_revision_product_revision_id
attributes.description: Reference to the previous product revision that this revision replaces. Maintains revision lineage and traceability.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when this revision was formally approved for release to manufacturing.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state of the revision within the engineering change control workflow.

attributes.name: bom_affected_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision includes changes to the product Bill of Materials structure or component list.
attributes.references: PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) Best Practices, MRP (Material Requirements Planning)

attributes.name: change_impact_level
attributes.description: Assessment of the magnitude and scope of impact this revision has on form, fit, function, manufacturing process, or supply chain.

attributes.name: change_reason_code
attributes.description: Categorization of the primary business reason or driver for this engineering revision.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this revision record was first created in the source system.

attributes.name: customer_notification_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether customers must be formally notified of this revision due to impact on form, fit, function, or regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: drawing_affected_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision includes changes to engineering drawings, CAD (Computer-Aided Design) models, or technical documentation.

attributes.name: effectivity_date
attributes.description: Date when this revision becomes effective for manufacturing and procurement. Determines when the new revision must be used in production.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, MRP (Material Requirements Planning) Standards

attributes.name: effectivity_serial_end
attributes.description: Ending serial number through which this revision is effective, when effectivity is controlled by serial number range.

attributes.name: effectivity_serial_start
attributes.description: Starting serial number from which this revision is effective, when effectivity is controlled by serial number.

attributes.name: effectivity_type
attributes.description: Method by which the revision effectivity is controlled (by date, serial number range, lot/batch, or immediate implementation).
attributes.references: PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) Best Practices, ISO 9001

attributes.name: interchangeability_code
attributes.description: Defines whether this revision can be substituted for previous revisions in existing assemblies or field installations.
attributes.references: PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) Best Practices, ISO 9001

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this revision record was last updated in the source system.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-text notes, comments, or instructions related to this revision for engineering, manufacturing, or quality teams.

attributes.name: obsolete_date
attributes.description: Date when this revision was marked as obsolete and no longer valid for new production orders.

attributes.name: ppap_required_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Required Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision requires a new Production Part Approval Process submission to customers.
attributes.references: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

attributes.name: process_affected_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision requires changes to manufacturing processes, work instructions, or shop floor procedures.

attributes.name: regulatory_approval_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision requires re-certification or approval from regulatory bodies (UL, CE, OSHA, EPA, etc.).
attributes.references: ISO 9001, UL Standards, CE Marking Directive

attributes.name: release_date
attributes.description: Date when this revision was officially released to production and made available for use in manufacturing operations.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) Best Practices

attributes.name: revision_code
attributes.description: Engineering revision level identifier (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Represents the sequential version of the product design.

attributes.name: revision_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the changes introduced in this revision, including design modifications, material changes, or process improvements.

attributes.name: revision_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the product revision indicating its approval and release state.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) Best Practices

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Name of the source PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) or ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system from which this revision record originated.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier of this revision record in the source PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) system for traceability and reconciliation.

attributes.name: specification_affected_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision includes changes to product specifications, performance requirements, or acceptance criteria.

attributes.name: testing_affected_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision requires changes to quality testing procedures, inspection plans, or validation protocols.

attributes.name: tooling_affected_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this revision requires changes to manufacturing tooling, fixtures, jigs, or molds.
attributes.references: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), DFM (Design for Manufacturability)

products.name: product_specification
products.description: Technical and commercial specifications for manufactured products, automation systems, and electrification solutions. Captures electrical ratings, mechanical dimensions, environmental ratings (IP class, NEMA class), operating temperature range, performance parameters, and application suitability. Includes references to associated technical documentation (datasheets, installation manuals, safety data sheets) managed in the engineering domain's document management system. Serves as the authoritative technical specification record referenced by engineering, quality, and sales teams. Managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP S/4HANA. Note: certification records (UL, CE, IEC, ATEX, RoHS/REACH) are tracked separately in the certification product.
products.reference: PLM Product Specification, IEC/UL/CE Technical Standards

attributes.name: product_specification_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the product specification record. Primary key.

attributes.name: drawing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: Drawing-to-specification linkage: product specifications reference the controlling technical drawing for dimensional and tolerance requirements. CE marking, UL certification, and customer qualificatio

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Specification derivation traceability: product specifications (commercial/manufacturing) are derived from engineering specifications (design intent). Regulatory submissions, customer qualification pac

attributes.name: product_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: product_specification.revision_number is a denormalized string reference to the engineering revision. A technical specification is authored and valid for a specific engineering revision of a product —

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent product in the product catalog. Links this specification to the master product record.

attributes.name: altitude_rating_meters
attributes.description: Maximum operating altitude in meters above sea level. Defines altitude limitations for product operation due to air pressure and cooling considerations.

attributes.name: application_suitability
attributes.description: Intended application areas and use cases. Describes the industrial applications, environments, or processes for which the product is designed and suitable.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when this specification version was formally approved. Tracks the approval milestone in the specification lifecycle.

attributes.name: color_finish
attributes.description: Product color and surface finish. Describes the external appearance and coating (e.g., RAL 7035 light grey, powder coated, anodized aluminum).

attributes.name: communication_protocol
attributes.description: Supported communication protocols for data exchange. Lists the industrial communication standards the product supports (e.g., PROFINET, EtherNet/IP, Modbus TCP, OPC UA).

attributes.name: connection_type
attributes.description: Electrical connection or terminal type. Describes the method for connecting wires or cables to the product (e.g., screw terminals, spring clamp, plug connector).

attributes.name: current_rating_amperes
attributes.description: Maximum continuous current rating in amperes. Defines the electrical current capacity of the product.

attributes.name: datasheet_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or document number for the associated product datasheet. Links to the detailed technical datasheet managed in the engineering document management system.

attributes.name: dimensions_height_mm
attributes.description: Product height dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical height for installation planning and space requirements.

attributes.name: dimensions_length_mm
attributes.description: Product length dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical length for installation planning and space requirements.

attributes.name: dimensions_width_mm
attributes.description: Product width dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical width for installation planning and space requirements.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when this specification version becomes effective and applicable for use in engineering, manufacturing, and sales processes.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when this specification version is no longer valid or has been superseded by a newer revision. Null for active specifications without planned obsolescence.

attributes.name: frequency_rating_hz
attributes.description: Operating frequency rating in Hertz. Specifies the electrical frequency the product is designed for (e.g., 50Hz, 60Hz, 50/60Hz).

attributes.name: humidity_rating_percent
attributes.description: Relative humidity rating or range for product operation. Specifies the acceptable humidity conditions (e.g., 5-95% RH non-condensing).

attributes.name: installation_manual_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or document number for the installation manual. Links to the installation instructions managed in the engineering document management system.

attributes.name: ip_rating
attributes.description: Ingress Protection rating per IEC 60529 standard. Defines the level of protection against solid objects and liquids (e.g., IP65, IP67, IP20).

attributes.name: material_composition
attributes.description: Primary materials used in product construction. Lists the key materials and their properties (e.g., stainless steel housing, polycarbonate cover, copper conductors).

attributes.name: mounting_type
attributes.description: Physical mounting method for the product. Specifies how the product is installed (e.g., DIN rail, panel mount, wall mount).

attributes.name: nema_rating
attributes.description: NEMA enclosure rating for North American markets. Defines environmental protection level (e.g., NEMA1, NEMA4, NEMA4X, NEMA12).

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the specification. Captures supplementary information for engineering, quality, or sales teams.

attributes.name: operating_temperature_max_c
attributes.description: Maximum operating ambient temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the upper temperature limit for safe and reliable product operation.

attributes.name: operating_temperature_min_c
attributes.description: Minimum operating ambient temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the lower temperature limit for safe and reliable product operation.

attributes.name: performance_parameter
attributes.description: Key performance parameters and metrics. Describes critical performance characteristics such as response time, accuracy, throughput, or efficiency ratings specific to the product type.

attributes.name: power_rating_watts
attributes.description: Electrical power rating in watts. Specifies the maximum power consumption or output capacity of the product.

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this specification record was first created in the system. Tracks the initial capture of the specification data.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this specification record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to any field in the specification record.

attributes.name: safety_data_sheet_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier for the Safety Data Sheet. Links to the SDS document for products containing hazardous materials, managed in the engineering document management system.

attributes.name: shock_resistance
attributes.description: Mechanical shock resistance specification. Describes the products ability to withstand mechanical shock per applicable test standards (e.g., IEC 60068-2-27).

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Operational system of record where this specification was originally created or is mastered. Identifies whether the specification originates from Siemens Teamcenter PLM, SAP S/4HANA, or manual entry.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier of this specification record in the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating system for data lineage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: specification_description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of the product specification. Provides comprehensive technical and commercial details not captured in structured fields.

attributes.name: specification_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or title of the product specification. Describes the specification purpose or scope.

attributes.name: specification_number
attributes.description: Unique business identifier for the product specification document. Used for external reference and traceability across engineering, quality, and sales teams.

attributes.name: specification_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the product specification. Tracks the approval and active state of the specification document.

attributes.name: specification_type
attributes.description: Classification of the specification document type. Indicates whether the specification covers technical parameters, commercial terms, or a combination.

attributes.name: storage_temperature_max_c
attributes.description: Maximum storage temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the upper temperature limit for product storage when not in operation.

attributes.name: storage_temperature_min_c
attributes.description: Minimum storage temperature in degrees Celsius. Defines the lower temperature limit for product storage when not in operation.

attributes.name: vibration_resistance
attributes.description: Vibration resistance specification. Describes the products ability to withstand mechanical vibration per applicable test standards (e.g., IEC 60068-2-6).

attributes.name: voltage_rating
attributes.description: Electrical voltage rating or range for the product. Specifies the nominal operating voltage and acceptable voltage range (e.g., 24VDC, 110-240VAC, 400V 3-phase).

attributes.name: weight_kg
attributes.description: Product weight in kilograms. Defines the mass for shipping, handling, and installation planning.

products.name: product_certification
products.description: Tracks regulatory and safety certifications obtained for manufactured products including UL listings, CE marking, IEC compliance, ATEX ratings, RoHS/REACH declarations, and export control classifications (ECCN). Captures certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, applicable markets/regions, product scope, and renewal status. Critical for market access compliance and customer-facing documentation. Supports ISO 9001 quality records and regulatory reporting to UL, CE, and OSHA.
products.reference: UL Listing, CE Marking, IEC 61131, RoHS/REACH

attributes.name: product_certification_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the product certification record. Primary key.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: product_certification already tracks cost_amount and cost_currency_code, confirming real certification spend. Linking to cost_center enables certification budget vs. actual reporting — a standard comp

attributes.name: quality_audit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: Product certifications (CE, UL, ISO) are validated through quality audits — the certifying body conducts an audit before issuing the certificate. Linking product_certification to the quality_audit tha

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manufactured product, automation system, electrification solution, or smart infrastructure component that holds this certification.

attributes.name: applicable_markets
attributes.description: Geographic markets, regions, or countries where this certification enables product sale and distribution (e.g., European Union, North America, APAC). Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region identifiers.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes, CE Marking Geographic Scope

attributes.name: applicable_standards
attributes.description: Technical standards, regulations, or directives against which the product was certified (e.g., IEC 61131-2, EN 60204-1, UL 508). Pipe-separated list of standard identifiers.
attributes.references: IEC Standards Catalog, ANSI Standards, ISO Standards

attributes.name: audit_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent audit or inspection conducted by the certifying body to verify ongoing compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Internal Audit, UL Follow-Up Service

attributes.name: certificate_document_url
attributes.description: URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification certificate document stored in the document management system.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, Siemens Teamcenter PLM Document Management

attributes.name: certification_level
attributes.description: Classification or rating level assigned by the certification (e.g., ATEX Zone 1, IP67, Safety Integrity Level SIL 2, Performance Level PLd).
attributes.references: IEC 61508 SIL Levels, ISO 13849 Performance Levels, IEC 60529 IP Ratings

attributes.name: certification_number
attributes.description: Unique certificate number or listing identifier issued by the certifying body (e.g., UL file number, CE declaration number, IEC certificate ID).
attributes.references: UL Certification Database, CE Marking Directive 2006/42/EC

attributes.name: certification_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the certification indicating whether it is valid, expired, pending renewal, or revoked.

attributes.name: certification_type
attributes.description: Type of regulatory or safety certification obtained. Indicates the certification standard or framework under which the product was evaluated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UL Listing|CE Marking|IEC Compliance|ATEX Rating|RoHS Declaration|REACH Declaration|ECCN Classification|FCC Certification|CSA Approval|TUV Certification — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO 9001, IEC 61131, UL Standards, CE Marking Directive

attributes.name: certifying_body
attributes.description: Name of the organization or authority that issued the certification (e.g., Underwriters Laboratories, TUV Rheinland, Notified Body, National Standards Authority).
attributes.references: ISO/IEC 17065 Conformity Assessment, UL, CE Notified Bodies

attributes.name: cost_amount
attributes.description: Total cost incurred to obtain and maintain this certification, including testing fees, audit fees, and certification body charges.
attributes.references: IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting, SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: cost_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, Informatica MDM Data Governance

attributes.name: cybersecurity_certification
attributes.description: Cybersecurity certification or compliance level achieved by the product (e.g., IEC 62443-4-1, IEC 62443-4-2, NIST Cybersecurity Framework).
attributes.references: IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity, NIST Cybersecurity Framework

attributes.name: declaration_of_conformity_number
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the manufacturer's Declaration of Conformity document associated with this certification, particularly for CE marking.
attributes.references: CE Marking Directive 2006/42/EC, ISO 17050 Supplier's Declaration of Conformity

attributes.name: eccn_code
attributes.description: Export Control Classification Number assigned under the U.S. Commerce Control List for dual-use items subject to export regulations.
attributes.references: U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), Commerce Control List

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date from which the certification becomes valid and the product is authorized for market access under the certification scope.
attributes.references: CE Marking Directive, UL Listing Requirements

attributes.name: energy_efficiency_rating
attributes.description: Energy efficiency classification or rating assigned to the product (e.g., Energy Star, EU Energy Label class A+++, IEC 60034-30-1 efficiency class IE3).
attributes.references: ISO 50001 Energy Management, IEC 60034-30-1 Motor Efficiency Classes

attributes.name: environmental_certification
attributes.description: Environmental management or sustainability certification obtained (e.g., ISO 14001, LEED, BREEAM, Cradle to Cradle).
attributes.references: ISO 14001 Environmental Management, EPA Environmental Standards

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which the certification expires and requires renewal. Null for certifications with indefinite validity subject to ongoing compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Records, UL Follow-Up Service

attributes.name: functional_safety_certification
attributes.description: Functional safety certification level achieved by the product (e.g., IEC 61508 SIL 2, ISO 13849 PLd, IEC 61511).
attributes.references: IEC 61508 Functional Safety, ISO 13849 Safety of Machinery

attributes.name: is_customer_facing
attributes.description: Indicates whether this certification is typically disclosed to customers in product documentation, datasheets, or sales materials as a market differentiator.

attributes.name: issue_date
attributes.description: Date on which the certification was officially issued or granted by the certifying body.

attributes.name: mandatory_for_markets
attributes.description: List of markets or regions where this certification is legally mandatory for product sale (as opposed to voluntary). Pipe-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.
attributes.references: CE Marking Directive, OSHA Regulations, EPA Regulations

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this certification record was last modified or updated in the system.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Document Control, Informatica MDM Data Governance

attributes.name: next_audit_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next surveillance audit or re-certification assessment by the certifying body.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Audit Schedule, UL Follow-Up Service Schedule

attributes.name: product_scope
attributes.description: Description of the product models, configurations, or variants covered under this certification. Defines the boundary of what is certified.
attributes.references: UL Certification Scope, CE Declaration of Conformity

attributes.name: quality_certification
attributes.description: Quality management system certification under which the product was manufactured (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, AS9100).

attributes.name: reach_compliant
attributes.business_glossary_term: Registration, Evaluation, Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant
attributes.description: Indicates whether the product complies with REACH regulation for chemical substance registration and restriction in the European Union.

attributes.name: remarks
attributes.description: Additional notes, conditions, limitations, or special requirements associated with the certification. May include restrictions on use, environmental conditions, or configuration dependencies.

attributes.name: renewal_date
attributes.description: Target or scheduled date for certification renewal or re-assessment to maintain continuous compliance.

attributes.name: rohs_compliant
attributes.description: Indicates whether the product complies with RoHS Directive restricting the use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.

attributes.name: test_report_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the test report or assessment documentation that supports the certification issuance.

attributes.name: weee_compliant
attributes.business_glossary_term: Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) Compliant
attributes.description: Indicates whether the product complies with WEEE Directive for proper disposal and recycling of electronic waste.

products.name: plant_data
products.description: Plant-specific master data extensions for each SKU, capturing manufacturing plant assignment, MRP type, lot size procedure, safety stock level, reorder point, planned delivery time, production storage location, and quality inspection type. Represents the SAP S/4HANA MM/PP plant view of the material master, enabling plant-level production planning, MRP runs, and inventory management. Each SKU may have different plant data records per manufacturing facility.
products.reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master Plant View (MRP1/MRP2/MRP3)

attributes.name: plant_data_id
attributes.description: Primary key for plant_data

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Plant‑level cost reporting requires linking plant data to the plant's cost center.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: plant_data carries valuation_class which in SAP-style manufacturing ERPs maps directly to an inventory GL account governing all goods movement postings. This product-plant-to-GL link is fundamental to

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: product.plant_data IS the plant-specific view of a SKU — it exists per plant and contains plant-specific MRP, procurement, and storage parameters. The plain plant_code attribute is a denormalized refe

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Associate plant‑specific data with the SKU it describes; enables plant‑level planning per SKU

attributes.name: abc_indicator
attributes.description: ABC classification for inventory management prioritization. A=High-value items requiring tight control, B=Medium-value items, C=Low-value items with relaxed control.
attributes.references: ABC Analysis - Inventory Management Best Practice

attributes.name: availability_check_group
attributes.description: Two-character key that controls which stock types are included in ATP (Available-to-Promise) checks for sales orders and production orders.

attributes.name: backflush_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether components are backflushed (automatically issued) when production order confirmations are posted. True=Backflush enabled, False=Manual goods issue required.

attributes.name: batch_management_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether batch/lot tracking is required for this material at this plant. True=Batch management active, False=No batch tracking.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this plant data record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: cycle_counting_indicator
attributes.description: Single-character code that determines the frequency of cycle counting for this material. Used to schedule physical inventory counts.

attributes.name: discontinuation_date
attributes.description: Date on which this material will be discontinued at this plant. Used for phase-out planning and last-time-buy notifications.

attributes.name: effective_out_date
attributes.description: Date from which this plant data record becomes inactive. Used to manage time-dependent plant data changes.

attributes.name: fixed_lot_size
attributes.description: Fixed order quantity used when lot size procedure is set to fixed lot size. MRP will always propose this quantity.

attributes.name: gr_processing_time_days
attributes.description: Number of days required for goods receipt processing, quality inspection, and stock placement after physical delivery.

attributes.name: in_house_production_time_days
attributes.description: Number of working days required to manufacture this material from raw materials to finished goods at this plant. Used in production scheduling and MRP.

attributes.name: inspection_setup_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a quality inspection setup (inspection lot creation) is required for goods receipts of this material at this plant. True=Inspection required, False=No inspection.

attributes.name: issue_storage_location
attributes.description: Four-character code identifying the default storage location from which materials are issued to production orders or sales orders.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Location Master

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this plant data record was last updated.

attributes.name: lot_size_procedure
attributes.description: Lot-sizing procedure used in MRP to determine order quantities. EX=Lot-for-lot, HB=Replenish to maximum stock level, FX=Fixed lot size, WB=Weekly lot size, PK=Period lot size, LS=Lot size key.

attributes.name: material_number
attributes.description: Unique material identifier (SKU) from the material master. Links this plant data record to the global material master.

attributes.name: maximum_lot_size
attributes.description: Maximum quantity that can be ordered or produced in a single lot for this material at this plant. Used in MRP calculations.

attributes.name: maximum_stock_level
attributes.description: Target maximum inventory level for this material at this plant. Used in replenishment planning to determine order quantities.

attributes.name: minimum_lot_size
attributes.description: Minimum quantity that must be ordered or produced in a single lot for this material at this plant. Used in MRP calculations.

attributes.name: minimum_remaining_shelf_life_days
attributes.description: Minimum number of days of remaining shelf life required for goods receipt acceptance. Batches with less remaining shelf life are rejected.

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID of the person who last modified this plant data record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: Three-character code identifying the person or group responsible for MRP and procurement activities for this material at this plant.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA PP MRP Master Data

attributes.name: mrp_type
attributes.description: MRP procedure that controls how material requirements are planned. PD=MRP, VB=Manual reorder point, VM=Forecast-based planning, VV=Time-phased planning, ND=No planning, R1=Reorder point planning, R2=Replenishment planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PD|VB|VM|VV|ND|R1|R2 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: negative_stock_allowed
attributes.description: Indicates whether negative stock balances are permitted for this material at this plant. True=Negative stock allowed, False=System blocks transactions that would create negative stock.

attributes.name: planned_delivery_time_days
attributes.description: Number of calendar days from order placement to goods receipt. Used by MRP to schedule procurement proposals and calculate reorder points.

attributes.name: plant_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this material at this plant. Active=Normal production/sales, Blocked=Temporarily unavailable, Discontinued=End-of-life, Phase_out=Being phased out, New_product_introduction=NPI stage.

attributes.name: procurement_type
attributes.description: Indicates how the material is procured at this plant. E=In-house production, F=External procurement (purchasing), X=Both production and external procurement.

attributes.name: production_storage_location
attributes.description: Four-character code identifying the default storage location within the plant where finished goods from production are received.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Location Master

attributes.name: profit_center
attributes.description: Ten-character code identifying the profit center responsible for this material at this plant. Used for internal profitability analysis.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_type
attributes.description: QM inspection type that determines when quality inspections are triggered. 01=Goods receipt from vendor, 02=Goods receipt from production, 03=Goods issue to customer, 04=Stock transfer, 05=Physical inventory, 08=Recurring inspection, 09=Audit inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|08|09 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: reorder_point
attributes.description: Stock level at which MRP triggers a procurement proposal. Calculated based on lead time demand plus safety stock.

attributes.name: rounding_value
attributes.description: Rounding value for lot sizes. MRP rounds proposed order quantities up to the nearest multiple of this value.

attributes.name: safety_stock_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum stock level maintained as buffer against demand fluctuations and supply disruptions. MRP triggers procurement when stock falls below this level plus reorder point.

attributes.name: scheduling_margin_key
attributes.description: Key that defines float times (opening period, float before production, float after production) used in production scheduling to add buffer time.

attributes.name: serialization_level
attributes.description: Controls serial number management for this material at this plant. 0=No serial numbers, 1=Serial number at goods receipt, 2=Serial number at goods issue, 3=Serial number at goods receipt and issue, 4=Serial number at production.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Serial Number Management

attributes.name: shelf_life_expiration_days
attributes.description: Total shelf life period in days from production date to expiration date. Used for batch management and FEFO (First Expired First Out) logic.

attributes.name: special_procurement_type
attributes.description: Special procurement key for subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or other special procurement scenarios. 10=Withdrawal from warehouse, 20=Production in another plant, 30=External procurement, 40=Subcontracting, 50=Phantom assembly, 51=Phantom assembly with lead time, 52=Collective order, 70=Stock transfer, 80=Consignment, 90=Pipeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 10|20|30|40|50|51|52|70|80|90 — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: valuation_class
attributes.description: Four-digit code that determines the general ledger accounts to which inventory movements are posted. Links material movements to financial accounting.

domains.name: sales
domains.description: Sales and commercial domain managing the revenue pipeline including opportunities, quotes, proposals, contracts, order intake, sales pipeline tracking, territory management, and commercial KPIs for industrial automation and electrification products via Salesforce Sales Cloud.

products.name: opportunity
products.description: Core sales opportunity record representing a potential deal for industrial automation, electrification, or smart infrastructure products. Tracks the full revenue pipeline from initial identification through close, including estimated value, probability, stage progression, expected close date, product lines of interest, competitive landscape, win/loss outcome, and primary loss reason. Sourced from Salesforce Sales Cloud Opportunity object. SSOT for all pipeline, forecast, and win/loss analysis data.

attributes.name: opportunity_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the sales opportunity record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account associated with this opportunity.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, opportunities target a specific customer plant (e.g., upgrading a production line at Plant X). Linking opportunity to account_site enables site-level pipeline reporting, c

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Pipeline reporting by legal entity: industrial manufacturers with multiple subsidiaries track opportunities per company code for revenue forecasting, transfer pricing compliance, and entity-level P&L

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Reference to the resulting contract or order if the opportunity was won and converted.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pipeline forecasting allocates expected costs to cost centers for budgeting purposes.

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Reference to the primary contact person at the customer account for this opportunity.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Opportunity tracking of a specific material enables demand forecasting and aligns inventory planning.

attributes.name: price_book_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: In industrial automation sales, an opportunity is worked against a specific price book (standard, customer-specific, or regional). Linking opportunity to price_book establishes which pricing framework

attributes.name: product_family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Opportunity reporting by product family is essential for portfolio performance analysis and strategic sales planning.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Opportunity stage forecasts profit per profit center, supporting sales‑finance alignment.

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: New product opportunities trigger an engineering project; linking enables tracking of project initiation and status.

attributes.name: sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Opportunity is owned by a sales rep; adding sales_rep_id FK enables proper ownership tracking.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Opportunity pipeline and forecasting require linking each opportunity to the exact SKU being pursued, enabling accurate demand planning and revenue projection.

attributes.name: territory_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Opportunity stores territory as free text; adding opportunity_territory_id creates a proper link to sales.territory, enabling consistent reporting and removing redundant string column.

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: Total estimated revenue value of the opportunity in the base currency. Represents the gross deal size before discounts.

attributes.name: close_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the opportunity is expected to close (win or loss decision).

attributes.name: closed_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the opportunity was marked as closed (won or lost). Null if still open.

attributes.name: competitor_name
attributes.description: Name of the primary competitor identified in this sales opportunity.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the opportunity location.

attributes.name: created_date
attributes.description: Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the opportunity amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: delivery_installation_status
attributes.description: Current status of product delivery and installation for won opportunities. Tracks post-sale fulfillment.

attributes.name: discount_percent
attributes.description: Total discount percentage applied to the opportunity amount. Used for pricing and margin analysis.

attributes.name: expected_revenue
attributes.description: Probability-weighted revenue calculated as amount multiplied by probability percentage. Used for forecast accuracy.

attributes.name: fiscal_quarter
attributes.description: Fiscal quarter in which the opportunity is expected to close (e.g., Q1 FY2024). Used for quota and forecast alignment.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Fiscal year in which the opportunity is expected to close. Used for annual revenue planning.

attributes.name: forecast_category
attributes.description: Sales forecast classification indicating confidence level for revenue planning and quota tracking.

attributes.name: has_open_activity
attributes.description: Boolean indicator whether there are open tasks or activities associated with this opportunity. Used for pipeline health monitoring.

attributes.name: industry_segment
attributes.description: Target industry vertical or market segment for this opportunity (e.g., automotive, food and beverage, pharmaceuticals).

attributes.name: is_closed
attributes.description: Boolean indicator whether the opportunity has reached a final closed state (won or lost).

attributes.name: is_private
attributes.description: Boolean indicator whether the opportunity is marked as private with restricted visibility.

attributes.name: is_won
attributes.description: Boolean indicator whether the opportunity was closed as won (deal successfully secured).

attributes.name: last_activity_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent sales activity (call, meeting, email) logged against this opportunity.

attributes.name: last_modified_date
attributes.description: Timestamp when the opportunity record was last updated.

attributes.name: lead_source
attributes.description: Original source or channel through which the opportunity was generated.

attributes.name: loss_reason
attributes.description: Primary reason why the opportunity was lost. Used for win/loss analysis and competitive intelligence.

attributes.name: next_step
attributes.description: Description of the next planned action or milestone to advance the opportunity.

attributes.name: opportunity_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the opportunity including customer needs, solution scope, and strategic context.

attributes.name: opportunity_name
attributes.description: Business-friendly name or title of the sales opportunity describing the potential deal.

attributes.name: opportunity_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible unique business identifier for the opportunity used in sales communications and reporting.

attributes.name: opportunity_type
attributes.description: Classification of the opportunity based on customer relationship status (new customer acquisition vs existing customer expansion).

attributes.name: order_number
attributes.description: Reference to the order number generated when the opportunity was converted to a sales order in the ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system.

attributes.name: probability_percent
attributes.description: Estimated probability of winning this opportunity expressed as a percentage (0-100). Used for weighted pipeline forecasting.

attributes.name: product_line
attributes.description: Primary product line or business unit category for the opportunity (e.g., automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure).

attributes.name: project_end_date
attributes.description: Planned or actual completion date for project delivery if the opportunity is won.

attributes.name: project_start_date
attributes.description: Planned or actual start date for project delivery if the opportunity is won.

attributes.name: region
attributes.description: Geographic region classification for the opportunity (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC).

attributes.name: sales_cycle_days
attributes.description: Number of days from opportunity creation to close date. Used for sales velocity analysis.

attributes.name: stage
attributes.description: Current stage of the opportunity in the sales pipeline lifecycle indicating progression toward close. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|qualification|needs_analysis|proposal|negotiation|closed_won|closed_lost — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: stage_changed_date
attributes.description: Timestamp when the opportunity stage was last changed. Used for stage duration and velocity analysis.

products.name: quote
products.description: Commercial quotation issued to a prospective or existing industrial customer for automation systems, electrification solutions, or smart infrastructure components. Captures quoted price, discount structure (including approved non-standard discounts), validity period, payment terms, delivery lead time, configuration details, and approval status. Linked to an opportunity and may progress to a formal proposal or order. Sourced from Salesforce CPQ / Sales Cloud Quote object.

attributes.name: quote_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the commercial quotation record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account (prospective or existing) to whom this quote is issued.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Industrial manufacturing quotes are frequently site-specific — quoting automation equipment for a particular plant. Linking quote to account_site enables site-level quote pipeline reporting, installed

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: In ETO/CTO industrial manufacturing, a quote is priced against a specific BOM that defines the configured product. Sales teams reference the engineering BOM for cost rollup and configuration validatio

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Quotes in multi-entity industrial manufacturers must be issued from a specific legal entity for correct VAT/tax calculation, intercompany pricing, and statutory reporting. Company code drives pricing

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Reference to the formal contract record created when the quote is accepted and converted to an order.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quote budgeting ties estimated costs to a cost center for cost‑control before order conversion.

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Reference to the primary customer contact person to whom the quote is addressed.

attributes.name: previous_quote_id
attributes.description: Reference to the prior version of this quote if this is a revision or amendment.

attributes.name: price_book_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: A commercial quotation is generated from a specific price book that defines the applicable list prices, discount policies, and currency for the customer segment and region. This FK is essential for tr

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Quote profitability analysis requires profit center linkage to calculate expected margin.

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: In ETO industrial manufacturing, a quote is prepared against a specific engineering project scope and cost estimate. Quote-to-project linkage enables sales to align pricing with engineering cost estim

attributes.name: sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Reference to the sales representative responsible for creating and managing this quote.

attributes.name: sales_territory_id
attributes.description: Reference to the sales territory to which this quote is assigned for pipeline tracking and commission purposes.

attributes.name: ship_to_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Quotes in industrial manufacturing CPQ processes specify a validated ship-to address for freight cost calculation, incoterms selection, and tax determination. A FK to customer.address enables accurate

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Quotes are generated based on a designated supplier's pricing; linking supports price validation, supplier cost analysis, and compliance with approved supplier lists.

attributes.name: accepted_date
attributes.description: Date on which the customer formally accepted the quote, triggering order creation.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the quote received final internal approval.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Status of internal approval workflow for the quote, particularly for non-standard pricing or terms.

attributes.name: competitor_name
attributes.description: Name of the primary competitor identified in the sales opportunity for this quote.

attributes.name: configuration_summary
attributes.description: High-level summary of the product configuration details included in the quote (e.g., system specifications, key components, customization notes).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the quote is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: delivery_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Estimated number of days from order confirmation to delivery of the quoted products.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value of all discounts applied to the quote (standard and non-standard).

attributes.name: discount_percentage
attributes.description: Overall discount rate applied to the quote, expressed as a percentage of the subtotal.

attributes.name: incoterm
attributes.description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, insurance, and risk transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the quote record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: non_standard_discount_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the quote includes discounts that exceed standard approval thresholds and require special authorization.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Internal notes and comments related to the quote, including negotiation history, customer feedback, and special considerations.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Contractual payment terms offered to the customer (e.g., 'Net 30', '50% upfront / 50% on delivery', 'Letter of Credit').

attributes.name: presented_date
attributes.description: Date on which the quote was formally presented to the customer.

attributes.name: quote_date
attributes.description: Date on which the quote was formally issued to the customer.

attributes.name: quote_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name or title of the quote, typically reflecting the project or product scope (e.g., 'Factory Automation System - Plant 5').

attributes.name: quote_number
attributes.description: Externally visible business identifier for the quote. Used in customer communications and contract references.

attributes.name: quote_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the quote in the sales workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|presented|accepted|rejected|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: quote_type
attributes.description: Classification of the quote based on the nature of the commercial transaction.

attributes.name: rejected_date
attributes.description: Date on which the customer formally rejected or declined the quote.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Explanation or reason provided by the customer for rejecting the quote (e.g., 'Price too high', 'Lead time unacceptable', 'Competitor selected').

attributes.name: service_level_agreement
attributes.description: Service level commitments included in the quote (e.g., response time, uptime guarantees, maintenance windows).

attributes.name: shipping_handling_amount
attributes.description: Estimated cost for shipping and handling of the quoted products to the customer site.

attributes.name: shipping_method
attributes.description: Planned method of shipment for the quoted products (e.g., 'Air Freight', 'Ocean Freight', 'Ground Transport', 'Courier').

attributes.name: special_terms_conditions
attributes.description: Additional contractual terms, conditions, or clauses specific to this quote that deviate from standard terms.

attributes.name: subtotal_amount
attributes.description: Total value of all quoted line items before discounts, taxes, and adjustments.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount calculated on the quote based on applicable tax jurisdictions and rates.

attributes.name: total_amount
attributes.description: Final total value of the quote including all line items, discounts, taxes, and fees.

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: Date from which the quoted prices and terms become effective.

attributes.name: valid_until_date
attributes.description: Expiration date after which the quote is no longer valid and prices/terms may change.

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version number of the quote, incremented when revisions are made to pricing, terms, or configuration.

attributes.name: warranty_terms
attributes.description: Description of warranty coverage included with the quoted products (duration, scope, exclusions).

attributes.name: win_probability_percentage
attributes.description: Estimated probability (0-100%) that this quote will be accepted and converted to an order, based on sales assessment.

products.name: quote_line
products.description: Individual line item within a commercial quotation, representing a specific SKU, product configuration, or service offering with its own quantity, unit price, discount, and extended amount. Supports multi-line complex industrial system quotes including hardware, software, installation, and commissioning services. Sourced from Salesforce CPQ Quote Line object.

attributes.name: quote_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the quote line item. Primary key.

attributes.name: bom_id

attributes.description: Reference to the parent quote line if this line is part of a product bundle. Enables hierarchical quote structures for complex system quotes.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Quote creation requires exact component master data for pricing, lead time, and compliance; linking ensures accurate component reference.

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product configuration record for complex configurable products. Links to CPQ (Configure Price Quote) configuration details.

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Quotes must specify the component revision being sold to guarantee correct version delivery and regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. The spec IS the product definition in ETO quoting. quote_line

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue account determination at line level: industrial manufacturing ERP assigns GL accounts per quote line for product-specific revenue classification (product revenue vs. service revenue vs. spare

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Quote line must reference internal material master for cost, inventory availability, and production planning.

attributes.name: price_book_entry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book_entry. Business justification: A quote line is priced from a specific price book entry (SKU-price association). Adding price_book_entry_id to quote_line establishes the authoritative pricing reference used when the line was quoted.

attributes.name: primary_quote_sku_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the product master record. Identifies the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit), product configuration, or service offering being quoted.

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_info_record. This link e

attributes.name: quote_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent commercial quotation header. Links this line item to its containing quote document.

attributes.name: sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Reference to the sales representative responsible for this line item. Used for commission calculation and territory management.

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, quoting specific serialized units (e.g., a particular drive or transformer in stock) is standard for capital equipment availability confirmation. Sales engineers reference

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the preferred supplier or vendor for this line item. Used for procurement planning and supply chain management.

attributes.name: approval_level
attributes.description: The approval authority level required for this line item based on discount depth or special terms. Escalates based on business rules.

attributes.name: commission_percent
attributes.description: The commission rate applicable to this line item for sales compensation. Business confidential.

attributes.name: committed_delivery_date
attributes.description: The delivery date committed by the manufacturer for this line item. Based on production schedule and capacity planning.

attributes.name: configuration_summary
attributes.description: Human-readable summary of the product configuration options selected. Describes customizations, options, and specifications for this line item.

attributes.description: The total cost to manufacture or procure the items on this line. Used for margin analysis and profitability calculations. Business confidential.

attributes.name: is_optional

attributes.name: last_modified_date
attributes.description: The timestamp when this quote line record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking.

attributes.name: lead_time_days

attributes.name: margin_amount

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Internal notes or comments about this quote line item. Used for collaboration between sales, engineering, and operations teams.

attributes.name: product_family

attributes.description: Any special terms, conditions, or notes specific to this line item. May include warranty extensions, service level agreements, or custom provisions.

attributes.name: subtotal_amount
attributes.description: The extended line amount before taxes and fees. Calculated as quantity multiplied by unit price.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The total tax amount applicable to this line item. May include VAT, sales tax, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction.

attributes.name: total_amount

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the quoted quantity. Defines how the product or service is quantified (e.g., each, kilogram, meter, hour for services). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|kg|meter|liter|hour|day|month|year|set — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: The actual selling price per unit after applying discounts and adjustments. This is the negotiated price for this specific quote.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Contract accounting requires mapping contract revenue/costs to a cost center for internal cost tracking.
