attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Linking contracts to credit profiles enables automated credit risk checks required for contract approval in manufacturing.

attributes.name: delivery_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Sales contracts in industrial manufacturing specify a validated delivery address governing incoterms, tax jurisdiction, and logistics obligations. 'delivery_location' is a denormalized text field; a F

attributes.name: fulfillment_warehouse_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Industrial manufacturing sales contracts (frame agreements, blanket orders) specify the fulfilling warehouse for delivery scheduling and SLA compliance. Contract managers and logistics planners use th

attributes.name: price_book_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: An executed sales contract in industrial manufacturing locks in pricing terms for the contract duration, typically referencing a specific price book (often a customer-specific or contracted price book

attributes.name: product_family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Contracts often cover entire product families; linking enables compliance checks, warranty terms, and pricing rules per family.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center assignment enables profit margin reporting per contract (mandatory for management reporting).

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Long-term sales contracts for industrial automation systems are tied to specific engineering projects that deliver the contracted scope. Contract-to-project linkage supports milestone-based billing, c

attributes.name: sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Reference to the sales representative or account manager responsible for negotiating and managing this sales contract. Links to employee or sales team master data.

attributes.name: sales_territory_id
attributes.description: Reference to the geographic or organizational sales territory to which this contract is assigned. Used for territory management, quota tracking, and commission calculation.

attributes.name: signatory_customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_contact. Business justification: Contract lifecycle management and legal audit trails require identifying the specific customer contact who signed the contract. 'customer_signatory_name' is a denormalized text field; a FK to customer

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Sales contracts often reference a supplier responsible for warranty or service parts; linking enables warranty claim routing and supplier performance monitoring.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the sales contract received final internal approval and was authorized for execution. Marks completion of internal review and sign-off process.

attributes.name: compliance_certifications_required
attributes.description: List of mandatory certifications, standards, or regulatory approvals that products or services must meet under the sales contract. Examples: CE marking, UL certification, ISO 9001, IEC 61131, OSHA compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management, IEC Standards, UL Certification

attributes.name: confidentiality_clause
attributes.description: Contractual provisions protecting proprietary information, trade secrets, technical specifications, and business data shared between parties. Defines confidential information, permitted uses, and disclosure restrictions.

attributes.name: contract_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the sales contract. Used for customer communication, legal reference, and cross-system tracking.

attributes.name: contract_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the sales contract. Draft contracts are being prepared; pending approval contracts await internal or customer sign-off; active contracts are in force; suspended contracts are temporarily on hold; completed contracts have fulfilled all obligations; terminated contracts ended early by mutual agreement; cancelled contracts were voided before activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|completed|terminated|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: contract_type
attributes.description: Classification of the sales contract based on commercial structure. Standard contracts use pre-approved terms; custom contracts are negotiated; framework contracts establish terms for multiple orders; blanket contracts cover recurring purchases; spot contracts are one-time transactions; turnkey contracts include design, supply, and commissioning.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this sales contract record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: delivery_schedule
attributes.description: Detailed timeline and milestones for delivery of products, systems, or services under the sales contract. May include phased deliveries, installation schedules, and commissioning dates.

attributes.name: dispute_resolution_method
attributes.description: Agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising from the sales contract. Litigation involves court proceedings; arbitration uses neutral arbitrators; mediation involves facilitated negotiation; negotiation is direct party-to-party resolution.

attributes.name: document_url
attributes.description: Uniform Resource Locator (URL) or file path to the digitally stored executed sales contract document. Links to document management system or secure repository.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the sales contract becomes legally binding and enforceable. Marks the start of the contract period and triggers obligations for both parties.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the sales contract ends and obligations cease. Nullable for open-ended contracts or contracts with indefinite terms subject to termination clauses.

attributes.name: force_majeure_clause
attributes.description: Contractual provisions excusing non-performance due to unforeseeable events beyond parties control such as natural disasters, war, pandemics, or government actions. Defines qualifying events and relief procedures.

attributes.name: governing_law
attributes.description: Legal jurisdiction and body of law that governs the interpretation, enforcement, and dispute resolution of the sales contract. Typically specifies country and state/province.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for delivery of goods. Examples: EXW (Ex Works), FOB (Free On Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: insurance_requirements
attributes.description: Mandatory insurance coverage that the supplier or customer must maintain during contract performance. May include general liability, professional indemnity, product liability, or cargo insurance with specified coverage limits.

attributes.name: intellectual_property_terms
attributes.description: Provisions defining ownership, licensing, and usage rights for intellectual property including patents, designs, software, documentation, and technical know-how. Specifies IP ownership and license grants.

attributes.name: liability_cap_amount
attributes.description: Maximum financial liability amount that either party can be held responsible for under the sales contract. Limits exposure for damages, losses, or claims arising from contract performance.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this sales contract record was last updated in the system. Part of audit trail for change tracking and compliance.

attributes.name: net_contract_value
attributes.description: Net monetary value of the sales contract after applying discounts, rebates, and before taxes. Represents the actual revenue to be recognized.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the sales contract. Used for internal communication and knowledge transfer.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: Instrument or mechanism through which the customer will remit payment. Wire transfer for electronic bank transfers; letter of credit for trade finance; bank guarantee for secured payments; check for paper-based payments; credit card for card payments; direct debit for automated withdrawals.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Detailed description of payment conditions including milestones, due dates, installment schedules, and payment triggers. Examples: Net 30, 50% upfront 50% on delivery, milestone-based payments.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: penalty_clause
attributes.description: Contractual provisions defining financial penalties or liquidated damages for non-performance, late delivery, or breach of SLA commitments. Specifies penalty amounts, triggers, and caps.

attributes.name: renewal_terms
attributes.description: Provisions governing automatic renewal, renewal options, or renegotiation terms at contract expiration. Defines renewal notice periods, pricing adjustments, and renewal conditions.

attributes.name: signature_date
attributes.description: Date when the sales contract was signed by both parties and became legally executed. May differ from effective date if contract has a future start date.

attributes.name: sla_resolution_time_hours
attributes.business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Resolution Time Hours
attributes.description: Maximum number of hours within which the supplier must resolve customer issues or restore service under the contract. Part of SLA commitments for uptime and availability.

attributes.name: sla_response_time_hours
attributes.business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Time Hours
attributes.description: Maximum number of hours within which the supplier must respond to customer service requests or incidents under the contract. Part of SLA commitments for support and maintenance.

attributes.name: sla_uptime_percentage
attributes.description: Guaranteed minimum uptime or availability percentage for systems, equipment, or services covered by the sales contract. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.5% uptime).

attributes.name: supplier_signatory_name
attributes.description: Full name of the authorized representative who signed the sales contract on behalf of the supplier. Used for legal verification and audit trail.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount applicable to the sales contract. Includes VAT, GST, sales tax, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction and product classification.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: termination_clause
attributes.description: Contractual provisions defining conditions, notice periods, and procedures under which either party may terminate the sales contract. Includes termination for cause, convenience, or force majeure.

attributes.name: value_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value of the sales contract representing the gross revenue commitment. Includes all line items, products, services, and deliverables before adjustments.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: value_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value. Defines the currency in which all financial terms, payments, and penalties are denominated.

attributes.name: warranty_period_months
attributes.description: Duration in months for which the supplier provides warranty coverage for products, systems, or equipment delivered under the sales contract. Covers defects in materials and workmanship.

attributes.name: warranty_terms
attributes.description: Detailed description of warranty provisions including coverage scope, exclusions, repair or replacement terms, and customer obligations. Defines what is covered and what is not under warranty.

products.name: territory
products.description: Sales territory definition representing a geographic, industry-vertical, or account-based assignment used to organize the sales force for industrial automation and electrification markets. Captures territory code, region hierarchy (global/regional/local), assigned revenue quota, product line scope, and effective dates. Enables territory-based pipeline reporting and quota attainment tracking.

attributes.name: territory_id
attributes.description: Primary key for territory

attributes.name: parent_territory_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent territory in the hierarchical territory structure. Null for top-level territories.

attributes.name: price_book_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: Sales territories in industrial manufacturing are often associated with a default price book that governs pricing for all deals originating in that territory (e.g., EMEA territory uses EUR price book,

attributes.name: account_count
attributes.description: Total number of customer accounts assigned to this territory. Used for territory balance and coverage analysis.

attributes.name: annual_revenue_quota
attributes.description: Target annual revenue quota assigned to this territory in the base currency. Used for quota attainment tracking and sales performance measurement.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval workflow status for territory definition changes: draft (being edited), pending_approval (submitted for review), approved (finalized), or rejected (not approved).

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the sales leader or executive who approved this territory definition. Used for governance and audit trail.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the territory definition was formally approved. Used for compliance and change tracking.

attributes.name: assignment_rule

attributes.name: commission_plan_code

attributes.name: country_codes

attributes.name: coverage_model
attributes.description: Sales coverage approach for this territory: direct (direct sales force), channel (partner/distributor), hybrid (combination), inside_sales (remote/phone), or field_sales (on-site visits).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this territory record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this territory definition expires or is superseded. Null for currently active territories with no planned end date.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this territory definition becomes active and operational. Used for territory planning and historical tracking.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Fiscal year for which this territory definition and quota are applicable (e.g., 'FY2024', 'FY2025'). Enables year-over-year territory comparison.

attributes.name: geographic_scope
attributes.description: Textual description of the geographic coverage (e.g., 'Germany, Austria, Switzerland', 'US Northeast Corridor', 'Asia-Pacific excluding China').

attributes.name: industry_vertical
attributes.description: Target industry sector or vertical market for this territory (e.g., 'Automotive Manufacturing', 'Food & Beverage', 'Pharmaceuticals', 'Energy & Utilities'). Applicable when territory_type includes industry_vertical.

attributes.name: is_overlay_territory
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is an overlay territory (True) that operates across other territories for specialized products or services, or a standard exclusive territory (False).

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the primary business language in this territory (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'zh', 'es'). Used for localization and communication planning.

attributes.name: market_segment
attributes.description: Target customer segment for this territory: enterprise (large corporations), mid_market (medium businesses), smb (small business), oem (Original Equipment Manufacturer), government (public sector), or education (academic institutions).

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this territory record was last modified. Used for change tracking and synchronization.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes and comments about the territory, including special instructions, historical context, or strategic considerations. Used for knowledge transfer and planning.

attributes.name: overlay_type
attributes.description: Type of overlay territory if is_overlay_territory is True: product_specialist (specific product expertise), solution_architect (technical pre-sales), key_account (strategic account support), or none (not an overlay).

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Strategic priority classification of the territory: strategic (top-tier), high (important growth area), medium (standard), low (maintenance), or emerging (new market development).

attributes.name: product_line_scope
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of product lines or families assigned to this territory (e.g., 'PLC,SCADA,HMI' or 'Electrification,Automation,Digitalization'). Defines which products the territory sales team is responsible for.

attributes.name: quota_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the revenue quota (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'CNY').

attributes.name: quota_period
attributes.description: Time period for quota measurement: annual (yearly), semi_annual (twice per year), quarterly (every quarter), or monthly.

attributes.name: region_level

attributes.name: sales_channel

attributes.description: Number of sales representatives assigned to this territory. Used for capacity planning and workload distribution.

attributes.name: source_system

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Unique identifier of this territory in the source system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data integration.

attributes.name: territory_code

attributes.name: territory_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the sales territory (e.g., 'North America Industrial Automation', 'EMEA Electrification Solutions').

attributes.name: territory_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the territory: active (operational), inactive (not in use), pending (awaiting activation), suspended (temporarily disabled), archived (historical), or planned (future territory).

attributes.name: territory_type
attributes.description: Classification of territory assignment model: geographic (region-based), industry_vertical (sector-based), account_based (strategic accounts), product_line (product family focus), hybrid (combination), or named_account (specific customer assignment).

attributes.name: time_zone
attributes.description: IANA time zone identifier for the territory (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/Berlin', 'Asia/Shanghai'). Used for scheduling and activity coordination.

attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Material Master Costing View

attributes.name: created_date
attributes.description: The timestamp when this price book entry record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which all prices in this entry are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: customer_segment
attributes.description: The customer segment or tier for which this pricing applies (e.g., Enterprise, Mid-Market, Small Business, Government). Supports segment-based pricing strategies.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date after which this price book entry is no longer valid. Null indicates no expiration. Used for promotional pricing and seasonal campaigns.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this price book entry becomes valid and can be used in sales transactions. Supports time-bound pricing strategies.

attributes.name: geography_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the geographic market for which this pricing applies (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Indicates whether this price book entry is currently active and available for use in quotes and orders. Inactive entries are retained for historical reference.

attributes.name: last_modified_date
attributes.description: The timestamp when this price book entry record was last modified. Part of the standard audit trail for change tracking.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: The standard lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this product. Used for delivery date estimation in quotes and orders.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Material Master MRP View

attributes.name: list_price
attributes.description: The standard published list price for this product in the specified currency. Represents the baseline price before any discounts or negotiations.

attributes.name: market_segment
attributes.description: The target market segment or industry vertical for this product pricing (e.g., Automotive, Food & Beverage, Pharmaceuticals, Energy).

attributes.name: maximum_discount_percent
attributes.description: The maximum allowable discount percentage that can be applied to this product without requiring special approval. Used to enforce pricing governance.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: The minimum quantity that must be ordered for this product at this price. Enforces Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) policies in Configure Price Quote (CPQ) systems.

attributes.name: minimum_price
attributes.description: The floor price below which this product cannot be sold. Used to enforce pricing policies and protect margin thresholds in Configure Price Quote (CPQ) processes.

attributes.name: price_book_entry_description
attributes.description: Additional notes or description providing context about this pricing entry, such as special terms, conditions, or the rationale for custom pricing.

attributes.name: price_book_entry_name
attributes.description: A descriptive name or label for this price book entry, typically combining product name, price book name, and currency for easy identification in reports and user interfaces.

attributes.name: price_type
attributes.description: The type or nature of the price (e.g., list price, negotiated price, promotional price, contract price, spot price). Indicates the pricing strategy applied.

attributes.name: pricing_method
attributes.description: The methodology used to determine the price (e.g., fixed, cost-plus, market-based, value-based). Supports pricing strategy analysis and governance.

attributes.name: product_line
attributes.description: The business product line or division responsible for this product (e.g., Industrial Automation, Building Technologies, Mobility Solutions).

attributes.name: sales_channel
attributes.description: The sales channel through which this pricing is applicable (e.g., direct sales, distributor, partner, Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM), online).

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: The tax classification code that determines applicable sales tax, Value Added Tax (VAT), or Goods and Services Tax (GST) rates for this product in the specified geography.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for pricing (e.g., Each, Box, Pallet, Kilogram, Meter). Defines the quantity basis for the unit price.

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: The effective selling price per unit for this product in the price book. May differ from list price due to volume discounts, promotions, or regional adjustments.

attributes.name: use_standard_price
attributes.description: Indicates whether this entry uses the standard price book price or a custom price. True means standard pricing applies; false indicates custom or negotiated pricing.

products.name: order_intake
products.description: Sales order intake record capturing the commercial handoff from a won opportunity or accepted quote to the order management domain. Records the intake date, confirmed order value, customer PO reference, requested delivery date, payment terms confirmed, and handoff status. This is NOT the SSOT for order fulfillment data — the authoritative order record lives in the order domain. The sales domain retains this record solely for order intake KPI tracking, sales-to-fulfillment cycle time measurement, and booking recognition against quota.
products.reference: SAP SD Order Intake / Salesforce Closed-Won to Order

attributes.name: order_intake_id
attributes.description: Primary key for order_intake

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account placing the order. Links to the master customer account record.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, orders are fulfilled to a specific customer plant/production site. Linking order_intake to account_site enables site-level delivery scheduling, installation planning, and

attributes.name: ar_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_item. Business justification: Order-to-cash process: booked orders generate AR items for receivables tracking. Industrial manufacturing DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) reporting and revenue recognition (IFRS 15) require linking order

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Sales order booking in industrial manufacturing ERP requires company code assignment for revenue GL posting, tax determination, and intercompany order processing. Every order intake record belongs to

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: In configure-to-order industrial manufacturing, the order intake must reference the specific product configuration to trigger the correct BOM and routing for production. Without this FK, manufacturing

attributes.name: contact_customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_contact. Business justification: Order confirmation, delivery coordination, and dispute resolution in industrial manufacturing require knowing which buyer contact placed the order. Order management teams use this for customer communi

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue allocation to cost center for cost accounting on each order intake (required for statutory cost reporting).

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Order intake records the exact material to be delivered, linking to inventory for allocation and picking.

attributes.name: opportunity_id
attributes.description: Reference to the won sales opportunity that generated this order intake. Links to the originating opportunity record in Salesforce Sales Cloud.

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability reporting per profit center for each order intake (used in profit analysis reports).

attributes.name: quote_id
attributes.description: Reference to the accepted quote that was converted into this order intake. May be null if order originated directly from opportunity without formal quote.

attributes.name: sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Reference to the sales representative or account manager who owns this order intake. Used for quota attainment and commission calculation.

attributes.name: ship_to_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Ship-to address drives logistics routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and incoterms application for industrial equipment delivery. 'delivery_location' is a denormalized text field; a validated FK

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Order intake processing must map incoming orders to the master product record for inventory allocation and production scheduling.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Order fulfillment planning requires assigning a primary supplier to each order intake for procurement scheduling and delivery coordination.

attributes.name: territory_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Order intake records a sales territory as text; adding order_intake_territory_id creates a proper FK to sales.territory and removes the ambiguous string column.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: Logistics tracking needs the specific supplier site fulfilling the order, enabling shipping, compliance, and performance reporting.

attributes.name: booking_recognition_date
attributes.description: The date when this order intake was recognized as a booking for quota and sales performance measurement. May differ from intake date based on validation rules.

attributes.name: booking_recognized_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this order intake has been recognized as a booking against sales quota. True when order meets booking recognition criteria per sales policy.

attributes.name: committed_delivery_date
attributes.description: The delivery date committed by sales to the customer. May differ from requested date based on production capacity and supply chain constraints.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when this order intake record was first created. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: credit_approval_date
attributes.description: Date when credit approval was granted for this order, if required. Null if order did not require credit approval or is still pending.

attributes.name: credit_check_status
attributes.description: Result of the customer credit check performed at order intake. Orders failing credit check may require advance payment or credit approval before handoff to fulfillment.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order value. Essential for multi-currency sales operations and financial consolidation.

attributes.name: customer_po_date
attributes.description: The date on the customer's purchase order. Used for contract compliance verification and order validity checks.

attributes.name: customer_po_number
attributes.description: The customer's purchase order reference number. Critical for order validation, invoicing, and customer reconciliation. May be required per customer contract terms.

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: The foreign exchange rate applied at intake date to convert order value to base currency. Locked at intake for consistent booking valuation.

attributes.name: fiscal_quarter
attributes.description: The fiscal quarter in which this order intake was booked. Format Q1-YYYY through Q4-YYYY. Used for quarterly sales performance reporting and quota tracking.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which this order intake was booked. Used for annual sales performance reporting and year-over-year growth analysis.

attributes.name: handoff_date
attributes.description: The date when the order intake was successfully handed off to the order management domain. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time KPI calculation.

attributes.name: handoff_status
attributes.description: Current status of the handoff from sales to order management domain. Tracks the intake record through validation, transfer, and acceptance by the fulfillment organization.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: The Incoterms rule defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Critical for international trade compliance and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: industry_segment
attributes.description: The customer's industry segment or vertical market. Used for market analysis, sales territory planning, and industry-specific KPI tracking.

attributes.name: intake_date
attributes.description: The date when the order was officially received and recorded in the sales system. This is the booking date used for sales quota recognition and revenue pipeline tracking.

attributes.name: intake_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the order intake record. Human-readable unique number used for tracking and reference in sales reporting and KPI dashboards.

attributes.name: intake_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when the order intake record was created in the system. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time measurement and operational analytics.

attributes.name: last_modified_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this order intake record.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when this order intake record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes and comments related to this order intake. May include customer requests, internal coordination notes, or special handling instructions.

attributes.name: order_management_reference
attributes.description: The order number or reference assigned by the order management system after handoff. Links this sales intake record to the authoritative order record in SAP SD or equivalent ERP.

attributes.name: order_priority
attributes.description: The priority level assigned to this order intake. Influences production scheduling and resource allocation in the fulfillment process.

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the order by business type. Distinguishes between new customer acquisition, existing customer expansion, and service orders for sales mix analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: New Business|Renewal|Upgrade|Add-On|Replacement|Service|Spare Parts — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: order_value
attributes.description: The total confirmed order value at intake. This is the gross order amount before any adjustments, used for sales booking recognition and quota attainment calculation.

attributes.name: order_value_base_currency
attributes.description: Order value converted to the company's base reporting currency using the exchange rate at intake date. Used for consolidated sales reporting and KPI tracking.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: The agreed payment terms for this order. Defines when payment is due relative to delivery or invoice date. Critical for cash flow forecasting and credit risk management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Net 30|Net 45|Net 60|Net 90|Advance Payment|Letter of Credit|Installment|Custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: payment_terms_days
attributes.description: Number of days until payment is due per the agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable forecasting and working capital planning.

attributes.name: product_line
attributes.description: The primary product line or business unit for this order. Used for sales performance segmentation and product portfolio analysis.

attributes.name: requested_delivery_date
attributes.description: The customer's requested delivery date as captured at order intake. Used for production planning coordination and customer expectation management.

attributes.name: shipping_method
attributes.description: The agreed shipping or transportation method for order delivery. Impacts delivery lead time and logistics cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Air Freight|Sea Freight|Road Transport|Rail|Courier|Customer Pickup|Multimodal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: special_terms_conditions
attributes.description: Any special commercial terms, conditions, or customer-specific agreements applicable to this order. May include warranty extensions, service level commitments, or pricing exceptions.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the sales user who created this order intake record in the system.

domains.name: order
domains.description: Order management and fulfillment domain governing the end-to-end order lifecycle from customer purchase orders through production scheduling, shipment, and delivery confirmation. Manages order headers, line items, delivery schedules, RMAs, fulfillment SLAs, and customer order lifecycle via SAP SD.

products.name: header
products.description: Core master record for customer purchase orders representing the full order commitment in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Captures customer reference, order type (standard, rush, blanket, consignment), requested delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, pricing date, total net value, currency, sales organization, distribution channel, division, and overall order status. Serves as the SSOT for all customer order commitments in the industrial manufacturing order lifecycle, driving downstream delivery, billing, and revenue recognition.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the sales order header.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the customer who placed the order.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Industrial equipment orders need an installation site for field service planning; site details are in customer.account_site.

attributes.name: blanket_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: An order_header may be created as a release against a blanket order (scheduling agreement). Adding blanket_order_id to order_header establishes the parent blanket agreement context for the order, enab

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi-entity statutory reporting: in industrial manufacturing groups, each sales order belongs to a legal entity (company code) for intercompany billing, VAT reporting, and segment P&L. Standard in SA

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Contract compliance: orders are executed under a sales contract; linking ensures legal and pricing terms are enforced per order.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Overall order budgeting: assigning a sales order to a cost center supports order‑level expense allocation and budgeting reports.

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Order processing uses a sold‑to contact for invoicing and communication; this contact is stored in customer.contact.

attributes.name: opportunity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Revenue attribution: linking orders to the originating sales opportunity enables pipeline‑to‑revenue reporting and commission calculations.

attributes.name: order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Order Management traceability: each order must reference the intake record that created it for audit and fulfillment reporting.

attributes.name: price_book_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, every order is priced against a specific price book (regional, customer-segment, or contract-specific). Pricing audit, discount compliance, and revenue recognition reports

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue attribution: linking the order to a profit center enables consolidated profit‑and‑loss reporting per sales order.

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: Engineer-to-order (ETO) manufacturing requires linking customer orders to engineering projects for delivery milestone tracking, cost-to-complete reporting, and project accounting. Industrial manufactu

attributes.name: quote_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Order-to-quote traceability is a core industrial manufacturing process: the accepted quote drives order pricing, configuration, and terms. Finance and operations teams run quote-conversion and order-a

attributes.name: sales_rep_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Commission & accountability: each order is credited to the responsible sales rep, supporting commission payout and performance metrics.

attributes.name: sales_territory_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Territory reporting: orders must be assigned to a sales territory for quota tracking, regional performance dashboards, and incentive calculations.

attributes.name: shipping_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Shipping logistics require a reference to the customer's shipping address; address data resides in customer.address.

attributes.name: sla_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Orders in industrial manufacturing are governed by specific SLA agreements defining on-time delivery targets and penalty clauses. Order fulfillment teams must reference the governing SLA for priority

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Single‑source orders may designate a preferred supplier at the header level; used in order approval and compliance reporting.

attributes.name: billing_block
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether billing of the order is blocked.

attributes.name: credit_status
attributes.description: Credit check result for the customer at order time.

attributes.name: currency_rate
attributes.description: Exchange rate from order currency to company code currency at pricing time.

attributes.name: customer_account_group
attributes.description: SAP account group categorizing the customer.

attributes.name: customer_purchase_order_date
attributes.description: Date on the customer's purchase order.

attributes.name: delivery_block
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether delivery of the order is blocked.

attributes.name: distribution_channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the order is distributed (e.g., wholesale, retail).

attributes.name: division
attributes.description: Business division handling the order.

attributes.name: freight_terms
attributes.description: Terms governing freight cost responsibility (e.g., prepaid, collect).

attributes.name: gross_weight_kg
attributes.description: Total gross weight of the order including packaging, measured in kilograms.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: internal_comments
attributes.description: Internal notes visible only to company personnel.

attributes.name: net_weight_kg
attributes.description: Total net weight of all items in the order, measured in kilograms.

attributes.name: order_currency
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order amounts.

attributes.name: order_number
attributes.description: External business identifier for the order as used in customer communications.

attributes.name: order_placed_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the customer placed the order.

attributes.name: order_priority
attributes.description: Priority level assigned to the order for processing.

attributes.name: order_reason
attributes.description: Free‑text description of why the order was created (e.g., new project, replacement).

attributes.name: order_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|partially_delivered|completed|cancelled|closed|on_hold — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: order_text
attributes.description: Long free‑text field for additional order instructions or remarks.

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the order based on fulfillment rules.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Contractual terms defining when payment is due (e.g., NET30).

attributes.name: price_group
attributes.description: Group determining price level for the customer.

attributes.name: pricing_date
attributes.description: Date on which the pricing conditions were determined.

attributes.name: purchase_order_number
attributes.description: Reference number supplied by the customer for their internal tracking.

attributes.name: record_audit_created
attributes.description: Timestamp when the order record was first captured in the system.

attributes.name: record_audit_updated
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order record.

attributes.name: requested_delivery_date
attributes.description: Date requested by the customer for order delivery.

attributes.name: route
attributes.description: Planned transportation route for delivering the order.

attributes.name: sales_document_type
attributes.description: SAP SD document type code (e.g., OR for standard order).

attributes.name: sales_group
attributes.description: Group of sales representatives responsible for the order.

attributes.name: sales_office
attributes.description: Geographic sales office handling the order.

attributes.name: sales_organization
attributes.description: Code of the sales organization responsible for the order.

attributes.name: shipping_condition
attributes.description: Condition governing the shipping method for the order.

attributes.name: shipping_point
attributes.description: Logistics location from which the order will be shipped.

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: Tax code applied to the order for tax calculation.

attributes.name: total_gross_amount
attributes.description: Sum of line item amounts before taxes, discounts, and surcharges.

attributes.name: total_net_amount
attributes.description: Final amount payable after taxes, discounts, and surcharges.

attributes.name: total_tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax calculated for the order.

attributes.name: transportation_group
attributes.description: Classification of transportation mode and carrier.

attributes.name: volume_m3
attributes.description: Total volume of the order in cubic meters.

products.name: order_line
products.description: Individual line item within a customer sales order representing a discrete product, automation system, or service being ordered. Captures material number, ordered and confirmed quantities, unit of measure, schedule line dates, net price, pricing conditions, plant assignment, storage location, delivery status, rejection reason, and blanket order release reference. Serves as the demand signal for MRP and production scheduling. May reference a parent blanket order for scheduling agreement releases.

attributes.name: order_line_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate key for each order line record.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Order lines in industrial manufacturing can be destined for different customer sites within a multi-plant account (split shipments). Site-level line assignment supports site-specific delivery scheduli

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Enables Production Planning to pull the exact BOM version for the ordered product, essential for material requirement planning (MRP) reports.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Required for Order‑to‑Manufacturing handoff report linking each sold line to the engineered component for traceability and production scheduling.

attributes.name: configuration_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: Configure-to-order is a core process in industrial manufacturing (automation systems, drives, switchgear). Each order line references the specific product configuration selected, which drives BOM expl

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting: each sales order line must be charged to a cost center for internal cost reporting and profitability analysis.

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Order lines in industrial manufacturing must capture the engineering revision valid at order placement to ensure the correct revision is manufactured and shipped. Drives production execution accuracy,

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the parent sales order (transaction header) to which this line belongs.

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Regulated products require batch traceability from order line to lot batch for quality, recall, and compliance management.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Order fulfillment requires linking each order line to the material master for inventory reservation, costing, and MRP planning; this is standard in manufacturing ERP systems.

attributes.name: price_book_entry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_book_entry. Business justification: Order line pricing in industrial manufacturing must be traceable to the specific price book entry used (list price, discount floor, UOM). Margin analysis, pricing compliance audits, and revenue recogn

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability tracking: linking order line revenue to a profit center enables margin reporting per product line.

attributes.name: quote_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Audit trail: order line must reference the originating quote line to validate pricing, configuration, and warranty obligations.

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: Serialized items need direct linkage between order line and specific serialized unit to support warranty, service, and traceability.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Required for Order Fulfillment & Costing report linking each order line to the master product record for pricing, compliance, and warranty tracking.

attributes.name: spare_part_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.spare_part. Business justification: Needed for Spare Part Sales process to tie sold line items to the spare‑part master for warranty and inventory tracking.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Picking process assigns a specific storage location to each order line to locate inventory in the warehouse; the link enables pick list generation and inventory accuracy.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Procurement assigns a supplier to each order line for purchasing; needed for supplier performance, cost analysis, and PO generation.

attributes.name: actual_delivery_date
attributes.description: Date the goods were actually received by the customer.

attributes.name: backorder_indicator
attributes.description: True if the line is on backorder, otherwise false.

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: Batch identifier for traceability of the material.

attributes.name: blanket_release_number
attributes.description: Identifier of the blanket order release that generated this line.

attributes.name: confirmed_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity confirmed by the system after availability check.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the order line record was created in the source system.

attributes.name: currency
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values on this line.

attributes.name: delivery_date
attributes.description: Actual date the line was delivered to the customer.

attributes.name: delivery_status
attributes.description: Current status of the line's delivery.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Monetary discount applied to this line.

attributes.name: distribution_channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the product is sold (e.g., online, direct).

attributes.name: division
attributes.description: Business division responsible for the product.

attributes.name: gross_price
attributes.description: Total price after taxes and before discounts.

attributes.name: gross_weight
attributes.description: Total weight of the line item including packaging (kilograms).

attributes.name: inspection_status
attributes.description: Current status of the quality inspection process.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Planned lead time in days from order to delivery.

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential number of the line within the order, used for ordering and reference.

attributes.name: net_price
attributes.description: Net price per unit before taxes and discounts.

attributes.name: net_weight
attributes.description: Weight of the product itself without packaging (kilograms).

attributes.name: plant
attributes.description: SAP plant code where the product is stocked or produced for this order line.

attributes.name: pricing_condition
attributes.description: Pricing condition type governing the price calculation.

attributes.name: product_description
attributes.description: Human‑readable description of the product or service on the line.

attributes.name: promised_date
attributes.description: Date promised to the customer for delivery.

attributes.name: quality_score
attributes.description: Numeric score representing quality assessment (0‑100).

attributes.name: quality_status
attributes.description: Result of quality inspection for the line item.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Reason provided when the line is rejected or cancelled.

attributes.name: requested_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity originally requested by the customer.

attributes.name: rma_reference
attributes.description: Reference to an RMA record if the line is a return.

attributes.name: sales_org
attributes.description: Organizational unit responsible for the sale.

attributes.name: sales_price
attributes.description: Price per unit used for revenue recognition.

attributes.name: sales_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity used for sales reporting, may differ from requested/confirmed units.

attributes.name: schedule_line_date
attributes.description: Planned date for delivery or production of this line item.

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: Serial number for serialized items.

attributes.name: storage_location
attributes.description: Warehouse or bin location from which the material will be shipped.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Tax amount applicable to this line item.

attributes.name: delivery_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: A goods issue posting is always triggered by an outbound delivery document in SAP SD. goods_issue.delivery_doc_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the delivery document number; normalizing th

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Link goods issue to the order header for end‑to‑end tracking, removing redundant sales order number.

attributes.name: journal_entry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Inventory valuation and COGS recognition: every goods issue in manufacturing triggers a journal entry (debit COGS, credit inventory). Auditors and controllers require direct traceability from goods mo

attributes.name: lot_batch_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Goods issues in batch-managed manufacturing must reference the specific lot_batch for FIFO-controlled inventory depletion, batch traceability reporting, and quality recall processes. batch_number is a

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Goods issue posting in manufacturing ERP requires the material master for inventory deduction, valuation class determination, and GL account assignment. Domain experts universally expect this FK. mate

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: COGS-by-segment reporting: goods_issue currently stores 'profit_center' as a plain text attribute (denormalized). A proper FK to finance.profit_center enables manufacturing controllers to report COGS

attributes.name: serialized_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: For serialized industrial assets, goods issue must reference the specific serialized unit to update stock status from 'available' to 'shipped' and enable asset lifecycle tracking. serial_number is a d

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Goods issue postings in industrial manufacturing must be traceable to the specific SKU for COGS posting, inventory valuation, and regulatory traceability. material_number is a denormalized text repres

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Goods issue transaction records the originating stock location, essential for inventory decrement, audit trails, and compliance reporting.

attributes.name: actual_delivery_date
attributes.description: Date when the goods were actually delivered to the customer.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this record was first created in the lakehouse.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values.

attributes.name: delivery_date
attributes.description: Planned date for delivery to the customer.

attributes.name: expected_delivery_date
attributes.description: System‑calculated expected delivery date based on lead times.

attributes.name: external_reference
attributes.description: Reference to external system such as carrier tracking number.

attributes.name: goods_issue_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the goods issue.

attributes.name: handling_unit
attributes.description: Identifier of the handling unit (e.g., pallet) used for the issue.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: International commercial terms governing delivery responsibilities.

attributes.name: inventory_account
attributes.description: General ledger account for inventory posting.

attributes.name: is_automated
attributes.description: Indicates whether the goods issue was generated automatically by a system.

attributes.name: issue_number
attributes.description: External document number assigned to the goods issue.

attributes.name: movement_type
attributes.description: SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the goods issue.

attributes.name: net_amount
attributes.description: Net monetary amount after tax.

attributes.name: plant
attributes.description: Plant where the goods issue originated.

attributes.name: posting_reason
attributes.description: Reason for the goods issue posting.

attributes.name: posting_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the goods issue was posted in the source system.

attributes.name: purchase_order_number
attributes.description: Purchase order associated with the material movement, if applicable.

attributes.name: quality_status
attributes.description: Quality inspection result for the issued material.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: Quantity of material issued.

attributes.name: remarks
attributes.description: Additional free‑text notes about the goods issue.

attributes.name: reversal_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this record is a reversal of a previous goods issue.

attributes.name: route
attributes.description: Planned transportation route for the shipment.

attributes.name: shipping_point
attributes.description: Logistics shipping point from which the goods are dispatched.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Originating source system that created the goods issue record.

attributes.name: storage_location
attributes.description: Storage location from which the goods were issued.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Tax amount applicable to the goods issue, if any.

attributes.name: total_value_cost
attributes.description: Total monetary value of the issued goods at standard cost.

attributes.name: uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the issued quantity.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.

attributes.name: valuation_area
attributes.description: Organizational area for inventory valuation.

attributes.name: valuation_type
attributes.description: Method used for inventory valuation of the issued material.

products.name: blanket_order
products.description: Long-term supply agreement or scheduling agreement from an industrial customer committing to purchase a defined quantity of products over a specified period at agreed pricing. Captures blanket order number, customer account, validity start and end dates, total committed quantity, total committed value, release frequency, minimum order quantity (MOQ), pricing conditions, and cumulative released quantity to date. Contains individual release call-offs as line-level records with release number, release date, quantity, requested delivery date, cumulative released quantity, and release status. Supports demand planning, JIT delivery scheduling, and production scheduling for high-volume OEM customers.
products.reference: SAP SD Scheduling Agreement / Blanket Order

attributes.name: blanket_order_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the blanket order.

attributes.name: account_id
attributes.description: Internal identifier of the customer that owns the blanket agreement.

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi-entity blanket order management: blanket orders in industrial manufacturing groups are issued by a specific legal entity for intercompany procurement, statutory spend reporting, and tax complian

attributes.name: contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Blanket orders in industrial manufacturing are always backed by a master sales contract (frame agreement) that defines pricing, delivery schedules, and liability terms. Contract compliance tracking, r

attributes.name: customer_contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_contact. Business justification: Blanket orders in industrial manufacturing have a designated customer procurement or supply chain contact who authorizes releases and manages the agreement. This contact is essential for release appro

attributes.name: ship_to_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Blanket orders in industrial manufacturing specify a default ship-to address for all scheduled releases. Linking to the customer address master enables release scheduling, logistics planning, and dock

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: A blanket purchase agreement is negotiated with a specific supplier; needed for contract management and spend analysis.

attributes.name: agreement_description
attributes.description: Free‑text description providing context or special notes about the blanket order.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the blanket order received formal approval.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: User identifier of the person who approved the blanket order.

attributes.name: blanket_order_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the blanket order.

attributes.name: contract_status_reason
attributes.description: Free‑text explanation for the current status of the agreement.

attributes.name: contract_type
attributes.description: Category of the agreement defining its purpose and governing rules.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the blanket order record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: cumulative_released_quantity
attributes.description: Total quantity that has been released to the customer to date.

attributes.name: cumulative_released_value
attributes.description: Monetary value of all released quantities to date.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the agreement.

attributes.name: distribution_channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the products will be delivered (e.g., wholesale, direct).

attributes.name: effective_from
attributes.description: Date on which the blanket agreement becomes binding.

attributes.name: effective_until
attributes.description: Date on which the blanket agreement expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).

attributes.name: is_jit_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement supports JIT delivery scheduling.

attributes.name: last_release_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent call‑off release.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Smallest quantity the customer may release in a single call‑off.

attributes.name: minimum_order_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity.

attributes.name: next_release_due_date
attributes.description: Planned date for the next scheduled release based on the release frequency.

attributes.name: plant
attributes.description: Manufacturing plant responsible for fulfilling releases under the agreement.

attributes.name: pricing_conditions
attributes.description: Textual description of pricing terms, discounts, and rebates applicable to the agreement.

attributes.name: quantity_uom
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the committed quantity (e.g., each, kilogram).

attributes.name: region
attributes.description: Geographic region (e.g., NA, EU) applicable to the agreement.

attributes.name: release_frequency
attributes.description: Scheduled interval at which the customer may release call‑offs against the blanket order.

attributes.name: sales_organization
attributes.description: Organizational unit responsible for the sales side of the agreement.

attributes.name: total_committed_quantity
attributes.description: Aggregate quantity of product(s) the customer has committed to purchase over the agreement period.

attributes.name: total_committed_value
attributes.description: Monetary value of the total committed quantity at the agreed pricing.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the blanket order record.

products.name: blanket_order_release
products.description: Individual release call-off against a blanket order or scheduling agreement, specifying the quantity and delivery date for a specific shipment within the long-term supply commitment. Captures release number, blanket order reference, release date, release quantity, requested delivery date, cumulative released quantity, and release status. Enables JIT delivery scheduling and production demand triggering for OEM and high-volume industrial customers.
products.reference: SAP SD Scheduling Agreement Delivery Schedule / Release

attributes.name: blanket_order_release_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the blanket order release record.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Blanket order releases in industrial manufacturing are triggered for specific customer factory sites (JIT replenishment). Linking to account_site enables site-level release scheduling, capacity planni

attributes.name: blanket_order_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the parent blanket order (scheduling agreement) to which this release belongs.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: A blanket_order_release (call-off against a scheduling agreement) generates a sales order (order_header) in SAP SD. The release is the trigger event; the order_header is the resulting fulfillment docu

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Each blanket order release triggers a purchase order to the supplier. Procurement and supply chain teams track release-to-PO fulfillment for JIT scheduling, cumulative quantity reconciliation, and bla

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the product/material being released for delivery.

attributes.name: blanket_order_release_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the release.

attributes.name: release_date
attributes.description: Date on which the blanket order release was created or issued.

attributes.name: release_number
attributes.description: Sequential number of the release within the blanket order, used for ordering and tracking.

domains.name: supplier
domains.description: Supplier relationship and performance management domain tracking supplier profiles, qualification status, scorecards, audit results, certifications, risk ratings, and long-term relationship data. Distinct from procurement transactions — this domain owns the supplier identity and master data used across procurement, quality, and supply chain.

products.name: supplier
products.description: Core master entity representing the authoritative supplier identity record (SSOT) for all vendors, sub-contractors, and raw material suppliers engaged by Manufacturing. Captures legal entity name, DUNS number, tax ID, SAP vendor master number (from SAP Ariba/S4HANA MM), business type (OEM, distributor, raw material, MRO), incorporation country, headquarters address, primary contact, preferred currency, payment terms, incoterms, and active status. This is the golden record used across procurement, quality, supply chain, and logistics domains.
products.reference: SAP Vendor Master (LFA1/LFB1), SAP Ariba Supplier Profile

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Primary key for supplier

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating supplier spend to internal cost centers (Procurement Cost Allocation Report).

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Default GL account for posting supplier invoices (AP posting rules).

attributes.name: business_type
attributes.description: Classification of the supplier based on the type of goods or services provided. OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) supplies finished components, distributor resells products, raw_material provides base materials, MRO (Maintenance Repair Operations) supplies consumables, subcontractor provides manufacturing services, service_provider delivers non-manufacturing services.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this supplier master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and master data governance.

attributes.name: duns_number
attributes.description: Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to identify business entities globally. Used for supplier credit checks, risk assessment, and compliance verification.

attributes.name: headquarters_address_line1
attributes.description: Primary street address line for the supplier's corporate headquarters. Used for legal correspondence, audit notices, and official communications.

attributes.name: headquarters_address_line2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for suite, building, or floor information at the supplier's headquarters location.

attributes.name: headquarters_city
attributes.description: City or municipality where the supplier's headquarters is located.

attributes.name: headquarters_country
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code for the supplier's headquarters location. May differ from incorporation country for multinational organizations.

attributes.name: headquarters_postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the supplier's headquarters location.

attributes.name: headquarters_state_province
attributes.description: State, province, or administrative region for the supplier's headquarters address.

attributes.name: incorporation_country
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the supplier is legally incorporated and registered. Used for trade compliance, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: Standard three-letter Incoterms code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and seller in international trade (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). Published by International Chamber of Commerce. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: iso14001_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier holds valid ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. True if certified, False if not certified or certification expired.

attributes.name: iso45001_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier holds valid ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. True if certified, False if not certified or certification expired.

attributes.name: iso9001_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier holds valid ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. True if certified, False if not certified or certification expired.

attributes.name: last_audit_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent supplier audit conducted by Manufacturing's quality or procurement team. Used to track audit frequency and schedule next audit per supplier management policy.

attributes.name: last_modified_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this supplier master record. Used for change accountability, audit trails, and data governance.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this supplier master record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.

attributes.name: last_scorecard_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent supplier scorecard evaluation. Scorecards typically assess quality, delivery, cost, and responsiveness on a quarterly or annual basis.

attributes.name: minority_owned
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier is certified as a minority-owned business enterprise. Used for diversity supplier programs and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: next_audit_due_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next supplier audit based on risk rating, performance, and audit frequency policy. Used for audit planning and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: on_time_delivery_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the requested delivery date over the evaluation period. Key performance indicator (KPI) for supplier scorecard and performance management. Expressed as percentage (0.00 to 100.00).

attributes.name: overall_scorecard_rating
attributes.description: Composite supplier performance score from the most recent scorecard evaluation, typically on a scale of 0.0 to 10.0 or 0.0 to 100.0. Aggregates quality, delivery, cost, and service metrics into a single performance indicator.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Standard payment terms negotiated with the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Defines the payment due date and any early payment discounts available.

attributes.name: preferred_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the supplier's preferred currency for invoicing and payment transactions (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
