attributes.name: primary_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the primary supplier contact for business communications, purchase orders, and quality notifications.

attributes.name: primary_contact_name
attributes.description: Full name of the primary business contact at the supplier organization for procurement, quality, and supply chain coordination.

attributes.name: primary_contact_phone
attributes.description: Business phone number for the primary supplier contact including country code and extension if applicable.

attributes.name: qualification_status
attributes.description: Quality and compliance qualification status indicating whether the supplier has passed required audits, certifications, and capability assessments. Qualified suppliers meet all requirements, conditional suppliers have minor gaps, not_qualified suppliers failed assessment, under_review suppliers are being evaluated.

attributes.name: quality_acceptance_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of received materials that passed incoming quality inspection without rejection or rework over the evaluation period. Key performance indicator (KPI) for supplier quality performance. Expressed as percentage (0.00 to 100.00).

attributes.name: relationship_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the business relationship with this supplier was terminated or the vendor record was deactivated. Null for active suppliers. Used for supplier lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.

attributes.name: relationship_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the business relationship with this supplier was established and the vendor record was first activated. Used for relationship tenure analysis and supplier lifecycle reporting.

attributes.name: risk_rating
attributes.description: Overall risk assessment for the supplier based on financial stability, geopolitical factors, quality performance, delivery reliability, and compliance history. Used for supply chain risk management and contingency planning.

attributes.name: sap_vendor_number
attributes.description: Vendor master number from SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management) or SAP Ariba. This is the authoritative supplier identifier in the ERP system used for procurement transactions.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Master Data Standards

attributes.name: small_business
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier qualifies as a small business based on revenue, employee count, or regulatory definitions. Used for small business procurement programs and compliance reporting.

attributes.name: supplier_category
attributes.description: Strategic classification indicating the supplier's relationship tier and procurement priority. Strategic suppliers are critical long-term partners, preferred suppliers have favorable terms, approved suppliers meet minimum standards, conditional suppliers are under review, blocked suppliers are prohibited from new business.

attributes.name: supplier_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the supplier relationship. Active suppliers are approved for procurement, inactive suppliers are no longer used, suspended suppliers are temporarily restricted, pending_approval suppliers are under qualification review, blocked suppliers are prohibited from new transactions.

attributes.name: tax_number
attributes.description: Government-issued tax identification number for the supplier (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, GST number in India). Used for tax reporting, invoice validation, and compliance.

attributes.name: vendor_code
attributes.description: Internal alphanumeric code assigned to the supplier for identification across procurement, quality, and supply chain systems. Often synchronized with SAP vendor master number.

attributes.name: vendor_name
attributes.description: Full legal name of the supplier organization as registered with incorporation authorities. Used for contracts, purchase orders, and legal documentation.

attributes.name: woman_owned
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier is certified as a woman-owned business enterprise. Used for diversity supplier programs and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who created this supplier master record. Used for accountability, audit trails, and data governance.

products.name: site
products.description: Represents a specific physical manufacturing, warehouse, or service site belonging to a vendor. A single vendor legal entity may operate multiple sites with distinct capabilities, certifications, and risk profiles. Captures site address, site type (manufacturing, distribution, R&D), production capacity, geographic region, ISO certification scope, and site-level contact. Used by procurement and quality to route sourcing decisions to the correct facility.
products.reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Site, ISO 9001 Site Scope

attributes.name: site_id
attributes.description: Primary key for site

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Site‑specific cost allocation for production capacity spend (Site Cost Allocation process).

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent vendor legal entity that owns this site. Links to the vendor master record.

attributes.name: active_from_date
attributes.description: Date when the vendor site became active and eligible for procurement transactions.

attributes.name: active_to_date
attributes.description: Date when the vendor site was deactivated or disqualified. Null for currently active sites.

attributes.name: address_line_1
attributes.description: Primary street address of the vendor site facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: address_line_2
attributes.description: Secondary address information such as building, suite, or floor number. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: approved_for_new_business
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor site is approved to receive new purchase orders and sourcing awards.

attributes.name: capacity_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the production capacity metric such as units, tons, liters, or square meters.

attributes.name: certification_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the primary quality management certification expires. Used to trigger recertification workflows.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City or municipality where the vendor site is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code for the vendor site location. Used for trade compliance, tariff calculation, and regional risk assessment.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the vendor site record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO currency code for transactions with this vendor site.

attributes.name: delivery_performance_score
attributes.description: On-time delivery performance score for the vendor site. Scale 0-100.

attributes.name: duns_number
attributes.description: Dun and Bradstreet Universal Numbering System identifier for the vendor site. Used for credit assessment and supplier verification.

attributes.name: geographic_region
attributes.description: Macro-geographic region classification for supply chain planning and regional sourcing strategy.

attributes.name: iatf_16949_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor site holds IATF 16949 automotive quality management certification. Required for automotive supply chain participation.

attributes.name: iso_14001_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor site holds ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification.

attributes.name: iso_45001_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor site holds ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification.

attributes.name: iso_9001_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor site holds ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Critical for supplier qualification.

attributes.name: last_audit_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent quality or compliance audit conducted at the vendor site.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the vendor site record was last updated.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the vendor site. Used for logistics optimization and distance calculations.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Standard procurement lead time in days from purchase order placement to delivery for this vendor site.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the vendor site. Used for logistics optimization and distance calculations.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum order quantity required by the vendor site for purchase orders.

attributes.name: moq_unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity such as pieces, kilograms, or pallets.

attributes.name: next_audit_due_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next quality or compliance audit at the vendor site.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or site-specific considerations relevant to procurement and quality teams.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Standard payment terms applicable to this vendor site such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30.

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the vendor site address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: preferred_supplier_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this vendor site has preferred supplier status for strategic sourcing decisions.

attributes.name: primary_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the primary site contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: primary_contact_name
attributes.description: Full name of the primary business contact at the vendor site for procurement and quality coordination.

attributes.name: primary_contact_phone
attributes.description: Primary telephone number for the vendor site contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: production_capacity_annual
attributes.description: Annual production capacity of the site in units relevant to the site type. Used for capacity planning and sourcing allocation decisions.

attributes.name: quality_score
attributes.description: Composite quality performance score for the vendor site based on defect rates, audit results, and corrective action responsiveness. Scale 0-100.

attributes.name: risk_rating
attributes.description: Overall risk classification of the vendor site based on quality performance, financial stability, geopolitical factors, and compliance history.

attributes.name: site_code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code identifying the vendor site. Used for procurement routing and supplier qualification tracking.

attributes.name: site_name
attributes.description: Business name or designation of the vendor site facility.

attributes.name: site_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the vendor site. Controls whether the site is eligible for new purchase orders and sourcing decisions.

attributes.name: site_type
attributes.description: Classification of the vendor site by primary function. Determines sourcing eligibility and capability matching.

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: State, province, or administrative region of the vendor site. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: tax_registration_number
attributes.description: Tax identification or VAT registration number for the vendor site. Used for tax compliance and invoice validation.

products.name: supplier_contact
products.description: Tracks individual contacts associated with a vendor organization, including account managers, quality engineers, logistics coordinators, and executive sponsors. Captures full name, job title, department, email, phone, preferred language, contact role (commercial, technical, quality, logistics), and active status. Supports multi-threaded supplier relationship management across procurement, quality, and after-sales functions.
products.reference: SAP Ariba Contact Management, Salesforce Account Contact

attributes.name: supplier_contact_id
attributes.description: Primary key for contact

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent vendor organization this contact belongs to. Links to the supplier master record.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Supplier contacts are physically located at specific supplier sites. The supplier_contact table has office_location (STRING) but no normalized site FK. Adding vendor_site_id (nullable) enables site-le

attributes.name: certification_held
attributes.description: Professional certifications or qualifications held by the contact relevant to their role (e.g., Six Sigma Black Belt, PMP, ISO Lead Auditor, APICS CPIM). Multiple certifications separated by semicolon.

attributes.name: communication_preference
attributes.description: Preferred method of communication for reaching this contact for routine supplier relationship activities.

attributes.name: contact_role
attributes.description: Primary functional role of the contact in the supplier relationship. Determines routing of communications and escalations.

attributes.name: contact_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the vendor contact. Inactive or terminated contacts should not receive communications.

attributes.name: contact_type
attributes.description: Classification of the contact based on their priority and purpose in the supplier relationship hierarchy.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or username of the person who created this vendor contact record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this vendor contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: department
attributes.description: Department or functional area within the vendor organization where the contact works (e.g., Sales, Quality Assurance, Logistics, Engineering, Customer Service).

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this contact ceased to be active or left the vendor organization. Null for currently active contacts.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this contact became active and available for supplier relationship management activities.

attributes.name: email_address
attributes.description: Primary email address for the vendor contact. Used for procurement communications, quality notifications, and supply chain coordination.

attributes.name: escalation_level
attributes.description: Numeric escalation tier for this contact in the supplier relationship hierarchy. Level 1 is first point of contact, higher numbers represent management escalation paths.

attributes.name: first_name
attributes.description: First name or given name of the vendor contact person.

attributes.name: full_name
attributes.description: Complete name of the vendor contact person, typically concatenation of first and last name.

attributes.name: interaction_frequency_days
attributes.description: Target frequency in days for regular business interactions with this contact. Used for relationship management planning and alerts.

attributes.name: is_decision_maker
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this contact has decision-making authority for commercial negotiations, contract approvals, or strategic supplier relationship matters.

attributes.name: is_primary_contact
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the vendor organization. Only one primary contact should exist per vendor.

attributes.name: job_title
attributes.description: Official job title or position of the vendor contact within their organization (e.g., Account Manager, Quality Engineer, Logistics Coordinator, Sales Director).

attributes.name: last_interaction_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent business interaction or communication with this contact. Used for relationship health monitoring.

attributes.name: last_modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or username of the person who most recently modified this vendor contact record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this vendor contact record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: last_name
attributes.description: Last name or family name of the vendor contact person.

attributes.name: linkedin_profile_url
attributes.description: URL to the contacts LinkedIn professional profile. Used for relationship intelligence and network mapping.

attributes.name: mobile_number
attributes.description: Mobile phone number for the vendor contact. Used for urgent supply chain escalations and on-call support.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes and observations about the contact, including communication preferences, relationship history, special considerations, or other relevant context for supplier relationship management.

attributes.name: phone_number
attributes.description: Primary telephone number for the vendor contact. Includes country code and extension where applicable.

attributes.name: preferred_language
attributes.description: ISO 639-2 three-letter language code representing the contacts preferred language for business communications (e.g., ENG, DEU, FRA, SPA, CHI, JPN).

attributes.name: relationship_strength_score
attributes.description: Quantitative score (0-100) representing the strength and quality of the business relationship with this contact. Based on interaction frequency, responsiveness, collaboration quality, and business outcomes.

attributes.name: specialization_area
attributes.description: Specific area of expertise or specialization for the contact (e.g., electrification products, automation systems, quality management, supply chain logistics, contract negotiation).

attributes.name: time_zone
attributes.description: IANA time zone identifier for the contacts primary work location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Used for scheduling meetings and coordinating real-time communications.

attributes.name: years_of_experience
attributes.description: Total years of professional experience the contact has in their field or industry. Used for relationship quality assessment.

products.name: qualification
products.description: Records the formal supplier qualification lifecycle for each vendor, tracking whether a vendor has been approved or disqualified for specific commodity categories or part families. Captures qualification status (prospective, under evaluation, approved, conditional, disqualified), qualification type (new supplier, re-qualification, commodity extension, disqualification), evaluation start/end dates, approving engineer, qualification basis (PPAP, APQP, audit, sample approval), expiry date, and for disqualification events: disqualification reason, affected commodities, transition plan reference, and re-qualification eligibility. Managed in SAP Ariba and aligned with PPAP/APQP standards.
products.reference: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP, SAP Ariba Supplier Qualification

attributes.name: qualification_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the supplier qualification record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Customer site-specific supplier qualification (PPAP/APQP): in industrial manufacturing, a supplier is qualified to supply a specific customer plant. Qualification scope is customer-site-bound (e.g., a

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Supplier qualification in industrial manufacturing is scoped per commodity category — qualification gates procurement for specific commodity groups. commodity_category is a plain-text denormalized col

attributes.name: family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Supplier qualification in industrial manufacturing is scoped to product families (e.g., PPAP qualification for servo drives family, IATF qualification for electrification components). The existing par

attributes.name: supplier_certification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_certification. Business justification: A qualification record requires specific certifications (currently stored as a string in certification_required). Normalizing this to a FK to supplier_certification.supplier_certification_id allows th

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier being qualified. Links to the supplier master data entity.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Supplier qualifications are frequently site-specific in industrial manufacturing — a supplier's site A may be qualified for aerospace components while site B is not. The qualification table already li

attributes.name: affected_commodities
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or description of commodity categories affected by a disqualification event. Null if not disqualified or if disqualification is global.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the supplier was formally approved for the specified scope. Null if not yet approved or if disqualified.

attributes.name: audit_date
attributes.description: Date when the on-site or remote supplier audit was conducted as part of the qualification process. Null if no audit was performed.

attributes.name: audit_score
attributes.description: Numerical score from the supplier qualification audit, typically expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Null if no audit was conducted.

attributes.name: basis
attributes.description: Primary method or standard used to qualify the supplier: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), on-site audit, sample approval, document review, third-party certification (ISO, UL, etc.), or legacy performance history. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ppap|apqp|on_site_audit|sample_approval|document_review|third_party_certification|legacy_performance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) AIAG Manual; APQP AIAG Manual; ISO 9001:2015

attributes.name: conditional_approval_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date by which the supplier must satisfy conditional approval requirements to achieve full approval status. Null if not conditionally approved.

attributes.name: conditional_approval_reason
attributes.description: Explanation of why the supplier was granted conditional approval rather than full approval, including specific restrictions or pending actions. Null if not conditionally approved.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: disqualification_date
attributes.description: Date when the supplier was formally disqualified or approval was revoked. Null if not disqualified.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 Nonconformity and Corrective Action

attributes.name: disqualification_reason
attributes.description: Detailed explanation of why the supplier was disqualified or rejected, including specific quality, delivery, compliance, or financial issues. Null if not disqualified.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 Nonconformity and Corrective Action

attributes.name: document_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or link to the formal qualification documentation package, including audit reports, PPAP submissions, certificates, and approval memos.

attributes.name: evaluation_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the qualification evaluation was completed and a decision was rendered.

attributes.name: evaluation_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field capturing additional observations, findings, or context from the qualification evaluation process.

attributes.name: evaluation_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the formal supplier qualification evaluation process was initiated.

attributes.name: evaluation_team
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or description of the cross-functional team members who participated in the supplier qualification evaluation (e.g., quality, engineering, procurement, operations).

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the qualification approval expires and re-qualification is required. Null for indefinite approvals or disqualified suppliers.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the qualification record was last updated, reflecting the most recent change to status, scope, or other attributes.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review or re-qualification assessment of the supplier. Null if no review is scheduled or if disqualified.

attributes.name: ppap_level
attributes.description: PPAP submission level required or achieved (Level 1 through Level 5), or not applicable if PPAP was not the qualification basis. Higher levels require more comprehensive documentation.
attributes.references: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) AIAG 4th Edition

attributes.name: qualification_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the qualification record, typically system-generated and used in external communications and audit trails.

attributes.name: qualification_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the supplier qualification: prospective (initial inquiry), under evaluation (assessment in progress), approved (fully qualified), conditional (approved with restrictions or pending actions), disqualified (rejected or revoked), expired (approval period lapsed).

attributes.name: qualification_type
attributes.description: Type of qualification event: new supplier onboarding, periodic re-qualification, extension to new commodity categories, disqualification action, or conditional approval pending corrective action.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.4 Control of Externally Provided Processes, Products and Services

attributes.name: re_qualification_conditions
attributes.description: Specific conditions or corrective actions the supplier must complete before being eligible for re-qualification. Null if not applicable or if permanently disqualified.
attributes.references: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 Nonconformity and Corrective Action

attributes.name: re_qualification_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether a disqualified supplier is eligible to apply for re-qualification in the future. True if eligible, False if permanently disqualified.

attributes.name: risk_rating
attributes.description: Overall risk assessment of the supplier at the time of qualification: low (minimal risk), medium (manageable risk), high (significant risk requiring mitigation), critical (unacceptable risk without immediate action).

attributes.name: scope_description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of the qualification scope, including specific materials, processes, or services the supplier is approved to provide.

attributes.name: transition_plan_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or document number for the supplier transition or phase-out plan in the event of disqualification. Null if not applicable.

products.name: scorecard
products.description: Periodic supplier performance scorecard capturing aggregated KPI results across quality, delivery, cost, and responsiveness dimensions for a defined evaluation period (monthly, quarterly, annual). Stores overall score, individual pillar scores (OTD — On-Time Delivery, PPM — Parts Per Million defect rate, cost variance, responsiveness index), scorecard period, rating tier (preferred, approved, conditional, development), and issuing buyer. Drives strategic sourcing decisions and supplier development programs.
products.reference: SAP Ariba Supplier Performance Management, AIAG Supplier Scorecard

attributes.name: scorecard_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the supplier performance scorecard record.

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Supplier scorecards are issued by purchasing organizations within a company code in multi-entity industrial manufacturers. Scorecard results feed into company-code-level spend analytics and supplier r

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier being evaluated in this scorecard.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Scorecards can be scoped to a specific supplier site, not just the supplier as a whole. A supplier with multiple manufacturing sites may have different delivery and quality performance per site. Addin

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the scorecard was officially approved by the designated approver.

attributes.name: capa_count
attributes.description: Total number of corrective and preventive action requests issued to this supplier during the evaluation period.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Additional comments, notes, or contextual information about the scorecard evaluation and results.

attributes.name: cost_score
attributes.description: Performance score for the cost pillar, measuring price competitiveness, cost variance, and total cost of ownership, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

attributes.name: cost_variance_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage variance between actual costs and target or budgeted costs during the evaluation period, where negative values indicate cost savings.

attributes.name: cost_weight_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage weight assigned to the cost pillar in the overall score calculation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the scorecard record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values in this scorecard.

attributes.name: delivery_score
attributes.description: Performance score for the delivery pillar, measuring on-time delivery performance and schedule adherence, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

attributes.name: delivery_weight_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage weight assigned to the delivery pillar in the overall score calculation.

attributes.name: evaluation_methodology
attributes.description: Description of the scoring methodology, weighting scheme, and calculation approach used for this scorecard evaluation.

attributes.name: evaluation_period_end_date
attributes.description: The end date of the performance evaluation period covered by this scorecard.

attributes.name: evaluation_period_start_date
attributes.description: The start date of the performance evaluation period covered by this scorecard.

attributes.name: improvement_actions_required
attributes.description: Summary of required improvement actions or corrective measures the supplier must implement based on scorecard results.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the scorecard record was last modified or updated.

attributes.name: ncr_count
attributes.description: Total number of non-conformance reports issued against this supplier during the evaluation period.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next performance review or scorecard evaluation for this supplier.

attributes.name: otd_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of deliveries received on or before the scheduled delivery date during the evaluation period, a key delivery performance indicator.

attributes.name: overall_score
attributes.description: Composite overall performance score for the supplier, typically calculated as a weighted average of individual pillar scores, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

attributes.name: period_type
attributes.description: The frequency or type of evaluation period for this scorecard (monthly, quarterly, annual, or ad-hoc).

attributes.name: ppm_defect_rate
attributes.description: Number of defective parts per million parts received during the evaluation period, a key quality performance indicator.

attributes.name: previous_rating_tier
attributes.description: The rating tier assigned to the supplier in the previous evaluation period, used for trend analysis and tier movement tracking.

attributes.name: published_date
attributes.description: The date when the scorecard was officially published and communicated to the supplier.

attributes.name: quality_score
attributes.description: Performance score for the quality pillar, measuring defect rates, non-conformances, and quality compliance, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

attributes.name: quality_weight_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage weight assigned to the quality pillar in the overall score calculation.

attributes.name: rating_tier
attributes.description: Strategic classification tier assigned to the supplier based on scorecard performance, driving sourcing decisions and supplier development programs.

attributes.name: responsiveness_index
attributes.description: Composite index measuring supplier responsiveness across communication, issue resolution, and collaboration dimensions, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

attributes.name: responsiveness_score
attributes.description: Performance score for the responsiveness pillar, measuring communication effectiveness, issue resolution speed, and collaboration quality, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

attributes.name: responsiveness_weight_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage weight assigned to the responsiveness pillar in the overall score calculation.

attributes.name: rma_count
attributes.description: Total number of return material authorizations processed for defective or non-conforming materials from this supplier during the evaluation period.

attributes.name: scorecard_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the scorecard, typically formatted as a human-readable code used in reports and communications.

attributes.name: scorecard_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the scorecard indicating its approval and publication state.

attributes.name: strengths_summary
attributes.description: Summary of the supplier's key strengths and positive performance areas identified during the evaluation period.

attributes.name: total_line_items_evaluated
attributes.description: Total number of purchase order line items included in the evaluation period for this scorecard.

attributes.name: total_orders_evaluated
attributes.description: Total number of purchase orders included in the evaluation period for this scorecard.

attributes.name: total_purchase_value
attributes.description: Total monetary value of purchases from this supplier during the evaluation period, used for spend-weighted performance analysis.
attributes.references: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: total_receipts_evaluated
attributes.description: Total number of goods receipts or deliveries included in the evaluation period for this scorecard.

attributes.name: weaknesses_summary
attributes.description: Summary of the supplier's key weaknesses and performance gaps identified during the evaluation period.

products.name: supplier_audit
products.description: Records formal supplier audits conducted at vendor sites, including quality system audits (ISO 9001, IATF 16949), process audits, environmental audits (ISO 14001), cybersecurity audits (IEC 62443), and social compliance audits. Captures audit type, audit date, lead auditor, audit scope, findings count by severity (critical, major, minor), overall audit result (pass, conditional pass, fail), and next scheduled audit date. Linked to CAPA records for finding remediation tracking.
products.reference: ISO 9001 Supplier Audit, SAP Ariba Audit Management, IATF 16949

attributes.name: supplier_audit_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the supplier audit record. Primary key.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Audit costs (cost, cost_currency_code fields) must be charged to a cost center for budget tracking and departmental spend reporting. Industrial manufacturing procurement teams track audit expenditure

attributes.name: engineering_specification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Specification compliance audit: supplier audits in industrial manufacturing verify compliance with specific engineering specifications (special processes, material standards). Linking audit→specificat

attributes.name: project_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: APQP supplier audit: during NPI/APQP projects, supplier audits are conducted as a formal project gate (e.g., production readiness review). Linking audit→engineering_project enables program managers to

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier being audited. Links to the supplier master data.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: Site‑specific audit information; each audit occurs at a particular supplier site, enabling site‑level risk tracking and reporting.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name of the quality manager or procurement director who approved the final audit report and findings.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the audit report was formally approved by the quality or procurement management.

attributes.name: audit_date
attributes.description: The date when the supplier audit was conducted at the vendor site. This is the principal business event timestamp for the audit.

attributes.name: audit_method
attributes.description: Method used to conduct the audit: on-site visit, remote audit via video conference, hybrid approach, or document review only.

attributes.name: audit_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique audit reference number assigned by the quality or procurement team for tracking and documentation purposes.

attributes.name: audit_result
attributes.description: Overall outcome of the supplier audit: pass (no critical or major findings), conditional pass (findings require corrective action within timeframe), fail (critical findings or inability to meet standards), or pending (awaiting final determination).

attributes.name: audit_scope
attributes.description: Detailed description of the audit scope including specific processes, departments, product lines, or standards being evaluated during the audit.

attributes.name: audit_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the audit: scheduled (planned but not started), in progress (fieldwork underway), completed (audit finished and report issued), cancelled (audit did not proceed), or report pending (fieldwork complete, awaiting final report).

attributes.name: audit_type
attributes.description: Classification of the audit based on its focus area: quality system audits (ISO 9001, IATF 16949), process audits, environmental audits (ISO 14001), cybersecurity audits (IEC 62443), social compliance audits, financial audits, or product audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality_system|process|environmental|cybersecurity|social_compliance|financial|product — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: auditor_comments
attributes.description: Free-text comments and observations from the lead auditor summarizing key findings, strengths, and areas for improvement.

attributes.name: capa_completion_date
attributes.business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Completion Date
attributes.description: Actual date when all required corrective and preventive actions were verified as complete and closed.

attributes.name: capa_due_date
attributes.business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Due Date
attributes.description: Target date by which the supplier must complete and submit corrective and preventive actions to address audit findings.

attributes.name: capa_required_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator whether corrective and preventive action (CAPA) is required from the supplier to address audit findings.

attributes.name: certification_standard
attributes.description: The specific certification standard or framework being audited against, such as ISO 9001, IATF 16949, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, IEC 62443, or other industry-specific standards.
attributes.references: ISO 9001 Quality Management, IATF 16949 Automotive Quality, ISO 14001 Environmental Management, IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity

attributes.name: cost
attributes.description: Total cost incurred for conducting the audit, including auditor fees, travel expenses, and administrative costs.

attributes.name: cost_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the audit cost (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the supplier audit record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: critical_findings_count
attributes.description: Number of critical non-conformances or findings identified during the audit that pose immediate risk to quality, safety, or compliance and require urgent corrective action.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when the audit fieldwork was completed, applicable for multi-day audits.

attributes.name: follow_up_audit_date
attributes.description: Scheduled or actual date of the follow-up audit conducted to verify corrective action effectiveness.

attributes.name: follow_up_audit_required_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator whether a follow-up audit is required to verify implementation and effectiveness of corrective actions.

attributes.name: frequency_months
attributes.description: Standard audit frequency interval in months for this supplier based on risk classification and business requirements (e.g., 12, 24, 36 months).

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the supplier audit record was last updated or modified in the system.

attributes.name: lead_auditor_name
attributes.description: Full name of the lead auditor responsible for conducting and overseeing the supplier audit.

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: Physical location or site where the audit was conducted, typically the supplier facility address or site name.

attributes.name: major_findings_count
attributes.description: Number of major non-conformances or findings identified during the audit that represent significant deviations from standards or requirements.

attributes.name: minor_findings_count
attributes.description: Number of minor non-conformances or findings identified during the audit that represent isolated or low-impact deviations from standards.

attributes.name: next_audit_due_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next supplier audit based on audit frequency policy, risk rating, and previous audit results.

attributes.name: observation_count
attributes.description: Number of observations or opportunities for improvement noted during the audit that do not constitute non-conformances but warrant attention.

attributes.name: report_date
attributes.description: Date when the formal audit report was issued to the supplier and internal stakeholders.

attributes.name: risk_rating_post_audit
attributes.description: Updated supplier risk rating assigned after the audit based on audit findings and overall performance evaluation.

attributes.name: risk_rating_pre_audit
attributes.description: Supplier risk rating assigned before the audit based on historical performance, criticality, and risk assessment criteria.

attributes.name: score
attributes.description: Numerical score assigned to the audit based on evaluation criteria, typically expressed as a percentage or weighted score reflecting overall supplier performance.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Name of the operational system from which this audit record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Ariba Supplier Management, or dedicated audit management system).

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when the audit fieldwork commenced, applicable for multi-day audits.

attributes.name: supplier_response
attributes.description: Free-text response or feedback from the supplier regarding the audit findings and proposed corrective actions.

attributes.name: team_members
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of additional auditor names who participated in the audit as team members.

attributes.name: total_findings_count
attributes.description: Total number of all findings (critical, major, minor) identified during the audit, excluding observations.

products.name: supplier_certification
products.description: Tracks all regulatory, quality, and industry certifications held by a vendor or vendor site, including ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, ISO 50001, IEC 62443, UL, CE Marking, IATF 16949, AS9100, and RoHS compliance. Captures certification standard, issuing body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, scope of certification, and renewal status. Drives automated alerts for expiring certifications and gates procurement decisions for regulated commodity categories.
products.reference: ISO 9001, ISO 14001, IEC 62443, UL, CE Marking, IATF 16949

attributes.name: supplier_certification_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the supplier certification record. Primary key for the supplier certification entity.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier who holds this certification. Links to the supplier master data entity.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific supplier site or facility where this certification applies. Nullable if certification applies to the entire supplier organization rather than a specific site.

attributes.name: accreditation_body
attributes.description: The name of the accreditation body that accredits the issuing certification body. Examples include ANAB (ANSI National Accreditation Board), UKAS (United Kingdom Accreditation Service), DAkkS (German Accreditation Body), or other national accreditation authorities.

attributes.name: alert_threshold_days
attributes.description: The number of days before expiry at which automated alerts should be triggered to notify stakeholders of the upcoming certification expiration. Typical values range from 30 to 180 days.

attributes.name: audit_report_url
attributes.description: The URL or file path to the most recent audit report document stored in the document management system or cloud storage.

attributes.name: audit_result
attributes.description: The outcome of the most recent audit. Passed indicates full compliance. Passed with observations indicates minor non-conformances that do not affect certification. Failed indicates major non-conformances. Conditional indicates certification granted pending corrective actions. Deferred indicates the audit decision is postponed.

attributes.name: audit_type
attributes.description: The type of audit that resulted in this certification or its most recent validation. Initial audit is the first certification audit. Surveillance audits are periodic checks. Recertification audits occur at the end of the certification cycle. Special audits address specific issues. Transfer audits occur when changing certification bodies.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether automatic renewal workflows and alerts are enabled for this certification. True if the system should automatically trigger renewal processes and notifications. False if renewal is managed manually.

attributes.name: business_criticality
attributes.description: The importance of this certification to the business relationship with the supplier. Critical certifications are mandatory for continued business. High criticality certifications are strongly preferred. Medium and low criticality certifications are informational or nice-to-have.

attributes.name: certificate_document_url
attributes.description: The URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification certificate document stored in the document management system or cloud storage.

attributes.name: certification_level
attributes.description: The tier, grade, or level of certification achieved, if the standard defines multiple levels. Examples include Level 1, Level 2, Level 3 for IEC 62443, or Gold, Silver, Bronze for tiered certification programs.

attributes.name: certification_number
attributes.description: The unique certificate number or registration number issued by the certifying body. This is the externally-known identifier for the certification.

attributes.name: certification_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of the certification. Active indicates the certification is valid and in good standing. Expired indicates the certification has passed its expiry date. Suspended indicates temporary suspension by the issuing body. Revoked indicates permanent withdrawal. Pending renewal indicates the certification is in the renewal process. Under review indicates the certification is being audited or reassessed.

attributes.name: certification_type
attributes.description: The category or domain of the certification. Classifies certifications into quality management, environmental management, health and safety, energy management, cybersecurity, product safety, industry-specific standards, or regulatory compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality|environmental|health_safety|energy|cybersecurity|product_safety|industry_specific|regulatory_compliance — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: certified_processes
attributes.description: A comma-separated or structured list of specific manufacturing processes, production lines, or operational activities that are covered under this certification. Examples include welding, machining, assembly, testing, or packaging.

attributes.name: certified_products
attributes.description: A comma-separated or structured list of specific product categories, product families, or SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) that are covered under this certification. Used to gate procurement decisions for regulated commodity categories.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context about this certification. May include information about special conditions, limitations, or historical context.

attributes.name: corrective_action_due_date
attributes.description: The deadline by which all required corrective actions must be completed and verified. Failure to meet this date may result in certification suspension or revocation.

attributes.name: corrective_actions_required
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether corrective actions are required to address audit findings. True if corrective actions are pending or in progress. False if no corrective actions are needed or all have been completed.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this certification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: The date from which the certification becomes effective and valid for business use. May differ from issue date if there is a grace period or transition period.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid. Critical for triggering renewal workflows and procurement gating decisions.

attributes.name: gated_commodity_categories
attributes.description: A comma-separated list of commodity categories or material groups for which this certification is mandatory. Procurement of these commodities from this supplier is blocked if the certification is not active and valid.

attributes.name: geographic_scope
attributes.description: The geographic regions, countries, or markets where this certification is recognized and valid. Examples include USA, EU, China, or Global. Uses 3-letter ISO country codes where applicable.

attributes.name: issue_date
attributes.description: The date on which the certification was originally issued by the certifying body. Represents the start of the certification validity period.

attributes.name: issuing_body
attributes.description: The name of the certification body, registrar, or regulatory authority that issued the certification. Examples include TUV, BSI, DNV, SGS, UL, Intertek, Bureau Veritas, LRQA, or government agencies.

attributes.name: last_audit_date
attributes.description: The date of the most recent surveillance audit or recertification audit conducted by the certifying body to verify ongoing compliance with the certification standard.

attributes.name: last_updated_by
attributes.description: The user identifier or system account that most recently modified this certification record. Audit trail field for accountability and data lineage.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this certification record was most recently modified. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: major_nonconformance_count
attributes.description: The number of major non-conformances identified during the most recent audit. Major non-conformances are serious violations that can lead to certification suspension or revocation.

attributes.name: minor_nonconformance_count
attributes.description: The number of minor non-conformances identified during the most recent audit. Minor non-conformances are less serious violations that require corrective action but do not immediately threaten certification status.

attributes.name: next_audit_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next surveillance audit or recertification audit. Used for planning and ensuring continuous compliance.

attributes.name: nonconformance_count
attributes.description: The total number of non-conformances (major and minor) identified during the most recent audit. Used to assess the supplier's compliance maturity and risk level.

attributes.name: procurement_gate_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this certification is used to gate procurement decisions. True if suppliers without valid certification in this standard cannot be selected for certain commodity categories. False if the certification is informational only.

attributes.name: renewal_date
attributes.description: The target date by which the certification renewal process must be completed to avoid expiration. Typically occurs before the expiry date to allow for processing time.

attributes.name: renewal_status
attributes.description: The current status of the certification renewal process. Not due indicates renewal is not yet required. Renewal initiated indicates the renewal process has started. Renewal in progress indicates active renewal activities. Renewal completed indicates successful renewal. Renewal overdue indicates the renewal deadline has passed.

attributes.name: risk_rating
attributes.description: The risk level associated with this certification based on its status, expiry proximity, audit findings, and business criticality. Low indicates minimal risk. Medium indicates moderate risk requiring monitoring. High indicates significant risk requiring action. Critical indicates immediate risk requiring urgent intervention.

attributes.name: scope_of_certification
attributes.description: A detailed description of the scope, activities, products, services, or processes covered by this certification. Defines the boundaries and applicability of the certification within the supplier's operations.

attributes.name: standard
attributes.description: The regulatory, quality, or industry standard that this certification represents. Examples include ISO 9001 (Quality Management), ISO 14001 (Environmental Management), ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety), ISO 50001 (Energy Management), IEC 62443 (Industrial Cybersecurity), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality), AS9100 (Aerospace Quality), UL (Product Safety), CE Marking (European Conformity), RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), and REACH (Chemical Safety). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO 9001|ISO 14001|ISO 45001|ISO 50001|IEC 62443|IATF 16949|AS9100|UL|CE Marking|RoHS|REACH|ISO 27001|ISO 13485|OHSAS 18001 — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ISO, IEC, UL, CE, EPA, OSHA Standards

attributes.name: verification_date
attributes.description: The date on which the certification was last verified by internal quality assurance or procurement teams. Verification includes confirming the certificate is authentic, current, and matches the supplier's claims.

attributes.name: verification_method
attributes.description: The method used to verify the certification. Document review indicates verification by examining the certificate document. Certifying body confirmation indicates direct verification with the issuing body. On-site inspection indicates physical verification during a supplier visit. Third-party verification indicates verification by an external auditor. Supplier declaration indicates acceptance of supplier-provided documentation without independent verification.

attributes.name: verified_by
attributes.description: The name or identifier of the internal employee or quality assurance team member who verified the authenticity and validity of this certification with the issuing body.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: The user identifier or system account that created this certification record. Audit trail field for accountability and data lineage.

products.name: approved_vendor_list
products.description: The Approved Vendor List (AVL) entry linking a qualified vendor to a specific commodity category, material group, or part family for which they are authorized to supply. Captures AVL status (active, suspended, phased-out), commodity code, material group, preferred/backup designation, MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity), lead time, geographic supply region, and approval date. The AVL is the operational gate used by MRP and procurement to select valid sources during purchase order creation in SAP S/4HANA MM.
products.reference: SAP Source List (ME01), AVL, SAP Ariba Sourcing

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the approved vendor list entry. Primary key for the AVL record linking a qualified supplier to authorized commodity categories.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Site-specific AVL management: in industrial manufacturing, AVLs are scoped to specific customer account sites (factories/plants) that dictate approved suppliers for their production lines. A procureme

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: AVL entries are typically governed by a framework agreement or quality agreement. The AVL currently stores contract_reference_number as a STRING. Normalizing to agreement_id FK enables direct navigati

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: AVL management in industrial manufacturing is a commodity-category-driven process — category managers maintain approved vendor lists per commodity. commodity_code and commodity_description are denorma

attributes.name: primary_approved_supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the qualified supplier authorized to supply materials or services for the specified commodity category.

attributes.name: qualification_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.qualification. Business justification: An AVL entry should be backed by a formal qualification record — a supplier should only appear on the AVL if they have passed qualification for the relevant commodity. Linking approved_vendor_list.qua

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: The AVL is the gating document for procurement: no PO can be issued for a SKU without an approved AVL entry. Industrial manufacturing ERP systems (SAP, Oracle) require AVL entries to reference the spe

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: AVL entries in industrial manufacturing are often site-specific — a supplier may be approved for a commodity at their Detroit facility but not their Mexico facility. The AVL has supply_plant_code (STR

attributes.name: annual_spend_target
attributes.description: Target annual procurement spend with this supplier for the specified commodity category, used for volume commitment tracking and supplier relationship management.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the supplier was officially approved for the specified commodity category following qualification audits and assessments.

attributes.name: approved_by_name
attributes.description: Name of the procurement manager, quality manager, or authorized approver who approved this AVL entry.

attributes.name: avl_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the AVL entry used in procurement documents and Material Requirements Planning (MRP) source selection.

attributes.name: avl_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the AVL entry determining whether the supplier can be selected during purchase order creation in Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and procurement processes.

attributes.name: capacity_rating
attributes.description: Assessment of the suppliers production capacity and ability to scale volume for this commodity category.
attributes.references: Supply Chain Risk Management - Capacity Assessment

attributes.name: certification_list
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of required certifications such as ISO 9001, ISO 14001, IATF 16949, AS9100, or industry-specific quality standards.

attributes.name: certification_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether specific certifications (ISO, industry-specific, regulatory) are required for the supplier to maintain AVL status for this commodity.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Free-text comments providing additional context about the AVL entry, special conditions, restrictions, or notes for procurement teams.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Source List Notes

attributes.name: cost_competitiveness_rating
attributes.description: Cost competitiveness rating comparing the suppliers pricing for this commodity against market benchmarks and other approved suppliers.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this AVL record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for pricing and invoicing transactions with this supplier for the specified commodity.

attributes.name: delivery_rating
attributes.description: Current on-time delivery performance rating for the supplier in this commodity category, used in Material Requirements Planning (MRP) source selection.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the AVL entry expires or is phased out. Null indicates an open-ended approval with no planned expiration.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Source List Validity

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which the AVL entry becomes active and the supplier can be selected for purchase orders in the specified commodity category.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Source List Validity

attributes.name: expedited_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Expedited lead time in days available for urgent orders, typically shorter than standard lead time but may incur additional costs.
attributes.references: Supply Chain Management - Lead Time Management

attributes.name: geographic_supply_region
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code or region code indicating the primary geographic region from which the supplier will ship materials for this commodity.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and supplier for shipments under this AVL entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: last_audit_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent supplier audit conducted for this commodity category, supporting ongoing qualification status.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this AVL record, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.

attributes.name: material_group
attributes.description: Material group classification code used in Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems to categorize materials and link to approved suppliers.

attributes.name: moq
attributes.description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required by the supplier for this commodity category. Used by Material Requirements Planning (MRP) to ensure order quantities meet supplier constraints.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record

attributes.name: moq_unit_of_measure
attributes.business_glossary_term: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) Unit of Measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), M (meter), L (liter).

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this AVL entry to assess continued supplier qualification and performance.

attributes.name: part_family
attributes.description: Part family or product line grouping for which the supplier is qualified to provide components or assemblies.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Standard payment terms applicable to purchase orders for this supplier and commodity combination, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30.

attributes.name: ppap_level
attributes.description: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) level required for parts supplied under this AVL entry, ranging from Level 1 (warrant only) to Level 5 (full submission with samples).

attributes.name: price_validity_end_date
attributes.description: End date for the current pricing agreement, after which prices must be renegotiated or updated.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record

attributes.name: price_validity_start_date
attributes.description: Start date for the current pricing agreement applicable to this AVL entry.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record

attributes.name: purchasing_group
attributes.description: Purchasing group or buyer team responsible for managing the supplier relationship and executing purchase orders for this commodity.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization
attributes.description: Purchasing organization code responsible for managing this AVL entry and executing procurement transactions with the supplier.

attributes.name: quality_rating
attributes.description: Current quality performance rating for the supplier in this commodity category, typically expressed as a percentage or score used in supplier selection decisions.

attributes.name: review_frequency_months
attributes.description: Frequency in months at which this AVL entry must be reviewed and revalidated to ensure continued supplier qualification.

attributes.name: risk_rating
attributes.description: Overall supply risk rating for this supplier and commodity combination considering financial stability, geopolitical factors, single-source dependency, and business continuity.

attributes.name: single_source_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this supplier is the only approved source for the commodity category, representing a supply chain risk requiring mitigation strategies.
attributes.references: Supply Chain Risk Management - Single Source Risk

attributes.name: standard_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Standard procurement lead time in days from purchase order placement to delivery for this supplier and commodity combination. Used by Material Requirements Planning (MRP) for scheduling.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record

attributes.name: supplier_designation
attributes.description: Designation indicating the suppliers priority level for sourcing decisions. Preferred suppliers are selected first by Material Requirements Planning (MRP), backup suppliers are used when preferred suppliers cannot fulfill demand.

attributes.name: supply_plant_code
attributes.description: Supplier plant or facility code from which materials will be shipped for this commodity category.

products.name: purchase_requisition
products.description: Master record for internal purchase requisition (PR) initiated by a department or MRP run. Captures the request for direct materials, indirect materials, MRO supplies, or capital equipment before a purchase order is raised. Tracks requestor, cost center, required delivery date, material/service description, estimated value, approval status, and sourcing assignment. Serves as the originating document in the procure-to-pay cycle.

attributes.name: purchase_requisition_id
attributes.description: Primary key for purchase_requisition

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: REQUIRED: Internal requisitions are raised for a specific plant/site; linking enables site‑wise spend analysis.

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: When a purchase requisition is created (especially from MRP), the source determination process selects an approved vendor from the AVL. Linking purchase_requisition.approved_vendor_list_id → approved_

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: PRs are raised within a company code context for budget availability checks and approval workflow routing in multi-entity industrial manufacturers. purchasing_organization_code alone is insufficient —

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Engineering Change Request triggers a purchase requisition for the affected component; linking enables traceability of component‑driven procurement.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Requisition budgeting and approval allocate planned spend to a cost center, essential for budget control.

attributes.name: demand_forecast_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.demand_forecast. Business justification: Demand forecasts drive requisitions; the relationship is needed for the 'Forecast‑Driven Requisition' analysis.

attributes.name: eco_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: Procurement requisitions are generated from Engineering Change Orders; linking provides audit of ECO‑driven spend.

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Revision-controlled procurement: PRs must reference the approved engineering revision to ensure the correct version is procured. This prevents ordering obsolete or unapproved revisions — a critical qu

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: CAPITAL ACQUISITION: Requisition must reference the equipment record for traceability of requested capital assets.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Requisition may be tied to a GL account for expense planning and forecast alignment.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the material or item being requested. Links to the material master data in SAP MM or inventory management system. Null for service or non-stock requisitions.

attributes.name: mrp_run_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.mrp_run. Business justification: MRP run creates purchase requisitions; the MRP run ID is required for the 'MRP Run to Requisition' traceability report.

attributes.name: opportunity_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Procurement creates a requisition based on a sales opportunity to ensure material availability for the promised delivery.

attributes.name: planned_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: Planned orders are converted to purchase requisitions; linking them enables the 'Planned Order Conversion' audit.

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the existing contract or blanket purchase order against which this requisition should be fulfilled. Null for non-contract purchases.

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_info_record. Business justification: In SAP-style procurement (standard in industrial manufacturing), a purchase requisition references a purchase info record for pricing, lead time, and source of supply. Adding purchase_info_record_id F

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: A purchase requisition is converted into a purchase order — this is the core procurement lifecycle transition. The PR currently stores po_number as a STRING. Normalizing to purchase_order_id FK enable

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Required for the 'Component Procurement Planning' report linking requisitions to the final product SKU they support, enabling traceability from requisition to product.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: REQUIRED: Requisition planning specifies target storage location for incoming material; needed for inbound logistics allocation and warehouse capacity reports.

attributes.name: supplier_contact_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_contact. Business justification: Needed for Supplier Relationship Management process: requisition records the primary supplier contact for communication and escalation.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the preferred or suggested supplier for this requisition. Links to the supplier master data. Null if no preference specified.

attributes.name: approval_level_required
attributes.description: Number of approval levels required for this requisition based on value thresholds and organizational policy. Higher values require more senior approvals.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the purchase requisition received final approval and became ready for sourcing. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this requisition requires special compliance review (e.g., environmental, safety, export control). True if compliance review is required.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the purchase requisition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and process cycle time analysis.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency procurement and financial reporting.

attributes.name: estimated_total_value
attributes.description: Total estimated value of the purchase requisition (quantity × estimated unit price). Used for budget control and approval routing.

attributes.name: estimated_unit_price
attributes.description: Estimated price per unit of the requested material or service. Used for budget estimation and approval thresholds. Actual price is determined during sourcing and PO creation.

attributes.name: justification_notes
attributes.description: Business justification or rationale provided by the requestor for the purchase. Required for high-value or non-standard requisitions.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the purchase requisition record was last updated. Tracks changes throughout the approval and sourcing lifecycle.

attributes.name: mrp_controller
attributes.description: MRP controller responsible for material planning and requisition generation for production materials. Used for MRP-driven procurement.

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: Manufacturing plant or facility where the material or service is required. Used for delivery planning and inventory allocation in SAP PP.

attributes.name: po_created_date
attributes.description: Date when the purchase order was created from this requisition. Marks the transition from requisition to order in the procure-to-pay cycle.

attributes.name: pr_date
attributes.description: Date when the purchase requisition was created or submitted. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the requisition initiation.

attributes.name: pr_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the purchase requisition, externally visible and used in procurement workflows. Typically system-generated in SAP Ariba or SAP MM.

attributes.name: pr_status
