attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the purchase requisition in the procure-to-pay workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval, sourcing, and conversion to purchase order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|pending_approval|approved|rejected|sourcing_assigned|po_created|cancelled|closed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: pr_type
attributes.description: Classification of the purchase requisition based on the nature of the procurement: direct materials for production, indirect materials for operations, MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) supplies, capital equipment (CapEx), services, or subcontracting work.

attributes.name: priority_code
attributes.description: Priority level of the purchase requisition. Urgent and emergency priorities expedite approval and sourcing processes.

attributes.name: purchasing_group_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the purchasing group or buyer responsible for sourcing this requisition. Used for workload distribution and supplier relationship management.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the purchasing organization responsible for procurement activities. Defines the organizational unit for supplier contracts and purchase orders.

attributes.name: quantity_requested
attributes.description: Quantity of the material or service being requested. Expressed in the unit of measure specified in the UOM field.

attributes.name: rejected_date
attributes.description: Date when the purchase requisition was rejected by an approver. Null if not rejected.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Explanation provided by the approver for rejecting the purchase requisition. Used for audit trail and process improvement.

attributes.name: requestor_department
attributes.description: Department or organizational unit of the requestor. Used for spend analysis and budget allocation tracking.

attributes.name: requestor_name
attributes.description: Full name of the employee who created the purchase requisition. Captured for audit trail and approval workflow visibility.

attributes.name: required_delivery_date
attributes.description: Date by which the requested materials, supplies, or services must be delivered to meet operational or production requirements. Used for MRP (Material Requirements Planning) scheduling and supplier lead time calculation.

attributes.name: source_determination_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates how the supplier source should be determined: automatic sourcing via system rules, manual buyer selection, contract-based assignment, or preferred supplier list.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the requested quantity. Standard units include EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter), HR (hour), etc. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|HR|SET|BOX|ROLL|SHEET — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: purchase_order
products.description: Core transactional document representing a legally binding purchase order (PO) issued to a supplier. Captures PO number, supplier, plant/delivery location, payment terms, incoterms, total PO value, currency, PO type (standard, blanket, framework, subcontracting), approval status, and confirmation status. Central document in the procure-to-pay process linking requisitions to goods receipts and supplier invoices.

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Unique system identifier for the purchase order record. Primary key.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: REQUIRED: PO delivery planning tracks which customer site receives purchased goods; used in site‑level inventory and cost reports.

attributes.name: address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: REQUIRED: Drop‑ship to customer needs PO delivery address for logistics and billing reports.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Purchase orders in industrial manufacturing are frequently issued against framework agreements or long-term supply agreements. Linking purchase_order.agreement_id → agreement.agreement_id enables cont

attributes.name: bom_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Purchase orders are raised against a specific BOM for a production run; linking supports BOM‑to‑PO cost tracking.

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: POs in industrial manufacturing are issued by a specific legal entity (company code) for AP processing, tax determination, and payment execution. company_code is a plain-text denormalized column on pu

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PO posting requires cost center for internal accounting; mandatory for cost allocation reports.

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Revision-controlled PO issuance: POs must specify the engineering revision to ensure suppliers manufacture to the correct drawing/specification version. This is a fundamental quality and traceability

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each PO expense is posted to a GL account for financial statements; required by accounting standards.

attributes.name: header_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Required for Make‑to‑Order production planning; links each purchase order to the sales order it fulfills, enabling the Order‑to‑Procure fulfillment report.

attributes.name: material_requirement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: Purchase orders are issued to satisfy material requirements; the link supports the 'Material Requirement Fulfilment' KPI.

attributes.name: sales_order_intake_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Each purchase order fulfills a specific sales order intake; linking enables end‑to‑end order‑to‑procurement tracking.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Needed for 'Order‑Driven Procurement' process where purchase orders are tied to the specific product SKU being manufactured, supporting make‑to‑order execution.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific delivery address or warehouse location for goods receipt.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier or vendor to whom this purchase order is issued.

attributes.name: supply_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Supply plans dictate purchase order creation; linking PO to supply_plan enables the 'Supply Plan Execution' report.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: Required for Logistics Planning Report: PO must reference the exact supplier site delivering goods, enabling site‑specific lead‑time and cost calculations.

attributes.name: acknowledgement_date
attributes.description: Date when the supplier acknowledged the purchase order.

attributes.name: acknowledgement_status
attributes.description: Status indicating whether the supplier has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the purchase order.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the purchase order received final approval.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval workflow status for the purchase order.

attributes.name: closed_date
attributes.description: Date when the purchase order was administratively closed after all goods were received and invoices processed.

attributes.name: compliance_status
attributes.description: Status indicating whether the purchase order meets all applicable procurement compliance policies and regulatory requirements.

attributes.name: confirmed_delivery_date

attributes.name: created_timestamp

attributes.name: currency_code

attributes.name: goods_receipt_status

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, insurance, and risk transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: incoterms_location
attributes.description: Named place or port specified in the Incoterms agreement where risk and cost transfer occurs.

attributes.name: invoice_receipt_status
