attributes.name: material_category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the type of materials or services being procured. Direct materials are used in production, indirect materials support operations, MRO is maintenance/repair/operations supplies.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp when the purchase order record was last modified.

attributes.name: net_po_value
attributes.description: Net total value of the purchase order after taxes and all adjustments.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional instructions, special requirements, or comments related to the purchase order.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Code representing the agreed payment terms with the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).

attributes.name: po_date
attributes.description: Date when the purchase order was created and issued to the supplier. Principal business event timestamp for the transaction.

attributes.name: po_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the purchase order. Used in supplier communications and invoice matching.

attributes.name: po_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the purchase order in the procure-to-pay workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|issued|acknowledged|in_progress|partially_received|fully_received|closed|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: po_type
attributes.description: Classification of the purchase order type. Standard for one-time orders, blanket for recurring orders with release schedules, framework for long-term agreements, subcontracting for external processing, consignment for supplier-owned inventory.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to the purchase order affecting processing and delivery urgency.

attributes.name: purchasing_group
attributes.description: Code identifying the buyer or procurement team responsible for this purchase order.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization
attributes.description: Code identifying the organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Represents the legal entity negotiating with suppliers.

attributes.name: requested_delivery_date
attributes.description: Date by which the buyer requests delivery of goods or completion of services.

attributes.name: shipping_method
attributes.description: Mode of transportation specified for delivery of goods.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount applicable to this purchase order.

attributes.name: total_po_value
attributes.description: Total gross value of the purchase order including all line items before taxes and charges.

attributes.name: wbs_element
attributes.description: Work breakdown structure element for project-based purchase orders, enabling cost tracking at the project task level.

products.name: po_line_item
products.description: Individual line item within a purchase order representing a specific material, service, or SKU being procured. Captures line number, material number, short text, quantity ordered, unit of measure, net price, delivery date, storage location, account assignment (cost center, WBS element, asset), and goods receipt indicator. Enables granular spend tracking, partial delivery management, and three-way match at the item level.

attributes.name: po_line_item_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the purchase order line item. Primary key for the po_line_item product.

attributes.name: component_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Engineering-to-procurement traceability: PO line items must be traceable to the engineering component for BOM cost rollup, ECN impact analysis, and spend analytics by component. `manufacturer_part_num

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑item expense allocation to cost center enables detailed cost reporting and variance analysis.

attributes.name: engineering_bom_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: Order line items are derived from BOM lines during material planning; linking enables line‑level reconciliation.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Line‑item posting to GL account is required for accurate expense classification in the general ledger.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record being procured. Links to the specific product, component, or service being ordered.

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_info_record. Business justification: Each PO line item is sourced from a specific purchase info record that defines the negotiated price, lead time, and approved source for that material-supplier combination. The po_line_item currently s

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent purchase order header. Links this line item to its containing purchase order document.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: REQUIRED: Line item defines where received material will be stored; required for put‑away execution and inventory valuation per location.

attributes.name: account_assignment_category
attributes.description: Classification determining how procurement costs are allocated in financial accounting (e.g., to cost center, asset, WBS element, or sales order).

attributes.name: buyer_name
attributes.description: Name of the procurement professional responsible for sourcing and managing this purchase order line item.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this purchase order line item record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the net price and line item value (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: deletion_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating this line item has been marked for deletion or cancellation. Prevents further goods receipt and invoice posting.

attributes.name: delivery_date
attributes.description: Requested or committed delivery date for this line item. Used for MRP planning, production scheduling, and supplier performance tracking.

attributes.name: final_invoice_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating the final invoice has been received and no further invoices are expected for this line item.

attributes.name: goods_receipt_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether goods receipt is required for this line item. Determines three-way match requirements for invoice verification.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: Standardized trade terms defining responsibilities for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP).

attributes.name: incoterms_location
attributes.description: Named place or port specified in the Incoterms clause where risk and cost transfer occurs.

attributes.name: invoice_receipt_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether invoice verification is required for this line item. Controls accounts payable processing workflow.

attributes.name: item_category
attributes.description: Classification of the procurement item type. Determines procurement processing rules, inventory treatment, and account assignment requirements.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this purchase order line item record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: line_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the purchase order line item. Tracks progression from open through receipt to closure.

attributes.name: material_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the material being procured. The externally-known SKU or part number used in procurement documents and supplier communication.

attributes.name: net_order_value
attributes.description: Total value of this line item calculated as (quantity_ordered / price_unit) * net_price. Excludes taxes and freight charges.

attributes.name: net_price
attributes.description: The negotiated price per unit of measure before taxes and additional charges. Used to calculate line item total value.

attributes.name: open_quantity
attributes.description: Outstanding quantity still to be delivered, calculated as quantity_ordered minus quantity_received. Used for supplier follow-up and expediting.

attributes.name: over_delivery_tolerance_percent
attributes.description: Acceptable percentage by which the supplier may over-deliver beyond the ordered quantity without requiring approval.

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: The manufacturing plant or facility where the material will be received and consumed. Determines receiving location and inventory posting.

attributes.name: price_unit
attributes.description: The quantity for which the net price is valid (e.g., price per 1, per 100, per 1000 units). Used in unit price calculation.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether incoming quality inspection is mandatory before goods receipt posting for this line item.

attributes.name: quantity_invoiced
attributes.description: Cumulative quantity invoiced by the supplier against this line item to date. Used for invoice verification and payment processing.

attributes.name: quantity_ordered
attributes.description: The quantity of material or service units being procured on this line item. Used for goods receipt matching and invoice verification.

attributes.name: quantity_received
attributes.description: Cumulative quantity of goods received against this line item to date. Used for partial delivery tracking and open quantity calculation.

attributes.name: requisitioner_name
attributes.description: Name of the employee or department that originated the purchase requisition leading to this line item.

attributes.name: shipping_instruction
attributes.description: Special instructions for packaging, labeling, or delivery requirements specific to this line item.

attributes.name: short_text
attributes.description: Brief description of the material or service being procured on this line. Provides human-readable context for the line item.

attributes.name: supplier_material_number
attributes.description: The suppliers own part number or SKU for the material being procured. Used for supplier communication and catalog matching.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount calculated for this line item based on tax code and net order value.

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: Tax classification code determining applicable tax rates and tax jurisdiction for this procurement line item.

attributes.name: under_delivery_tolerance_percent
attributes.description: Acceptable percentage by which the supplier may under-deliver below the ordered quantity without penalty or rejection.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit in which the ordered quantity is expressed (e.g., EA, KG, M, L, HR). Must align with material master and supplier agreement.

products.name: procurement_goods_receipt
products.description: Goods receipt (GR) document recorded when materials or services are physically received from a supplier against a purchase order. Captures GR document number, posting date, delivery note number, received quantity, unit of measure, storage location, batch number, quality inspection flag, and GR/IR clearing status. Triggers inventory update and initiates the three-way match process (PO–GR–Invoice) for accounts payable.

attributes.name: procurement_goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the goods receipt document. Primary key for the procurement goods receipt entity.

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: GR postings generate GR/IR clearing account entries scoped to a company code in SAP-style manufacturing ERP. receiving_plant_code alone does not determine the legal entity. Company code FK is required

attributes.name: engineering_revision_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Incoming inspection revision verification: goods receipt must confirm the received revision matches the ordered revision. This is a critical incoming quality control step — receiving the wrong revisio

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: GOODS RECEIPT: Receiving equipment updates the equipment register for inventory, commissioning, and warranty start dates.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the material or service received. Links to the material master for product specifications and inventory management.

attributes.name: po_line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific line item on the purchase order for which goods are being received. Enables line-level three-way matching.

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the purchase order against which this goods receipt is recorded. Links the GR to the originating procurement document.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the specific storage location within the warehouse where the received goods are placed. Enables precise inventory tracking.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the supplier from whom the goods or services were received. Key counterparty in the procurement transaction.

attributes.name: warehouse_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the warehouse or receiving facility where the goods were physically received.

attributes.name: accounting_document_number
attributes.description: The financial accounting document number generated when the goods receipt value is posted to the General Ledger (GL). Links the GR to financial transactions.

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: The batch or lot number assigned to the received material. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management in regulated industries.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this goods receipt record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the goods receipt value is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).

attributes.name: damage_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the received goods were damaged during transit or delivery. True if damage was observed; false otherwise. Triggers quality hold and supplier notification.

attributes.name: delivery_date
attributes.description: The actual date on which the goods were physically delivered to the receiving location. Used for supplier performance evaluation and lead time analysis.

attributes.name: delivery_note_number
attributes.description: The delivery note or packing slip number provided by the supplier. Used to match physical delivery documentation with the goods receipt record.

attributes.name: document_date
attributes.description: The date printed on the goods receipt document. May differ from the posting date for backdated or forward-dated transactions.

attributes.name: document_number
attributes.description: The externally-known unique document number assigned to this goods receipt transaction. Used for tracking, auditing, and cross-system reconciliation.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The date on which the received material expires or becomes unusable. Critical for perishable goods, chemicals, and materials with shelf-life constraints.

attributes.name: goods_receipt_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt document. Indicates whether the GR is in draft, posted to inventory, blocked for quality issues, cancelled, or reversed.

attributes.name: goods_receipt_value
attributes.description: The total monetary value of the goods received, calculated as received quantity multiplied by the purchase order unit price. Posted to inventory and General Ledger (GL) accounts.

attributes.name: gr_ir_clearing_status
attributes.business_glossary_term: Goods Receipt / Invoice Receipt (GR/IR) Clearing Status
attributes.description: Indicates the clearing status of the GR/IR account in accounts payable. Open means no invoice received; partially cleared means partial invoice match; fully cleared means invoice fully matched and cleared.

attributes.name: invoice_verification_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether an invoice has been received and verified against this goods receipt as part of the three-way match process (PO-GR-Invoice). True if invoice verified; false otherwise.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this goods receipt record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: manufacturing_date
attributes.description: The date on which the received material was manufactured by the supplier. Used for shelf-life calculation and quality tracking.

attributes.name: material_document_number
attributes.description: The SAP material document number generated when the goods receipt is posted. Links the GR to inventory movements and financial postings in the ERP system.

attributes.name: material_document_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which the material document was created. Used in combination with material document number for unique identification in SAP.

attributes.name: movement_type
attributes.description: The SAP movement type code that classifies the type of goods receipt transaction (e.g., 101 for GR against PO, 501 for GR without PO). Drives inventory and financial posting logic.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments recorded by the receiving personnel regarding the condition of the goods, packaging issues, or any discrepancies observed during receipt.

attributes.name: ordered_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity originally ordered on the purchase order line. Used to calculate over-delivery or under-delivery variances.

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: The date on which the goods receipt was posted to the inventory and financial systems. This is the accounting date for inventory valuation and General Ledger (GL) posting.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the received goods must undergo quality inspection before being released to unrestricted inventory. True if inspection is required; false otherwise.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_status
attributes.description: Current status of the quality inspection process for the received goods. Determines whether goods can be moved to unrestricted stock or must remain in quality hold.

attributes.name: quantity_variance
attributes.description: The difference between the received quantity and the ordered quantity. Positive values indicate over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery.

attributes.name: received_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of material or service units physically received and recorded in this goods receipt. Used for inventory update and three-way match validation.

attributes.name: receiving_person_name
attributes.description: The name of the individual who physically received and inspected the goods. Used for accountability and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: receiving_plant_code
attributes.description: The code of the manufacturing plant or facility that received the goods. Used for multi-plant inventory and cost center allocation.

attributes.name: return_authorization_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a Return Material Authorization (RMA) has been initiated for the received goods due to quality issues, damage, or incorrect delivery. True if RMA initiated; false otherwise.

attributes.name: reversal_date
attributes.description: The date on which this goods receipt was reversed. Used for audit trail and financial period reconciliation.

attributes.name: reversal_document_number
attributes.description: The document number of the reversal transaction if this goods receipt has been reversed. Links to the cancelling document for audit trail.

attributes.name: reversal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled. True if reversed; false otherwise. Reversed GRs do not contribute to inventory or financial balances.

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: The unique serial number of the received item, applicable for serialized inventory such as equipment, tools, or high-value components.

attributes.name: stock_type
attributes.description: The inventory stock type to which the received goods are posted. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection and blocked stock are not.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Must align with the purchase order and material master UOM.

attributes.name: vendor_batch_number
attributes.description: The batch or lot number assigned by the supplier to the delivered goods. Used for supplier traceability and quality issue root cause analysis.

products.name: invoice_line_item
products.description: Individual line item on a supplier invoice corresponding to a specific PO line, service entry sheet, or unplanned delivery cost. Captures line number, material or service description, invoiced quantity, unit price, line amount, tax code, account assignment, and three-way match status (matched, quantity variance, price variance, blocked). Enables granular invoice verification and exception management in logistics invoice verification.

attributes.name: invoice_line_item_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the invoice line item. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑level cost allocation to cost center supports detailed profitability and variance reporting.

attributes.name: equipment_register_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: INVOICE ALLOCATION: Invoice line items for equipment need a FK to the equipment register for accurate asset cost tracking.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each invoice line must map to a GL account for correct posting in the general ledger.

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Reference to the material master record for the product or material being invoiced. Links to the material catalog for direct materials, indirect materials, or MRO supplies.

attributes.name: po_line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the purchase order line item that this invoice line corresponds to. Used for three-way matching between PO, goods receipt, and invoice.

attributes.name: procurement_goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Three-way matching (PO → GR → Invoice) is the cornerstone of procurement invoice verification in industrial manufacturing. The invoice_line_item currently stores goods_receipt_number as a STRING. Norm

attributes.name: profit_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis requires linking invoice lines to profit centers for revenue/expense attribution.

attributes.name: baseline_date
attributes.description: The baseline date from which payment terms are calculated for this line item. Typically the invoice date or goods receipt date depending on payment term configuration.

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: The batch or lot number for the material being invoiced. Critical for traceability, quality management, and recall management in regulated industries.

attributes.name: blocking_reason
attributes.description: The reason code or description explaining why this invoice line is blocked from payment. Examples include price variance exceeds tolerance, quantity mismatch, missing goods receipt, or quality hold.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the line amount and unit price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Must match the invoice header currency.

attributes.name: delivery_note_number
attributes.description: The supplier delivery note or packing slip number referenced on this invoice line. Used for traceability and shipment reconciliation.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Any discount amount applied at the line level. Reduces the line amount before tax calculation. May represent early payment discounts, volume discounts, or promotional discounts.

attributes.name: discount_percent
attributes.description: The discount percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5% discount).

attributes.name: goods_receipt_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity recorded on the goods receipt document. Used to compare against invoiced quantity for quantity variance detection.

attributes.name: invoiced_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of material or service units being invoiced on this line. Used for three-way match verification against PO quantity and goods receipt quantity.

attributes.name: line_amount
attributes.description: The total amount for this invoice line before taxes. Typically calculated as invoiced quantity multiplied by unit price, adjusted for any line-level discounts or surcharges.

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number within the invoice. Determines the ordering and position of this line item on the invoice document.

attributes.name: line_type
attributes.description: Classification of the invoice line item type. Distinguishes between material purchases, services, freight charges, handling fees, taxes, discounts, surcharges, and other cost elements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: material|service|freight|handling|tax|discount|surcharge|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: match_status
attributes.description: The three-way match status of this invoice line. Indicates whether the line passed matching validation or has variances requiring resolution. Blocked status prevents payment until resolved.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Logistics Invoice Verification

attributes.name: material_description
attributes.description: Textual description of the material, service, or item being invoiced. Provides human-readable detail about what was purchased.

attributes.name: material_number
attributes.description: The material number or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) as specified on the invoice line. May differ from internal material master number in case of supplier-specific identifiers.

attributes.name: net_amount
attributes.description: The net amount for this invoice line after applying discounts and adding taxes. Represents the final cost impact of this line item.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments related to this invoice line. May contain variance explanations, special handling instructions, or resolution details.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: The payment terms applicable to this invoice line if different from header-level terms. May specify early payment discounts or extended payment periods.

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: The manufacturing plant or facility code where the material or service is consumed or delivered. Used for multi-site cost allocation.

attributes.name: po_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity specified on the original purchase order line. Used as the baseline for three-way match verification.

attributes.name: po_unit_price
attributes.description: The unit price specified on the original purchase order line. Used as the baseline for price variance detection during invoice verification.

attributes.name: price_variance
attributes.description: The difference between invoiced unit price and PO unit price, multiplied by invoiced quantity. Indicates pricing discrepancies requiring review.

attributes.name: quantity_variance
attributes.description: The difference between invoiced quantity and goods receipt quantity. Positive values indicate over-invoicing, negative values indicate under-invoicing.

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: The serial number for serialized materials or equipment. Enables individual unit tracking for warranty, maintenance, and asset management.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Serial Number Management

attributes.name: storage_location
attributes.description: The storage location within the plant where the material is received or stored. Used for inventory management and warehouse assignment.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The calculated tax amount for this invoice line based on the tax code and line amount. Added to line amount to determine total line cost.

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: The tax code applied to this invoice line. Determines the tax rate and tax jurisdiction for calculating line-level tax amounts.

attributes.name: tax_rate_percent
attributes.description: The tax rate percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 7.50 for 7.5% tax rate).

attributes.name: tolerance_group
attributes.description: The tolerance group that defines acceptable variance thresholds for this line item. Different tolerance groups may apply to different material categories or supplier relationships.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Invoice Verification Configuration

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the invoiced quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters, HR for hours). Must align with PO and material master UOM for proper matching.

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: The price per unit of measure as stated on the invoice line. Used to calculate line amount and compared against PO price for variance detection.

attributes.name: valuation_type
attributes.description: The valuation type for split valuation scenarios where the same material is valued differently based on procurement source, quality grade, or other criteria.

attributes.name: verification_date
attributes.description: The date when this invoice line was verified and approved for payment. Critical for tracking invoice processing cycle time and SLA compliance.

attributes.name: verification_status
attributes.description: The current verification status of this invoice line in the approval workflow. Tracks progress through invoice verification and approval process.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM Invoice Verification Workflow

attributes.name: wbs_element
attributes.description: The WBS element for project-related procurement. Links invoice line to specific project phases or deliverables for project cost tracking.

products.name: purchase_info_record
products.description: Approved source master record linking a material or service to a qualified supplier, storing the negotiated price, standard order quantity, planned delivery time, tolerance limits, validity period, and plant-level sourcing assignments. Captures info record type (standard, subcontracting, pipeline, consignment), net price, price unit, minimum order quantity, reminder lead times, approved source indicators, MRP relevance flags, fixed source flags, valid-from/to dates, and outline agreement references. Drives automatic price proposals during PO creation and ensures MRP-driven orders are directed to approved suppliers at the correct plant.

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the purchase info record.
attributes.references: SAP Ariba (Procurement) - Info Record Master Data

attributes.name: approved_vendor_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: A purchase info record is the operational pricing/lead-time detail that backs an AVL entry. Linking purchase_info_record.approved_vendor_list_id → approved_vendor_list.approved_vendor_list_id creates

attributes.name: material_master_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the material or service for which this info record is maintained.

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: The Purchase Info Record is the ERP master record for supplier-SKU pricing and sourcing conditions (net_price, lead time, validity). In industrial manufacturing MRP, the PIR is always tied to a specif

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the supplier linked to this info record.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Purchase info records in industrial manufacturing are often site-specific — the negotiated price and lead time for a material may differ between a supplier's manufacturing sites. The purchase_info_rec

attributes.name: approved_source_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier is an approved source for the material (true = approved).

attributes.name: contract_reference
attributes.description: Reference to an outline agreement or contract that governs this price.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the info record was initially created.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the net price is expressed.

attributes.name: fixed_source_flag
attributes.description: True if procurement must always source from this supplier for the material.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities; limited to six most common values.

attributes.name: info_record_number
attributes.description: Business identifier assigned to the purchase info record, used for reference in procurement processes.
attributes.references: SAP Ariba (Procurement) - Info Record Master Data

attributes.name: info_record_type
attributes.description: Classification of the info record indicating the sourcing strategy (e.g., standard, subcontracting, pipeline, consignment).

attributes.name: last_price_update_timestamp
attributes.description: Date‑time when the price information was last updated in the system.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the approved source.

attributes.name: mrp_relevant_flag
attributes.description: True if the info record should be considered during MRP runs.

attributes.name: net_price
attributes.description: Negotiated net price for the material/service from the approved supplier.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions.

attributes.name: order_quantity_multiple
attributes.description: Quantity increment that must be respected when ordering (e.g., multiples of 10).

attributes.name: planned_delivery_time_days
attributes.description: Expected number of days from order creation to delivery for this source.

attributes.name: plant_code
attributes.description: Code of the plant where the approved source is valid.

attributes.name: price_change_date
attributes.description: Date on which the most recent price change became effective.

attributes.name: price_change_indicator
attributes.description: Indicates whether the price has increased, decreased, or remained unchanged since the previous version.

attributes.name: price_change_reason
attributes.description: Business justification for the latest price adjustment.

attributes.name: price_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the net price (e.g., per piece, per kilogram).

attributes.name: purchase_info_record_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the info record.

attributes.name: purchasing_group
attributes.description: Group of buyers assigned to handle transactions for this info record.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization
attributes.description: Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities for this info record.

attributes.name: reminder_lead_time_days
attributes.description: Number of days before the planned delivery date when a reminder is generated.

attributes.name: source_of_supply_category
attributes.description: Broad classification of the source (e.g., internal, external, third‑party).

attributes.name: source_priority
attributes.description: Numeric rank indicating preference order among multiple approved sources for the same material.

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: Tax classification applicable to the purchase price.

attributes.name: tolerance_limit_percent
attributes.description: Maximum allowed deviation (percentage) from the negotiated price before a tolerance check is triggered.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the info record.

attributes.name: validity_end_date
attributes.description: Date after which the info record is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).

attributes.name: validity_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which the info record becomes effective.

attributes.name: vendor_material_number
attributes.description: Supplier's own material identifier for the supplied item.

attributes.name: vendor_price_list
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier's price list or catalogue containing this price.

products.name: agreement
products.description: Master record for supplier-level framework agreements, quality agreements, NDAs, long-term supply agreements (LTAs), and consignment agreements that govern the overall commercial and quality relationship between Manufacturing and a vendor. Distinct from individual purchase orders (owned by procurement) — this captures the umbrella contractual instruments that set terms, pricing frameworks, IP protections, and quality expectations across multiple POs. Captures agreement type (framework, quality, NDA, LTA, consignment, tooling loan), effective date, expiry date, auto-renewal terms, governing law, key commercial terms summary, associated commodity scope, and agreement status (draft, active, expired, terminated).

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the supplier agreement record.

attributes.name: account_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Site-specific supply agreements: in industrial manufacturing, supplier agreements are scoped to specific customer factories/plants (e.g., JIT supply agreement for a single production site). agreement

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Supply agreements are managed by commodity category in industrial manufacturing — category managers own contract coverage per commodity. commodity_scope is a plain-text denormalization of the commodit

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Supply agreements are legally bound to a specific company code in multi-entity industrial manufacturers. Payment terms, total_value, and governing_law are all company-code-scoped. SAP-style contract m

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Agreement linked to cost center for budgeting of contracted spend (Supplier Agreement Cost Tracking).

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Agreement defines GL account for expense posting (Contractual GL mapping).

attributes.name: master_agreement_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on agreement (master_agreement_id)

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) that is party to the agreement.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Framework agreements and quality agreements in industrial manufacturing are often scoped to a specific supplier site (e.g., a quality agreement with a supplier's ISO-certified facility). The agreement

attributes.name: agreement_number
attributes.description: External business identifier or contract number assigned to the agreement.

attributes.name: agreement_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the agreement.

attributes.name: agreement_type
attributes.description: Classification of the agreement (e.g., framework, quality, NDA, long‑term agreement, consignment, tooling loan).

attributes.name: amendment_date
attributes.description: Date on which the amendment became effective.

attributes.name: amendment_description
attributes.description: Narrative description of changes introduced by the amendment.

attributes.name: amendment_number
attributes.description: Identifier of the specific amendment applied to the base agreement.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the agreement received formal approval.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiry.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_term_months
attributes.description: Length of each renewal period in months when auto‑renewal is enabled.

attributes.name: confidentiality_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a confidentiality clause is included in the agreement.

attributes.name: contractual_term_months
attributes.description: Original length of the agreement in months, excluding renewals.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date‑time when the agreement record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary terms.

attributes.name: discount_terms
attributes.description: Conditions under which discounts apply (e.g., volume rebates, early‑pay discounts).

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date on which the agreement expires or terminates (null for open‑ended).

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date on which the agreement becomes legally binding.

attributes.name: esg_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement includes ESG (environmental, social, governance) compliance requirements.

attributes.name: governing_law
attributes.description: Jurisdiction whose law governs the agreement.

attributes.name: ip_protection_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether intellectual‑property protection terms are present.

attributes.name: jurisdiction
attributes.description: Geographic or legal jurisdiction applicable to the agreement.

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent formal review of the agreement terms.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Planned date for the next scheduled review of the agreement.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Standard payment conditions (e.g., net 30, milestone‑based) defined in the agreement.

attributes.name: performance_kpi_summary
attributes.description: Key performance indicators and targets defined in the agreement (e.g., OEE, delivery reliability).

attributes.name: price_index
attributes.description: Reference to any price index or formula used to adjust pricing over time.

attributes.name: quality_expectations_summary
attributes.description: High‑level description of quality standards and metrics required from the supplier.

attributes.name: renewal_notice_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days prior to expiry that a renewal notice must be issued.

attributes.name: termination_notice_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days required to give notice before terminating the agreement.

attributes.name: total_value
attributes.description: Maximum monetary value covered by the agreement, expressed in the specified currency.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date‑time of the most recent modification to the agreement record.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Sequential version of the agreement document (incremented on each amendment).

domains.name: finance
domains.description: Corporate finance and cost management domain governing general ledger, cost centers, profit centers, accounts payable, CapEx/OpEx tracking, EBITDA reporting, budgeting, financial planning and analysis, and financial consolidation via SAP FI/CO. Serves as SSOT for all internal financial structures and management accounting aligned with IFRS/GAAP.

products.name: procurement_contract
products.description: Master procurement contract or framework agreement established with a supplier for recurring supply of materials or services over a defined term. Captures contract number, contract type (blanket PO, scheduling agreement, value contract, quantity contract), effective/expiration dates, total contract value, target/release quantities, auto-renewal flag, SLA terms, penalty clauses, price escalation/de-escalation mechanisms, compliance status, and contract owner. Distinct from sales contracts owned by the sales domain. Supports contract coverage KPI tracking and maverick spend identification.
products.reference: SAP MM Outline Agreement (EKKO type MK/WK) / SAP Ariba Contract

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the procurement contract record. Primary key.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.agreement. Business justification: Procurement contracts (release orders/scheduling agreements in finance) are executed under overarching supplier framework agreements. This hierarchy is fundamental to contract management in industrial

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Procurement contracts contain a `material_category` STRING field that maps to the commodity category taxonomy. Replacing this with commodity_category_id FK normalizes contract classification to the au

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Procurement contracts are established under a specific company code (legal entity) in SAP MM. This FK links contracts to the legal entity master, enabling entity-level contract spend reporting, compli

attributes.name: product_family_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Supports 'Family‑Level Supply Contract Management' allowing contracts to be associated with product families for volume‑discount negotiations.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier or vendor party with whom this procurement contract is established.

attributes.name: vendor_site_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Procurement contracts in industrial manufacturing are often site-specific — delivery terms, lead times, and MOQs are negotiated per supplier site. delivery_location is a plain-text denormalization of

attributes.name: amendment_count
attributes.description: Total number of amendments or change orders issued against this contract since its original approval.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the contract was formally approved by authorized signatories and became legally binding.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the contract automatically renews upon expiration if not explicitly terminated. True for auto-renewing contracts, False otherwise.

attributes.name: compliance_status
attributes.description: Current compliance state of the contract with internal procurement policies, regulatory requirements, and governance standards. Compliant indicates full adherence, non-compliant flags violations, under review indicates active audit, waived indicates approved exception.

attributes.name: confidentiality_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the contract includes confidentiality or non-disclosure provisions protecting proprietary information. True if confidentiality terms are present, False otherwise.

attributes.name: contract_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the scope, purpose, and key terms of the procurement contract.

attributes.name: contract_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name or title of the procurement contract for easy identification and reference.

attributes.name: contract_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the procurement contract, typically assigned by SAP Ariba or ERP system.

attributes.name: contract_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the procurement contract. Draft indicates initial creation, pending approval awaits authorization, active is in force, suspended is temporarily halted, expired has passed end date, terminated is cancelled before expiration, closed is completed and archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: contract_type
attributes.description: Classification of the procurement contract structure. Blanket PO for recurring purchases with predefined terms, scheduling agreement for delivery schedules, value contract for spend-based limits, quantity contract for volume-based commitments, framework agreement for multi-supplier arrangements, or master agreement for overarching terms.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this procurement contract record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the procurement contract becomes legally binding and operational.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the procurement contract term ends and is no longer in force. Nullable for open-ended contracts.

attributes.name: incoterms
attributes.description: Standardized trade terms defining responsibilities for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer between buyer and supplier per ICC Incoterms 2020. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: last_amendment_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the contract terms. Nullable if no amendments have been made.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this procurement contract record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: lead_time_days
attributes.description: Standard procurement lead time in days from purchase order release to delivery, as committed by the supplier in the contract.

attributes.name: minimum_order_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum quantity per release or purchase order required by the supplier under this contract. Nullable if no MOQ is specified.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Standard payment terms negotiated in the contract (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30 for early payment discounts).

attributes.name: penalty_clause
attributes.description: Description of financial penalties or liquidated damages applicable for supplier non-compliance with contract terms, SLA breaches, or quality failures.

attributes.name: price_deescalation_mechanism
attributes.description: Formula or methodology for reducing contract prices based on volume commitments, market conditions, or continuous improvement targets.

attributes.name: price_escalation_mechanism
attributes.description: Formula or methodology for adjusting contract prices over time based on indices (e.g., CPI, commodity indices), exchange rates, or negotiated percentage increases.

attributes.name: purchasing_group
attributes.description: Buyer group or category team within the purchasing organization responsible for this contract, typically aligned to commodity or material category.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization
attributes.description: Organizational unit or division responsible for negotiating and executing this procurement contract within the ERP system.

attributes.name: quality_requirements
attributes.description: Specific quality standards, certifications, inspection criteria, and acceptance testing requirements mandated in the contract (e.g., ISO 9001, PPAP, APQP, specific Cpk targets).

attributes.name: quantity_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for target quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters, HR for hours).

attributes.name: release_quantity
attributes.description: Cumulative quantity already released or called off against this contract through purchase orders or delivery schedules.

attributes.name: release_value
attributes.description: Cumulative monetary value already released or spent against this contract through purchase orders.

attributes.name: remaining_quantity
attributes.description: Quantity still available for release under this contract, calculated as target quantity minus release quantity.

attributes.name: remaining_value
attributes.description: Monetary value still available for release under this contract, calculated as total contract value minus release value.

attributes.name: renewal_term_months
attributes.description: Duration in months for each automatic renewal period if auto renewal is enabled. Nullable if auto renewal is not applicable.

attributes.name: sla_terms
attributes.description: Service level commitments and performance targets defined in the contract, such as lead time, on-time delivery rate, quality standards, and response times.

attributes.name: target_quantity
attributes.description: Planned or committed quantity of materials or services to be procured under this contract for quantity-based agreements. Nullable for value-based contracts.

attributes.name: termination_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the contract before expiration.

attributes.name: total_contract_value
attributes.description: Maximum monetary value committed under this procurement contract for value-based contracts, or estimated total spend for quantity-based contracts.

attributes.name: warranty_terms
attributes.description: Warranty coverage and duration provided by the supplier for materials or services under this contract, including defect remediation and replacement terms.

products.name: supplier_invoice
products.description: Supplier invoice document received from a vendor and processed for three-way match verification (PO–GR–Invoice). Captures invoice number, supplier invoice reference, invoice date, posting date, gross amount, tax amount, currency, payment due date, payment terms, invoice status (parked, posted, blocked, cleared), and tolerance check results. Serves as the SSOT for procurement-side payables within the procure-to-pay cycle.
products.reference: SAP MM/FI Logistics Invoice Verification (MIRO / RBKP)

attributes.name: supplier_invoice_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the supplier invoice record. Primary key.

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Supplier invoices contain a `material_category` STRING field that maps to the commodity category taxonomy. Replacing this with commodity_category_id FK normalizes spend classification to the authorita

attributes.name: company_code_id
attributes.description: FK to finance.company_code

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Invoice posting requires cost center to allocate vendor expenses to the correct internal unit.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor invoices are posted to GL accounts for expense recognition per accounting policy.

attributes.name: plant_id
attributes.description: FK to production.plant

attributes.name: procurement_contract_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Supplier invoices are frequently issued against procurement contracts (blanket/framework agreements) in industrial manufacturing. Linking supplier_invoice to procurement_contract enables contract util

attributes.name: procurement_goods_receipt_id
attributes.description: Reference to the goods receipt document used in three-way match verification.

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice is matched in the three-way match process (PO–GR–Invoice).

attributes.name: sku_master_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: supplier_invoice has material_number as a plain denormalized text field. Replacing with FK to sku_master enables material spend analytics, three-way match validation by SKU, and purchased component co

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) who issued this invoice.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date the invoice was approved for payment.

attributes.name: baseline_date
attributes.description: The reference date from which payment terms are calculated, typically the invoice date or goods receipt date.

attributes.name: blocking_reason
attributes.description: The reason why the invoice is blocked for payment, such as price variance, quantity variance, or quality issues.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Any discount amount applied to the invoice, such as early payment discounts or volume discounts.

attributes.name: document_date
attributes.description: The date recorded on the invoice document, which may differ from the invoice date or posting date.

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: The exchange rate applied for currency conversion if the invoice currency differs from the company currency.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice is posted.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which the invoice is posted for accounting purposes.

attributes.name: freight_amount
attributes.description: The freight or shipping charges included in the invoice.

attributes.name: gross_amount
attributes.description: The total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments.

attributes.name: invoice_date
attributes.description: The date the invoice was issued by the supplier. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice.

attributes.name: invoice_number
attributes.description: The unique invoice number assigned by the supplier. This is the externally-known business identifier for the invoice.

attributes.name: invoice_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the procure-to-pay workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parked|posted|blocked|cleared|cancelled|rejected|pending_approval — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: invoice_type
attributes.description: The type or category of the invoice document.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the invoice record was last modified.

attributes.name: net_amount
attributes.description: The total invoice amount after all taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the amount payable to the supplier.

attributes.name: payment_block_indicator
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the invoice is blocked for payment.

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: The actual date the payment was made to the supplier.

attributes.name: payment_due_date
attributes.description: The date by which payment must be made to the supplier according to the payment terms.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: The method by which payment will be made to the supplier.

attributes.name: payment_reference_number
attributes.description: The reference number assigned to the payment transaction when the invoice is paid.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current payment status indicating whether the invoice has been paid.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: The agreed payment terms between the buyer and supplier, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30.

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: The date the invoice was posted to the financial accounting system.

attributes.name: purchasing_group
attributes.description: The purchasing group or buyer responsible for the procurement transaction.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization
attributes.description: The organizational unit responsible for procurement activities related to this invoice.

attributes.name: reference
attributes.description: Additional reference number or code provided by the supplier on the invoice document for their internal tracking.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The total tax amount applied to the invoice, including VAT, sales tax, or other applicable taxes.

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: The tax code applied to the invoice for tax calculation purposes.

attributes.name: tax_jurisdiction
attributes.description: The tax jurisdiction or authority under which the invoice tax is calculated.

attributes.name: three_way_match_status
attributes.description: Status of the three-way match verification process comparing purchase order, goods receipt, and invoice.

attributes.name: tolerance_check_status
attributes.description: Result of the tolerance check comparing invoice amounts against purchase order and goods receipt amounts.

attributes.name: tolerance_variance_amount
attributes.description: The amount by which the invoice differs from the expected amount based on the purchase order and goods receipt.

attributes.name: tolerance_variance_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage variance between the invoice amount and the expected amount.

attributes.name: wbs_element
attributes.description: The WBS element for project-related invoices, linking the invoice to a specific project phase or deliverable.

attributes.name: withholding_tax_amount
attributes.description: The amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment.

products.name: commodity_category
products.description: Commodity and spend category taxonomy used to classify all purchased materials and services into a hierarchical structure aligned with UNSPSC or internal category codes. Captures category code, category name, category level (L1/L2/L3), parent category, spend type (direct/indirect/MRO/CapEx), category owner, preferred supplier flag, and strategic sourcing priority. Enables category management, spend cube construction, and sourcing strategy alignment.
products.reference: UNSPSC Commodity Classification / SAP Ariba Category Hierarchy

attributes.name: commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Primary key for commodity_category

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Commodity categories are mapped to GL accounts in SAP MM for automatic account determination. This FK links the spend taxonomy to the chart of accounts, enabling automatic GL account assignment during

attributes.name: parent_category_commodity_category_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent category in the hierarchy. Null for top-level (L1) categories.

attributes.name: category_code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code identifying the commodity category. May align with UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code) or internal taxonomy.

attributes.name: category_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the commodity category scope, including typical materials, services, or products classified under this category.

attributes.name: category_level
attributes.description: Hierarchical level of the category within the taxonomy structure. L1 represents top-level, L2 sub-category, L3 detailed category, etc.

attributes.name: category_manager_name
attributes.description: Full name of the category manager responsible for strategic sourcing and supplier relationships within this category.

attributes.name: category_name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the commodity category (e.g., Electrical Components, Raw Steel, MRO Supplies).

attributes.name: category_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the commodity category within the procurement taxonomy.

attributes.name: compliance_requirement_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this category has specific regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., RoHS, REACH, conflict minerals, ISO certifications).

attributes.name: contract_coverage_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this category is covered by active procurement contracts or framework agreements.

attributes.name: cost_reduction_target_pct
attributes.description: Annual cost reduction target percentage for this category as part of strategic sourcing initiatives.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this commodity category record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when this commodity category classification became effective in the procurement taxonomy.

attributes.name: environmental_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this category requires environmental compliance certifications such as ISO 14001, RoHS, REACH, or conflict minerals reporting.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when this commodity category classification expires or is scheduled for review. Null for active categories without planned expiration.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this commodity category record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date when this category was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and alignment with business needs.

attributes.name: lead_time_days_avg
attributes.description: Average procurement lead time in days for materials and services within this category, from requisition to delivery.

attributes.name: moq_applicable_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether suppliers in this category typically enforce minimum order quantity requirements.

attributes.name: negotiation_frequency
attributes.description: Typical frequency of contract negotiations and RFx events for this commodity category.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this commodity category classification and sourcing strategy.

attributes.name: preferred_supplier_count
attributes.description: Number of preferred suppliers currently approved and active for this commodity category.

attributes.name: preferred_supplier_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this category has designated preferred suppliers with negotiated contracts or framework agreements.

attributes.name: price_volatility_index
attributes.description: Classification of price volatility for commodities in this category based on historical price fluctuations and market dynamics.

attributes.name: purchasing_group
attributes.description: Code identifying the purchasing group (buyer team) responsible for operational procurement within this category.

attributes.name: purchasing_organization
attributes.description: Code identifying the purchasing organization responsible for procurement activities within this category. Aligns with SAP organizational structure.

attributes.name: quality_certification_required
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of required quality certifications for suppliers in this category (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, AS9100).

attributes.name: remarks
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this commodity category.

attributes.name: risk_classification
attributes.description: Risk classification based on supply chain vulnerability, supplier concentration, geopolitical factors, and material criticality.

attributes.name: sourcing_strategy
attributes.description: Defined sourcing strategy for this category: single-source, dual-source, multi-source, global sourcing, or local sourcing approach.

attributes.name: spend_type
attributes.description: Classification of spend type: direct materials (production inputs), indirect materials (non-production), MRO (Maintenance, Repair, Operations), CapEx (Capital Expenditure), or services.

attributes.name: strategic_sourcing_priority
attributes.description: Priority level assigned to this category for strategic sourcing initiatives based on spend volume, supply risk, and business impact.

attributes.name: unspsc_class
attributes.description: Third-level UNSPSC class name within the family (e.g., Cutting and Forming Machines).

attributes.name: unspsc_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC)
attributes.description: Eight-digit UNSPSC classification code for global commodity standardization and cross-industry spend analysis.

attributes.name: unspsc_commodity
attributes.description: Fourth-level UNSPSC commodity name, the most granular classification level (e.g., CNC Milling Machines).

attributes.name: unspsc_family
attributes.description: Second-level UNSPSC family name within the segment (e.g., Metalworking Machinery and Equipment).

attributes.name: unspsc_segment
attributes.description: Top-level UNSPSC segment name (e.g., Industrial Manufacturing and Processing Machinery and Accessories).

products.name: cost_center
products.description: Master record for all SAP CO cost centers representing organizational units responsible for incurring costs. Captures cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area assignment, profit center assignment, currency, validity period, cost center category (production, administration, R&D, sales), and standard hierarchy position. Serves as the SSOT for internal cost allocation, overhead absorption, and management accounting structures. The primary controlling object for budget assignment, actual cost collection, and variance analysis in manufacturing operations.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the cost center.

attributes.name: parent_cost_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the immediate parent cost center in the hierarchy.

attributes.name: actual_cost
attributes.description: Actual costs incurred by the cost center.

attributes.name: allocation_basis
attributes.description: Method used to allocate costs from this center to other entities.

attributes.name: budget_amount
attributes.description: Planned budget allocated to the cost center for the fiscal period.

attributes.name: controlling_area_code
attributes.description: Code of the controlling area to which the cost center belongs.

attributes.name: cost_center_code
attributes.description: Business key used to identify the cost center in financial transactions.

attributes.name: cost_center_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the cost center's purpose and scope.

attributes.name: cost_center_group
attributes.description: Logical grouping of cost centers for reporting or allocation purposes.

attributes.name: cost_center_name
attributes.description: Human‑readable name of the cost center.

attributes.name: cost_center_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center.

attributes.name: cost_center_type
attributes.description: Classification of the cost center by functional area.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the cost center record was first created.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Currency in which the cost center's financials are recorded.

attributes.name: external_reference
attributes.description: Identifier used to map the cost center to external systems or legacy codes.

attributes.name: hierarchy_level
attributes.description: Depth of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy.

attributes.name: hierarchy_path
attributes.description: Slash‑delimited path representing the cost center's position in the hierarchy (e.g., "/100/200/300").

attributes.name: is_overhead
attributes.description: Indicates whether the cost center is used for overhead cost allocation.

attributes.name: location_code
attributes.description: Code of the physical location or plant associated with the cost center.

attributes.name: owner_department
attributes.description: Organizational department that owns the cost center.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost center record.

attributes.name: variance_amount
attributes.description: Difference between budgeted and actual costs.

attributes.name: valid_from
attributes.description: Date when the cost center becomes effective.

attributes.name: valid_to
attributes.description: Date when the cost center is retired or becomes inactive.

products.name: profit_center
products.description: Master record for profit centers representing autonomous business segments within the manufacturing enterprise used for internal profitability reporting. Captures profit center hierarchy, controlling area, responsible manager, segment classification, currency, and validity dates. Enables P&L reporting at sub-entity level per SAP EC-PCA (Profit Center Accounting) aligned with IFRS segment reporting.

attributes.description: Link to the primary cost center that supplies cost data for this profit center.

attributes.name: parent_profit_center_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the immediate parent profit center in the hierarchy, if any.

attributes.name: actual_profit
attributes.description: Realized profit for the profit center in the reporting period.

attributes.name: budget_amount
attributes.description: Approved budget amount for the profit center for the fiscal period.

attributes.name: controlling_area
attributes.description: Code of the controlling area to which the profit center belongs for internal cost accounting.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the profit center record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 currency code in which the profit center records its functional amounts.

attributes.description: Line of financial reporting hierarchy (e.g., segment, division, business unit) used for IFRS segment reporting.

attributes.name: hierarchy_level
attributes.description: Numeric level indicating the profit center's depth in the organizational hierarchy.

attributes.name: is_reportable

attributes.name: oee_target_percent
attributes.description: Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage assigned to the profit center for performance benchmarking.

products.name: gl_account
products.description: General ledger account master record serving as the SSOT for the chart of accounts used across all financial postings. Captures account number, account type (balance sheet, P&L), account group, reconciliation account flag, currency, tax category, field status group, cost element category (primary/secondary for CO integration), functional area, and IFRS/GAAP classification. Bridges financial accounting and management accounting by carrying cost element attributes that enable controlling postings. Governs all financial postings and is the backbone of the enterprise chart of accounts.

products.name: company_code

Captures company code identifier, company name, country, currency, fiscal year variant, chart of accounts assignment, controlling area assignment, consolidation group, consolidation method, and elimination scope. Serves as the organizational anchor for all financial postings, statutory reporting under IFRS/GAAP, and the consolidation unit for group financial statement preparation.

products.name: journal_entry
products.description: Core transactional record capturing all general ledger postings at both document header and line item level, serving as the single source of truth for the complete financial audit trail. Header attributes include document number, document type, posting date, fiscal year, period, company code, currency, exchange rate, reference document, reversal indicator, and IFRS/GAAP compliance flags. Line item attributes include line number, GL account, debit/credit indicator, amount in transaction and local currencies, cost center, profit center, WBS element reference, internal order, tax code, assignment field, and posting text. Covers manual journal entries, automated system postings, accruals, reversals, reclassifications, and period-end closing entries. Foundation for trial balance, financial statements, reconciliation, and audit evidence.
products.reference: SAP FI Journal Entry / Accounting Document (FB50)
